  • Primary sources: earnings calls and quarterly result releases (2023–2024), Form 20-F / interim filing disclosures, financial press characterization

Firm Overview

Bitdeer Technologies Group is a Singapore-headquartered, Nasdaq-listed digital-asset computing company whose core business during 2017–2024 was bitcoin mining plus related cloud hash rate and hosting services. By 2023–2024 it was also developing and supplying proprietary mining rigs and expanding datacenter/power capacity, while beginning to layer in AI/HPC activity alongside its crypto operations. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The dominant exposure is miners. Across 2023 and 2024 disclosures, Bitdeer repeatedly reported self-mining bitcoins, self-mining hash rate, mining-machine deployment, and large self-mining revenue; management discussed the effect of the April 2024 halving and global network hash rate on results. (sec.gov)

A meaningful secondary exposure is finserv because Bitdeer also sold cloud hash rate and hosting products tied directly to digital-asset computing, with separate revenue lines for Cloud Hash Rate, General Hosting, and Membership Hosting in both 2023 and 2024. Those activities directly intermediate crypto-computing capacity for customers, making them closer to crypto financial/platform services than to generic business services. (sec.gov)

A small infrastr weight is warranted because Bitdeer explicitly operated mining datacenters, provided hosting capacity, and by 2024 was emphasizing power capacity and proprietary SEALMINER rig deployment. However, infrastructure is not the primary mechanism because the company’s economics remained more directly tied to mining and crypto-service revenues than to being a pure picks-and-shovels supplier. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

A weight of 0.6 miners is assigned because self-mining was the clearest and most persistent exposure: self-mining revenue reached US$163.1 million in 2024, versus total revenue of US$349.8 million, and management repeatedly framed quarter-to-quarter performance around self-mining hash rate, bitcoins mined, and halving effects. 0.3 finserv captures the longstanding cloud-hash-rate and hosting businesses, which generated distinct crypto-related service revenue streams in 2023–2024. 0.1 infrastr reflects Bitdeer’s mining-datacenter, hosting-capacity, and mining-rig platform role, but that remains subordinate to its direct mining and crypto-service activities. No holders weight is assigned because disclosures state the company generally did not hold self-mined crypto and promptly converted it into fiat; year-end crypto balances appear operational rather than a deliberate treasury-investment strategy under the project rules. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main boundary issue is between finserv and infrastr. Hosting and cloud hash rate could be read as infrastructure support, but because Bitdeer monetized crypto-computing access through customer-facing crypto-linked service lines, I place more weight in finserv than infrastr. A second issue is the company’s cryptocurrency balances: Bitdeer reported US$77.5 million of cryptocurrencies at December 31, 2024, but available evidence indicates these were primarily generated through operations rather than held under an explicit treasury-accumulation policy, so I exclude holders. Reuters evidence specifically on crypto-proxy investor flows for BTDR was limited, but broad market coverage of bitcoin miners as trading proxies for bitcoin supports a cautious invbase = 1 inference for this name. (sec.gov)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Nov. 18, 2024, Q3 2024 earnings call transcript: management opened the call as “Bitdeer’s third quarter 2024 earnings conference call,” anchoring contemporaneous discussion of the firm’s crypto-linked results. — Seeking Alpha transcript
  • Nov. 18, 2024, Q3 2024 results / management remarks: self-mining revenue was “US$31.5 million” and cloud hash rate plus hosting revenue declined as machines were reallocated and hosting economics changed after the halving. — SEC 6-K exhibit / Q3 2024 results
  • Nov. 9, 2023, Q3 2023 results / management remarks: “Bitdeer is committed to providing comprehensive digital asset computing solutions,” while reporting self-mining, cloud hash rate and hosting as separate business lines. — SEC 6-K exhibit / Q3 2023 results

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Apr. 28, 2023, Form 20-F for FY2022: Bitdeer filed its annual report as a foreign private issuer, confirming the listed entity and reporting structure. — SEC filing index
  • Mar. 4, 2024, preliminary Q4 2023 results: self-mining revenue was about US$46.9 million, cloud hash rate revenue US$16.3 million, general hosting revenue US$25.2 million, and membership hosting revenue US$23.4 million. — SEC 6-K exhibit / Q4 2023 preliminary results
  • Nov. 18, 2024, Q3 2024 results: as of September 30, 2024, the company held US$39.7 million in cryptocurrencies and total assets of US$1.046 billion. — SEC 6-K exhibit / Q3 2024 results
  • Feb. 25, 2025, FY2024 results covering Dec. 31, 2024 year-end: 2024 total revenue was US$349.8 million, including US$163.1 million self-mining, US$39.8 million cloud hash rate, US$67.6 million general hosting, and US$64.0 million membership hosting; crypto balance was US$77.5 million. — SEC 6-K exhibit / Q4 & FY2024 results
  • Aug. 2024, interim financial filing: “For the six months ended June 30, 2024 and 2023, the Group’s revenue is substantially generated from cryptocurrency-related operations.” — SEC interim filing exhibit

Financial news / other:

  • Nov. 18, 2024, market summary of Q3 2024 release: Bitdeer was presented as a blockchain and high-performance-computing company with US$39.7 million of cryptocurrencies on hand, reinforcing market treatment as a crypto-linked equity. — Seeking Alpha news summary
  • Broad 2024–2025 market coverage and company communications consistently positioned BTDR among publicly traded bitcoin-mining names whose shares trade with crypto sentiment; this supports coding the investor-base amplifier as present, though evidence is less direct than the operating evidence. — Bitdeer FY2024 results exhibit

BANXA HOLDINGS

  • Rank: 30 | Expo: 5.96 | id_str: CA06683R1010
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.9, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call / webcast transcript (Q3 FY2024), FY2024 and FY2025 Q1 MD&A, interim financial statements

Firm Overview

Banxa Holdings Inc. is a listed fintech and crypto-payments company that provides fiat-to-crypto and crypto-to-fiat on/off-ramp services for wallets, exchanges, and other digital-asset platforms. Across its filings and management presentations, the firm describes itself as a gateway between traditional payment rails and cryptocurrencies, with a business model built around crypto transaction processing, liquidity, compliance, and embedded integration for partners. (investor.banxa.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The dominant exposure channel is finserv. Banxa explicitly states that it earns revenue from the sale of cryptocurrencies, commissions, and spreads; its filings distinguish principal and agency crypto transaction revenue, and management repeatedly frames the business as a fiat/crypto conversion platform embedded into wallets and digital-asset applications. (investor.banxa.com)

A small infrastr weight is also warranted because Banxa presents itself as an “infrastructure provider” enabling embedded crypto and highlights its API/widget, payment rails, licensing stack, fraud controls, and compliance tooling for third-party platforms. However, that infrastructure role is subordinate to its direct handling and sale/facilitation of crypto transactions, so it is not the primary mechanism. (banxa.investorroom.com)

No holders weight is assigned. Banxa does report cryptocurrency inventories, but filings say these are held for resale in the ordinary course of business as inventory and, in some cases, under custody for customer transactions; under the project rules that is operational finserv exposure rather than treasury holding. (investor.banxa.com)

Weight Assignment

A weight of 0.9 finserv is assigned because Banxa directly handles crypto conversion flows and records crypto-related transaction activity as its core business. FY2024 revenue was A$321.2 million, of which A$302.8 million came from “sale and purchase of cryptocurrencies” and A$17.2 million from “commissions and spread from services,” while management described the firm as a gateway between fiat and crypto and the Q3 FY2024 call focused on processing crypto volume, wallets, licenses, and partner expansion. 0.1 infrastr captures the firm’s embedded-crypto rails, widget/API, regulatory and payments stack, and self-description as an infrastructure provider, but these features mainly support its primary financial-service function rather than constituting a standalone picks-and-shovels model. (investor.banxa.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main classification boundary is between finserv and infrastr. Banxa’s own language often emphasizes “infrastructure,” but the underlying economics in filings show a company that directly sells or facilitates cryptocurrency transactions and earns related spreads/fees, which fits finserv more closely under the stated rules. Evidence for invbase = 1 is somewhat less direct than the operating evidence, but management discussed share-price underperformance during a positive crypto cycle and external coverage framed Banxa as a crypto-linked equity whose growth depends on broader crypto adoption, supporting a cautious investor-base amplifier assignment. (investor.banxa.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • May 22, 2024, Q3 FY2024 webcast transcript: “My name is Holger Arians, I'm co-Founder, CEO and Chairman of Banxa. I'm please to present the quarter 3, FY '24 unaudited financials to you today.” Management then highlighted crypto volume, wallet partners, and the firm’s ability to scale crypto transactions profitably. — Alpha Spread transcript
  • May 22, 2024, Q3 FY2024 earnings presentation: management said Banxa was “entering another positive crypto cycle,” had sold over USD$2.4B of crypto, and was embedded in applications reaching 1bn users. — Banxa Q3 FY24 presentation
  • May 23, 2024, earnings-call summary: Banxa reported profitability in Q3 FY2024, over $2.4 billion in processed crypto sales, and continued work on UK, US, and EU crypto licensing. — Investing.com earnings-call summary

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Dec. 5, 2024, FY2024 MD&A: Banxa said it focuses on “acting as a gateway between the traditional fiat currencies and cryptocurrencies” and earns revenue from sale of cryptocurrencies, commissions, and spreads. — FY2024 MD&A
  • Dec. 5, 2024, FY2024 MD&A: FY2024 revenue was A$321.2 million, including A$302.8 million from sale and purchase of cryptocurrencies and A$17.2 million from commissions and spread from services. — FY2024 MD&A
  • Dec. 5, 2024, FY2024 MD&A: cryptocurrency inventory was only A$348,255 versus total assets of A$14.4 million, indicating operational inventory rather than a large crypto treasury. — FY2024 MD&A
  • May 21, 2024, interim financial statements for period ended Mar. 31, 2024: Banxa disclosed that cryptocurrencies are held for sale in the ordinary course of business and that the company may control cryptocurrencies in custody or with exchanges before sale to customers. — Q3 FY24 financial statements
  • Dec. 5, 2024, FY2025 Q1 MD&A: Banxa described its B2B product as a fiat aggregator for the digital asset industry, offering conversion widget/API products, managed AML compliance, and crypto-chargeback expertise. — FY2025 Q1 MD&A

Financial news / other:

  • Feb. 22, 2024, company release: Banxa described itself as “the leading infrastructure provider for enabling embedded crypto,” emphasizing payment solutions and regulatory licenses. — Banxa investor release
  • May 23, 2024, Investing.com: management acknowledged the stock price did not reflect operating progress and linked outlook to broader crypto adoption and investor interest. — Investing.com earnings-call summary

US GLOBAL INVRS.

  • Rank: 31 | Expo: 5.51 | id_str: US9029521005
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.4, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.6
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (2021, 2024), SEC filings (FY2024 10-K, FY2025 10-Q/10-K), company press releases

Firm Overview

U.S. Global Investors, Inc. is a San Antonio-based registered investment adviser and asset manager listed on Nasdaq as GROW. Its core business is managing mutual funds and ETFs, but during 2017–2024 it also maintained a meaningful crypto-linked exposure through its sponsorship, investment and ongoing economic relationship with HIVE, a public crypto miner, and management repeatedly framed that relationship as the company’s way to participate in the crypto ecosystem.

Exposure Channels Identified

The main identifiable crypto mechanism is finserv, not mining or direct treasury holding. U.S. Global Investors is itself a financial-services firm, and management repeatedly described HIVE as the company’s “proxy” or “way to play” the crypto space after being unable to launch a Bitcoin ETF, indicating that crypto exposure reached GROW primarily through investment/asset-management channels rather than through direct operation of mining infrastructure. The company did not itself mine Bitcoin or Ethereum, and its filings describe holdings in HIVE securities and related consulting income rather than direct ownership of cryptocurrency on its own balance sheet. The evidence is therefore substantive but somewhat indirect, so a large residual unclass weight remains.

Weight Assignment

A weight of 0.4 finserv is assigned because crypto exposure was repeatedly discussed by management as part of GROW’s investment/asset-management strategy, with HIVE treated as a crypto proxy and the company holding HIVE debentures and shares while earning related consulting income. The remaining 0.6 unclass reflects that GROW’s principal business remained conventional fund management, most reported revenue came from advisory fees rather than crypto activities, and the firm neither directly mined cryptocurrency nor clearly operated a crypto exchange, custody, payment, or fund product during the 2017–2024 sample period.

Ambiguities / Limitations

The key boundary is between finserv and unclass. GROW had clear crypto-linked exposure through HIVE, but much of that exposure was indirect and investment-based rather than a standalone crypto operating segment. No evidence was found that GROW itself held Bitcoin as a treasury asset, directly mined coins, or launched a dedicated crypto ETF during the sample period. Transcript evidence was available, but some transcripts were accessible only through secondary transcript hosts rather than the company’s own archive for the full sample.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • September 11, 2024, Q4 FY2024 transcript: “We still have to try to launch the first Bitcoin ETF… We couldn’t do that in 2017… And we went out and we created the first crypto mining company to go public, Hive.” — Investing.com transcript
  • February 2025, Q2 FY2025 transcript: “HIVE Digital was a company we launched and created, and co-founded in 2017 because it was, we were unable to launch a Bitcoin ETF, and it’s been our proxy in that whole space.” — Investing.com transcript
  • May 2021, Q3 FY2021 transcript: “What for investors in GROW, what you recognize is that HIVE is our model. It’s our way to play the crypto phenomena that’s taking place globally.” — Roic AI transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • September 2025, FY2025 10-K: U.S. Global disclosed HIVE investments with fair value of about $1.6 million at June 30, 2025 and $4.4 million at June 30, 2024; it had purchased HIVE convertible debentures in January 2021. — FY2025 Form 10-K
  • September 2025, FY2025 10-K: the company said it earned advisory-fee revenue of $6.6 million from ETF clients in fiscal 2025 and $9.4 million in fiscal 2024, showing crypto was not the dominant revenue line. — FY2025 Form 10-K
  • May 2025, March 31 2025 Form 10-Q: the company stated that HIVE convertible securities purchased for $15.0 million in January 2021 had a fair value of $2.3 million as of March 31, 2025, and highlighted volatility tied to cryptocurrency markets and related securities. — March 31, 2025 Form 10-Q
  • February 2025, December 31 2024 Form 10-Q: the filing included explicit risk disclosure on cryptocurrency volatility and noted a $750,000 receivable for HIVE principal repayments as of December 31, 2024. — December 31, 2024 Form 10-Q
  • May 10, 2021, company release: management said it sought to keep participating in the “crypto-mining ecosystem” while reducing portfolio volatility by repositioning its HIVE investment. — GlobeNewswire release

Financial news / other:

  • January 11, 2022, company release: U.S. Global said its “principal exposure to the burgeoning crypto-mining industry remains HIVE Blockchain Technologies Ltd.” — GlobeNewswire release
  • May 9, 2022, company release: management told investors that HIVE’s crypto exposure and the volatility of Bitcoin and Ethereum affected the value of GROW’s HIVE position. — GlobeNewswire release
  • June 2021, company commentary: Frank Holmes wrote from the Bitcoin 2021 Conference alongside HIVE representatives and discussed HIVE’s Bitcoin and Ether mining, reinforcing market perception of GROW as a crypto-linked equity via HIVE. — USFunds article

REALBOTIX

  • Rank: 32 | Expo: 5.31 | id_str: CA75604K1075
  • Weight summary: holders 0.8, miners 0.0, finserv 0.1, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.1
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 3
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (Q3 2023, Q1 2024, Q2 2024, Q3 2024), annual results / corporate updates (2022–2024)

Firm Overview

REALBOTIX, formerly Tokens.com Corp, is a Canada-listed micro-cap that shifted during 2024 from a crypto- and web3-branded company toward AI humanoid robotics after acquiring Simulacra and later rebranding as Realbotix. Across 2017–2024, the clearest recurring crypto exposure came from holding a sizable inventory of cryptocurrencies—primarily Ethereum—and earning some staking income on those holdings, rather than from mining or running a full-service crypto exchange or custody platform. (businesswire.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The dominant channel is holders. Management repeatedly described the company as owning substantial crypto inventory, mainly ETH, using that inventory as part of treasury/capital allocation, and recognizing quarterly mark-to-market gains and losses from those holdings. On the Q1 2024 call, management said it was “sitting on some -- a lot of Ethereum,” and on the Q3 2024 call explained that the firm must “revalue our digital assets or crypto every quarter,” with those changes flowing through net income. (alphaspread.com)

A smaller finserv weight is warranted because Tokens.com also generated explicit staking revenue from owned tokens, and management described a “crypto staking segment” in which its token inventory was used to validate blockchain transactions and earn additional tokens. However, this appears secondary to balance-sheet holding exposure, and there is no strong evidence during 2017–2024 that the company operated a broad crypto brokerage, exchange, or institutional custody platform. (businesswire.com)

The firm also qualifies for invbase = 1 because management explicitly framed the company as a crypto-facing equity and discussed the Tokens.com name as “too crypto-centric,” while also saying the domain was “probably the best name in crypto right now”; in April 2024 it further told investors the company would provide “exposure to: (a) blockchain, via its cryptocurrency inventory.” That is direct evidence that the stock was being positioned and marketed as a crypto proxy to investors. (alphaspread.com)

Weight Assignment

A weight of 0.8 holders is assigned because the most consistent and economically material crypto mechanism across the sample is balance-sheet ownership of ETH and other tokens, with management emphasizing token inventory, opportunistic selling, and recurring revaluation gains/losses. A smaller 0.1 finserv reflects the company’s staking activity and staking revenue, which involved direct participation in proof-of-stake validation but remained modest relative to the firm’s overall crypto-asset exposure. The remaining 0.1 unclass captures business-model transition noise—metaverse, gaming, domains, and robotics activities—which means crypto was important but not the sole organizing principle of the company by 2024. (businesswire.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main classification boundary is between holders and finserv. Because the firm earned staking revenue, one could argue for a larger finserv allocation, but the evidence shows staking was primarily a yield activity on treasury-like crypto inventory rather than a standalone customer-facing crypto financial-service franchise. No evidence was found of proof-of-work mining, purpose-built mining infrastructure, or non-financial service provision to third-party crypto clients as the firm’s core crypto mechanism. Transcript coverage before 2023 was limited in accessible public search results, but the available 2023–2024 transcript evidence was sufficient to stabilize the classification.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • August 2023, Q3 2023 transcript: “we ended the quarter strong with $4.3 million in cash and $7.9 million in liquid Tokens” and management added “if there was another crypto run, you might see us taking some of our tokens off the table and converting into fiat.” — Alpha Spread transcript
  • February 2024, Q1 2024 transcript: “We recorded a $2.6 million gain on the revaluation of the cryptos, mostly the Ethereum” and CEO said “we're sitting on some -- a lot of Ethereum here.” — Alpha Spread transcript
  • May 2024, Q2 2024 transcript: “Our revenue for the quarter was $286,000 from staking... primarily, that is from staking.” — Alpha Spread transcript
  • August 2024, Q3 2024 transcript: “we have to revalue our digital assets or crypto every quarter” and “we did receive close to 200 Ethereum back from Genesis.” — Alpha Spread transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • December 29, 2022, FY2022 results: the company said its strategy was to “limit our exposure to Layer 1 cryptocurrencies like Ethereum,” reported staking revenue of $552k for 2022, and disclosed large digital-asset revaluation swings. — Business Wire FY2022 results
  • November 8, 2023, corporate update: Tokens.com said it “focused on building web3 projects and holding an inventory of crypto assets for staking,” listed 3,313 ETH and other tokens worth about $8.1 million, and said the crypto inventory “largely remains staked and earning revenue.” — Business Wire corporate update
  • January 31, 2024, FY2023 results: the company reported digital assets / cryptocurrency and NFTs of $6.0 million at September 30, 2023 and disclosed detailed token holdings led by Ethereum. — Business Wire FY2023 results
  • January 28, 2025, FY2024 results covering September 30, 2024 year-end: Realbotix reported total digital assets and cash of $9.7 million and said it continued to stake about 60% of owned ETH while keeping about 40% unstaked for opportunistic selling; it also sold 250 ETH in December 2024 and January 2025 for working capital. — Business Wire FY2024 results

Financial news / other:

  • April 4, 2024, acquisition announcement: management said that after acquiring Simulacra, Tokens.com would still provide investors exposure to “blockchain, via its cryptocurrency inventory.” — Business Wire acquisition release
  • May 2024, Q2 2024 transcript: CEO said the company determined its name was “too crypto-centric” and that Tokens.com was “probably the best name in crypto right now as a domain name,” indicating explicit crypto-proxy investor positioning. — Alpha Spread transcript

WISDOMTREE

  • Rank: 33 | Expo: 5.09 | id_str: US97717P1049
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings releases / call materials (2021–2024), 10-K filings (2022–2024)

Firm Overview

WisdomTree, Inc. is a U.S.-listed asset manager focused on ETFs, ETPs and related investment products, with operations spanning the U.S. and Europe. During 2017–2024, its crypto exposure fit primarily within digital-asset financial products and services, including crypto ETPs, a bitcoin fund, blockchain-enabled digital funds, and the WisdomTree Prime wallet/platform.

Exposure Channels Identified

The company fits finserv because management explicitly described crypto and tokenized-asset activity as part of its financial-services strategy rather than as treasury investing or mining. In January 2022, the CEO said WisdomTree Prime would support “saving, spending, and investing” in “native crypto assets” and tokenized assets, directly anchoring the classification in an earnings-related management statement. Subsequent 2022–2024 filings and releases show continued operation and expansion of crypto ETPs, the U.S. WisdomTree Bitcoin Fund (BTCW), blockchain-enabled mutual funds, digital wallet services, and institutional digital-asset infrastructure. No evidence was found that WisdomTree mined crypto, held crypto as a discretionary balance-sheet treasury position, or primarily served the ecosystem through non-financial business services.

Weight Assignment

A weight of 1.0 finserv is assigned because the evidence consistently points to direct provision, issuance, distribution and servicing of crypto-linked financial products. The most economically relevant crypto mechanism is fee-based asset management and platform activity around crypto ETPs, bitcoin products, digital funds and wallet-based digital-asset services. No separate holders weight is warranted because the record supports operational and product-linked crypto exposure rather than a treasury accumulation policy, and no evidence supports miners, bizserv or infrastr.

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main boundary is between finserv and holders because WisdomTree’s filings note gains and losses related to gold and crypto earned from management fees on physically backed ETPs. However, the instructions exclude crypto held to back or collateralize a financial product from holders, so this remains finserv. Publicly accessible full transcript pages were partly restricted, but at least one earnings-call-anchoring management quote was retrievable through the company’s earnings release pages, and corroborating 10-K evidence was strong enough to stabilize the classification. No direct evidence was found of a crypto-proxy investor-base narrative specific enough to code invbase = 1.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • January 28, 2022, FY2021 results / earnings release: “WisdomTree Prime is being built for saving, spending, and investing – in both native crypto assets or in tokenized versions of mainstream financial assets” — WisdomTree FY2021 results
  • January 28, 2022, FY2021 results / earnings release: management said digital assets initiatives were expected to “generate new revenue streams” and noted a team focused exclusively on “digital assets, blockchain and decentralized finance.” — WisdomTree FY2021 results
  • February 3, 2023, FY2022 results: WisdomTree said it had received broker-dealer approval “enabling it to facilitate transactions in blockchain-enabled funds offered in the WisdomTree Prime mobile application.” — WisdomTree FY2022 results
  • July 26, 2024, Q2 2024 results: the company said it was “one of the first issuers to list Crypto ETPs on the London Stock Exchange,” including the WisdomTree Physical Bitcoin ETP and Physical Ethereum ETP. — WisdomTree Q2 2024 results
  • January 31, 2025, Q4 2024 results covering December 31, 2024: WisdomTree reported that its Crypto ETP range exceeded $1.5 billion in AUM and described WisdomTree Prime digital wallet and digital asset services plus WisdomTree Connect as active platforms. — WisdomTree Q4 2024 results

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 3, 2023, 2022 Form 10-K: WisdomTree said its digital-assets strategy included Prime, tokenized gold and dollars, and SEC-effective digital or blockchain-enabled mutual funds; it also stated that WisdomTree Digital Movement sought to operate a platform for the “purchase, sale and exchange of digital assets.” — SEC 2022 Form 10-K
  • April 29, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K / annual report: the chairman’s letter said WisdomTree Prime offered access to blockchain-enabled mutual funds and select cryptocurrencies, and that availability had expanded to 41 states and nearly 75% of the U.S. population. — WisdomTree 2023 Annual Report
  • April 29, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K / annual report: the company said it was exploring partnerships that could “unlock additional tokenization revenue streams in the future.” — WisdomTree 2023 Annual Report
  • April 29, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K / annual report: the filing described Digital Funds issued through WisdomTree Digital Trust and wallet services through WisdomTree Prime facilitating tokenized-asset activity. — WisdomTree 2023 Annual Report
  • February 2, 2024, 8-K / FY2023 results: WisdomTree disclosed the January 2024 launch of the WisdomTree Bitcoin Fund (BTCW) in the U.S. and fee reductions across its European Crypto ETP range. — WisdomTree FY2023 8-K

Financial news / other:

  • May 13, 2024, stockholder letter: WisdomTree said its digital assets efforts began in 2021 and highlighted them as part of the company’s strategy for continued growth. — WisdomTree stockholder letter
  • January 31, 2025, Q4 2024 results: the company disclosed that gains and losses can arise from “gold and crypto earned from management fees paid by our physically-backed gold and crypto ETPs,” supporting product-linked rather than treasury-linked crypto exposure. — WisdomTree Q4 2024 results

ROBINHOOD MARKETS A

  • Rank: 34 | Expo: 4.97 | id_str: US7707001027
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (Q1 2022, Q2 2024), 2023 annual report / 10-K, Q4 2023 and Q4 2024 results releases, financial news

Firm Overview

Robinhood Markets, Inc. is a U.S.-listed retail financial platform offering brokerage, cash, retirement, and crypto products. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, crypto exposure appears as a direct financial-services line through Robinhood Crypto, customer custody, transfers, wallet/connect products, and crypto-linked transaction revenues rather than as mining or treasury investing.

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports finserv exposure only. Robinhood explicitly offers commission-free cryptocurrency trading, routes customer crypto orders to market makers, holds settled cryptocurrencies in custody on behalf of customers, supports transfers, and launched Robinhood Connect to fund Web3 wallets, all of which fit direct crypto financial intermediation. (sec.gov)

No evidence in the reviewed sources supports a holders classification, because the crypto assets discussed are customer assets held in custody or operational balances linked to the platform rather than a discretionary treasury accumulation policy. No evidence was found of mining activity, non-financial service provision to crypto firms, or crypto-directed infrastructure supply. (sec.gov)

The investor-base amplifier is coded 1 because external financial media and market commentary repeatedly treat Robinhood shares as highly sensitive to crypto trading sentiment and crypto-volume swings, and Robinhood’s own results show crypto revenues as a material driver of quarterly performance. That is consistent with a crypto-proxy shareholder narrative rather than merely generic sector adjacency. (coindesk.com)

Weight Assignment

A weight of 1.0 finserv is assigned because the strongest and most repeated evidence concerns Robinhood’s direct handling of crypto trading, custody, transfers, wallet funding, and crypto-related fee/rebate revenues. The business model clearly touches the asset itself through brokerage-style execution and custody services, which places the firm squarely in crypto financial services. No separate holders weight is warranted because the filings describe customer custody arrangements, not a treasury crypto investment policy, and no evidence supports miners, bizserv, or infrastr. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main boundary is between finserv and holders because Robinhood custodies large amounts of customer crypto. However, the instructions exclude crypto held to back or intermediate a financial service from holders, and the filing language makes clear these are customer custodial assets in omnibus wallets rather than treasury holdings. Public access to some IR transcript pages was partially restricted, but at least one transcript quote was retrievable and corroboration from filings and results releases was strong enough to make the classification stable. (sec.gov)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • May 5, 2022, Q1 2022 earnings call transcript: “And crypto revenue was $54 million” — Robinhood First Quarter 2022 Earnings Conference Call
  • August 7, 2024, Q2 2024 earnings call transcript: management said the Bitstamp acquisition would “accelerate our crypto road map, enabling us to serve a broader user base, enhance our capabilities, and provide additional liquidity for crypto trading.” — Motley Fool Q2 2024 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • April 2024, 2023 Annual Report / 10-K: Robinhood said it offers commission-free cryptocurrency trading in the U.S. through Robinhood Crypto and, as of December 2023, in select EU jurisdictions through Robinhood Europe. — SEC 2023 Annual Report
  • April 2024, 2023 Annual Report / 10-K: the filing states customers can buy, sell, and hold cryptocurrencies, and that Robinhood offers crypto recurring investments and cryptocurrency transfers. — SEC 2023 Annual Report
  • April 2024, 2023 Annual Report / 10-K: Robinhood disclosed that it holds settled cryptocurrencies in hot and cold wallets, does not use third-party custodians for settled cryptocurrencies, and maintains omnibus wallets on behalf of customers. — SEC 2023 Annual Report
  • February 13, 2024, Q4 2023 results: transaction-based revenues were driven in part by “cryptocurrencies revenue of $43 million,” and the company highlighted its EU crypto launch and addition of spot bitcoin ETFs. — Robinhood Q4 2023 results
  • May 8, 2024, Q1 2024 results: transaction-based revenues were driven by “cryptocurrencies revenue of $126 million, up 232%.” — Robinhood Q1 2024 results
  • February 12, 2025, Q4 2024 results covering December 31, 2024: transaction-based revenues were primarily driven by “cryptocurrencies revenue of $358 million, up over 700%.” — Robinhood Q4 2024 results

Financial news / other:

  • May 8, 2024, CoinDesk: Robinhood’s Q1 crypto notional volume rose 224% year over year and users’ crypto in custody reached $26.2 billion, reinforcing the materiality of direct crypto platform activity. — CoinDesk
  • January 11, 2024, CNBC: Robinhood shares were discussed alongside Coinbase as market instruments reacting to bitcoin ETF approval, supporting a crypto-proxy investor narrative. — CNBC
  • March 11, 2025, Reuters syndication: analyst commentary linked HOOD share weakness to slowing crypto revenues and bitcoin declines, indicating investor-base sensitivity to crypto sentiment. — Reuters syndication via TradingView

GOLDMONEY

  • Rank: 35 | Expo: 4.48 | id_str: VGG4001R1047
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: Goldmoney earnings call transcript (FY2018), company results releases (FY2018–FY2019), annual reports/shareholder letters (FY2020–FY2024)

Firm Overview

Goldmoney Inc. is a Canadian-listed financial company centered on precious-metals dealing, custody, storage, and related services. During 2017–2019, it also operated a crypto dealing and custody business within its platform, but by the 2020–2024 period its disclosed operating focus had shifted back to precious metals, lending, and later property-related activities.

Exposure Channels Identified

The relevant crypto mechanism is finserv. Goldmoney explicitly offered clients the ability to buy and sell crypto assets and provided cold-storage custody, which is direct crypto financial intermediation rather than a mere software or consulting service. Management discussed cryptocurrency revenue on earnings calls, and company releases described the crypto business as a custody and exchange/trading operation. There is no evidence in the sample period of mining activity, crypto-focused infrastructure supply, or a discretionary corporate crypto treasury policy that would justify miners, infrastr, or holders weights.

Weight Assignment

A weight of 1.0 finserv is assigned because the strongest evidence concerns Goldmoney’s direct handling of crypto assets for clients through dealing, custody, and exchange-style services. The FY2018 transcript states the firm had built a “cryptocurrency custody exchange trading business,” and results releases quantified dedicated cryptocurrency revenue, indicating an actual operating line rather than incidental exposure. No separate holders weight is warranted because the 2017 release refers to “accumulated cryptocurrencies from digital currency-processed deposits,” which appears operational and transactional rather than an explicit treasury accumulation policy.

Ambiguities / Limitations

Goldmoney’s crypto exposure appears concentrated in the earlier part of the January 2017 to December 2024 sample, with later annual reports and shareholder letters emphasizing precious metals and no longer presenting crypto as a core current segment. That creates a time-variation issue: the company was clearly crypto-exposed historically, but not evidently by fiscal 2022–2024. Still, the instructions classify based on the sample period as a whole, and the earlier transcript-plus-filing evidence is strong enough to support a non-zero classification. No clear evidence was found that the stock itself was widely treated in financial media as a primary crypto proxy, so invbase remains 0.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • June 28, 2018, FY2018 earnings call transcript: “We built out an entire cryptocurrency custody exchange trading business.” — Goldmoney Year-End 2018 Earnings Conference Call Transcript
  • June 28, 2018, FY2018 earnings call transcript: “We saw $30.6 million of cryptocurrency revenue compared to $22 million in the prior quarter.” — Goldmoney Year-End 2018 Earnings Conference Call Transcript
  • June 28, 2018, FY2018 earnings call transcript: management said Goldmoney had generated “around $60 million dollars of cryptocurrency revenue,” describing it as an indicator of volume. — Goldmoney Year-End 2018 Earnings Conference Call Transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • September 28, 2017, product launch release: Goldmoney said it added “digital asset dealing and cold storage custody,” becoming one of the first public financial-service companies to offer custody for blockchain assets. — Goldmoney Adds Bitcoin and Ethereum to the Goldmoney Holding
  • August 14, 2017, Q1 FY2018 results: management cited “realized gains from the liquidation of accumulated cryptocurrencies from digital currency-processed deposits.” — Goldmoney Reports Financial Results for First Quarter 2018
  • November 14, 2018, Q2 FY2019 results: Goldmoney reported “Cryptocurrency Business revenue of $11 million compared to $17 million in Q1.” — Goldmoney Reports Financial Results for Second Quarter 2019
  • 2022 annual report for fiscal year ended March 31, 2022: the nature-of-operations section describes Goldmoney as a “precious metal focused investment company,” with no crypto segment among principal businesses, showing the later retreat of crypto materiality. — Goldmoney Annual Report 2022
  • June 29, 2020, FY2020 shareholder letter: management stated it would invest balance-sheet assets into precious metal bullion, supporting the exclusion of a crypto treasury holders classification. — Goldmoney Fiscal 2020 Shareholder Letter

Financial news / other:

  • March 1, 2018, Cointelegraph: coverage reported Goldmoney had expanded its cryptocurrency cold-storage offering to additional coins, corroborating the custody-service model. — Cointelegraph
  • March 1, 2018, Sputnik: external coverage described Goldmoney as expanding cryptocurrency “cold storage,” again consistent with direct crypto custody and dealing services. — Sputnik International

MOGO

  • Rank: 36 | Expo: 4.23 | id_str: CA60800C2085
  • Weight summary: holders 0.4, miners 0.0, finserv 0.5, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.1
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (Q4 2021), company results releases/MD&A (2021, 2023–2024), financial news (2021 Reuters/CoinDesk)

Firm Overview

Mogo Inc. is a Canadian-listed fintech company focused on digital wealth, lending, and payments. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample, its crypto exposure came primarily from direct consumer crypto trading services and strategic crypto-related balance-sheet/investment positions rather than mining or infrastructure supply.

Exposure Channels Identified

The main crypto mechanism is finserv. Mogo operated MogoCrypto, a consumer product for buying and selling bitcoin, and management repeatedly discussed crypto-related revenue and plans to integrate crypto trading into its broader wealth platform. A secondary mechanism is holders because Mogo also adopted a corporate crypto allocation strategy: it announced Bitcoin treasury purchases in December 2020, later disclosed BTC and ETH purchases, and by March 2024 had formally expanded treasury policy to include Bitcoin and Bitcoin ETFs. There is also evidence for invbase = 1, as Reuters explicitly grouped Mogo among public companies whose shares rose on bitcoin-bet enthusiasm, indicating direct crypto-sentiment spillover into the stock. No evidence supports miners, bizserv, or infrastr.

Weight Assignment

A weight of 0.5 finserv and 0.4 holders best fits the sample-period evidence, with 0.1 unclass retained to reflect some time variation and the fact that crypto was not the dominant operating line by 2023–2024. Finserv receives the largest share because Mogo directly enabled retail bitcoin trading and discussed crypto-related revenue in earnings materials and transcripts. Holders is also substantial because the company made explicit treasury investments in BTC and ETH and later revised treasury policy to include Bitcoin and Bitcoin ETFs, but those holdings remained relatively modest versus total assets, so they do not outweigh the operating-service channel.

Ambiguities / Limitations

The sample shows time variation. Crypto financial services were clearest in 2020–2021, while by 2023–2024 Mogo’s reported operating focus had shifted more toward wealth, payments, and crypto-related equity investments such as WonderFi rather than a prominently disclosed in-house crypto trading line. In addition, Mogo’s large WonderFi/CoinSquare equity stakes are crypto-related investments but are not cleanly a mechanism category under the coding rules unless treated as treasury-like exposure; therefore the classification relies more heavily on the explicit MogoCrypto service and explicit BTC/ETH treasury disclosures. At least one usable earnings-call transcript quote was retrieved, so certainty is not capped.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 23, 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: “Crypto-related revenue was volatile during the quarter but increased significantly during the year.” — Mogo Q4 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
  • March 23, 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: management said MogoTrade would ultimately include “other assets like crypto,” and that retail crypto trading would flow to integrated equity-and-crypto accounts. — Mogo Q4 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
  • March 23, 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: management highlighted Coinsquare’s regulatory positioning as a crypto exchange and said Mogo was “super excited” about that strategic partner. — Mogo Q4 2021 Earnings Call Transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • May 13, 2021, Q1 2021 results: CFO said subscription-and-services growth was led by “strong gains from MogoCrypto,” and the release said Q1 revenue growth was driven partly by increased revenue from MogoCrypto. — Mogo Reports First Quarter 2021 Financial Results
  • March 20, 2024, Q4/FY2023 results: Mogo ended 2023 with $55.6 million of cash and investments, including $27.7 million of crypto-related investments, and said the board had approved a treasury strategy including Bitcoin and Bitcoin ETFs with an initial authorization of up to $5.0 million. — Mogo Reports Results for Q4 & FY 2023
  • March 20, 2025, Q4/FY2024 results covering fiscal 2024: management reported FY2024 wealth and payments growth, while net income reflected gains on marketable securities rather than a disclosed core crypto operating segment, supporting lower 2024 crypto operating materiality. — Mogo Reports Results for Q4 & FY 2024

Financial news / other:

  • May 3, 2021, CoinDesk: Mogo had bought about 146 ETH and about 18 BTC, and planned to allocate up to 5% of its cash and investment portfolio to cryptocurrencies. — CoinDesk
  • February 9, 2021, Reuters: Mogo was identified as a company that had invested in bitcoin, and Reuters reported its shares jumped sharply after Tesla’s bitcoin announcement, evidencing crypto-sentiment spillover into the stock. — Reuters via Investing.com

XUNLEI CL.A ADR 1:5

  • Rank: 37 | Expo: 4.20 | id_str: US98419E1082
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.5, infrastr 0.5, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (Q4 2018, Q4 2022, Q4 2023), 20-F filings (2023–2024), financial news (Bloomberg 2017)

Firm Overview

Xunlei Ltd. is a China-based technology company listed on Nasdaq through ADRs, historically centered on download acceleration, subscriptions, shared cloud computing, and online entertainment. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample, its crypto-related exposure came not from holding coins or running an exchange, but from building blockchain-related platforms and associated cloud/edge-computing infrastructure for enterprise and ecosystem users. (fool.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The strongest mechanism is infrastr because management tied Xunlei’s blockchain push to shared cloud computing, edge computing, hardware-related cloud revenues, and the ThunderChain infrastructure platform. On the Q4 2018 call, management said Xunlei had become “a well-known Blockchain player” providing “Blockchain services with high performance,” and that projects had been launched on its “ThunderChain open platform”; the same call also linked cloud/IBS revenue partly to hardware sales, consistent with infrastructure provision rather than financial intermediation. A secondary mechanism is bizserv because the company also described blockchain application services for enterprises across industries, and its 2024 20-F states that in 2022 it launched a “blockchain-based enterprise digital collection service platform” offering minting, showcasing, and management services. No evidence in the sample supports holders, miners, or finserv under the coding rules. (fool.com)

Weight Assignment

A split of 0.5 infrastr and 0.5 bizserv best reflects the evidence. Infrastr receives half because the transcript evidence repeatedly connects blockchain activity to Xunlei’s shared cloud, edge-computing, bandwidth, hardware, and ThunderChain platform stack. Bizserv also receives half because Xunlei marketed blockchain applications and enterprise service tools, including digital-collection management and industry-specific use cases, but the available sources do not cleanly quantify whether enterprise service revenue or infrastructure provision was the dominant crypto-related economic channel. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Xunlei clearly discussed blockchain extensively, but the retrieved sources do not provide a clean standalone revenue breakout for blockchain as distinct from broader cloud computing and internet value-added services, so materiality is only modestly observable. The evidence also points to blockchain technology and tokenized/digital-collection infrastructure rather than direct cryptocurrency handling, so finserv was excluded. No transcript or filing evidence was found for balance-sheet crypto holdings or proprietary mining operations. At least one usable earnings-call transcript quote was retrieved, so certainty is not capped. (fool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 7, 2019, Q4 2018 earnings call transcript: “we also became a well-known Blockchain player, providing Blockchain services with high performance” and said projects had been launched on the “ThunderChain open platform.” — Motley Fool transcript
  • March 7, 2019, Q4 2018 earnings call transcript: CFO said cloud computing and IBS revenue changes were “mainly due to a decline in sales of hardware,” tying the segment to physical/cloud infrastructure rather than pure software. — Motley Fool transcript
  • March 16, 2023, Q4 2022 earnings call transcript: management said 2022 revenue growth was driven by live streaming and cloud computing, while the call contained no evidence of crypto trading, custody, treasury holdings, or mining revenue. — Fintool transcript
  • March 14, 2024, Q4 2023 earnings call transcript: management emphasized subscription and cloud computing as the operating pillars; no direct crypto-financial-services activity was discussed. — Investing.com transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2024 Form 20-F: Xunlei said users can “develop and operate blockchain-based services and applications” on its platform, indicating service/infrastructure exposure rather than coin holding. — SEC 20-F excerpt
  • 2024 Form 20-F: the filing states, “In 2022, we launched a blockchain-based enterprise digital collection service platform” providing minting, showcasing, and management services. — SEC 20-F excerpt
  • 2024 Form 20-F: the filing says Xunlei “launched ThunderChain, a blockchain infrastructure platform, in 2018.” — SEC 20-F excerpt

Financial news / other:

  • November 22, 2017, Bloomberg: Xunlei’s stock rally was linked to its “Wanke coin mining” cryptocurrency project, indicating the stock was treated by markets as a crypto/blockchain proxy even though the company’s lasting exposure in the sample is better classified as infrastructure/services. — Bloomberg
  • January 9, 2018, Motley Fool: Xunlei’s 2017 share-price surge was explicitly tied to blockchain and cryptocurrency enthusiasm, supporting invbase = 1 as investor sentiment spilled over to the stock. — Motley Fool

SWISSQUOTE 'R'

  • Rank: 38 | Expo: 4.17 | id_str: CH0010675863
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: annual reports and ad hoc results releases (2021–2024), plus Swissquote crypto product press releases

Firm Overview

Swissquote Group Holding SA is a Swiss-listed online banking and trading group headquartered in Gland, operating brokerage, banking, foreign exchange, custody, and investment services for retail and institutional clients. Within the 2017–2024 sample, crypto is presented as a direct product line of the bank through cryptocurrency trading, custody, staking access, and, from 2022, its own crypto exchange. (swissquote.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports finserv exclusively. Swissquote’s own disclosures describe “trading and custody of crypto assets,” “staking services,” and operation of “its own crypto exchange,” which are textbook crypto financial services rather than non-financial support activity. (swissquote.com)

There is no evidence in the sample that Swissquote was a proprietary crypto miner, a strategic treasury holder of crypto for balance-sheet investment purposes, or an infrastructure supplier to third-party miners/blockchain operators. The annual report explicitly says crypto assets credited to client accounts are held in a nominee or fiduciary capacity for clients and that the group itself has no claim to those assets, which excludes a holders classification. (swissquote.com)

The stock also appears to have a crypto-sensitive investor base. Bloomberg characterized Swissquote in 2025 as seeking to reduce its crypto link despite a long share-price rally, and UBS/Reuters-republished coverage tied the 2024 share move partly to strength in trading and crypto markets, supporting invbase = 1 as an amplifier rather than a separate mechanism. (bloomberg.com)

Weight Assignment

A full 1.0 finserv weight is appropriate because every substantive crypto-related source points to Swissquote acting as a bank/broker/exchange/custodian in digital assets. Management materials and filings consistently frame crypto as a customer-facing financial service generating fees and trading income, with no competing evidence for mining, treasury holding, business services, or infrastructure as distinct mechanisms. (swissquote.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

No dedicated earnings-call transcript from a third-party transcript repository was retrievable in the sample, so the evidence anchor comes from Swissquote’s own ad hoc results releases and annual reports rather than a conventional call transcript. That still provides direct management-language evidence, but it limits comparability with firms that have fully transcribed analyst calls. Asset materiality is coded 0 because disclosed crypto assets are customer assets held off balance sheet in fiduciary custody rather than Swissquote’s own balance-sheet crypto holdings. (swissquote.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 16, 2023, FY 2022 results / ad hoc management release: “Net crypto assets income was impacted significantly by the crypto market turmoil, declining 72.9 percent to CHF 27.7 million” and “Swissquote’s own crypto exchange SQX was successfully launched in September 2022.” — Swissquote FY 2022 results
  • August 9, 2023, H1 2023 results / ad hoc management release: “Net crypto assets income declined by 61.5% to CHF 7.5 million” and the company described core competencies including “trading and custody of crypto assets.” — Swissquote H1 2023 results
  • 2024 shareholder report, management commentary: Swissquote said 2024 growth benefited from “crypto trading,” adding that it had “launched its first crypto offering in 2017.” — Swissquote Annual Report 2024

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Annual Report 2023: business model lists “Up to 35 cryptocurrencies, custody, trading, staking, crypto exchange.” — Swissquote Annual Report 2023
  • Annual Report 2023: operating income by product shows Crypto assets CHF 18.9m in 2023 versus CHF 27.7m in 2022, indicating a meaningful but non-dominant revenue line. — Swissquote Annual Report 2023
  • Annual Report 2023: Swissquote states it holds client crypto assets “solely as a nominee (fiduciary capacity)” and that “The Group itself has no claim to the crypto assets.” — Swissquote Annual Report 2023
  • 2021 press release: Swissquote said crypto trading had “become an important source of income” and reported CHF 1.8 billion in client crypto assets under custody in March 2021. — Swissquote expands cryptocurrency offering
  • October 3, 2022 press release: Swissquote announced “its own crypto exchange” providing trading, liquidity, execution, and custody services. — Swissquote launches its own crypto exchange: SQX

Financial news / other:

  • 2024 shareholder letter: net crypto assets income increased to CHF 85.5 million, about 13% of total operating income in 2024. — Report to the Shareholders 2024
  • 2025 Bloomberg: Swissquote was described as trying to shake its market association with crypto despite that link having helped define the stock’s profile. — Bloomberg
  • 2024 Investing.com article citing Reuters/UBS: Swissquote’s year-to-date share rise was linked to earlier strength in trading and crypto markets, supporting crypto-sentiment spillovers into the stock. — Investing.com / Reuters-cited coverage

RIVALRY

  • Rank: 39 | Expo: 3.91 | id_str: CA76803P1036
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: Q1–Q2 2024 earnings-call/results materials, company crypto product pages, investor presentation materials

Firm Overview

Rivalry Corp is a Toronto-based listed online sportsbook and iGaming operator focused on esports, sports betting, and casino products through Rivalry Limited. Within the sample period, crypto became part of its customer-facing gambling and payments offering, culminating in a native token strategy and broader cryptocurrency-enabled wagering experience.

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports finserv exposure because Rivalry directly accepts cryptocurrencies for deposits and withdrawals and in 2024 launched/announced a native blockchain-based utility token integrated into its wagering and casino ecosystem. Management explicitly described a strategic expansion into the “crypto gambling market,” a “more fulsome crypto wallet experience,” and token-driven monetization, which fits direct handling of digital-asset-enabled financial/gambling transactions rather than mere ancillary services. There is no evidence of mining, treasury-style balance-sheet crypto accumulation, or crypto infrastructure provision. The case for invbase is weaker: management discussed targeting crypto users as customers, but the retrieved sources do not explicitly document crypto-driven shareholder flows or the stock being treated as a market crypto proxy.

Weight Assignment

A full 1.0 finserv weight is appropriate because the substantive crypto-related evidence all points to Rivalry operating a crypto-enabled betting and gaming platform with direct cryptocurrency payment functionality and a native utility token embedded in the user transaction flow. The crypto activity is not incidental marketing alone: management tied it to customer acquisition, retention, wallet connection, and incremental revenue, with no competing evidence for holders, miners, business services, or infrastructure as distinct mechanisms.

Ambiguities / Limitations

No conventional third-party transcript text from Seeking Alpha or Motley Fool was retrievable for Rivalry within the sample, so the transcript anchor comes from the company’s own Q1 and Q2 2024 investor conference call/results materials and webcast-linked releases rather than a fully syndicated analyst-call transcript. That supports a non-unclassified assignment, but it keeps certainty at 3 rather than 4. Asset materiality is coded 0 because the retrieved sources show crypto as a customer-facing payments/token product, not as a meaningful balance-sheet crypto asset held by Rivalry itself.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • May 30, 2024, Q1 2024 results / investor conference call release: “Today we also announced a decisive move to more deeply tap into the significant global crypto gambling market through Rivalry Token” and “released… a more fulsome crypto wallet experience” — Rivalry Q1 2024 results
  • May 30, 2024, Q1 2024 PDF release: “Rivalry Token will be a utility token integrated within Rivalry’s product suite – from sportsbook to casino” and each wager/spin is rewarded with “a decentralized asset that delivers meaningful real money utility for the player.” — Rivalry Q1 2024 PDF release
  • August 29, 2024, Q2 2024 results: “At Rivalry we have narrowed our focus primarily to two areas… crypto expansion led by tokenization” and “Rivalry Token has delivered $1.7 million in revenue” after quarter-end — Rivalry Q2 2024 results

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • May 30, 2024, quarterly results release: the company announced “strategic expansion into Web3 gambling segment with Rivalry Token and extended cryptocurrency payment experience.” — Rivalry Q1 2024 results
  • August 29, 2024, quarterly results release: management said the firm was building “engagement loops for players” and expanding “within the crypto gambling segment.” — Rivalry Q2 2024 results
  • May 24, 2023, investor presentation archive: Rivalry maintained an active investor-relations presentation program before the 2024 crypto push, helping place the later crypto strategy within the operating business rather than a one-off announcement. — Rivalry Business Overview presentation page

Financial news / other:

  • Rivalry crypto product page: the company states its sportsbook and casino “accepts a multitude of the most popular cryptocurrencies” including BTC, ETH, USDT, XRP, SOL and others for deposits/withdrawals. — Rivalry token / crypto casino page
  • Rivalry crypto product page: the company says “Rivalry Token will act as a nexus between Rivalry, Web3 gambling, and crypto users” and users can connect wallets to opt in. — Rivalry token / crypto casino page
  • May 30, 2024, investor conference call event page: Rivalry formally scheduled a call to discuss “its expansion into the cryptocurrency space,” supporting that crypto was a management-level strategic topic rather than only consumer-site branding. — First Quarter 2024 Investor Conference Call

WISDOMTREE PHYSICAL PLATINUM ETC

  • Rank: 40 | Expo: 3.88 | id_str: JE00B1VS2W53
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: product page and issuer prospectus/accounting documents for WisdomTree Metal Securities Limited; WisdomTree corporate crypto-ETP press materials used only to test for spillover from the broader group

Firm Overview

WisdomTree Physical Platinum ETC is a precious-metals exchange traded commodity issued by WisdomTree Metal Securities Limited, a Jersey issuer within WisdomTree’s European ETP platform. The retrieved product and issuer documents describe it as a physically backed platinum/precious-metals security whose returns are tied to allocated metal bullion rather than to digital assets.

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive crypto exposure was identified for this specific listed security within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period. The issuer documentation and product materials consistently describe the instrument as backed by physical metal bullion, with creations and redemptions settled in precious metal and valuation linked to LBMA/LPPM-compliant bullion rather than cryptocurrencies. Although the broader WisdomTree group also operates crypto ETPs elsewhere in Europe, the retrieved evidence does not show that this platinum ETC itself handles crypto, holds crypto, mines crypto, provides crypto business services, or supplies crypto infrastructure.

Weight Assignment

A full 1.0 unclass weight is appropriate because all firm-specific evidence points to a conventional physically backed platinum/precious-metals ETC, not a crypto-exposed issuer or operating company under the project taxonomy. The broader corporate group’s crypto activity is not sufficient to assign exposure to this security absent evidence that this ETC’s economics, collateral, revenues, assets, or investor-base behavior were directly tied to crypto.

Ambiguities / Limitations

No firm-specific earnings-call transcript was retrievable for this ETC or its special-purpose issuer, which is typical for an exchange traded commodity vehicle rather than an operating company. That limitation does not affect the unclassified outcome because the available prospectus, product-page, and issuer-account evidence is clear that the instrument is metal-backed; however, it means there is no transcript quote to report. Group-level WisdomTree crypto-ETP activity was reviewed only as a possible contamination risk and was excluded from classification because the instructions require firm-specific evidence.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • No issuer-specific earnings-call transcript located for WisdomTree Physical Platinum ETC / WisdomTree Metal Securities Limited within 2017–2024 after targeted search of transcript-style sources and issuer materials.

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • April 5, 2024, base prospectus: “Metal Securities are intended to offer investors means of investing in the precious metals market without the necessity of taking physical delivery of or storing Bullion.” — WisdomTree Metal Securities Limited Base Prospectus
  • June 30, 2025 interim account referencing 2024 annual statements: the issuer states that “Metal Bullion is held to provide the security holders with the exposure to changes in the fair value of Metal Bullion” and reports Metal Bullion at Fair Value on balance sheet, consistent with precious-metal collateral rather than crypto assets. — WisdomTree Metal Securities Limited Interim Account
  • Product regulatory page: the security is issued by WisdomTree Metal Securities Limited, with annual account and interim account links under the product’s regulatory materials. — WisdomTree Physical Precious Metals / issuer regulatory page

Financial news / other:

  • Product page: “WisdomTree Physical Precious Metals is... designed to offer security holders a simple, cost-efficient and secure way to access Physical Precious Metals” and is “backed by physical, allocated Physical Precious Metals held by HSBC Bank plc.” — WisdomTree Physical Precious Metals product page
  • Product page: authorised participants “create and redeem... securities by delivering or receiving precious metals” and pricing is based on the spot price of the underlying metal basket. — WisdomTree Physical Precious Metals product page
  • November 7, 2024, WisdomTree group press release: WisdomTree said it managed 10 physically backed institutional-grade crypto ETPs in Europe, confirming that crypto exposure exists at group level but in separate products, not in this platinum ETC. — WisdomTree Partners with CoinDesk Indices to Launch Physical CoinDesk 20 ETP

VIVOPOWER INTERNATIONAL

  • Rank: 41 | Expo: 3.47 | id_str: GB00BD3VDH82
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 1.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 3 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: VivoPower/Caret Digital press releases and SEC-filed disclosures (2021–2024); December 5, 2024 management presentation announcement; financial news on the later XRP-treasury pivot used only for investor-base amplification context

Firm Overview

VivoPower International PLC is a London-headquartered, Nasdaq-listed company historically focused on sustainable energy, electric-vehicle and solar-development activities, with Caret as its U.S. solar and “Power-to-X” platform. During the sample period, the company developed a substantive crypto exposure through Caret Digital, a wholly owned subsidiary pursuing renewable-powered digital asset mining and a planned spin-off.

Exposure Channels Identified

The firm fits miners because management and company disclosures repeatedly describe Caret Digital as a digital asset mining business, initially aimed at Bitcoin/Ethereum/Litecoin and later focused on Dogecoin and Litecoin mining. The evidence shows operating intent, financing, fleet deployment, projected mining revenues, and management discussion of mining as the “highest and best” use case for renewable power capacity. No non-zero holders weight is assigned for 2017–2024 because the available firm-specific evidence indicates mined tokens were generally to be sold or converted rather than held as a balance-sheet treasury investment. No finserv, bizserv, or infrastr weight is assigned because VivoPower’s relevant exposure was direct mining activity rather than exchange/custody/payment services, support services, or selling infrastructure to third parties.

Weight Assignment

A full 1.0 miners weight is appropriate because the crypto exposure identified within January 2017 to December 2024 is overwhelmingly tied to Caret Digital’s own mining operations and mining build-out plans. Although VivoPower’s later 2025 XRP treasury pivot would support a holders/finserv discussion outside the sample window, it falls after December 31, 2024 and is therefore excluded from the mechanism weights here; it is used only to help assess whether the stock functions as a crypto market proxy for invbase.

Ambiguities / Limitations

A clean full-text earnings-call transcript quote specifically mentioning crypto mining was not readily retrievable from the transcript pages surfaced in search; the accessible December 5, 2024 company call announcement nonetheless confirms that management planned to discuss the acceleration of Caret Digital’s Dogecoin mining. Because the strongest crypto evidence comes from company press releases and SEC-filed disclosures rather than a fully scraped transcript body, certainty is kept at 3 rather than 4. Revenue materiality appears potentially significant based on disclosed revenue potential of up to $25 million annually for the initial Dogecoin fleet and up to $150 million for a 55MW build-out, but these are projected rather than fully reported segment revenues, so RevShare is coded conservatively.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • December 5, 2024, VivoPower investor call announcement: strategic updates for the annual-results presentation would include “the acceleration of Caret Digital’s Dogecoin mining and spin-off” — VivoPower Announces Date For 20-F Annual Results and Strategic Update Presentation
  • Seeking Alpha transcript index shows VivoPower had earnings-call transcript coverage across the sample period, including a December 5, 2024 event relevant to Caret Digital. — VivoPower International PLC Earnings Call Transcripts

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • November 16, 2021, company press release: Caret signed an LOI to launch a “renewable-powered digital asset mining business” with expected capability to mine “Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Litecoin” and forecast revenue potential of approximately $270m per annum when fully operational. — VivoPower subsidiary, Caret, announces LOI to launch renewable powered digital asset mining business
  • October 28, 2024, company press release: Caret Digital’s strategy would “initially focus on cryptocurrency mining, with an emphasis on mining Dogecoin (DOGE)” and “convert mined DOGE into Bitcoin (BTC)”. — VivoPower International PLC Announces Intent to Spin-Off of Caret Digital via a Direct Listing on Nasdaq and Dividend Share Distribution
  • December 13, 2024, company press release: Caret Digital would commence Dogecoin mining operations, with “annualised revenue potential of up to US$25m” and the company stated it “does not intend to hold most of the Dogecoin that is mined but will sell or forward sell them as appropriate.” — VivoPower’s Caret Digital to activate Dogecoin mining operations
  • November 14, 2024, SEC-filed release: the board approved a plan for Caret Digital to develop up to 55MW of solar-farm portfolio for “Dogecoin and Litecoin mining purposes” with potential annual revenues of up to US$150m. — SEC filed Exhibit 99.1 - Caret Digital 55MW mining plan
  • FY2023 annual report: the group structure lists both Caret, LLC and Caret Decimal, LLC, corroborating that the mining vehicle sat within VivoPower’s consolidated structure during the sample period. — VivoPower Annual Report for the year ended 30 June 2023

Financial news / other:

  • May 28, 2025, CoinDesk reported VivoPower had raised $121 million to pivot to an XRP-focused treasury strategy, characterizing it as a public-market crypto proxy; this is outside the sample for mechanism weights but supports invbase = 1. — VivoPower Raises $121M to Launch XRP Treasury Strategy With Saudi Royal Backing
  • May 28, 2025, company release said VivoPower was transforming into the “world’s first XRP-focused digital asset enterprise,” reinforcing that investors could plausibly trade the stock as a crypto-theme vehicle. — VivoPower Announces XRP-Focused Digital Asset Treasury Strategy

TERAWULF

  • Rank: 42 | Expo: 3.47 | id_str: US88080T1043
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 1.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2022), company earnings releases / call materials (2024), financial news / market characterization (2024–2025)

Firm Overview

TeraWulf Inc. is a U.S.-listed company that, during the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, operated primarily as a vertically integrated bitcoin miner with facilities in New York and Pennsylvania. By late 2024 it also began expanding into AI/HPC hosting, but the retrieved evidence shows that its crypto exposure in-sample remained centered on its own bitcoin mining operations rather than on financial intermediation or treasury-style crypto accumulation. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports a pure miners classification. TeraWulf’s 2022 10-K states that its “primary source of revenue is from sustainably mining bitcoin,” that it commenced industrial-scale mining in March 2022, and that it does not hold, sell, or transact in bitcoin for anyone other than itself. (sec.gov)

The 2024 earnings materials continue to frame operations around self-mined bitcoin, mining capacity, hash rate, and mining economics. For example, the Q2 2024 release reports 699 bitcoin self-mined across facilities and attributes revenue growth to mining scale and bitcoin prices, while the Q3 2024 release again ties results to self-mining hash rate and bitcoin price. (investors.terawulf.com)

No non-zero holders weight is assigned. Although TeraWulf at times had cash-and-bitcoin on its balance sheet, the 2022 10-K says it sells bitcoin on a daily, weekly, and monthly basis to pay operating expenses, and the available record does not show a distinct treasury-investment or HODL policy separate from mining operations. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

A full 1.0 miners weight is appropriate because essentially all substantive crypto exposure identified for 2017–2024 runs through TeraWulf’s own bitcoin mining business: mining facilities, miners deployed, hash rate, bitcoin self-mined, and mining-related revenue. The late-2024 HPC hosting buildout is real, but it is not itself crypto infrastructure under the project definitions unless explicitly directed at crypto workloads; here it appears as diversification away from pure mining rather than an additional in-sample crypto mechanism. invbase = 1 is appropriate because market coverage explicitly characterizes WULF as a bitcoin miner and its stock moved on crypto/bitcoin-miner narratives, indicating investor-base spillovers tied to crypto sentiment. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A fully scraped transcript body from Seeking Alpha was blocked, so the earnings-call anchor is taken from accessible company earnings-call materials and the Seeking Alpha transcript index rather than a complete scraped transcript page. Even so, the classification remains highly stable because the 10-K and multiple 2024 company earnings releases are explicit about the business model and economics. The balance-sheet bitcoin disclosed in 2024 is treated as incidental mined-coin inventory/cash management rather than evidence of a separate holders mechanism. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 19, 2024, company earnings-call materials: TeraWulf announced it would host its “fourth quarter and full year 2023 earnings call and business update” for investors that day, anchoring the 2024 call event. — Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Earnings Conference Call
  • March 19, 2024, Seeking Alpha transcript listing: transcript coverage exists for the firm’s earnings calls, including the March 2024 event used to anchor the classification. — TeraWulf Inc. (WULF) Earnings Call Transcripts
  • August 12, 2024, Q2 2024 earnings release / call materials: “Self-mined 699 bitcoin across the Lake Mariner and Nautilus Cryptomine facilities” and revenue rose to $35.6 million. — TeraWulf Reports Second Quarter 2024 Financial Results
  • November 12, 2024, Q3 2024 earnings release / call materials: revenue increased 42.8% to $27.1 million, attributed to hash-rate growth and higher bitcoin prices. — TeraWulf Reports Third Quarter 2024 Financial Results

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2022 Form 10-K: “Our primary source of revenue is from sustainably mining bitcoin” and “We do not hold, sell or transact in bitcoin or any other digital assets for anyone other than ourselves.” — TeraWulf 2022 Form 10-K
  • 2022 Form 10-K: “We sell bitcoin on a daily, weekly and monthly basis to pay for all operating expenses of the Company.” — TeraWulf 2022 Form 10-K
  • March 19, 2024, FY2023 results release: as of December 31, 2023, the company held $56.2 million in cash, cash equivalents, and bitcoin on its balance sheet. — TeraWulf Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Results
  • February 28, 2025, FY2024 results release covering sample-end 2024: 2024 revenue increased 102% to $140.1 million, driven by increased bitcoin production and higher bitcoin prices; balance sheet included $274.5 million in cash and bitcoin at December 31, 2024. — TeraWulf Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results

Financial news / other:

  • October 3, 2024, financial news: TeraWulf was characterized as a “Bitcoin miner” whose stock rose after selling its Nautilus stake and shifting focus toward AI data centers. — Bitcoin Miner TeraWulf Sells Stake in Nuclear-Powered Facility, Shifts Focus to AI Data Centers
  • August 19, 2025, financial news reflecting investor-base framing: TeraWulf was described as a “bitcoin miner and data center operator” and the stock jumped on Google’s increased stake, supporting treatment as a crypto-theme equity proxy. — TeraWulf stock jumps as Google ups stake in bitcoin miner to 14%

WISEKEY N

  • Rank: 43 | Expo: 3.40 | id_str: CH1276062754
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.5, bizserv 0.1, infrastr 0.4, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: 2024 Form 20-F / annual report, H1 2024 and FY 2024 earnings-call transcripts, 2024 company press releases

Firm Overview
