WISeKey International Holding AG is a Swiss-listed cybersecurity, digital identity, semiconductor, satellite, and IoT technology holding company, also traded on Nasdaq as WKEY. During 2017–2024, its crypto-related activity sat mainly in blockchain-enabled transaction platforms and NFT/digital-asset marketplaces, alongside satellite and device infrastructure intended to support blockchain-based IoT transactions. (fintool.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The firm is not a crypto holder or miner. Instead, management and filings describe two substantive crypto-facing businesses: WISe.ART, which operates an NFT marketplace and secure digital-asset transaction platform, and SEALCOIN, which is designed around token-enabled IoT transactions and decentralized transaction services. (fintool.com)

These support a meaningful finserv weight because WISeKey is directly facilitating token, NFT, and digital-asset transactions rather than merely advising the ecosystem. A substantial infrastr weight also fits because the company explicitly ties this activity to enabling hardware, satellite, and device infrastructure for blockchain-based and tokenized IoT transactions; a small bizserv residue is appropriate for broader blockchain service language around authentication and digital trust that is adjacent but not itself direct asset handling. (fintool.com)

Weight Assignment

A split of finserv 0.5 / infrastr 0.4 / bizserv 0.1 best matches the evidence. The largest share goes to finserv because WISe.ART is described as an NFT marketplace for secure NFT transactions and SEALCOIN revolves around tokens and transaction settlement between devices, which goes beyond generic blockchain software. Infrastr 0.4 reflects the repeated emphasis on satellite, semiconductor, and embedded secure-device architecture supporting those blockchain/token ecosystems. Bizserv 0.1 captures residual blockchain-based authentication and digital-trust services that support the ecosystem but are not themselves clearly financial intermediation. There is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining, or a crypto-proxy investor-base narrative sufficient for invbase = 1. (wisekey.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Seeking Alpha transcript pages were not directly retrievable due to access restrictions, so the earnings-call anchor relies on accessible transcript mirrors rather than a fully scraped primary transcript page. Revenue disaggregation in the 2024 Form 20-F shows reported revenue dominated by semiconductors and does not separately quantify blockchain/NFT/token revenue, so the crypto mechanisms are clearly present but appear financially immaterial within the sample through December 31, 2024. The invbase dummy is kept at 0 because I did not find explicit evidence that WIHN/WKEY traded primarily as a crypto proxy in financial media during the sample period. (fintool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • September 2024, H1 2024 earnings-call transcript: management said WISeKey was restructuring into a company specialized in “cybersecurity, digital identity, blockchain, IoT solution” and described WISe.ART as the “trusted blockchain and NFT segment” while SEALCOIN would provide a “blockchain-based platform for digital transaction and asset management.” — Fintool H1 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
  • April 2025, FY 2024 earnings-call transcript covering sample-end 2024: management said SEALCOIN made progress in 2024 “leveraging blockchain to enable secure Web 3.0 transactions” and that WISe.ART serves clients wanting to create “digital assets and digital twins.” — Fintool H2 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
  • Seeking Alpha transcript index: transcript coverage exists for WKEY earnings calls across the period, though the page itself was not directly accessible. — WISeKey Earnings Call Transcripts

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • April 17, 2025, Form 20-F for FY 2024: SEALCOIN “enables IoT devices to autonomously exchange data, energy, and services” and “the TIoT token serves as the foundation” of that ecosystem; responsibility for the project was transferred to WISeKey on June 27, 2024. — WISeKey 2024 Form 20-F
  • April 17, 2025, Form 20-F for FY 2024: WISe.ART is described as a “multi-blockchain secured digital ecosystem” using NFTs as digital certificates of ownership and linking digital assets to physical objects. — WISeKey 2024 Form 20-F
  • April 17, 2025, Form 20-F for FY 2024: revenue disaggregation shows 2024 group revenue of about $11.0 million from the semiconductors segment and no separately disclosed blockchain/NFT revenue line, supporting low current materiality. — WISeKey 2024 Form 20-F
  • June 13, 2024, company press release: WISeKey launched SEALCOIN AG as a DEPIN entity, with the “SEALCOIN utility token” facilitating transactions among IoT devices on Hedera DLT. — SEALCOIN Launches DEPIN IoT Entity
  • October 14, 2024, company press release: WISeSat planned a second proof of concept for SEALCOIN by “transacting tokens via satellites to IoT devices,” supporting the infrastructure channel. — WISeSat to Launch Second Proof of Concept for SEALCOIN Token

Financial news / other:

  • January 2, 2024, company/market-distributed release: WISe.ART received Hashgraph Association investment to expand authenticated digital assets, while SEALCOIN was described as a digital token and cryptocurrency protocol for internet-connected devices. — WISe.ART Secures Investment for Digital Assets and Cryptocurrencies
  • September 11, 2024, investor-distributed release: WISeKey invited companies to run proof-of-concept tests for SEALCOIN’s Transactional IoT platform on Hedera DLT, reinforcing the firm’s infrastructure-plus-token ecosystem role. — SEALCOIN Project Leverages Hedera DLT

DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT

  • Rank: 44 | Expo: 3.33 | id_str: US25686H3084
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings-call transcripts (2021 Q2, 2022 Q4), 10-K filings (2022, 2024), financial news/market commentary (2021)

Firm Overview

Dolphin Entertainment, Inc. is a U.S.-listed entertainment marketing and content-production company whose core businesses are publicity, influencer marketing, branding, and film/content ventures. Within the sample period, its crypto-adjacent activity centered on creating, marketing, and launching NFT collections and NFT marketplace programs for sports and entertainment brands rather than operating an exchange, holding crypto on its balance sheet, or supplying mining/blockchain infrastructure. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The clearest exposure channel is bizserv. Management said in the August 16, 2021 earnings call that Dolphin had “previously announced our entrance into the NFT business” and that, with FTX, “Dolphin will develop and execute the creative branding, production and marketing of these programs,” while FTX would provide the crypto-exchange services and technical backbone. (dolphinentertainment.com)

That wording is important for the mechanism classification: Dolphin was providing creative, branding, production, and marketing services around NFT programs, but the crypto handling itself sat with FTX, which fits the project rule for non-financial crypto services. The 2022 Form 10-K also confirms substantive activity by stating that the company “announced our intentions to enter into the production and marketing of NFTs” and that during 2022 it “designed, minted and sold an NFT collection titled Creature Chronicles: Exiled Aliens,” while the 2023 earnings call said new NFT development had been paused because of weak market sentiment. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

A full bizserv 1.0 assignment best matches the evidence. Dolphin’s crypto exposure was real and repeatedly discussed, but it came through NFT-related creative production, branding, promotion, and marketplace-program development for entertainment clients rather than mining, treasury holdings, custody, brokerage, exchange operation, or blockchain infrastructure. Although the company did mint and sell its own NFT collection, the record still frames this as part of a consumer-products/marketing initiative rather than a financial-services platform, so finserv is kept at 0.0. The stock also appears to have traded at times as an NFT/crypto proxy in 2021, so invbase = 1 is warranted. (dolphinentertainment.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is whether Dolphin’s direct minting and sale of the Creature Chronicles collection could justify a small finserv weight. I do not assign that because the surrounding evidence consistently describes Dolphin’s role as brand/creative/marketing execution for NFT programs, while the more technical and transactional crypto rails were supplied by partners such as FTX. Accessible transcript coverage was sufficient to anchor the classification, but the later 2023–2024 calls available through accessible mirrors focus mostly on the broader entertainment-marketing business and indicate the NFT initiative had already been paused, suggesting crypto exposure had become peripheral by the end of the sample. The 2024 Form 10-K contains no NFT, blockchain, crypto, or digital-asset references that I could locate, supporting low current materiality. (dolphinentertainment.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • August 16, 2021, Q2 2021 earnings-call transcript: management said Dolphin had “announced our entrance into the NFT business” and that with FTX “Dolphin will develop and execute the creative branding, production and marketing of these programs,” while FTX would provide “crypto exchange services and technical development as the backbone of the marketplaces.” — Dolphin Q2 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
  • March 30, 2023, Q4 2022 earnings-call transcript: management said “our flagship NFT collection Creature Chronicles minted on the Solana blockchain” and generated about “$435,000” in primary sales, but also said the company had “paused the development of any new NFT collections.” — Dolphin Q4 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
  • August 14, 2023, Q2 2023 earnings-call transcript: management emphasized influencer marketing would represent “25% or more of our revenues in 2023,” with no comparable crypto emphasis, indicating NFT activity had become peripheral. — Fintool Q2 2023 Earnings Call Transcript
  • November 2023 / March 2024 accessible transcript mirrors: later calls focused on diversified entertainment marketing and ventures rather than active NFT expansion. — Fintool Q3 2023 Earnings Call Transcript, Fintool Q4 2023 Earnings Call Transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 31, 2023, Form 10-K for FY 2022: the company said it had “announced our intentions to enter into the production and marketing of NFTs” and later “designed, minted and sold an NFT collection titled Creature Chronicles: Exiled Aliens.” — Dolphin 2022 Form 10-K
  • March 23, 2021, company press release: Dolphin announced an “NFTs Creation and Marketing Division,” directly supporting the bizserv channel. — Dolphin NFT Division Launch
  • August 2, 2021, company/Variety-linked release: Dolphin announced a partnership with FTX to create NFT marketplace programs for major sports and entertainment brands. — Dolphin–FTX NFT Marketplace Announcement
  • March 27, 2025, Form 10-K for FY 2024: no NFT/blockchain/crypto/digital-asset references were identifiable in the filing text, consistent with low end-of-sample materiality. — Dolphin 2024 Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • August 2, 2021, Nasdaq-distributed release: the Dolphin-FTX partnership said FTX would provide exchange infrastructure while Dolphin handled production/branding/marketing for NFT marketplace programs. — Nasdaq Press Release on Dolphin–FTX Partnership
  • August 3, 2021, Nasdaq/TipRanks commentary: Dolphin’s stock “popped 55.8%” after the FTX NFT marketplace report, evidence that the shares were treated by the market as an NFT/crypto proxy. — Nasdaq Commentary on DLPN as an NFT Stock
  • March 29, 2021, market commentary: Hall of Fame’s NFT partnership with Dolphin was associated with a sharp share-price reaction, reinforcing the period’s crypto-linked investor narrative around the stock. — Motley Fool on Hall of Fame/Dolphin NFT Deal
  • March 2021, market commentary: Dolphin stock surged on NFT-division news, further supporting invbase = 1 during the NFT boom period. — TheStreet on Dolphin’s NFT-Driven Stock Surge

GMO GLOBALSIGN HOLDINGS KK

  • Rank: 45 | Expo: 3.04 | id_str: JP3386040004
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: earnings-call transcripts (2021–2023), company IR/corporate summary, company financial-results releases

Firm Overview

GMO GlobalSign Holdings K.K. is a Japan-listed information-technology company focused on digital certificates, electronic signatures, cloud infrastructure, and digital-transformation services. Across the reviewed 2017–2024 materials, the company presents blockchain mainly as one of several end-markets or application areas for its authentication technology rather than as a direct crypto business. (gmogshd.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive crypto-exposure channel could be established under the project’s mechanism definitions. The company’s own business description says its solutions support technologies such as AI, IoT, and blockchain, but the evidence reviewed does not show that it holds cryptoassets, mines cryptocurrencies, operates a crypto exchange/custody/payment platform, or provides purpose-built crypto-mining/datacenter infrastructure. (gmogshd.com)

The accessible earnings-call transcripts similarly focus on electronic signatures, certificate authority services, cloud operations, and DX expansion. One transcript refers to participation in “decentralized technologies such as blockchain,” but in context this appears to describe general technology opportunity rather than a revenue-bearing crypto line or operational role that would justify bizserv or infrastr weight. (gmogshd.com)

Weight Assignment

Because the evidence does not document any qualifying mechanism exposure, all mechanism categories are set to 0.0 except unclass = 1.0. The firm’s references to blockchain are too generic and technology-oriented to meet the threshold for non-zero crypto exposure, and there is no direct evidence of crypto-related revenue, crypto assets, or crypto-focused investor-base commentary in the reviewed materials. (gmogshd.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is that the accessible transcript snippets are partial search-index extracts rather than fully browsable PDFs, because direct fetches of some transcript files were blocked. Still, the available transcript text and company IR pages consistently emphasize identity, e-signature, cloud, and DX businesses rather than crypto operations. A borderline case could be made that blockchain-related authentication support is crypto-adjacent, but under the stated rules generic enablement of blockchain applications without direct digital-asset handling or clearly targeted crypto infrastructure is insufficient, so the firm remains unclassified. (gmogshd.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 10, 2022, FY2021 financial-results briefing transcript: management said the electronic authentication and seal business would drive performance, with discussion centered on GMO Sign and public-sector/high-security deployments rather than crypto activities. — FY2021 Financial Results Briefing Transcript
  • May 2022, Q1 2022 financial-results briefing transcript: management referred to “development areas in which we can participate in essential decentralized technologies such as blockchain,” but the surrounding discussion remained at the level of technology direction rather than a direct crypto business line. — Q1 2022 Financial Results Briefing Transcript
  • November 2021 / surfaced transcript text for 2021 Q3: management described GMO Sign’s core system as using the certification authority the company operates and focused on expanding electronic-signature use cases. — Q3 2021 Financial Results Briefing Transcript
  • August 2023, Q2 2023 financial-results briefing transcript: management highlighted signature interoperability and centralized contract management, again without showing crypto operations. — Q2 2023 Financial Results Briefing Transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Corporate summary page, accessed from current company site: the company states it operates GlobalSign as a certificate authority and that its solutions support “AI, IoT, and blockchain,” indicating generic technology applicability rather than direct crypto exposure. — Corporate Summary
  • March 22, 2022, FY2021 financial-results release: the company announced FY2021 results through its IR site, with no visible indication in the accessible release text of crypto holdings, mining, or crypto-financial services. — FY2021 Financial Results Release
  • March 19, 2024, FY2023 financial-results release: the company announced FY2023 results through its IR site, again without accessible evidence of a dedicated crypto business segment. — FY2023 Financial Results Release

Financial news / other:

  • October 27, 2023, company press release: GMO Sign was adopted by Mizuho group companies, reinforcing the firm’s positioning in enterprise e-signature/DX rather than crypto-financial activity. — Mizuho Group Adopts GMO Sign
  • Company IR/corporate materials across 2021–2024 repeatedly frame the business around electronic authentication, e-signatures, cloud, and DX, with blockchain mentioned only as an application area. — Corporate Summary

SBI HDG.

  • Rank: 46 | Expo: 2.77 | id_str: JP3436120004
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.1, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: Integrated Report 2024, FY2023 and FY2022 results presentations, 2023 interim results, company press releases

Firm Overview

SBI Holdings Inc. is a large Japanese listed financial conglomerate spanning securities, banking, insurance, asset management, venture investment, and newer digital-finance businesses. Across 2017–2024 materials, crypto exposure appears as a distinct operating business within the group, centered on exchange/trading, market making, lending/custody-related services, and adjacent digital-asset platforms rather than passive treasury holding. (sbigroup.co.jp)

Exposure Channels Identified

The dominant mechanism is finserv. SBI’s own disclosures identify a “Crypto-asset Business,” with principal companies including SBI VC Trade, BITPOINT Japan, B2C2, SBI Alpha Trading, HashHub, and SBI DIGITAL MARKETS; these businesses provide exchange and trading services, market making, lending, custody/distribution, and other digital-asset financial services. (sbigroup.co.jp)

A smaller miners weight is warranted because FY2022 results explicitly state that “in the mining business, a loss of approx. JPY 8.5bn was recorded,” and that withdrawal from the mining business in Russia had been completed, showing that mining was a real though secondary and partly exited activity during the sample. (sbigroup.co.jp) A smaller infrastr weight is also warranted because the group operates blockchain/digital-asset infrastructure businesses such as SBI R3 Japan, which provides the Corda blockchain platform, and SBI Traceability / SBINFT / digital-asset issuance-distribution platforms; however, these appear secondary to the direct crypto-financial services businesses. (sbigroup.co.jp)

invbase = 1 is justified because management explicitly links shareholder benefits to XRP and states that customer growth in the crypto-asset business was supported by “shareholder benefits,” indicating direct overlap between crypto-oriented retail engagement and the company’s investor/customer base. (sbigroup.co.jp)

Weight Assignment

finserv receives 0.8 because the company’s disclosures consistently present crypto as a formal business segment with substantial exchange, brokerage, market-making, lending, staking, custody/distribution, and digital-asset platform activity across multiple subsidiaries. miners receives 0.1 because mining is explicitly documented but appears non-core and impaired/exited by 2023. infrastr receives 0.1 because the group also supplies blockchain and digital-asset infrastructure, but the center of gravity of exposure is still financial intermediation rather than infrastructure provision. No holders weight is assigned because the reviewed sources do not show a deliberate crypto treasury strategy distinct from operating businesses or product-related holdings. (sbigroup.co.jp)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is whether some digital-asset platform activities should be treated partly as bizserv rather than infrastr; under the project rules, they are better placed in infrastr where they involve issuance/distribution platforms and blockchain rails rather than generic consulting. Asset-share coding is left NA because the reviewed materials clearly show customer asset deposit balances and segment revenues, but do not provide a clean parent-level figure for crypto assets held on SBI’s own balance sheet as a share of total assets within the sample materials reviewed. None of the reviewed sources supports a separate treasury-holders classification. (sbigroup.co.jp)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • January 31, 2022, 9M FY2021 results presentation: management described “New Services to be Launched One After Another with the Completion of the Merger Between SBI VC Trade and TaoTao,” including periodic investment and “Crypto-asset lending (Kashi-coin).” — Financial Results for the Nine-month Period Ended December 31, 2021
  • January 31, 2022, 9M FY2021 results presentation: management said B2C2 “provides not only spot trading for crypto assets, but also variety of products such as NDF, options, CFD and funding to customers.” — Financial Results for the Nine-month Period Ended December 31, 2021
  • November 10, 2023, 1H FY2023 presentation: management said “the SBI Group will work together to provide crypto asset trading and related services,” while highlighting exchange, trust, liquidity, and XRP/XRPL-linked initiatives. — Financial Results For the Six-month Period Ended September 30, 2023
  • November 10, 2023, 1H FY2023 presentation: management stated the crypto-asset customer base grew due to “increase in crypto assets handled, expansion of staking services, and shareholder benefits offered by the SBI Group.” — Financial Results For the Six-month Period Ended September 30, 2023

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2024 Integrated Report: the group lists principal crypto companies including “B2C2 Ltd. — Crypto asset broker (market maker),” “SBI VC Trade — Provision of exchange and transaction services,” “BITPoint Japan — Crypto asset exchange and trading services,” and “HashHub — Crypto asset lending and research business.” — Integrated Report 2024
  • 2024 Integrated Report: FY2023 crypto-asset business overview reports revenue of ¥57.1 billion and says the combined asset deposit balance of SBI VC Trade and BITPOINT exceeded ¥260 billion at FY2023 end. — Integrated Report 2024
  • May 12, 2023, FY2022 results presentation: SBI disclosed that “in the mining business, a loss of approx. JPY 8.5bn was recorded” and that withdrawal from the mining business in Russia had been completed. — FY2022 Financial Results
  • August 2, 2023, interim results: the company states that the “Crypto-asset Business primarily consists of crypto-asset exchange business which provides crypto-asset exchange and trading services,” with quarterly revenue of ¥6,845 million. — Interim Results Announcement for the Three Months Ended June 30, 2023

Financial news / other:

  • February 14, 2023, company release: SBI announced acquisition of the remaining 49% of BITPoint Japan, making the crypto exchange a wholly owned subsidiary. — Additional Acquisition of Shares of BITPoint Japan
  • 2022 shareholder-benefit presentation: SBI offered eligible shareholders crypto-asset XRP benefits, evidencing explicit crypto-linked shareholder engagement. — Financial Results for the Nine-month Period Ended December 31, 2021

SEMLER SCIENTIFIC

  • Rank: 47 | Expo: 2.72 | id_str: US81684M1045
  • Weight summary: holders 1.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 4
  • Primary sources: 2024 annual report / 10-K, 2024 earnings calls, financial news coverage of the bitcoin treasury strategy

Firm Overview

Semler Scientific, Inc. is a U.S.-listed healthcare technology company focused on products and services for chronic disease management, especially cardiovascular testing and related software. During 2024, it also adopted bitcoin as its primary treasury reserve asset, making crypto exposure primarily a balance-sheet and capital-allocation story rather than an operating-business line. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports a pure holders classification. In its 2024 Form 10-K, Semler states that in May 2024 it “adopted bitcoin as [its] primary treasury reserve asset” and that its strategy includes acquiring and holding bitcoin using excess operating cash as well as potential debt or equity issuance. (sec.gov)

There is no evidence in the reviewed sample that Semler mined bitcoin, ran an exchange, provided custody or brokerage, or sold crypto-related infrastructure or business services. Instead, the firm’s crypto exposure comes from deliberate treasury accumulation and the resulting gains/losses and market sensitivity tied to bitcoin holdings. (sec.gov)

invbase = 1 is warranted because the company’s own risk disclosures explicitly acknowledge that investors may view Semler’s stock as a vehicle for bitcoin exposure, and that spot bitcoin ETP availability could affect the market price of its shares. That is direct evidence of a crypto-linked investor-base channel. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

holders receives 1.0 because the reviewed sources consistently frame bitcoin as a treasury reserve strategy, not as an operating input or service offering. The 2024 10-K is explicit that Semler adopted bitcoin as its “primary treasury reserve asset,” while 2024 earnings materials emphasize bitcoin holdings, BTC yield, and unrealized gains from those holdings. No reviewed source supports allocating weight to miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Transcript access was partially constrained because some direct transcript pages were blocked, but at least one earnings-call transcript quote was still retrievable through indexed transcript coverage, and company earnings releases also summarize the same treasury strategy. The main judgment call is materiality over time: crypto exposure begins only in May 2024, so Semler is unclassified for most of 2017–early 2024, but by year-end 2024 the balance-sheet exposure is so large that holders = 1.0 is still the best full-period mechanism classification. AssetShare is coded 4 because the 10-K shows bitcoin investments of about $214.6 million against total assets of about $218.3 million at December 31, 2024, implying bitcoin represented well over 45% of assets. (investing.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 18, 2025, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript: “One of our major accomplishments was adopting Bitcoin as our primary treasury reserve asset in May 2024.” — Investing.com transcript coverage
  • February 18, 2025, Q4 2024 results release / call notice: Semler reported a “BTC Yield of 107% since July 1, 2024” and said it “Now holds 3,192 BTC.” — Semler Scientific IR release
  • November 2024, Q3 2024 results release: pre-tax income included an unrealized gain from the change in fair value of bitcoin holdings, showing treasury-holding exposure rather than service revenue. — Semler Scientific IR release

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2024 Form 10-K: “In May 2024, we adopted bitcoin as our primary treasury reserve asset on an ongoing basis.” — SEC Form 10-K
  • 2024 Form 10-K: as of December 31, 2024, Semler held 2,298 bitcoins acquired for an aggregate purchase price of $189.7 million. — SEC Form 10-K
  • 2024 Form 10-K: fair-value table shows “Bitcoin investments” of $214.633 million and “Total Assets” of $218.271 million at year-end 2024, indicating bitcoin dominated assets. — SEC Form 10-K
  • 2024 Form 10-K: the company states it does not currently intend to hedge its bitcoin holdings, reinforcing a deliberate hold strategy. — SEC Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • May 28, 2024, CNBC: Semler announced a new bitcoin treasury strategy and shares surged on the news, showing market recognition of the company as a bitcoin proxy. — CNBC
  • 2024 Form 10-K risk disclosure: Semler says investors may view its stock as providing exposure to bitcoin and may compare it with spot bitcoin ETPs. — SEC Form 10-K

COMPOSECURE A

  • Rank: 48 | Expo: 2.52 | id_str: US20459V1052
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.3, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.7, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: 2024 Form 10-K, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript, company press release / product announcements

Firm Overview

CompoSecure, Inc. is a U.S.-listed technology and manufacturing company best known for premium metal payment cards, with an additional Arculus security business focused on authentication and digital-asset wallet products. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, its crypto relevance comes from supplying wallet/security infrastructure and related wallet-enabled functionality rather than from holding crypto on balance sheet or mining it. SEC Form 10-K

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports primarily infrastr exposure because CompoSecure explicitly markets Arculus Cold Storage as a hardware cold-storage wallet that stores users’ private keys offline in a metal card and supports digital assets including bitcoin and ethereum. In the 2024 Form 10-K, the company describes Arculus Cold Storage as allowing consumers to “store the private keys to their digital assets” and as a consumer product for secure digital-asset storage, which is best classified as crypto infrastructure rather than a generic business service. SEC Form 10-K

A smaller finserv weight is also warranted because the company’s wallet product goes beyond passive hardware security: the 10-K states that the Arculus Cold Storage Wallet allows users to “buy and swap digital assets,” and management/product announcements describe integrations that let users convert cash into USDC and interact with digital-asset rails. That indicates some direct enablement of crypto transactions, even if CompoSecure is not itself a major exchange, broker, or custodian. SEC Form 10-K CompoSecure / GlobeNewswire, April 21, 2025 announcement about 2025 integration describing the product model

There is no evidence in the reviewed sources that CompoSecure mined crypto or adopted a treasury bitcoin strategy, so miners and holders remain zero. invbase = 0 is appropriate because the reviewed filings and transcript discuss Arculus as a product/business line, but do not explicitly say the stock trades as a crypto proxy or that management sees crypto-driven shareholder flows. SEC Form 10-K Investing.com Q4 2024 transcript coverage

Weight Assignment

infrastr receives 0.7 because the core crypto-related activity evidenced in both the 10-K and earnings call is the provision of wallet/security hardware and embedded cold-storage functionality for digital assets. finserv receives 0.3 because Arculus also enables crypto purchases, swaps, and related transaction functionality, but the sources still frame CompoSecure primarily as a technology/security provider rather than as a financial intermediary whose main business is custody, exchange, or brokerage. No reviewed source supports positive weights for holders, miners, or bizserv. SEC Form 10-K Investing.com Q4 2024 transcript coverage

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is whether Arculus should be treated as entirely infrastr or partly finserv. The exclusion rules point toward infrastr for firms supplying hardware/security infrastructure, but CompoSecure’s own disclosures also mention wallet-based buying, swapping, and payment functionality, which justifies a minority finserv allocation. Transcript retrieval for earlier years was limited, but at least one in-sample earnings-call transcript quote was retrievable and consistent with the 2024 filing evidence. RevShare is coded 1 because the Q4 2024 earnings call states Arculus generated $10.5 million of 2024 revenue versus total company net sales of $420.6 million, implying low-single-digit crypto-related revenue. AssetShare is coded 0 because no material crypto assets on CompoSecure’s own balance sheet were identified. Investing.com Q4 2024 transcript coverage SEC Form 10-K

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 5, 2025, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript: “Arculus cold storage enables users to safeguard their digital asset keys with advanced encryption.” — Investing.com transcript coverage
  • March 5, 2025, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript: management said “Arculus generated $10,500,000 of revenue” in 2024, indicating a small but real crypto-related revenue stream. — Investing.com transcript coverage
  • March 5, 2025, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript: management said Q4 marked the “first quarter of positive net contribution from Arculus,” confirming it as an operating business line rather than a passing experiment. — Investing.com transcript coverage

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2024 Form 10-K: Arculus Cold Storage lets consumers “make transactions and store the private keys to their digital assets in the same metal cards.” — SEC Form 10-K
  • 2024 Form 10-K: consumer-product disclosure says the Arculus Cold Storage Wallet is for “simple and secure storage of digital assets for consumers.” — SEC Form 10-K
  • 2024 Form 10-K: the wallet “allows users to easily and securely buy and swap digital assets,” supporting a minority finserv component. — SEC Form 10-K
  • 2024 Form 10-K: the wallet supports “Bitcoin, Ethereum, non-fungible tokens (NFTs) and others,” confirming substantive crypto functionality. — SEC Form 10-K
  • 2024 Form 10-K: company-wide 2024 net sales were $420.6 million, useful for calibrating Arculus materiality against the transcript-stated $10.5 million revenue figure. — SEC Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • March 5, 2025, company results release: CompoSecure said it ended 2024 with its “first quarter of positive net contribution from Arculus,” corroborating the transcript. — GlobeNewswire results release
  • April 21, 2025, company announcement: Arculus integrated with MoneyGram Access to let users add and withdraw USDC in cash, illustrating transaction-enabling digital-asset functionality around the wallet product. — CompoSecure announcement
  • August 10, 2022, company release: CompoSecure described itself as providing “cryptocurrency and digital asset storage and security solutions,” consistent with infrastructure exposure over time. — CompoSecure IR release

NANO LABS A

  • Rank: 49 | Expo: 2.49 | id_str: KYG6391Y1281
  • Weight summary: holders 0.4, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.6, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 4
  • Primary sources: 2024 Form 20-F, 2024 interim and full-year earnings releases, December 2024 Bitcoin-holdings announcement

Firm Overview

Nano Labs Ltd is a China-based fabless chip designer listed on Nasdaq that develops high-throughput and high-performance computing chips and related product solutions. Within the sample period, its crypto relevance comes from selling computing products designed for cryptocurrency mining while, by 2024, it also adopted Bitcoin as a treasury reserve asset. SEC Form 20-F Nasdaq Sept. 18, 2024 release PR Newswire Mar. 25, 2025 release

Exposure Channels Identified

infrastr is the primary category because Nano Labs explicitly describes its HTC chips, HPC chips, and iPollo blockchain mining machines as products designed for cryptocurrency mining, including Bitcoin, ETC, Grin, and Filecoin. The 2024 Form 20-F says historical revenue was primarily derived from sales of mining-related HTC and HPC solutions, and the first-half 2024 earnings release still ties revenue changes to sales volume of iPollo V-series products. SEC Form 20-F Nasdaq Sept. 18, 2024 release

holders also applies because by late 2024 management had formally established “Bitcoin value investment” as a new business sector, adopted Bitcoin as its primary reserve asset, and disclosed roughly 361–401 BTC holdings around year-end 2024. That is a deliberate treasury policy rather than incidental operational custody, so a meaningful minority weight belongs in holders. SEC Form 20-F SEC filing / Dec. 2024 press release excerpt Nasdaq Dec. 12, 2024 coverage

Weight Assignment

infrastr receives 0.6 because the firm’s core, repeatedly documented crypto exposure over 2017–2024 is supplying chips and machines for crypto-mining workloads rather than mining on its own account or providing financial intermediation. holders receives 0.4 because the 2024 transition to a stated Bitcoin treasury strategy is explicit and economically material by year-end, but it is still newer than, and secondary to, the firm’s longstanding mining-hardware/product business. No reviewed source supports positive weights for miners, finserv, or bizserv. SEC Form 20-F PR Newswire Mar. 25, 2025 release

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is transcript access: a 2025 earnings-call transcript page was identifiable, but full direct scraping was blocked, so the earnings-call anchor relies on the accessible earnings-release text announcing the call and management comments rather than a fully scraped transcript webpage. Because the instructions require at least one earnings-call quote, certainty is kept at 3 rather than 4. There is also some timing sensitivity: the strongest holders evidence appears in late 2024 and early 2025 disclosures describing 2024 year-end holdings, so the classification emphasizes the 2024 endpoint of the sample. invbase is set to 0 because no explicit evidence was found that Nano Labs was discussed by management or mainstream financial media as a crypto-proxy stock driven by crypto-focused shareholder flows. RevShare is coded 2 because the 2024 Form 20-F shows total 2024 revenue of RMB40.6 million and indicates that revenue still primarily comes from mining-solution product sales, making crypto-related infrastructure revenue effectively the main operating business, though the exact 2024 crypto-only share is not separately segmented. AssetShare is coded 4 because the 20-F reports US$33.8 million of cryptocurrencies at December 31, 2024, versus total current assets of about RMB110.8 million (US$15.5 million) at June 30, 2024 in the interim release and a company profile indicating a very large balance-sheet role for crypto by year-end; this supports a major, balance-sheet-dominant crypto-asset presence. SEC Form 20-F Nasdaq Sept. 18, 2024 release Seeking Alpha transcript index page

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 25, 2025, Second Half 2024 earnings call / results statement: “We have formally established Bitcoin value investment as a new business sector and adopting Bitcoin as our primary reserve asset.” — PR Newswire results release
  • March 25, 2025, Second Half 2024 earnings call / results statement: “As of the date of this press release, the Company holds about 400 BTC with a total purchase price of around $40 million.” — PR Newswire results release
  • September 18, 2024, First Half 2024 earnings call / results statement: management said the company had been “heavily investing in the research and development of our upgraded Cuckoo series products,” linking recovery expectations to mining-chip product upgrades. — Nasdaq first-half 2024 results release

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • April 11, 2025, Form 20-F: “Our products... are currently designed primarily for the mining of various cryptocurrencies, such as Bitcoin, Ethereum Classic, Grin and Filecoin.” — SEC Form 20-F
  • April 11, 2025, Form 20-F: “Historically, our revenues were primarily derived from the sales of our HTC solutions... and HPC solutions for Bitcoin mining.” — SEC Form 20-F
  • April 11, 2025, Form 20-F: product table lists Darkbird 1.0 for “BTC and BCH” and iPollo blockchain mining machines, confirming mining-hardware infrastructure exposure. — SEC Form 20-F
  • April 11, 2025, Form 20-F: “We have formally established Bitcoin value investment as a new business sector and adopted Bitcoin as our primary reserve asset.” — SEC Form 20-F
  • April 11, 2025, Form 20-F: “As of December 31, 2024, we carried US$33.8 million of cryptocurrencies on our balance sheet, consisting of approximately 361 Bitcoins and 9 USDTs.” — SEC Form 20-F
  • December 10–12, 2024, company filing / release: Nano Labs said it invested in Bitcoin through HashKey and later said holdings exceeded 360 BTC, valued at over US$36 million. — SEC filing excerpt

Financial news / other:

  • December 12, 2024, Nasdaq/TipRanks coverage: Nano Labs announced Bitcoin holdings of about 360 BTC worth over US$36 million, corroborating the treasury-investment strategy. — Nasdaq coverage
  • March 25, 2025, company release: second-half 2024 revenue fell primarily because of lower iPollo V Series sales, reinforcing that crypto-mining hardware remained the core operating exposure through 2024. — PR Newswire results release
  • Seeking Alpha transcript index shows in-sample earnings-call coverage exists for Nano Labs, but direct page retrieval was blocked during scraping. — Seeking Alpha transcript index

METROPOLITAN BANK HDG.

  • Rank: 50 | Expo: 2.43 | id_str: US5917741044
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcript index and Q1 2023 call excerpt, 2022–2024 10-K filings, company press releases, Reuters/CoinDesk/S&P Global coverage

Firm Overview

Metropolitan Bank Holding Corp. is the parent of Metropolitan Commercial Bank, a New York-based commercial bank serving business, commercial and retail clients. Within the sample period, its crypto exposure came through banking and payments services for digital-asset businesses and their customers, rather than through owning crypto or mining activity. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The firm fits finserv because management and filings describe services to digital-currency businesses as cash management, debit card, payment and account services, and the bank’s broader Global Payments Group provided BaaS, cash settlement and custodian deposit services. In the 2022 Form 10-K, the company stated that it had four active institutional crypto-asset-related clients and that it did not hold crypto-assets on its balance sheet and had no loans outstanding to those clients, which rules out holders and miners. (sec.gov)

The exposure appears to have been economically peripheral and in wind-down mode by 2023. Management announced on January 9, 2023 that the company would fully exit the crypto-asset-related vertical, and subsequent filings reported declining crypto-related deposits as that exit progressed. (tech.yahoo.com)

Weight Assignment

A full 1.0 weight to finserv is appropriate because the identifiable crypto mechanism was direct provision of banking/payment infrastructure to crypto firms and their customers, which falls squarely within crypto financial services under the coding rules. No evidence supports treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, or non-financial crypto services, and the bank explicitly said it did not hold crypto-assets on balance sheet. Because crypto-related clients generated only about 1.5%–2% of revenue and the business was exited in 2023, RevShare is coded 1 and AssetShare 0. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is transcript access. A transcript index and a specific Q1 2023 transcript page were identifiable, but direct scraping of the full transcript page was blocked, so the earnings-call anchor relies on the accessible transcript metadata plus company and filing language that is clearly contemporaneous with the earnings discussion. Because a full retrievable transcript text could not be scraped directly, certainty is kept at 3 rather than 4. invbase is set to 0 because no explicit evidence was found that management discussed crypto-focused shareholders or that major financial media characterized MCB stock itself as a retail crypto proxy. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • April 18, 2023, Q1 2023 earnings call transcript page: “Welcome to Metropolitan Commercial Bank’s first quarter 2023 earnings call.” The in-sample transcript page is identifiable, anchoring the period of management discussion around the crypto exit, though full page scraping was blocked. — Seeking Alpha transcript page
  • Q1 2023 transcript coverage index: transcript listings show MCB had earnings-call transcripts including Q1 2023 and Q4 2022 during the relevant period. — Seeking Alpha transcript index

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 28, 2023, Form 10-K for 2022: “The Company has four active institutional crypto-asset related clients where the Company’s activities are limited to providing debit card, payment, and account services.” — SEC 2022 Form 10-K PDF
  • February 28, 2023, Form 10-K for 2022: “The Company has no loans outstanding to any of these clients, does not hold crypto-assets on its balance sheet and does not market or sell crypto-assets to its customers.” — SEC 2022 Form 10-K PDF
  • February 28, 2023, Form 10-K for 2022: the bank said it would “fully exit the digital currency business” following developments in the crypto industry and regulatory environment. — SEC 2022 Form 10-K PDF
  • April 18, 2023, Q1 2023 results: “Total core deposits, which do not include crypto related deposits of $278.5 million, were $4.9 billion at March 31, 2023.” — Company Q1 2023 results page
  • July 20, 2023, Q2 2023 results: deposits increased in non-crypto verticals and were “partially offset by a decrease of $220.4 million in crypto-related deposits.” — Company Q2 2023 results page
  • March 14, 2025, Form 10-K for 2024: the current business description no longer includes digital-currency activity, consistent with the completed exit. — SEC 2024 Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • January 9, 2023, Reuters: Metropolitan said it would exit the cryptocurrency-related asset vertical as the industry crisis deepened. — Reuters/Yahoo syndication
  • January 9, 2023, The Block: CEO Mark DeFazio said crypto-related clients, assets and deposits had never represented a material portion of the company’s business or exposed it to material financial risks. — The Block
  • April 20, 2023, CoinDesk: the bank was almost done exiting crypto, with only $278.5 million in crypto-related deposits left; the article also reported the crypto clients had contributed about $1 million of revenue and 6% of deposits in Q3 2022. — CoinDesk
  • January 2023, S&P Global: Metropolitan’s crypto business was about 1.5% of revenue and 6% of deposits, corroborating low materiality. — S&P Global Market Intelligence

FLOW TRADERS

  • Rank: 51 | Expo: 2.42 | id_str: BMG3602E1084
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: company earnings-call transcripts (2021–2023), annual reports (2021–2022), Reuters coverage

Firm Overview

Flow Traders N.V. is a Dutch listed proprietary trading and market-making firm best known for providing liquidity in exchange-traded products across Europe, the Americas, and Asia. During 2017–2024 it expanded that core model into digital assets, acting as a market maker and liquidity provider in crypto ETPs and OTC spot/digital-asset markets.

Exposure Channels Identified

The relevant crypto channel is finserv. Flow Traders explicitly described itself as providing crypto liquidity, trading cryptocurrencies since 2017, making markets in crypto ETPs, supporting spot Bitcoin ETF launches, and operating in OTC spot and digital-asset markets, which fits direct crypto financial services rather than ancillary support. (flowtraders.com)

No evidence supports holders as a treasury-investment story: the annual report instead states that digital assets were held because the group acts as a broker-trader, with positions presented as trading assets and liabilities and results included in gross trading income. That excludes a balance-sheet HODL interpretation under the coding rules. There is also no evidence of mining, or of infrastructure provision such as ASICs, hosting, or power supply. (flowtraders.com)

Weight Assignment

A full 1.0 weight to finserv is appropriate because the company’s crypto exposure is consistently described as direct market making, liquidity provision, and trading in crypto ETPs and spot digital assets. The evidence is repeated across multiple earnings-call transcripts and annual reports, and Reuters independently characterized the firm as becoming a significant player in European crypto ETPs with about a 45% market share in EMEA in early 2022. RevShare is coded 1 because crypto appears meaningful but clearly not dominant within total group income; the 2023 transcript says fixed income, commodity, crypto and currency together accounted for only 7% of group NTI, implying crypto alone was below that level. AssetShare is coded 1 because the firm carried digital-asset trading positions on balance sheet, but the available evidence does not suggest they were a dominant share of total assets. (flowtraders.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is materiality precision. Flow Traders clearly disclosed the existence and importance of digital-asset trading, but publicly accessible sources retrieved here do not isolate crypto-only revenue or crypto-only asset balances with enough precision for a higher materiality code than the conservative 1/1 assignment. invbase is set to 0 because no explicit evidence was found that management discussed crypto-focused shareholders or that major financial media framed Flow Traders stock itself as a retail crypto proxy. (flowtraders.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 10, 2022, Q4/FY 2021 results transcript: “From a crypto perspective, the trend has been more positive. Crypto ETP Value Traded recovered strongly in the fourth quarter” and the firm said it was prepared for spot crypto ETFs in the U.S. — Flow Traders Q4/FY 2021 transcript
  • February 10, 2022, Q4/FY 2021 results transcript: “We expanded our coverage of crypto ETPs” and were accelerating the footprint in “ETP, spot and derivative products.” — Flow Traders Q4/FY 2021 transcript
  • February 9, 2023, Q4/FY 2022 results transcript: “We have continued to grow our presence in the global crypto financial ecosystem.” — Flow Traders Q4/FY 2022 transcript
  • February 9, 2023, Q4/FY 2022 results transcript: the firm said it acted “as a market maker on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange first virtual asset ETF.” — Flow Traders Q4/FY 2022 transcript
  • February 8, 2024, Q4/FY 2023 results transcript: management said it was “prepared for the first US spot Bitcoin ETF launches, acting as a lead market maker for our partners.” — Flow Traders Q4/FY 2023 transcript
  • February 8, 2024, Q4/FY 2023 results transcript: fixed income, commodity, crypto and currency together accounted for 7% of group NTI in 2023. — Flow Traders Q4/FY 2023 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2021 Annual Report: Flow Traders said it increased focus on digital assets, became market maker in the first U.S. crypto ETF, and was the initial liquidity provider for TP ICAP’s digital-assets wholesale trading platform. — Flow Traders Annual Report 2021
  • 2021 Annual Report: “Flow Traders’ crypto liquidity provision commenced 4 years ago and spans OTC spot as well as ETPs.” — Flow Traders Annual Report 2021
  • 2021 Annual Report: the group said it acts as a broker-trader for digital assets, with holdings measured as inventories held for sale in the normal course of business. — Flow Traders Annual Report 2021
  • 2022 Annual Report: “Flow Traders’ crypto liquidity provision commenced 5 years ago and spans OTC spot as well as ETPs. We are connected to 20 trading venues globally and trade 300 coins.” — Flow Traders Annual Report 2022
  • 2022 Annual Report: the firm said it was the number one liquidity provider in crypto ETPs in Europe with about 45% market share and 27% global on-exchange share. — Flow Traders Annual Report 2022
  • 2022 Annual Report: auditors identified digital-asset positions and related income/expenses as a key audit matter, with positions presented as trading assets and trading liabilities. — Flow Traders Annual Report 2022

Financial news / other:

  • November 17, 2022, company statement: “Flow Traders remains committed to building the digital asset ecosystem as both a market maker and strategic investor.” — Company statement
  • November 17, 2022, company statement: “Flow Traders has been trading crypto currencies since 2017.” — Company statement
  • February 10, 2022, Reuters: Reuters reported that Flow Traders was becoming a significant player in European crypto ETPs and had roughly 45% market share among 125 EMEA crypto ETP listings. — Reuters / MarketScreener syndication

ZYMEWORKS

  • Rank: 52 | Expo: 2.18 | id_str: US98985Y1082
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcript listings (2022–2024), SEC filings / 10-K, company investor relations materials

Firm Overview

Zymeworks Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing multifunctional biotherapeutics for oncology and other difficult-to-treat diseases. Across the retrieved investor-relations, transcript-listing, and SEC filing materials for the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, the firm appears to be discussed entirely as a drug-development company rather than as a crypto-exposed business. (seekingalpha.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto exposure channel was identified. The available evidence does not show balance-sheet crypto holdings, mining activity, crypto financial services, non-financial crypto services, or crypto-focused infrastructure. (sec.gov)

The transcript retrieval step did identify that Zymeworks has public earnings-call transcripts available for 2022–2024, satisfying the search requirement, but no accessible source retrieved here indicated any substantive discussion of Bitcoin, blockchain, digital assets, or cryptocurrency. Because the classification is stable as non-crypto after transcript/filing/news searches, unclass is set to 1.0. (seekingalpha.com)

Weight Assignment

All mechanism categories are assigned 0.0 and unclass is assigned 1.0. This is because the firm’s retrieved disclosures and investor materials center on oncology pipeline progress, licensing/collaboration economics, cash runway, and regulatory milestones, with no credible evidence of substantive crypto-related operations or assets during 2017–2024. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that several direct transcript or IR pages returned access errors through the browser tool, so no long-form call text could be scraped directly from those pages. However, the available transcript listings confirmed the existence of calls in 2022–2024, and the SEC 2024 Form 10-K contained no matches for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” “blockchain,” “digital asset,” or “cryptocurrency,” which makes the non-crypto classification stable. No evidence was found that Zymeworks stock was treated as a crypto proxy, so invbase is set to 0. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 24, 2022 / March 7, 2023 / November 7, 2023 / March 6, 2024 / October 31, 2024, Seeking Alpha transcript index: public transcript listings show regular Zymeworks earnings calls, but the retrieved listings and linked materials did not indicate crypto-related subject matter. — Seeking Alpha ZYME transcript index
  • March 7, 2023, company release: “2022 was a very exciting year for Zymeworks” and discussion centered on revenue, cash runway, clinical data, and the Jazz collaboration rather than any digital-asset activity. — Zymeworks Q4/FY 2022 results release
  • March 6, 2024, company release: the Q4/FY 2023 materials highlighted cash resources, BLA filing progress, Phase 3 activity, and partner milestones, with no crypto exposure described. — Zymeworks Q4/FY 2023 results release

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 6, 2025 filing for FY 2024 Form 10-K: SEC filing searched for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” “blockchain,” “digital asset,” and “cryptocurrency,” with no matches found. — Zymeworks 2024 Form 10-K
  • March 6, 2024, FY 2023 results release: management described projected cash runway into the second half of 2027 and regulatory/commercial milestones for zanidatamab, again without crypto references. — Zymeworks Q4/FY 2023 results release
  • March 5, 2025, FY 2024 results release: company materials continued to frame the business around biotech development and capital planning rather than crypto-related activities. — Zymeworks Q4/FY 2024 results release

Financial news / other:

  • October 31, 2024, investor relations event page: supporting materials for the third-quarter 2024 results call were standard biotech earnings materials, with no indication of digital-asset operations. — Zymeworks Third Quarter 2024 Results Conference Call
  • February 8, 2022, investor relations notice: the firm described itself as a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company developing multifunctional biotherapeutics. — Zymeworks to Host Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2021 Results Conference Call

NUVEI SUBORDINATE VOTING DEAD - DELIST.19/11/24

  • Rank: 53 | Expo: 2.18 | id_str: CA67079A1021
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 2 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: company earnings materials (2022–2024), annual information form / annual report, company crypto product press releases

Firm Overview

Nuvei Corp. was a Canadian payments technology company headquartered in Montreal that operated a global payments platform for merchants before being taken private in 2024. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, the retrieved sources show that crypto exposure sat inside its payments and on/off-ramp business, especially after the 2021 acquisition of Simplex and the rollout of merchant cryptocurrency payment capabilities. (nuvei.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The relevant exposure channel is finserv. Nuvei explicitly offered crypto payment acceptance, pay-in/payout support, and fiat-to-crypto on-ramp/off-ramp capabilities, and it acquired Simplex specifically to deepen that functionality for exchanges, brokers, wallets, and liquidity providers. (nuvei.com)

There is no evidence of mining, and the small digital-asset balances disclosed in filings were operational working-capital balances rather than a treasury investment strategy, so holders is not assigned. The firm also does not fit bizserv or infrastr, because the crypto activity described in sources involved directly processing, enabling, and facilitating crypto transactions rather than merely selling adjacent software or hardware. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

finserv is assigned 1.0 because the crypto exposure described across transcripts, filings, and company releases is entirely financial-services based: merchant crypto payments, direct on-ramp services, and infrastructure for buying/selling cryptocurrency. Although management later said digital assets and cryptocurrencies were no longer material to Nuvei, that statement affects materiality rather than mechanism; the mechanism itself remained clearly finserv. RevShare is coded 2 because crypto-related revenue was clearly meaningful but not dominant: fourth-quarter 2022 crypto-related revenue was $19.2 million against total quarterly revenue of $220.3 million, which is roughly 8.7%. AssetShare is coded 0 because the AIF states Nuvei’s own digital-asset holdings were less than 0.1% of consolidated assets. (fintool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that some direct transcript pages were inaccessible through the browser tool, so the earnings-call anchor relies on transcript aggregators and contemporaneous reporting of management comments rather than a fully scraped IR-hosted transcript page for every period. That does not materially affect classification because the company’s own filings and press releases independently and explicitly document the crypto payment and on-ramp business. No direct evidence was found that Nuvei’s stock was treated as a primary crypto proxy investor vehicle, so invbase is set to 0. (fintool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 8, 2023, Q4/FY 2022 earnings materials: Nuvei separately disclosed “revenue attributable to digital assets and cryptocurrencies,” indicating crypto was tracked as a distinct revenue contributor in reported results. — Nuvei Announces Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2022 Results
  • March 8, 2023, earnings-call coverage: Philip Fayer told analysts that “digital assets and cryptocurrencies [are] no longer material to Nuvei,” confirming both prior exposure and its declining importance by that date. — The Logic - Crypto woes hit Nuvei’s Q4 revenue
  • March 8, 2023, transcript aggregator: management stated, “it’s no longer material to us,” in discussion of the digital-assets business on the Q4 2022 earnings call. — Fintool NVEI Q4 2022 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 5, 2024, Annual Information Form: “Nevada Services holds digital assets representing less than 0.1% of Nuvei’s consolidated assets” and the company “does not engage in speculative behavior,” indicating no meaningful treasury-holder exposure. — Nuvei 2023 Annual Information Form (SEC exhibit)
  • March 5, 2024, Annual Information Form: the filing states the digital assets are used “for working capital purposes to reduce the number of transaction and related fees on the blockchain,” supporting exclusion from holders. — Nuvei 2023 Annual Information Form (SEC exhibit)
  • March 8, 2023, results coverage of SEC filing: crypto-related revenue was $19.2 million in Q4 2022 versus total revenue of $220.3 million, indicating moderate but non-dominant crypto revenue exposure. — CoinDesk - Payments Technology Firm Nuvei Q4 Crypto Revenue Drops 58% From Year Ago

Financial news / other:

  • March 29, 2021, company release: Nuvei “added pay-in and payout support for nearly 40 of the world’s leading cryptocurrencies,” showing direct crypto payment functionality for merchants. — Nuvei Adds Payment Solution for Nearly 40 Cryptocurrencies
  • May 6, 2021, company release: Nuvei announced it would acquire Simplex, described as “a leading fiat-cryptocurrency gateway” connecting exchanges, brokers, wallet and liquidity providers. — Nuvei to Acquire Simplex, a Payment Solution Provider to the Cryptocurrency Industry
  • March 16, 2022, company release: Nuvei partnered with Ledger to enable direct on-ramps for “125+ cryptocurrencies” via Simplex by Nuvei. — Nuvei Partners with Ledger to Offer Direct Crypto On-Ramp for Millions of Users

RYVYL

  • Rank: 54 | Expo: 2.17 | id_str: US39366L3078
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 4 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: 2021–2023 earnings calls, 2021 and 2023 10-K filings, company press releases

Firm Overview

RYVYL Inc., formerly GreenBox POS, is a U.S.-listed fintech company focused on payment processing, merchant acquiring, and related banking-as-a-service products. Across filings, earnings calls, and company releases during 2017–2024, its crypto-adjacent activity appears through blockchain-based payments infrastructure and its coyni USD-pegged token platform rather than through mining or treasury crypto holdings. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The firm fits finserv because it directly provides payment-processing and transaction products built around blockchain and tokenized payment rails, and management repeatedly described the business as a digital-payments platform with a stablecoin-based transactional foundation. In its 2023 10-K, RYVYL said it develops “blockchain-based payment solutions,” collects fees from merchant payment activity, and offers the Coyni Platform with a digital token in a closed-loop ecosystem. (sec.gov)

The transcript anchor is the Q4 2022 earnings call, where management referenced “failing crypto exchanges” and described RYVYL’s long-term objective in digital payments, while the company’s Q2 2023 results release said it was “reinventing the future of financial transactions” using the coyni stablecoin platform. This is direct handling of payment and tokenized financial products, so finserv applies rather than bizserv or infrastr. (fintool.com)

There is no evidence of mining activity, and the 2023 10-K explicitly states adoption of the new FASB crypto-asset guidance was not expected to affect the company because it “currently do[es] not hold any crypto assets,” so holders is excluded. The evidence also does not show RYVYL primarily as a seller of hardware, compute, or energy infrastructure to crypto firms, so infrastr is not assigned. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

finserv is assigned 1.0 because the crypto-related mechanism evidenced across transcripts, filings, and press releases is entirely financial-services based: payment processing, merchant acquiring, tokenized transaction flows, e-wallet/off-ramp functionality, and the coyni stablecoin/payment platform. RevShare is coded 4 because the company’s core reported revenue appears to come overwhelmingly from payment-processing activities that management and filings describe as blockchain-ledger-based and token-enabled; this indicates crypto-linked financial-services exposure is a major part of the business model rather than a side activity. AssetShare is coded 0 because the 2023 10-K states the company did not hold crypto assets, so there is no meaningful crypto-asset balance-sheet exposure. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A key limitation is that RYVYL often framed itself as a blockchain payments company rather than a conventional cryptocurrency firm, and one company statement explicitly said it had “no exposure to crypto firms” that had filed for bankruptcy. That statement does not negate crypto-related finserv exposure under this framework, because the same statement and related filings describe a USD-pegged digital token, wallets, custodial backing, and blockchain-settled payment activity. No clear evidence was found that RVYL functioned as a primary crypto-proxy stock in financial media or management commentary, so invbase is set to 0. (tipranks.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • April 24, 2023, Q4 2022 earnings call transcript: management said, “Against failing crypto exchanges, volatility, and more recently bank failures, RYVYL continues to make meaningful strides toward achieving its long-term objective of becoming a disruptive force in the digital payments market.” — Fintool RVYL Q4 2022 transcript
  • April 24, 2023, Q4 2022 earnings call transcript: management attributed growth to “the expansion and growth of our advanced blockchain ledger-based payment solutions product offering.” — Fintool RVYL Q4 2022 transcript
  • August 14, 2023, Q2 2023 earnings call transcript: management discussed coyni and said it had released the “coyni mPOS app” for contactless payments. — Fintool RVYL Q2 2023 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 26, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K: RYVYL said it “develops, markets, and sells innovative blockchain-based payment solutions.” — RYVYL 2023 Form 10-K
  • March 26, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K: the filing says the Coyni Platform “features a digital token” and that QuickCard includes “off-ramp e-wallet management.” — RYVYL 2023 Form 10-K
  • March 26, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K: the company said the new FASB crypto-asset guidance was not expected to affect results because “we currently do not hold any crypto assets.” — RYVYL 2023 Form 10-K
  • March 31, 2022, 2021 results release: 2021 revenue rose to $26.3 million and management tied growth to blockchain-ledger-based payment solutions. — GreenBox POS Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2021 Financial Results

Financial news / other:

  • August 14, 2023, company release: RYVYL said it was leveraging “USD-pegged stablecoin technology” and using its coyni platform “as a transactional foundation.” — RYVYL Reports Second Quarter 2023 Financial Results
  • November 21, 2022, company release carried by Nasdaq: management said it launched the coyni platform “including our US dollar pegged stablecoin.” — RYVYL Reports Third Quarter 2022 Financial Results
  • March 13, 2023, company statement carried by TipRanks/The Fly: RYVYL said it was “not a cryptocurrency firm” but described coyni as a USD-pegged stablecoin backed dollar-for-dollar in a custodial account. — RYVYL Inc. says ‘no exposure to crypto firms’ that filed for bankruptcy

SPRINGWORKS THERAPEUTICS DEAD - DELIST.02/07/25

  • Rank: 55 | Expo: 2.13 | id_str: US85205L1070
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2022, 2024), Q1 2024 earnings call transcript

Firm Overview

SpringWorks Therapeutics, Inc. was a U.S.-listed commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on rare diseases and cancer, including OGSIVEO for desmoid tumors and a pipeline of oncology and rare-disease candidates. Across the sampled filings and earnings-call materials, the company is described as a precision-medicine drug developer and commercializer, with no identified crypto-related business line or balance-sheet exposure. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive crypto exposure channel was identified for SpringWorks Therapeutics during the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period. The available earnings-call transcript reviewed focused on drug commercialization, reimbursement, pipeline development, and cash runway, with no mention of bitcoin, blockchain, crypto assets, mining, exchanges, custody, tokenization, or crypto-linked infrastructure. (fintool.com)

The company’s 2022 and 2024 Form 10-K filings likewise describe a biotechnology business centered on developing and commercializing medicines for rare diseases and cancer, and text searches within those filings returned no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain.” This rules against assignment to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr on the evidence retrieved. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

unclass is assigned 1.0 because the searched transcript, annual reports, and supporting web results provide no credible evidence of any meaningful crypto mechanism exposure. There is no indication of treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, crypto financial services, non-financial crypto services, or crypto-directed infrastructure provision, so all mechanism weights other than unclass are set to 0.0. (fintool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

At least one earnings-call transcript was retrievable and reviewed, satisfying the transcript search step, but no crypto-related discussion was found in that call. Additional transcript listings were visible via transcript aggregators, but the classification was already stable as unclassified after review of the call transcript plus SEC filings; no evidence was found in financial news that SpringWorks was treated as a crypto proxy stock or had a crypto-linked investor base. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • May 2, 2024, Q1 2024 earnings call transcript: management said, “We are focused on delivering Ogsiveo to the broad desmoid tumor community,” reflecting a biotech commercialization focus rather than any crypto activity. — Fintool SWTX Q1 2024 transcript
  • May 2, 2024, Q1 2024 earnings call transcript: the call discussed product revenue, payer access, pipeline programs, and profitability funding runway, with no crypto-related terms found in transcript text search. — Fintool SWTX Q1 2024 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 20, 2025, 2024 Form 10-K: SpringWorks described itself as a registrant with audited consolidated financial statements for a commercial-stage therapeutics business; text searches found no references to crypto, bitcoin, or blockchain. — SpringWorks 2024 Form 10-K
  • February 28, 2023, 2022 Form 10-K: the filing described the company’s drug-development and commercialization activities; text searches found no references to crypto, bitcoin, or blockchain. — SpringWorks 2022 Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • Transcript listing coverage across 2020–2025 was found, but no result indicated any crypto-related event, product, or business line for SpringWorks. — Seeking Alpha SWTX transcript index
  • January 10, 2025, company release summary: SpringWorks was described as a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on severe rare diseases and cancer, with no crypto angle noted. — SpringWorks preliminary Q4/full-year 2024 results release

APPLIED DIGITAL

  • Rank: 56 | Expo: 1.99 | id_str: US0381692070
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 4 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: 2024 Form 10-K, Q3 2024 and Q3 2023 earnings call transcripts

Firm Overview

Applied Digital Corporation is a U.S.-listed digital infrastructure company that evolved from Applied Blockchain into a builder and operator of data centers across North America. During the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, its crypto linkage came through providing powered hosting infrastructure to bitcoin and crypto-mining customers rather than through direct crypto investing, custody, or exchange activity. (ir.applieddigital.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports classification as infrastr only. The company’s 2024 Form 10-K states that its Data Center Hosting Business “provides energized infrastructure services to crypto mining customers,” served seven crypto-mining customers, and accounted for about 83% of fiscal 2024 revenue, which makes crypto-directed infrastructure provision economically central rather than incidental. (ir.applieddigital.com)

Earnings-call evidence is consistent with that framing. In the April 11, 2024 Q3 2024 call, management discussed rising demand for “hosting capacity” as bitcoin prices increased, and in the April 6, 2023 Q3 2023 call management described its expertise in “hosting Bitcoin mining,” linking the firm’s infrastructure directly to mining workloads. There is no retrieved evidence of direct treasury-style crypto holdings, active mining operations during the sample endpoint, crypto financial services, or non-financial advisory/service exposure that would justify holders, miners, finserv, or bizserv. (fintool.com)

Weight Assignment

infrastr is assigned 1.0 because the firm’s identified crypto exposure operates entirely through purpose-built data center and energized-space infrastructure for crypto miners, and this segment represented the majority of revenue in the latest in-sample 10-K reviewed. The exclusion rules point away from bizserv because this is physical compute/power infrastructure, away from miners because the company states it no longer mines crypto and has no plans to return to mining, and away from holders because no deliberate balance-sheet crypto investment policy was evidenced. (ir.applieddigital.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Applied Digital pivoted over time from earlier crypto-mining activity toward hosting and then broader HPC/AI infrastructure, so classification must focus on mechanism rather than brand identity. The 2024 filing still references possible residual value of cryptoassets mined before cessation of mining operations, but the retrieved evidence does not show a current strategic treasury-holding policy or material crypto-asset balance-sheet exposure, so no holders weight is assigned. No direct evidence was found that management explicitly described the shareholder base as crypto-driven, so invbase remains 0 despite the company’s visible market association with bitcoin-mining infrastructure. (ir.applieddigital.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • April 11, 2024, Q3 2024 earnings call transcript: management said, “we are getting a lot more calls about hosting capacity… as the price of Bitcoin has went up significantly,” directly tying demand for its hosting infrastructure to bitcoin-mining conditions. — Fintool APLD Q2 2024 transcript
  • April 6, 2023, Q3 2023 earnings call transcript: management said, “We are well positioned for success in this space, given our expertise in hosting Bitcoin mining,” describing the firm’s infrastructure capabilities as rooted in crypto-mining workloads. — Insider Monkey APLD Q3 2023 transcript
  • April 6, 2023, Q3 2023 earnings call transcript: CFO said the company expected nearly 500 megawatts of hosting capacity once facilities were online, with energy costs generating hosting revenues at the Jamestown site. — Insider Monkey APLD Q3 2023 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • August 29, 2024, 2024 Form 10-K: “Our Data Center Hosting Business provides energized infrastructure services to crypto mining customers.” The filing adds that this segment accounted for approximately 83% of fiscal 2024 revenue. — Applied Digital 2024 Form 10-K
  • August 29, 2024, 2024 Form 10-K: the filing states, “In March 2022, we decided to terminate our crypto mining operations,” supporting exclusion of miners in the in-sample endpoint classification. — Applied Digital 2024 Form 10-K
  • July 5, 2024, shelf/prospectus filing: the company stated, “we no longer mine cryptoassets and have no plans to return to crypto mining operations,” while describing experience in “crypto mining/hosting operations.” — Applied Digital prospectus supplement

Financial news / other:

  • April 15, 2025, Reuters-carrying report: Applied Digital’s “data center hosting business, which provides energized space to cryptocurrency mining customers,” reported revenue decline, corroborating market characterization of the firm’s crypto exposure as infrastructure hosting. — Investing.com / Reuters report on Applied Digital results
