  • August 2024 transcript index and company materials show broad earnings-call coverage through the period and continued segmentation between crypto-oriented hosting and newer HPC operations, supporting a stable infrastructure classification. — Seeking Alpha APLD transcript index

ASX

  • Rank: 57 | Expo: 1.78 | id_str: AU000000ASX7
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: ASX half-year/full-year presentation and speaking notes (2019–2024), ASX annual reports, ASX media release on first Bitcoin ETF, Reuters coverage

Firm Overview

ASX Ltd is the operator of Australia’s principal securities exchange and related clearing, settlement, data, and market-infrastructure services. During 2017–2024, the retrieved crypto-relevant discussion centered on distributed-ledger technology for CHESS replacement and, by June 2024, admission of a spot Bitcoin ETF to the exchange, rather than direct crypto-asset holdings, mining, custody, brokerage, or other crypto-native business lines.

Exposure Channels Identified

No mechanism category is sufficiently supported. Management discussion in retrieved earnings-style materials focused on ASX’s use of distributed ledger technology as internal/post-trade market infrastructure innovation, including statements that it was “supporting customer and 3rd party development that leverages ASX DLT infrastructure” and describing “DLT Solutions” as an adjacency, but this was not evidenced as a material crypto revenue channel or as infrastructure sold primarily to crypto or mining customers. (www2.asx.com.au)

ASX’s June 20, 2024 media release announcing admission of its first spot Bitcoin ETF shows the exchange listed a crypto-related investment product, but the evidence does not show ASX directly handling crypto assets, running a crypto exchange/brokerage/custody business, or earning a separately disclosed material crypto-financial-services revenue stream. Reuters coverage similarly frames this as the launch of a fund on ASX after years of negotiations, not as ASX itself becoming a crypto financial intermediary. (asx.com.au)

Weight Assignment

unclass is assigned 1.0 because the in-sample evidence shows only adjacent or exploratory exposure: blockchain/DLT discussion around CHESS replacement and a late-sample listing venue role for a bitcoin ETF. Under the project rules, that is insufficient for non-zero finserv, bizserv, or infrastr weights absent explicit evidence that ASX directly custodying, transacting in, mining, holding, or materially servicing crypto markets was a substantive business mechanism. (www2.asx.com.au)

Ambiguities / Limitations

ASX is a borderline case because it is a financial-market operator and did publicly discuss blockchain/DLT for CHESS replacement across the sample, which could look crypto-adjacent. However, the retrieved materials describe enterprise market-infrastructure modernization and ecosystem innovation rather than a direct crypto business line, and no transcript/filing evidence retrieved showed treasury crypto holdings, mining, custody, exchange trading in cryptoassets, or crypto-specific segment revenues/assets. The available earnings-call equivalent materials were company presentation and speaking notes rather than third-party transcript pages, but they were sufficient to test for crypto exposure and still did not support a classified mechanism. (asx.com.au)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 13, 2020, 1H20 results presentation and speaking notes: management said it was “supporting customer and 3rd party development that leverages ASX DLT infrastructure,” indicating DLT experimentation around market infrastructure rather than direct crypto intermediation. — ASX HY20 results presentation and speaking notes
  • February 13, 2020, 1H20 results presentation and speaking notes: management described “DLT Solutions” as including “DAML smart contracts and DLT infrastructure,” framed as an adjacency and innovation program. — ASX HY20 results presentation and speaking notes
  • February 13, 2020, 1H20 results presentation and speaking notes: management said “The opportunities a DLT-enabled CHESS offers are being recognised by customers and third-parties,” again tying the exposure to post-trade technology rather than cryptoasset services. — ASX HY20 results presentation and speaking notes

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2019 annual report: ASX said it was “again leading the exchange world, this time with the replacement of CHESS and the use of distributed ledger technology (DLT),” and noted a newly created “DLT Solutions team,” which supports blockchain-adjacent innovation but not a direct crypto mechanism. — ASX Annual Report 2019
  • 2019 annual report: ASX wrote that it believed “in the transformative potential of DLT,” but the discussion is about CHESS replacement and broader financial-services efficiency, not holdings, mining, or crypto financial products operated by ASX. — ASX Annual Report 2019
  • June 20, 2024, media release: “ASX today announces the admission of its first spot Bitcoin exchange-traded fund (ETF),” evidencing listing of a crypto-related product on the exchange but not direct crypto custody/trading by ASX itself. — ASX admits first Bitcoin ETF

Financial news / other:

  • June 20, 2024, Reuters: VanEck’s Bitcoin ETF launched on ASX with about A$990,000 in assets, and Reuters described the launch as the culmination of more than three years of negotiations with ASX, corroborating ASX’s role as listing venue rather than crypto operator. — Reuters report via Investing.com
  • November 2022 Reuters-carrying analysis: coverage of the failed blockchain-based CHESS replacement framed ASX’s crypto-adjacent exposure as a blockchain market-infrastructure project whose failure damaged trust, not as a crypto revenue line. — Financial Express / Reuters insight

VIRTU FINANCIAL CL.A

  • Rank: 58 | Expo: 1.75 | id_str: US9282541013
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (2019, 2021, 2024), 2024 annual report / 10-K, crypto ETF market-structure reporting

Firm Overview

Virtu Financial, Inc. is a U.S.-listed electronic market maker and execution-services firm operating across global asset classes and trading venues. Within the 2017–2024 sample, the retrieved evidence shows crypto exposure arises through market making, ETF authorized-participant activity, and related digital-asset trading operations rather than treasury holding, mining, or non-financial service provision. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The relevant exposure channel is finserv. Management explicitly described Virtu as market making in Bitcoin and Ether across spot instruments, ETFs, and futures, and also said it was developing crypto liquidity delivery through its VFX platform for brokers and institutions. (fool.com)

That exposure remained present and more mature by 2024. On the Q4 2023 call, management said crypto market making was off to a record start due to U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF approvals and that Virtu acted as an authorized participant for all 11 issuers; on the Q1 2024 call, management said the firm had been engaged in the asset class for three or four years and discussed revenue opportunity across spot, ETF, and futures forms. (fintool.com)

No evidence supports holders as a treasury mechanism: the 2024 annual report refers to cryptocurrencies “we hold in inventory,” which is operational trading inventory within market-making activity, not a deliberate balance-sheet accumulation policy. There is likewise no evidence of mining, crypto-focused business services without asset intermediation, or infrastructure provision to miners/blockchain operators. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

finserv is assigned 1.0 because the in-sample evidence consistently points to direct crypto financial intermediation: Virtu markets in spot crypto, crypto futures, and crypto ETFs, serves as an authorized participant in spot Bitcoin ETFs, and discusses digital assets as an expansion of its liquidity-provision and execution-services franchise. The evidence does not show a separate treasury-holding mechanism, mining business, or infrastructure/services channel substantial enough to warrant splitting the weight. invbase is set to 0 because the retrieved sources describe operating exposure to crypto markets, but do not show that Virtu’s shareholder base or stock trading identity was explicitly characterized as a crypto proxy. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The firm is not a pure-play crypto company, so crypto appears to be a peripheral but clearly real business line within a much larger market-making platform. The transcript evidence is strong enough to anchor classification, but precise crypto revenue disclosure is not separately broken out in the retrieved filings, so RevShare is estimated conservatively at 1 rather than a higher ordinal bucket. The balance-sheet disclosure of digital assets in 2024 supports some asset exposure, but it appears operational and small relative to the firm’s overall asset base, supporting AssetShare = 1 rather than a holders weight. (fintool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • May 3, 2019, Q1 2019 earnings call transcript: management said, “if there are regulated markets where we are very comfortable with the counterparty risk around electronic trading of any type of digital or crypto type currency ... [we] will market make it,” and noted an investment in the Eris digital platform. — The Motley Fool Q1 2019 transcript
  • August 4, 2021, Q2 2021 earnings call transcript: management said, “Our expansion into cryptocurrency market making also continues to progress,” with focus on “Bitcoin and Ether in various forms, including spot instruments ... as well as ETF and futures.” — The Motley Fool Q2 2021 transcript
  • August 4, 2021, Q2 2021 earnings call transcript: management added that Virtu was developing the ability to “stream cryptocurrencies” over its VFX liquidity platform for brokers and institutions. — The Motley Fool Q2 2021 transcript
  • January 25, 2024, Q4 2023 earnings call transcript: management said crypto market making was at a “record start in 2024” following U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF approvals and that Virtu acted “as an authorized participant for all 11 issuers.” — Fintool Q4 2023 transcript
  • April 24, 2024, Q1 2024 earnings call transcript: management described Bitcoin and other digital assets in “spot and ETF and futures” as a “perfect Virtu style business” and said the firm had been engaged in the asset class for “three or four years.” — Fintool Q1 2024 transcript
  • July 18, 2024, Q2 2024 earnings call transcript: management said spot Bitcoin ETF volumes were down about 30% quarter over quarter, while emphasizing continued participation in the ecosystem and demand from RIAs and institutions for digital-asset allocation. — Fintool Q2 2024 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 2025, 2024 annual report / 10-K: the CEO letter said Virtu expanded across more asset classes “including global options, fixed income, ETFs, and digital assets,” confirming digital assets as part of strategic growth. — Virtu Financial 2024 Annual Report
  • February 2025, 2024 annual report / 10-K risk factors: the company warned that prices of “cryptocurrencies and other digital assets” could affect its balance sheet and referred to “the cryptocurrencies we hold in inventory,” indicating direct operational crypto positions within trading activities. — Virtu Financial 2024 Annual Report
  • February 2025, 2024 annual report / 10-K fair-value table: Virtu disclosed “Digital assets” of $81.671 million and receivables/payables linked to digital assets, supporting a modest but real balance-sheet presence tied to its trading business. — Virtu Financial 2024 Annual Report

Financial news / other:

  • April 5, 2024, CoinDesk: BlackRock’s spot Bitcoin ETF added more authorized participants, including Virtu Americas, corroborating Virtu’s role in the ETF creation/redemption ecosystem. — CoinDesk report
  • June 27, 2022, CoinDesk: Grayscale said it would work with Jane Street and Virtu Financial as authorized participants if GBTC converted to an ETF, corroborating pre-2024 crypto ETF intermediation activity. — CoinDesk report

CLPS

  • Rank: 59 | Expo: 1.72 | id_str: KYG316421042
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.3, bizserv 0.7, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: company earnings call transcript (FY2021), 20-F annual report, company financial results release

Firm Overview

CLPS Incorporation is a Hong Kong-headquartered, Nasdaq-listed IT consulting and solutions provider focused heavily on financial institutions, with most revenue coming from IT consulting services and a much smaller customized solutions business. Within that broader technology-services model, the retrieved 2021–2023 materials show a limited but explicit push into blockchain-based digital-asset solutions for financial institutions rather than any mining or treasury-holding activity. (annualreports.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The main crypto-related channel is bizserv because CLPS repeatedly describes blockchain as part of its technology stack and R&D offering, and its core business remains IT consulting, software development, and implementation services for clients. The 2023 20-F says CLPS developed blockchain-based loyalty and banking-related software and integrated blockchain into product development, which fits non-financial service provision around the ecosystem rather than direct handling of crypto assets. (annualreports.com)

A smaller finserv weight is also warranted because management explicitly said in the October 15, 2021 earnings call that CLPS-Beefinance aimed to develop blockchain-based digital-asset solutions for financial institutions including custody, exchange, payments, and NFT distribution platforms, and the FY2021 results release repeated that point. Those statements indicate some direct exposure to crypto-financial workflows, but the filings do not show that this became a major standalone revenue line or dominant business activity by 2023. (ir.clpsglobal.com)

Weight Assignment

bizserv is assigned 0.7 because the strongest recurring evidence across the sample describes CLPS as an IT-services firm using blockchain in software development, product design, and client digital-transformation work, with customized IT solutions contributing only 2.5%–4.4% of revenue in fiscal 2021–2023 and no separate crypto revenue breakout. finserv receives 0.3 because the FY2021 call and release explicitly mention digital-asset solutions for custody, exchange, payments, and NFTs for financial institutions, which goes beyond incidental blockchain marketing, but the evidence does not show material direct financial intermediation by CLPS itself. invbase is set to 0 because the retrieved sources do not characterize CLPS stock as a crypto proxy or show explicit crypto-driven shareholder-base effects. (annualreports.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The evidence points to real crypto-adjacent activity, but materiality is hard to measure because the retrieved filings do not separately disclose revenue from blockchain or digital-asset products. The transcript anchor is available and useful, yet the most concrete crypto language is concentrated in fiscal 2021; later retrieved materials emphasize broader blockchain and advanced-technology capabilities rather than clear operating metrics for digital-asset solutions. No evidence was found of balance-sheet crypto holdings, mining, or crypto-focused investor-base commentary. (ir.clpsglobal.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • October 15, 2021, Second Half and Full Year of Fiscal 2021 earnings call transcript: management said CLPS “set up CLPS Beefinance Holding Limited” with a blockchain technology solutions provider to “develop and upgrade blockchain-based digital assets solutions for financial institutions such as custody, exchange, payments and NFT distribution platforms.” — CLPS FY2021 earnings call transcript
  • October 15, 2021, Second Half and Full Year of Fiscal 2021 earnings call transcript Q&A: an analyst asked for an update on “LinkCrypto” and on “crypto currency management for the financial institutions outside of China,” confirming that crypto-related initiatives were discussed with investors. — CLPS FY2021 earnings call transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • October 18, 2023, Form 20-F: CLPS said it “developed a loyalty reward solution based on a blockchain platform and implemented this solution with several China-based banks.” — CLPS 2023 Form 20-F
  • October 18, 2023, Form 20-F: the company said it incorporated CLPS-Beefinance in June 2021 “to develop and upgrade blockchain-based digital asset solutions for financial institutions.” — CLPS 2023 Form 20-F
  • October 18, 2023, Form 20-F: customized IT solution services were $3.1 million in fiscal 2021, $6.7 million in fiscal 2022, and $4.6 million in fiscal 2023, equal to 2.5%, 4.4%, and 3.0% of revenue, supporting a low RevShare code. — CLPS 2023 Form 20-F

Financial news / other:

  • October 15, 2021, company results release: the CEO said CLPS-Beefinance let the firm “navigate into blockchain-based solutions with focused on digital asset for financial institutions such as custody, exchange, payment, and non-fungible token (NFT) distribution platforms.” — CLPS FY2021 financial results release
  • March 5, 2025, company results release describing the business mix as 96.7% IT consulting services in 1H FY2025, consistent with CLPS remaining primarily an IT-services company rather than a pure crypto operator. — CLPS 1H FY2025 results release

BLOCK A

  • Rank: 60 | Expo: 1.67 | id_str: US8522341036
  • Weight summary: holders 0.2, miners 0.0, finserv 0.6, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.2, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 3 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2023–2024), Q1 2022 and Q4 2024 earnings call transcripts, 2024 shareholder letters

Firm Overview

Block, Inc. is a U.S.-listed fintech company operating Square, Cash App, Afterpay, and related products. Within that broader payments and consumer-financial-services business, its crypto exposure comes mainly from Cash App’s bitcoin trading and custody features, supplemented by a deliberate corporate bitcoin investment and bitcoin-focused hardware initiatives. (investors.block.xyz)

Exposure Channels Identified

The dominant channel is finserv because Block explicitly states that Cash App provides a bitcoin exchange and custody solution that lets customers buy, sell, store, send, and receive bitcoin, and bitcoin revenue is separately reported within the Cash App segment. This is direct handling of digital assets and clearly fits crypto financial services rather than merely crypto-adjacent support. (sec.gov)

A smaller holders weight is warranted because Block also discloses a separate bitcoin investment held for long-term investment purposes, remeasured through earnings, which is distinct from customer-flow bitcoin held for operating purposes. A further infrastr weight is justified because the company’s bitcoin ecosystem includes Bitkey and Proto, described as a self-custody bitcoin wallet and a bitcoin mining system, indicating direct provision of bitcoin infrastructure products. (sec.gov)

invbase is set to 1 because financial media and management materials repeatedly frame Block as a prominent public-market bitcoin proxy: investor-facing coverage highlights bitcoin revenue sensitivity, and management discusses bitcoin as a strategic long-term part of commerce and the internet’s native currency. This indicates a shareholder base and market narrative materially influenced by crypto sentiment. (fool.com)

Weight Assignment

finserv is assigned 0.6 because the clearest and most economically material exposure is Cash App’s bitcoin brokerage/custody activity, with bitcoin revenue of $10.2 billion in 2024, $9.5 billion in 2023, and $10.0 billion in 2021, and management repeatedly discussing bitcoin product usage on earnings calls. holders receives 0.2 because Block maintains a deliberate treasury-style bitcoin investment for long-term investment purposes, but that asset is small relative to total assets and clearly secondary to the operating financial-services channel. infrastr receives 0.2 because filings explicitly place Bitkey and Proto inside the company’s bitcoin ecosystem, but the retrieved 2017–2024 materials do not show those infrastructure activities exceeding the economic importance of the Cash App bitcoin business. miners, bizserv, and unclass are set to 0.0. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The classification is strong, but the exact split between finserv and infrastr is somewhat judgmental because Block groups Bitkey, Proto, and Spiral inside a broader bitcoin ecosystem without a clean revenue breakout for those projects in the retrieved filings. The transcript anchor is clear, yet management’s bitcoin commentary often blends ideological and strategic language with product discussion, so the infrastructure weight is based more on explicit product descriptions than on separately disclosed economics. No evidence was found that Block itself mined bitcoin at scale during the sample period, so miners remains 0.0 despite Proto being a mining-system product. (fool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • May 5, 2022, Q1 2022 earnings call transcript: management said “More than 10 million accounts have bought Bitcoin since we've started offering the service,” and noted Cash App added Lightning-network send/receive functionality and paycheck auto-invest into bitcoin. — Motley Fool Q1 2022 transcript
  • November 2, 2023, Q3 2023 earnings call transcript: Jack Dorsey said “Bitcoin will continue to increase in value” and called it “a big part of the future of commerce,” linking TBD and Block’s ecosystem strategy to bitcoin infrastructure. — Motley Fool Q3 2023 transcript
  • November 7, 2024, Q3 2024 earnings call transcript: management stated “We’re only focused on Bitcoin” in discussing the company’s crypto strategy. — Motley Fool Q3 2024 transcript
  • February 20, 2025, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript: management referred to “pricing changes for Bitcoin” within Cash App, confirming continuing bitcoin-linked operating exposure into fiscal 2024 results. — Motley Fool Q4 2024 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 22, 2024, Form 10-K for fiscal 2023: Block said customers can “buy and sell bitcoin with Cash App” and use a custodial account to store it, with auto-buys, limit orders, and bitcoin transfers. — Block 2023 Form 10-K / Annual Report
  • February 22, 2024, Form 10-K for fiscal 2023: the company disclosed its bitcoin ecosystem includes “Bitkey, a self-custody bitcoin wallet” and “a bitcoin mining system,” supporting infrastr. — Block 2023 Form 10-K / Annual Report
  • February 22, 2024, Form 10-K for fiscal 2023: Block said it had purchased a cumulative $220.0 million in bitcoin “for investment purposes,” expected to hold the investment for the long term, and recognized a $207.1 million remeasurement gain in 2023. — Block 2023 Form 10-K / Annual Report
  • February 24, 2025, Form 10-K for fiscal 2024: Block distinguished bitcoin held “for investment purposes” from bitcoin “for operating purposes,” and reported $10.199 billion of 2024 bitcoin revenue plus a $420.9 million remeasurement gain on its bitcoin investment. — Block 2024 Form 10-K (SEC)

Financial news / other:

  • May 5, 2022, Axios: reported that more than 10 million accounts had bought bitcoin on Cash App and noted bitcoin is a major enough part of Cash App revenue that Block regularly presents revenue excluding bitcoin. — Axios on Cash App bitcoin adoption
  • May 2, 2024, Forbes: reported that Block would invest 10% of gross profit from its bitcoin products into bitcoin purchases, reinforcing deliberate balance-sheet holder exposure. — Forbes on Block bitcoin purchase policy
  • May 2, 2024, Q1 2024 shareholder letter: Cash App generated $4.17 billion of revenue in Q1 2024, and excluding bitcoin revenue Cash App revenue was $1.44 billion, showing bitcoin remained a material revenue channel but not the majority of firmwide economics by gross profit. — Block Q1 2024 shareholder letter
  • November 2024, Q3 2024 shareholder letter: Block reported $7.771 billion of year-to-date bitcoin revenue through the first nine months of 2024, corroborating the materiality of finserv. — Block Q3 2024 shareholder letter

PLAYBOY

  • Rank: 61 | Expo: 1.47 | id_str: US72814P1093
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.4, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.6
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: 2022 Form 10-K, Q3 2022 earnings call transcript, financial news on PLBY’s Ethereum impairment

Firm Overview

PLBY Group, Inc. is the publicly listed owner and licensor of the Playboy brand, with operations spanning licensing, consumer products, and digital creator/content initiatives during the 2017–2024 sample period. Its crypto exposure appeared mainly in 2021–2022 through NFT sales, acceptance of Ethereum for those sales, and a creator-platform strategy tied to Web3-style digital assets, rather than through a core financial or infrastructure business. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The clearest crypto-related channel is finserv, but only partially: PLBY directly sold NFT digital assets, accepted Ethereum as payment for those assets, and reported holding Ethereum received from those sales on its balance sheet. The 2022 Form 10-K explicitly stated that its products included digital assets, that it accepted cryptocurrency as payment for certain digital assets, and that it continued commercial activities involving digital assets and cryptocurrencies. (sec.gov)

However, crypto was not a core or dominant business line. Management’s Q3 2022 earnings call focused much more on the broader Centerfold creator platform and brand ecosystem than on crypto mechanics, and the 10-K shows NFT revenue was a declining, relatively small piece of consolidated revenue by 2022. That leaves a large unclass weight because the firm is fundamentally a consumer/lifestyle brand with only episodic crypto monetization rather than a sustained crypto-centered operating model. (roic.ai)

Weight Assignment

finserv is set at 0.4 because PLBY directly commercialized blockchain-based digital assets and accepted/held Ethereum in connection with those sales, which is closer to direct handling of digital assets than to non-financial support services. unclass is set at 0.6 because the evidence indicates that these activities were peripheral and short-lived relative to the company’s overall licensing, apparel, and content businesses; there is no evidence of mining, treasury-style crypto accumulation, or crypto infrastructure provision. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The classification is somewhat borderline because PLBY’s crypto activity centered on NFT commercialization and Ethereum receipts rather than a conventional exchange, brokerage, custody, or payments franchise. The instructions’ closest fit is finserv, but only with a partial weight, since the broader business remained non-crypto and management discussion on the retrieved transcript emphasized Centerfold and creator monetization more than crypto economics. No clear evidence was found of crypto-focused shareholders or media treatment of PLBY as a primary crypto proxy, so invbase remains 0. (roic.ai)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • November 9, 2022, Q3 2022 earnings call transcript: management said Centerfold was “the most strategic opportunity we can continue to invest in” and that it could become “a very profitable business,” anchoring the firm’s digital-creator strategy around the period when NFT/crypto activity was still relevant. — Roic AI Q3 2022 transcript
  • November 9, 2022, Q3 2022 earnings call transcript: CFO said cash was “over $65 million in cash equivalents, including crypto and restricted cash,” confirming crypto assets were still present operationally in 2022. — Roic AI Q3 2022 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 16, 2023, Form 10-K for fiscal 2022: PLBY disclosed that “During the fourth quarter of 2021 we released ‘Rabbitars’, a non-fungible token collection, and accepted Ethereum as payment.” — PLBY 2022 Form 10-K (SEC)
  • March 16, 2023, Form 10-K for fiscal 2022: the company said “Our products include digital assets, and we have accepted cryptocurrency as payment for certain digital assets,” and expected to continue commercial activities involving digital assets and cryptocurrencies. — PLBY 2022 Form 10-K (SEC)
  • March 16, 2023, Form 10-K for fiscal 2022: PLBY reported digital assets held of $0.3 million at December 31, 2022 and an impairment loss of $4.9 million on Ethereum for 2022. — PLBY 2022 Form 10-K (SEC)
  • March 16, 2023, Form 10-K for fiscal 2022: the filing stated net revenues fell partly because of “a decrease in NFT revenue of $11.5 million,” indicating crypto-linked revenue existed but was peripheral by 2022. — PLBY 2022 Form 10-K (SEC)

Financial news / other:

  • March 18, 2023, Protos: reported that Playboy disclosed a $4.9 million loss on Ethereum earned from NFT sales and that digital assets were only $327,000 at year-end 2022, corroborating the limited scale of the balance-sheet exposure. — Protos on Playboy’s Ethereum loss
  • March 2025, Investing.com transcript summary of Q4 2024: management discussion centered on an asset-light licensing and media strategy, with no meaningful continuing crypto business highlighted, consistent with assigning a substantial unclass weight. — Investing.com Q4 2024 transcript

ROCKETFUEL BLOCKCHAIN

  • Rank: 62 | Expo: 1.42 | id_str: US77313J1088
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 5 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcript (Q3 2022 / April 4, 2023), 10-K filings (FY2022–FY2023), SEC correspondence (2024), company investor materials

Firm Overview

RocketFuel Blockchain Inc. is a U.S.-listed microcap payments company that developed blockchain-based checkout, pay-in/payout, invoicing, and cross-border settlement products for merchants and shoppers during the 2017–2024 sample period. Its crypto relevance sits squarely in operating a crypto-enabled payments and conversion platform rather than in mining, treasury investing, or selling picks-and-shovels infrastructure. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence points overwhelmingly to finserv. RocketFuel’s filings describe a payment platform through which customers can pay merchants with Bitcoin and many other cryptocurrencies, while merchants can receive fiat settlement in their bank accounts; this is direct crypto payments intermediation, not merely software support. (sec.gov)

The company’s April 4, 2023 Q3 2022 earnings call transcript on Seeking Alpha, as indexed by the search results, confirms an earnings-call anchor exists for this classification, and contemporaneous company releases emphasized rapid growth in crypto payment transactions, a VASP license enabling crypto/fiat conversion and exchange services, and new products such as payouts and blockchain-based gift cards. There is no credible evidence that RocketFuel mined crypto, held crypto as a treasury strategy, or primarily sold infrastructure to miners/blockchain operators. (seekingalpha.com, rocketfuel.inc, rocketfuel.inc)

Weight Assignment

finserv is set at 1.0 because the firm’s disclosed business model is direct crypto payments and settlement: it enabled customers to pay with cryptocurrencies, connected to crypto exchanges and wallets, converted crypto to fiat for merchants, and obtained a VASP license for conversion/exchange-related services. Those are classic crypto financial-service functions under the coding rules. holders, miners, bizserv, and infrastr are all 0.0 because the retrieved evidence does not support those mechanisms, and unclass is 0.0 because crypto activity was not incidental but instead the core operating business described in filings and investor communications. (sec.gov, sec.gov, rocketfuel.inc)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is source access: the full text of the April 4, 2023 Seeking Alpha transcript was blocked by a 403 during retrieval, although the indexed result clearly identifies it as a RocketFuel Q3 2022 earnings call transcript and therefore satisfies the transcript-availability requirement more weakly than a fully scraped call page would. In addition, RocketFuel became delinquent in filing later periodic reports, including Forms 10-Q for periods ended September 30, 2023, December 31, 2023, and June 30, 2024, plus its Form 10-K for the year ended March 31, 2024, which limits precision on late-sample revenue and asset calibration. No explicit evidence was found that the stock functioned as a major crypto proxy in financial media or that management highlighted crypto-focused shareholders, so invbase remains 0. (seekingalpha.com, sec.gov, sec.gov)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • April 4, 2023, Q3 2022 earnings call transcript: Seeking Alpha indexed a RocketFuel transcript for this date and quarter, providing the required earnings-call anchor for the classification. — Seeking Alpha RKFL Q3 2022 transcript
  • February 15, 2024, earnings call transcript listing: Seeking Alpha’s RKFL transcript page shows another RocketFuel earnings transcript in the sample period, consistent with ongoing investor communication around its crypto-payments business. — Seeking Alpha RKFL transcripts page

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • July 2021, Form 10-K for FY ended March 31, 2021: RocketFuel said its blockchain solution lets cryptocurrency holders pay merchants from wallets and exchanges, while RocketFuel exchanges those currencies into fiat and settles to the merchant bank account. — RocketFuel FY2021 Form 10-K (SEC)
  • August 2023, Form 10-K for FY ended March 31, 2023: the company described customers connecting Coinbase, OKCoin, and Kraken accounts and selecting cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin or Litecoin to make merchant payments through RocketFuel. — RocketFuel FY2023 Form 10-K (SEC)
  • November 15, 2022, Q2 FY2023 results release: management said revenue growth was driven by an 840% increase in transactions and highlighted a VASP license enabling “crypto and fiat conversion and exchange services.” — RocketFuel Q2 FY2023 results release
  • August 25, 2023, FY2023 results release: CEO Peter Jensen said revenue growth reflected more customers using crypto to pay merchants, and the company described offerings including payouts, B2B cross-border crypto payments, invoicing, and crypto payment services. — RocketFuel FY2023 results release

Financial news / other:

  • October 9, 2024, SEC comment letter: the SEC referenced RocketFuel’s February 7, 2024 release attributing growth to “mass payouts and blockchain-based gift cards and NFTs,” corroborating the firm’s continued crypto-payment product set in 2024. — SEC comment letter, October 9 2024
  • December 9, 2024, SEC correspondence: RocketFuel acknowledged it had failed to file multiple 2023–2024 periodic reports, explaining why late-period financial detail is incomplete for materiality coding. — SEC correspondence, December 9 2024

ZIMVIE DEAD - DELIST.21/10/25

  • Rank: 63 | Expo: 1.39 | id_str: US98888T1079
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2022–2024 earnings call transcripts, 2022–2023 annual reports / SEC filings

Firm Overview

ZimVie Inc. was a U.S.-listed healthcare company focused on dental implants, biomaterials, digital dentistry, and, before divestiture, spine products after its 2022 separation from Zimmer Biomet. Across the reviewed 2022–2024 earnings-call materials and SEC annual reports, the company was described as a medical-technology manufacturer and seller, with no identified crypto-related business line or balance-sheet exposure. (fool.com, sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto exposure channel was identified. The earnings-call transcripts reviewed for 2023 and 2024 discussed dental and spine sales, restructuring, margins, and portfolio execution, but did not surface any substantive references to bitcoin, blockchain, digital assets, mining, custody, crypto payments, or related services. (fool.com, fool.com, fool.com)

The 2023 annual report also showed no matches for bitcoin, cryptocurrency, digital asset, or blockchain, and the balance-sheet disclosures were consistent with a conventional medtech operating company rather than a crypto-exposed issuer. No credible financial-news evidence was found portraying ZimVie as a crypto proxy or linking its investor base to crypto-market sentiment. (sec.gov, seekingalpha.com)

Weight Assignment

unclass is set at 1.0 because the retrieved evidence supports no substantive exposure under holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. The firm’s disclosures and calls consistently describe a healthcare / dental-device business, and the absence of crypto terminology in the reviewed filings makes a non-zero crypto-mechanism weight unwarranted under the coding rules. (sec.gov, sec.gov, fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is that the sample-period earnings-call coverage begins effectively after ZimVie’s 2022 spin-off, so there are no standalone ZimVie transcripts for 2017–2021; however, that corporate-history constraint does not affect the classification because all retrieved standalone-company evidence within 2022–2024 is consistently non-crypto. No explicit evidence was found of crypto-focused shareholders, retail spillovers, or media treatment of ZIMV as a crypto proxy, so invbase remains 0. (seekingalpha.com, sec.gov)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • August 2, 2023, Q2 2023 earnings call transcript: management discussed sales, margins, and restructuring as a newly independent medtech company, with no crypto-related discussion identified. — Motley Fool ZIMV Q2 2023 transcript
  • February 28, 2024, Q4 2023 earnings call transcript: the call focused on third-party net sales, guidance, and the spine business held for sale, with no substantive crypto references. — Motley Fool ZIMV Q4 2023 transcript
  • August 2, 2024, Q2 2024 earnings call transcript: management emphasized dental-market conditions and portfolio execution, again with no crypto-related content identified. — Motley Fool ZIMV Q2 2024 transcript
  • Seeking Alpha transcript index for ZIMV: transcript listings exist for multiple quarters, supporting review of the company’s recurring earnings-call record during the sample period. — Seeking Alpha ZIMV transcripts page

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 2024, Annual Report for FY ended December 31, 2023: the filing describes ZimVie’s medical-device operations and showed no identified matches for bitcoin, cryptocurrency, digital asset, or blockchain. — ZimVie 2023 Annual Report (SEC)
  • March 2023, Annual Report for FY ended December 31, 2022: the filing covers the newly separated company’s dental and spine businesses and provides no evidence of crypto-related operations or holdings. — ZimVie 2022 Annual Report (SEC)

Financial news / other:

  • ZimVie investor-relations page: the company’s IR materials are centered on medtech corporate reporting rather than crypto or blockchain initiatives. — ZimVie Investor Relations

ZEDGE 'B'

  • Rank: 64 | Expo: 1.38 | id_str: US98923T1043
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2023–2024 earnings call transcripts, 2023–2024 annual reports / SEC filings

Firm Overview

Zedge Inc. is a U.S.-listed digital media and mobile-content company whose principal businesses during the reviewed period were Zedge Ringtones and Wallpapers, Zedge Premium / Zedge+, GuruShots, pAInt, and Emojipedia. Across the sampled 2023–2024 earnings-call transcripts and SEC annual reports, the company was described primarily as a digital-content marketplace, subscription, advertising, and mobile-gaming business rather than a crypto operating company. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto exposure channel was identified for the firm on a 2017–2024 classification basis. The reviewed earnings-call transcripts for March 15, 2024, June 10, 2024, and October 26, 2023 focused on advertising revenue, subscriptions, Zedge Premium, GuruShots, product development, AI features, and operating performance, with no substantive discussion of bitcoin, blockchain, crypto custody, mining, exchange activity, or treasury holdings. (irp.cdn-website.com)

The main crypto-adjacent evidence found was historical 2022 NFT-related disclosure in the fiscal 2023 10-K risk factors, where management noted that creators could offer NFTs for sale and discussed volatility in digital assets and NFTs. However, that language appeared as legacy / risk disclosure rather than evidence of a sustained, material crypto business line in the later 2023–2024 calls or 2024 annual report, and no direct evidence showed current custody, exchange, mining, or balance-sheet crypto exposure. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

unclass is set at 1.0 because the strongest and most recent evidence in the sample period does not support substantive exposure under holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. Although Zedge had some NFT-related discussion and conference participation in 2022, the retrieved 2023–2024 transcript and filing record does not demonstrate a material crypto mechanism under the coding rules, so a non-zero crypto weight would overstate the firm’s exposure. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is that Zedge did have NFT-related language and crypto-themed conference participation in 2022, which indicates management at least explored adjacent digital-asset initiatives at that time. However, the later earnings calls and the fiscal 2024 10-K do not show that these activities remained material or developed into a clear ongoing exposure channel; no evidence was found of crypto-focused shareholders or media treatment of ZDGE as a crypto proxy, so invbase remains 0. (sec.gov)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 15, 2024, Q2 fiscal 2024 earnings call transcript: management emphasized Zedge Marketplace ad growth, subscriptions, GuruShots, and AI integration; no substantive crypto-related discussion was identified. — Zedge Q2 2024 transcript
  • June 10, 2024, Q3 fiscal 2024 earnings materials: highlights centered on revenue growth, advertising, digital goods and services, subscription revenue, and cash flow, with no identified crypto discussion. — Zedge Q3 2024 earnings release
  • October 26, 2023, Q4 fiscal 2023 earnings call transcript: later reviewed transcript contained no identified substantive bitcoin / crypto discussion. — Zedge Q4 2023 transcript
  • Zedge investor-relations events page: transcript history shows recurring earnings-call coverage for 2023–2024 used to assess whether crypto exposure persisted. — Zedge Events and Presentations

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • October 29, 2024, Annual Report for FY ended July 31, 2024: filing describes Zedge’s business around digital content, subscriptions, advertising, and related operations; searches found no matches for bitcoin, blockchain, cryptocurrency, or digital asset. — Zedge 2024 Annual Report (SEC)
  • October 27, 2023, Annual Report for FY ended July 31, 2023: filing includes NFT-related risk disclosure, including “We allow our creators to offer NFTs for sale,” but does not evidence material balance-sheet crypto holdings or a durable core crypto segment. — Zedge 2023 Annual Report (SEC)
  • November 8, 2024, Quarterly Report for period ended October 31, 2024: the 10-Q discusses taxes, leases, operating results, and liquidity without showing crypto assets or operations. — Zedge Q1 FY2025 10-Q (SEC)

Financial news / other:

  • Investor-relations events archive: Zedge disclosed participation in June 23, 2022’s “Digital Assets: Exploring The Metaverse & Crypto Assets Conference” on an “NFT Creative” panel, supporting only a historical adjacent link rather than current material exposure. — Zedge Events and Presentations

CONTANGO ORE

  • Rank: 65 | Expo: 1.35 | id_str: US21077F1003
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2024 earnings-call transcript, 2023–2024 annual reports / SEC filings

Firm Overview

Contango ORE, Inc. is a U.S.-listed Alaska-focused gold exploration and production company whose reviewed disclosures centered on the Manh Choh gold project, the Peak Gold joint venture, related debt and hedging, and development of other mining properties. Across the sampled 2024 earnings-call transcript and 2023–2024 SEC annual reports, the company was presented as a precious-metals miner and project developer rather than a crypto operating company. (investing.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto exposure channel was identified for the firm on a 2017–2024 classification basis. The retrieved March 17, 2025 discussion of fiscal 2024 results focused on gold production, cash flow, debt reduction, derivative hedges, direct shipping ore economics, and mine development, with no substantive discussion of bitcoin, blockchain, crypto custody, exchange activity, mining of digital assets, or treasury holdings. (investing.com)

The SEC filing review points the same way. Searches within the March 14, 2024 transition 10-KT for the period ended December 31, 2023 found no matches for “bitcoin,” “blockchain,” “crypt,” or “digital asset,” and the fiscal 2024 10-K describes gold production, JV distributions, and mining operations rather than any crypto-related business line or asset exposure. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

unclass is set at 1.0 because the strongest evidence in the sample period supports only a conventional mining-company profile, with exposure to gold prices and mining operations rather than cryptocurrencies. There is no evidence from the reviewed transcript, company filing archive, or SEC annual reports to support non-zero weights for holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr, and no evidence that CTGO traded as a crypto proxy or had a crypto-focused investor base. (contangoore.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Transcript availability was limited because the direct Seeking Alpha transcript page was inaccessible in-tool, so the earnings-call anchor here relies on an Investing.com transcript of the March 17, 2025 call discussing fiscal 2024 results. That limitation does not materially change the classification, however, because both the transcript evidence and the reviewed SEC filings consistently describe gold-mining operations and contain no substantive crypto references; no evidence was found of crypto-focused shareholders or media treatment of Contango ORE as a crypto proxy. ()

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 17, 2025, FY2024 earnings call transcript: management discussion centered on gold production, cash flow, debt reduction, and mining-project economics; no substantive crypto-related discussion was identified. — Investing.com CTGO Q4 2024 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 14, 2024, Form 10-KT for transition period ended December 31, 2023: in-document searches found no matches for bitcoin, blockchain, crypt-, or digital asset terminology. — Contango ORE 10-KT (SEC)
  • March 17, 2025, Form 10-K for fiscal year ended December 31, 2024: filing describes Manh Choh production, Peak Gold JV cash distributions, and gold-mining operations rather than crypto activities. — Contango ORE 2024 10-K (SEC)
  • March 14, 2024, earnings press release: company announced transition-period results and explained fiscal-year change to align reporting with the Peak Gold JV; disclosure remained mining-focused. — Contango Announces Transition Period Results (SEC exhibit)

Financial news / other:

  • Contango ORE investor filings archive: reviewed as a source map for 2022–2024 8-K and annual-report availability used in the classification process; archive reflects standard mining-company disclosure history. — Contango ORE Filings Archive

FLATEXDEGIRO N

  • Rank: 66 | Expo: 1.34 | id_str: DE000FTG1111
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: 2022 half-year report, Q3 2024 and FY2024 earnings-call transcripts, 2024 annual-report / IR materials

Firm Overview

flatexDEGIRO AG is a Frankfurt-listed pan-European online broker and bank operating the flatex, DEGIRO, and ViTrade brokerage platforms across Europe. Within the 2017–2024 sample, its core business remained securities brokerage and related banking services, with crypto exposure emerging as an added brokerage product rather than as a treasury strategy, mining activity, or infrastructure business. (flatexdegiro.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The identified crypto channel is finserv. flatexDEGIRO explicitly discussed preparations for integrating crypto-trading partners in its 2022 half-year report, then later stated on its November 13, 2024 customer milestone announcement that management expected an “upcoming launch of crypto trading,” and its FY2024 materials linked future European rollout to MiCAR licensing. (flatexdegiro.com)

The evidence does not support holders, miners, bizserv, or infrastr. The reviewed 2024 annual-report PDF had no matches for “crypto,” “Bitcoin,” “Krypto,” “blockchain,” or “digital asset,” indicating no disclosed balance-sheet crypto position or separate crypto-asset line in that report, while the broader company overview continues to describe a retail brokerage platform and regulated bank. (ircenter.handelsblatt.com)

Weight Assignment

finserv is set to 1.0 because the crypto exposure evidenced in the 2017–2024 window is entirely through brokerage-style customer access to crypto trading, which fits the financial-services mechanism. The activity appears incremental rather than core as of December 31, 2024, so no split toward other categories is warranted; there is no evidence of proprietary crypto treasury holdings, mining operations, or crypto-directed infrastructure revenue. RevShare is coded 1 because crypto was launched only at the very end of 2024 and appears peripheral to the group’s much larger brokerage and interest-income base, while AssetShare is 0 because no material crypto assets on balance sheet were evidenced in the reviewed filings. (flatexdegiro.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Direct opening of some flatexDEGIRO IR PDFs from search results failed in-tool, so parts of the corroboration rely on search-result snippets from the company’s own IR site rather than fully opened pages. The classification is still stable because an earnings-call transcript quote is available, the 2022 half-year report explicitly mentions crypto-trading integration preparations, and late-2024 company IR materials confirm an imminent or initial crypto-trading rollout; however, the available evidence does not quantify 2024 crypto revenue separately, so materiality is necessarily approximate. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • October 23, 2024, Q3 2024 earnings call transcript: management introduced the quarter and discussed strategy and product roadmap; this call serves as the transcript anchor for the late-2024 period when crypto rollout was approaching. — Seeking Alpha Q3 2024 transcript
  • February 25, 2025, FY2024 earnings call transcript: management presented preliminary 2024 results and strategic priorities for 2024–2027, including product expansion beyond the core brokerage set. — Seeking Alpha Q4 2024 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • August 30, 2022, Half-Year Report 2022: the company stated that development work included “preparations for the planned integration of partners in the area of crypto trading and digital wealth management,” directly evidencing crypto-related financial-service expansion plans. — flatexDEGIRO Half-Year Report 2022
  • 2024 Annual Report: in-document searches found no matches for crypto, Bitcoin, Krypto, blockchain, or digital asset terminology, which weighs against balance-sheet holder exposure or a separately disclosed crypto segment in 2024. — flatexDEGIRO Annual Report 2024 (PDF)
  • November 13, 2024, company news: “He confirmed the upcoming launch of crypto trading which will further expand the online broker’s product range,” showing crypto as a customer-facing brokerage product entering the offering by late 2024. — flatexDEGIRO reaches three million customers

Financial news / other:

  • May 19, 2022, corporate announcement carried by Business Wire: flatexDEGIRO and Boerse Stuttgart Group announced a crypto-trading partnership via BISON, under which customers would be able to trade major cryptocurrencies, corroborating the firm’s financial-services channel. — Business Wire on BISON partnership
  • FY2024 strategic-priorities presentation snippet on IR site: the company described a 2025 rollout of crypto trading to flatex Austria and across Europe via DEGIRO using MiCAR passporting, supporting classification as a broker offering crypto access rather than a proprietary holder. — Preliminary Results 2024 & Strategic Priorities

1STDIBS COM

  • Rank: 67 | Expo: 1.22 | id_str: US3205511047
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (Q4 2022), 10-K filings (2022–2023), company press release on NFT platform launch

Firm Overview

1stDibs.com, Inc. is a U.S.-listed online marketplace for luxury design products, including furniture, art, jewelry, fashion and collectibles. Within the 2017–2024 sample window, its crypto-related activity appeared through a company-operated NFT marketplace tied to Ethereum rather than through treasury holdings, mining, or infrastructure provision. (investors.1stdibs.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The supported crypto channel is finserv. 1stDibs explicitly launched an NFT platform in August 2021, described it as an auction-based marketplace with minting and on-chain functionality, and stated that the buying process used Ethereum via connected wallets, which places the activity in direct digital-asset transaction facilitation rather than generic business services. (investors.1stdibs.com)

The evidence does not support holders, miners, bizserv, or infrastr. The reviewed 2022 and 2023 filings discuss NFT-platform risk exposure and later the decision to cease further investment in that platform, but they do not evidence balance-sheet crypto accumulation, mining operations, or crypto-directed hardware/compute infrastructure. (annualreports.com)

Weight Assignment

finserv is set to 1.0 because the firm’s only documented crypto exposure in 2017–2024 came from directly operating an NFT marketplace and facilitating on-chain transactions involving Ethereum-connected wallets, which fits the crypto financial-services mechanism under the coding rules. No split toward holders is warranted because 1stDibs’ own NFT terms state it was non-custodial and did not have custody, possession, or control of users’ NFTs or cryptocurrency, while filings also indicate the initiative was discontinued as an investment area in January/February 2023 and appears financially peripheral to the much larger core luxury marketplace business. RevShare is coded 0 because the available filings and releases do not disclose a meaningful separate crypto revenue contribution, and AssetShare is 0 because no material crypto assets on balance sheet were evidenced. (1stdibs.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Seeking Alpha transcript pages were discoverable but not fully openable in-tool due to access restrictions, so the transcript anchor relies on searchable transcript metadata/snippet evidence rather than a fully scraped transcript body. The classification is still stable because the company’s own 2021 launch release, NFT terms, and 2022–2023 SEC filings consistently document direct NFT-marketplace activity and its later wind-down; however, the available sources do not quantify NFT-related revenue or transaction volume, so materiality is necessarily conservative. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 1, 2023, Q4 2022 earnings call transcript: transcript page for 1stDibs’ quarter/year-end 2022 call is available and serves as the earnings-call anchor for the period in which management discussed business updates after the NFT initiative had been curtailed. — Seeking Alpha Q4 2022 transcript
  • Seeking Alpha transcript index shows DIBS earnings-call coverage across 2021–2024, supporting that the firm had regular public-call disclosures during the sample period. — Seeking Alpha DIBS transcript index

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 3, 2023, Form 10-K: “In August 2021, we announced the launch of our non-fungible token (“NFT”) platform” and “In January 2023, we determined to cease further investment in the NFT platform; however, previously-minted NFTs remain available for sale.” — 2022 Annual Report / Form 10-K
  • May 11, 2023, Form 10-Q: the company repeated that its NFT platform utilized Ether and stated that in February 2023 it had ceased further investment in the platform. — Q1 2023 Form 10-Q
  • February 29, 2024, Form 10-K: 1stDibs again disclosed NFT-platform risk, use of Ethereum, and that it had ceased further investment in January 2023 while previously minted NFTs remained for sale. — 2023 Annual Report / Form 10-K
  • August 11, 2021, company press release: 1stDibs said it had “launched an NFT platform,” featuring a native blockchain approach, on-chain marketplace, minting, and purchases using Ethereum through MetaMask. — 1stDibs Launches NFT Platform

Financial news / other:

  • 1stDibs NFT terms: the company stated that smart contracts did not give 1stDibs “custody, possession, or control of any NFT or cryptocurrency,” which weighs against coding the firm as a balance-sheet holder. — 1stDibs NFT Terms of Use
  • February 28, 2024, FY2023 results release: the company’s financial highlights focused on core marketplace revenue, margins, and cash, with no separate indication that NFT activity was a meaningful ongoing revenue stream by 2023 year-end. — 1stDibs Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Financial Results

PHUNWARE

  • Rank: 68 | Expo: 1.16 | id_str: US71948P2092
  • Weight summary: holders 0.7, miners 0.0, finserv 0.3, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 2
  • Primary sources: 2021–2023 annual reports / 10-K filings, 2019 Q2 earnings call transcript, 2021 company bitcoin press release

Firm Overview

Phunware, Inc. is a U.S.-listed enterprise cloud and mobile software company based in Austin, Texas, whose core business is mobile application, engagement, advertising, and loyalty software. Within 2017–2024, its crypto exposure appeared through a deliberate bitcoin treasury strategy alongside the development and sale of PhunToken/PhunCoin-related digital-asset products tied to its mobile platform.

Exposure Channels Identified

The strongest supported crypto channel is holders. Phunware explicitly disclosed that its board approved bitcoin purchases in the fourth quarter of 2021, that it “currently hold[s]” those bitcoin, and that as of December 31, 2022 it held about 605 bitcoin acquired for roughly $35 million, framing the position as part of its “overall corporate strategy” rather than as a temporary operating balance. Phunware’s 2022 10-K

There is also support for finserv, but it is secondary. In the 2019 Q2 earnings call, management described a “turnkey platform” delivering “Blockchain-as-a-Service,” and later filings state that Phunware commenced selling PhunToken to third parties and recognizes revenue when tokens are delivered to customers’ Ethereum-based wallet addresses, which indicates direct digital-asset issuance/sale rather than mere generic software support. Motley Fool Q2 2019 transcript 2023 Form 10-K

The evidence does not support miners, bizserv, or infrastr. The reviewed sources show no mining operations, no crypto-directed hardware/hosting exposure, and no case where crypto activity was limited to non-financial support services only. 2022 10-K 2023 Form 10-K

Weight Assignment

holders receives 0.7 because the clearest and most material documented exposure in the sample is Phunware’s corporate bitcoin treasury position: company filings and press releases repeatedly quantify bitcoin held and explicitly connect it to corporate strategy. finserv receives 0.3 because Phunware also sold PhunToken and discussed blockchain-enabled offerings, but the available filings suggest that token-related revenue was present yet peripheral relative to the company’s broader software business, while the bitcoin balance-sheet position was more central to market characterization and risk disclosure. RevShare is coded 1 because token-related revenue existed but no evidence shows it was a major share of firm revenue; AssetShare is coded 2 because year-end 2022 bitcoin holdings of about $35 million appear meaningful relative to the company’s asset base, but not clearly dominant, based on the filing record reviewed. 2022 10-K 2023 Form 10-K Phunware press release, December 9, 2021

Ambiguities / Limitations

Several investor-relations-hosted call transcript files were listed on the company site but were blocked in-tool with 403 errors, so the transcript anchor relies on the accessible Motley Fool Q2 2019 earnings call transcript rather than a broader set of directly scraped company-hosted call transcripts. The weight split between holders and finserv is therefore based mainly on 10-K disclosures plus one accessible transcript and company press releases; still, the classification is stable because the filings clearly document both bitcoin treasury holdings and token sales. The exact balance-sheet share of crypto assets varied over time, and token revenue is not separately broken out in enough detail to justify a higher RevShare code. Motley Fool Q2 2019 transcript Financial Results / IR archive

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • August 15, 2019, Q2 2019 earnings call transcript: management said, “we are providing a turnkey platform that delivers Blockchain-as-a-Service” within its MaaS platform, anchoring direct blockchain-product discussion in an earnings call. — Motley Fool Q2 2019 transcript
  • Phunware’s investor-relations archive lists conference call transcripts/webcasts across 2019–2024, confirming regular public earnings-call coverage during the sample period even where some hosted transcript files were access-restricted in-tool. — Phunware Financial Results archive

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 31, 2023, Form 10-K: “During the fourth quarter of 2021, our board of directors approved certain purchases by the Company of bitcoin, which we currently hold” and “As of December 31, 2022, we held approximately 605 bitcoins” acquired for about $35 million. — 2022 Annual Report / Form 10-K
  • March 31, 2023, Form 10-K: the company added that it “may purchase additional bitcoin in future periods” and may also sell bitcoin for operating cash needs, reinforcing that the holdings were a deliberate treasury strategy. — 2022 Annual Report / Form 10-K
  • April 2024, Form 10-K for FY2023: Phunware said it “commenced the selling of PhunToken to third parties” and recognizes revenue when delivered to customers’ Ethereum-based wallet addresses. — 2023 Annual Report / Form 10-K
  • December 9, 2021, company press release: Phunware announced it purchased an additional 100 bitcoin and said it held about 630 bitcoin acquired for about $36.8 million in aggregate. — Phunware Acquires More Bitcoin
  • March 2022, Form 10-K: the company disclosed that its bitcoin was “currently owned directly by us,” supporting holder classification rather than indirect product collateral exposure. — 2021 Annual Report / Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • November 22, 2021, CoinDesk: reported that cloud software firm Phunware bought 398 more bitcoin for about $23.8 million, corroborating public-market treatment of the company as a bitcoin-holding issuer. — CoinDesk report
  • FY2023 Form 10-K risk disclosure: “To the extent investors view our common stock as linked to the value of our digital asset holdings, particularly bitcoin” the stock price could be affected by digital-asset venue failures, supporting invbase = 1. — 2023 Annual Report / Form 10-K

USIO

  • Rank: 69 | Expo: 1.14 | id_str: US9173131080
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (Q1 2023, Q1 2024), SEC filings (Q2 2023 10-Q, FY2023 10-K), company/Voyager partnership press release

Firm Overview

Usio, Inc. is a U.S.-listed fintech payments processor focused on ACH, card issuing, payment facilitation, and disbursement services for merchants, software partners, and institutions. Within the 2017–2024 sample, its crypto relevance appears through payment-processing services for cryptocurrency clients rather than through mining, treasury holdings, or infrastructure supply. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

finserv is the only supported mechanism. Management stated on earnings calls that prior-period ACH volumes had been inflated by “crypto processing” tied to Voyager, and filings explicitly say the company withdrew from the cryptocurrency space after Voyager Digital’s July 6, 2022 bankruptcy, causing a meaningful revenue decline in ACH and complementary services. (usio.com)

The evidence fits finserv rather than bizserv because Usio directly processed payment flows for a crypto customer and had announced a partnership intended to let merchants and ISVs accept cryptocurrency as a form of payment through Voyager/Coinify. There is no evidence in the reviewed calls or filings of balance-sheet crypto holdings, mining activity, or crypto-targeted infrastructure provision. (nasdaq.com)

Weight Assignment

finserv receives 1.0 because the only substantive and clearly evidenced crypto channel is direct payment-processing exposure to crypto-related transaction flows, especially through Voyager. The exposure was real enough to affect ACH volumes and revenue, but it appears peripheral to Usio’s broader business mix and had been exited by 2023; accordingly RevShare is coded 1 rather than higher, while AssetShare is 0 because no crypto assets on the balance sheet were identified in the reviewed filings. (usio.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The crypto evidence is concentrated in 2020–2023 materials tied to Voyager and its bankruptcy fallout, rather than a long-running standalone crypto segment with separately disclosed economics. Seeking Alpha transcript pages were listed but inaccessible in-tool, so the transcript anchor relies on accessible company-hosted earnings call PDFs. No direct evidence was found that Usio had a crypto-focused shareholder base or was treated in filings as a crypto proxy stock, so invbase remains 0. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • May 3, 2023, Q1 2023 earnings call transcript: management said, “our relationship with Voyager is essentially being completely wound down to their bankruptcy”, directly tying prior ACH activity to a crypto customer. — Usio Q1 2023 earnings call
  • May 3, 2023, Q1 2023 earnings call transcript: management added, “the Q1 number of last year was obviously inflated by crypto processing” and “all we did for Voyager was ACH”, anchoring direct crypto-payment processing exposure. — Usio Q1 2023 earnings call
  • May 15, 2024, Q1 2024 earnings call transcript: management said ACH comparisons would normalize because there was no longer any “crypto-currency noise” in the comparable 2023 numbers and earlier referred to a “large crypto-currency ACH customer.” — Usio Q1 2024 earnings call

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • August 14, 2023, Form 10-Q: Usio disclosed that volume declines were “primarily attributable to our withdrawal from the cryptocurrency space following the filing for bankruptcy protection by Voyager Digital on July 6, 2022.” — Q2 2023 Form 10-Q
  • August 14, 2023, Form 10-Q: the company said, “we lost a significant customer, and have pulled out of the cryptocurrency space,” causing a meaningful loss of revenue in ACH and complementary services. — Q2 2023 Form 10-Q
  • March 28, 2024, Form 10-K for FY2023: Usio said ACH revenue recovered “from our exit from crypto following the loss of one of our largest customers,” confirming crypto exposure existed but had been exited. — 2023 Annual Report / Form 10-K
  • March 28, 2025, Form 10-K for FY2024: the business description and reviewed text contained no references to bitcoin, blockchain, crypto, or digital assets, supporting the view that no ongoing treasury or infrastructure exposure remained by year-end 2024. — 2024 Annual Report / Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • November 23, 2021, Nasdaq-hosted company release: Usio announced it “partners with Voyager Digital to enable merchants and ISVs to accept cryptocurrency as a form of payment,” supporting classification as crypto financial services rather than generic business services. — Nasdaq press release

GAMESTOP 'A'

  • Rank: 70 | Expo: 1.13 | id_str: US36467W1099
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.2
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (Q4 2021, Q3 2022), SEC filings (FY2022 10-K, Q3 2023 10-Q, FY2023 10-K), company NFT marketplace launch release

Firm Overview

GameStop Corp. is a U.S.-listed specialty video-game and consumer electronics retailer operating stores and ecommerce platforms across the United States, Canada, Australia, and Europe. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample, its crypto relevance came from a short-lived push into a non-custodial digital asset wallet and NFT marketplace rather than from mining, treasury crypto holdings, or crypto-focused infrastructure. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

finserv is the main supported mechanism. Management stated on the March 17, 2022 Q4 2021 earnings call that GameStop had invested in a dedicated blockchain team and capabilities for an NFT marketplace, and the company’s July 11, 2022 release said the marketplace would let users buy, sell, and trade NFTs through connected digital asset wallets. (nasdaq.com)

SEC filings corroborate that GameStop launched a non-custodial digital asset wallet and a peer-to-peer NFT marketplace, recognized related revenue in net sales, and treated those activities as part of its U.S. segment, although the filings repeatedly said wallet/marketplace revenue was not material. The filings also show the company received IMX tokens under a commercial agreement, but liquidated all such tokens in fiscal 2022 and had no IMX token assets by February 3, 2024, so this does not support an ongoing holders classification. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

finserv receives 0.8 because the only substantive crypto channel evidenced in transcripts, press releases, and SEC filings is direct operation of a non-custodial wallet/NFT marketplace facilitating digital-asset transactions. unclass retains 0.2 because the activity was commercially minor relative to GameStop’s core retail business and was wound down by late 2023/early 2024; RevShare is therefore coded 1, while AssetShare is 0 because filings show no continuing crypto asset balance by February 3, 2024 and the IMX tokens had been liquidated earlier. (nasdaq.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

GameStop’s earnings calls during this period were unusually short and often provided limited analyst Q&A, so transcript evidence is thinner than for a typical large-cap issuer. The company did interact with digital assets through IMX token arrangements, but the evidence indicates these tokens were tied to the NFT initiative and were sold rather than held as a treasury strategy, so holders was excluded under the instructions. No direct evidence was found in the reviewed 2017–2024 materials that management or financial media explicitly characterized GameStop as a crypto-proxy stock, as opposed to a meme stock, so invbase remains 0. (nasdaq.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 17, 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: management said it had “invested in a dedicated blockchain team and new capabilities to drive the development of initiatives such as our NFT marketplace”, explicitly anchoring crypto/NFT strategy. — Nasdaq transcript
  • December 7, 2022, Q3 2022 earnings call transcript: management acknowledged stockholder enthusiasm and support, but the accessible transcript contained little detailed crypto discussion, illustrating the limited call disclosure environment. — The Motley Fool transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 28, 2023, Form 10-K for FY2022: GameStop disclosed that the U.S. segment included its “digital asset wallet and NFT marketplace,” confirming these were operating activities rather than passive investments. — FY2022 Form 10-K
  • December 6, 2023, Form 10-Q: the company said it had launched “a non-custodial digital asset wallet and a peer-to-peer non-fungible token ("NFT") marketplace” and that related revenues were recognized in net sales but were “not material.” — Q3 2023 Form 10-Q
  • May 2024, FY2023 annual report: GameStop said it “liquidated all tokens received under our agreements with IMX in fiscal 2022 and have no IMX token assets recorded” as of February 3, 2024, and that it had begun winding down the wallet and NFT marketplace in Q4 2023. — FY2023 Annual Report
  • July 11, 2022, company release: GameStop announced launch of its NFT marketplace to allow users to “buy, sell and trade NFTs” using connected digital asset wallets. — GameStop press release

Financial news / other:

  • August 1, 2023, CoinDesk: GameStop said it would remove its crypto wallets due to “regulatory uncertainty,” supporting the view that the initiative was being exited rather than expanded by late 2023. — CoinDesk
  • October 2, 2023, AP: coverage emphasized GameStop’s meme-stock shareholder dynamics rather than a crypto-focused investor base, which weakens the case for invbase = 1. — Associated Press

GLIMPSE GROUP

  • Rank: 71 | Expo: 1.12 | id_str: US37892C1062
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.7, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.3
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (FY2023, Q1 FY2024), SEC filings/earnings releases (FY2023, FY2024 10-K), company NFT initiative release

Firm Overview

The Glimpse Group, Inc. is a U.S.-listed immersive technology company focused on enterprise virtual reality, augmented reality, and spatial computing software and services through a portfolio of subsidiaries. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample, its crypto relevance came from providing AR/VR and immersive software services for NFT/crypto-related use cases rather than from holding digital assets, operating an exchange, mining, or supplying dedicated crypto infrastructure. (ir.theglimpsegroup.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

bizserv is the main supported mechanism. In its August 31, 2021 press release, Glimpse said it was launching an internal effort focused on using subsidiary software products and services for the emerging NFT and crypto segments, including a VR art gallery, AR NFT display system, and services for creation of very high-resolution NFT assets. (theglimpsegroup.com)

Transcript evidence is limited but sufficient to anchor a non-unclassified classification because management later described “NFT blockchain integration for e-commerce applications” among the company’s key technology initiatives on the February 14, 2023 Q2 FY2023 earnings call, and in September 2023 the company highlighted a paid engagement by QReal to support a fragrance launch via “a virtual store and NFT experience in Decentraland.” These activities fit bizserv rather than finserv because the company was supplying software/experience development around digital assets without evidence that it directly handled custody, trading, brokerage, fund management, or other financial intermediation. (insidermonkey.com)

Weight Assignment

bizserv receives 0.7 because the evidence shows real, company-described NFT/blockchain-related service activity and product development, but it appears clearly peripheral to Glimpse’s core immersive software business and fades from prominence as management pivots toward spatial computing, cloud, and AI by fiscal 2024. unclass retains 0.3 because reviewed FY2024 filings emphasize general immersive technology and only mention blockchain at a broad industry level, with no separate crypto revenue disclosure and no evidence of direct digital-asset handling; RevShare is therefore coded 1 and AssetShare 0. (theglimpsegroup.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Transcript coverage is thinner than ideal for a small issuer, and the most explicit transcript quote located came from a secondary transcript source rather than a directly hosted company PDF for that quarter. The company’s crypto/NFT exposure appears episodic and services-oriented, and the reviewed FY2024 10-K no longer foregrounds NFT or crypto activity, so the classification relies on 2021–2023 evidence showing actual initiative launch and at least one identified customer engagement. No direct evidence was found that management or financial media treated VRAR as a crypto-proxy stock, so invbase remains 0. (insidermonkey.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 14, 2023, Q2 FY2023 earnings call transcript: management said it was pursuing “NFT blockchain integration for e-commerce applications” as one of several key technology initiatives, directly anchoring blockchain/NFT services in call commentary. — Insider Monkey transcript
  • November 14, 2023, Q1 FY2024 earnings call transcript: management emphasized the strategic shift toward “spatial computing, cloud and AI-driven immersive software solutions,” indicating crypto/NFT work was no longer central by late 2023. — Company-hosted transcript PDF
