During 2019–2021, its crypto exposure came through an operating partnership with Ripple in which MoneyGram used Ripple’s ODL platform and XRP for foreign-exchange settlement in remittance corridors, making the crypto link part of its payments infrastructure rather than a treasury strategy. (fool.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The identified mechanism is finserv because MoneyGram directly used Ripple’s ODL platform and XRP to facilitate cross-border non-U.S.-dollar exchange settlements, which is a core payments/transaction service and therefore falls squarely within crypto financial services. Management described using XRP to execute near-instant FX trades, and SEC filings explicitly state that the company used ODL and XRP in settlement activity. (fool.com)

holders is not assigned because the XRP received from Ripple was compensation tied to the commercial agreement, not a deliberate treasury accumulation policy; the project instructions exclude operational holdings backing a financial product or service from holders. miners, bizserv, and infrastr are unsupported: MoneyGram did not mine crypto, did not merely advise/support crypto users, and did not provide crypto-directed hardware or compute infrastructure. (sec.gov)

invbase is set to 1 because financial media explicitly treated MoneyGram as a crypto-linked equity proxy during the Ripple deal: its shares more than doubled after the partnership announcement, showing direct investor sentiment spillover from crypto news into the stock. (bloomberg.com)

Weight Assignment

finserv receives 1.0 because the evidence shows direct operational use of XRP and Ripple’s ODL rails in MoneyGram’s remittance and FX-settlement business, with management repeatedly discussing transaction flow, corridor expansion, and fee economics from the arrangement. There is no evidence of a separate treasury-investment motive that would justify holders, and the other channels do not fit the company’s role. RevShare is set to 1 because Ripple-related fee benefits were meaningful but still a minority of total revenue/cost base rather than a dominant segment; AssetShare is set to 1 because XRP appeared on the balance sheet as an intangible asset, but available disclosures indicate a peripheral rather than balance-sheet-dominant position. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is that transcript availability in the browsing environment was partial, and some direct company press-release pages were less accessible than SEC-filed materials and syndicated transcript pages. However, the classification is still unambiguous because multiple accessible earnings-call transcripts and SEC filings directly describe the XRP/ODL activity, its accounting treatment, and its termination in March 2021. None identified beyond that. (fool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 1 November 2019, Q3 2019 earnings call transcript: “Currently, money Graham is using ripple to facilitate What are almost instant foreign exchange trades.” — The Motley Fool transcript
  • 1 November 2019, Q3 2019 earnings call transcript: management said MoneyGram purchased XRP on a U.S. exchange, transferred it to an exchange in Mexico, and sold it for pesos “all within about 60 seconds.” — The Motley Fool transcript
  • 1 November 2019, Q3 2019 earnings call transcript: management said “The third quarter was the first time we recorded revenue from this agreement” and that roughly 10% of daily Mexican peso volume was going through the technology. — The Motley Fool transcript
  • 30 October 2020, Q3 2020 earnings call transcript: CEO said “We’ve been doing ODL with them. We’re focused on the expansion of the RippleNet service as well.” — The Motley Fool transcript
  • 22 February 2021, Q4 2020 earnings call transcript: management said it had “suspended trading on the Ripple platform” and that MoneyGram had generated a $12.1 million EBITDA benefit from Ripple market development fees in Q1 2020. — The Motley Fool transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 24 February 2020, 2019 Form 10-K: MoneyGram said it entered a commercial agreement with Ripple to use ODL and XRP “to facilitate cross-border non-U.S. dollar exchange settlements.” — SEC 2019 Form 10-K
  • 24 February 2020, 2019 Form 10-K: the company said it was “compensated by Ripple in XRP” and accounted for the XRP received as an “indefinite-lived intangible asset.” — SEC 2019 Form 10-K
  • 24 February 2020, 2019 Form 10-K: MoneyGram disclosed 2019 market development fees from Ripple of $11.3 million and a related receivable of $0.9 million. — SEC 2019 Form 10-K
  • 30 October 2020, Q3 2020 Form 10-Q: the commercial agreement allowed MoneyGram to use ODL and XRP for FX trading, and the company received a net benefit of $8.9 million in the quarter from Ripple-related fees less trading expenses. — SEC Q3 2020 Form 10-Q
  • 25 February 2022, 2021 Form 10-K: MoneyGram said it ceased transacting under the commercial agreement in early December 2020 and formally terminated the agreement with Ripple effective 7 March 2021; it received no market development fees in 2021, versus $50.2 million in 2020 and $11.3 million in 2019. — SEC 2021 Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • 17 June 2019, Bloomberg: MoneyGram shares surged after Ripple bought a stake and announced the partnership, indicating immediate crypto-linked investor-base spillover into the stock. — Bloomberg
  • 18 June 2019, Reuters syndication: Reuters reported MoneyGram shares jumped after partnering with “bitcoin-rival Ripple,” further supporting invbase = 1. — Investing.com / Reuters syndication
  • 18 June 2019, CNBC: Ripple bought $30 million of MoneyGram shares and the companies said they would partner on cross-border payments and foreign-exchange settlement. — CNBC
  • 8 March 2021, CoinDesk: Ripple and MoneyGram agreed to wind down the partnership after it had involved Ripple paying MoneyGram to use XRP in international settlement. — CoinDesk
  • 22 June 2019, Fortune: financial media framed the partnership as a significant crypto-sector investment into MoneyGram, reinforcing that the equity was being traded as a crypto-linked name. — Fortune

NVIDIA

  • Rank: 87 | Expo: 0.89 | id_str: US67066G1040
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (2018, 2021, 2022), SEC filings (2019 Form 10-K, 2018 Form 10-Qs), SEC enforcement release, financial news

Firm Overview

NVIDIA is a U.S.-listed semiconductor and computing platform company whose core businesses span gaming GPUs, data-center accelerators, professional visualization, and automotive computing. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, its crypto exposure came through supplying GPU hardware specifically used for cryptocurrency mining and, at times, dedicated crypto-mining processors rather than through directly holding, mining, or transacting in digital assets. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The relevant mechanism is infrastr. Management explicitly discussed crypto-specific GPUs/CMP products, stated that mining demand affected GPU demand, and reported dedicated CMP revenue in earnings calls. (fool.com)

holders, miners, and finserv do not fit. The evidence shows NVIDIA sold chips and related hardware into the mining ecosystem, but there is no evidence in the sample period that it held crypto as treasury, operated mining rigs itself, or directly provided exchange, custody, brokerage, or other crypto financial services. (sec.gov)

bizserv is also less appropriate than infrastr because the exposure was not consulting or software support around crypto; it was hardware supply for mining workloads. The SEC’s 6 May 2022 release further confirms cryptomining was a significant driver of gaming-related demand in parts of fiscal 2018, reinforcing that the mechanism was infrastructure provision to the crypto ecosystem. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

A full infrastr = 1.0 assignment is appropriate because all substantive crypto references point to NVIDIA’s role as a supplier of mining-capable GPUs and dedicated CMP products. There is no support for splitting weight into other categories: crypto exposure affected revenue through hardware demand from miners, not through owned crypto assets or direct crypto intermediation. RevShare is set to 1 because disclosed crypto-specific revenue was real but peripheral relative to total company revenue—for example, fiscal 2019 cryptocurrency-specific product revenue was $306 million against total revenue of $11.716 billion, and Q1 FY2022 CMP revenue was $155 million. AssetShare is set to 0 because no evidence indicates material crypto assets on NVIDIA’s balance sheet. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is that some miner demand flowed through general-purpose GeForce gaming GPUs, which NVIDIA said was hard to quantify precisely, so the exact crypto share of gaming revenue was not always cleanly segmented. However, classification is still unambiguous because management repeatedly referenced mining demand and dedicated CMP products, the 2019 Form 10-K disclosed cryptocurrency-specific product revenue, and the SEC formally stated that cryptomining materially affected the gaming business in fiscal 2018. (fool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 16 May 2018, Q1 FY2019 earnings call transcript: “Cryptocurrency demand was, again, stronger than expected” and crypto-specific GPUs in OEM were $289 million. — The Motley Fool transcript
  • 16 May 2018, Q1 FY2019 earnings call transcript: Jensen Huang said NVIDIA’s strategy was to create a SKU so “crypto miners” could fulfill demand that way. — The Motley Fool transcript
  • 26 May 2021, Q1 FY2022 earnings call transcript: management said gaming also benefited from crypto-mining demand and that CMP revenue was $155 million in Q1, with Q2 CMP sales expected at $400 million. — The Motley Fool transcript
  • 16 February 2022, Q4 FY2022 earnings call transcript: management said GPUs were capable of cryptocurrency mining, noted limited visibility on mining-driven GPU demand, and disclosed CMP revenue of $24 million. — The Motley Fool transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 21 February 2019, 2019 Form 10-K: NVIDIA disclosed revenue from cryptocurrency-specific products of $306 million in fiscal 2019 and $273 million in fiscal 2018. — SEC 2019 Form 10-K
  • 21 February 2019, 2019 Form 10-K: the company said demand for desktop gaming GPUs used for cryptocurrency mining and its after-effects distorted gaming revenue trends. — SEC 2019 Form 10-K
  • 16 August 2018, Q2 FY2019 Form 10-Q: GPU business results were led by gaming, professional visualization, and datacenter, offset by a substantial decline in cryptocurrency GPUs. — SEC Q2 FY2019 Form 10-Q

Financial news / other:

  • 6 May 2022, SEC enforcement release: the SEC said NVIDIA failed to disclose that material growth in gaming revenue in parts of fiscal 2018 was driven in significant part by cryptomining. — SEC press release
  • 9 February 2018, CNBC: NVIDIA shares surged after earnings as analysts highlighted strong GPU demand from the cryptocurrency mining market. — CNBC
  • 16 November 2018, CNBC: financial media described NVIDIA as suffering a “crypto hangover” as mining demand evaporated, indicating direct crypto-sentiment spillover into the stock. — CNBC
  • 17 November 2021, CNBC: the outlet reported NVIDIA’s crypto-mining chip sales dropped sharply and quoted management on limited visibility into mining-driven GPU demand. — CNBC

IG GROUP HOLDINGS

  • Rank: 88 | Expo: 0.88 | id_str: GB00B06QFB75
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: annual reports (2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2024), H1 FY23 interim results, IG crypto product documentation

Firm Overview

IG Group Holdings plc is a UK-listed online trading and investment platform whose core business is spread betting, CFDs, exchange-traded derivatives and related brokerage services. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, its crypto exposure came from offering crypto-linked trading products to clients and, later in the tastytrade business, facilitating client cryptocurrency trading through Zero Hash rather than from treasury holdings or mining.

Exposure Channels Identified

finserv is the clear mechanism. IG’s own filings state that it offered a CFD on bitcoin from 2013 onward, sought to increase its range of cryptocurrencies, and in later reports disclosed an equity stake in Zero Hash, described as a “cryptocurrency trading platform,” which facilitated cryptocurrency trading for tastytrade clients. These are direct crypto financial services activities because the firm provided trading access and related intermediation to clients.

holders does not apply because the 2017 annual report explicitly says bitcoins were purchased and sold to hedge client positions and were “not held with a view to making a profit or loss for the Group.” miners, bizserv, and infrastr also do not fit: there is no evidence that IG mined crypto, supplied mining infrastructure, or merely provided non-financial support services around the ecosystem.

Weight Assignment

A full finserv = 1.0 assignment is appropriate because every substantive crypto reference in the sample period points to IG acting as a broker/platform offering crypto-linked trading exposure or enabling client crypto trading through an associated platform. There is no evidence for splitting weight into holders, since the bitcoin inventory disclosed in 2017 was operational hedging inventory backing client products and is excluded from holders under the coding rules. RevShare is set to 1 because crypto activity is clearly present but appears peripheral relative to group revenue; the only directly disclosed tastytrade/Zero Hash revenue in H1 FY23 was £0.1 million. AssetShare is set to 0 because no evidence shows material crypto assets on IG’s balance sheet during 2017–2024.

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is transcript availability: IG’s investor-relations archive confirms that transcripts exist for results presentations, but the accessible materials retrieved here were mainly annual/interim reports rather than full archived call-text PDFs for multiple years. To satisfy the transcript anchor requirement, the classification relies on an accessible management statement in the 2020 annual report describing bitcoin CFD offering history, which functions as management-authored results-period evidence, but this is weaker than a full third-party call transcript. There is also some boundary ambiguity because the 2017 bitcoin position was used for hedging client exposure; however, the annual report explicitly characterises it as operational hedging rather than proprietary investment, so holders was excluded.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 2020 annual-report management discussion: “We first offered a CFD on bitcoin in 2013” — IG Group Annual Report 2020
  • IG investor-relations archive states that results materials include transcripts and associated presentations for reporting periods, confirming the company’s use of transcripted results communications. — IG results, reports and presentations archive

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2017 annual report: IG said it would “offer clients an ever-increasing range of products… for example, increasing our range of crypto currencies.” — IG Group Annual Report 2017
  • 2017 annual report accounting note: “The Group offers Bitcoin as a product that can be traded on its platform” and “purchases and sells Bitcoins to hedge the clients’ positions.” — IG Group Annual Report 2017
  • 2017 annual report accounting note: bitcoin was “not held with a view to making a profit or loss for the Group.” — IG Group Annual Report 2017
  • 2018 annual report: “The Company first offered a CFD on bitcoin in 2013.” — IG Group Annual Report 2018
  • 2019 annual report: “The Company first offered a CFD on bitcoin in 2013.” — IG Group Annual Report 2019
  • 2024 annual report: “The Group has an investment in Zero Hash Holdings Limited (Zero Hash), a cryptocurrency trading platform.” — IG Group Annual Report 2024
  • H1 FY23 interim report: “Zero Hash facilitates cryptocurrency trading for clients of tastyworks, Inc. … tastyworks recognised £0.1 million revenue from Zero Hash during the period.” — IG Group H1 FY23 results statement

Financial news / other:

  • 29 April 2013, CNBC: IG was reported to be widening bitcoin spread-betting/binary exposure after introducing bitcoin-linked bets earlier that month. — CNBC
  • February 2024, IG product documentation listed retail CFD margin schedules for Bitcoin, Ether, Dogecoin, Solana and other crypto instruments, corroborating ongoing crypto trading-product availability within the sample end-period. — IG Markets cryptocurrencies tiered margins (CFDs) retail

BROADRIDGE FINL.SLTN.

  • Rank: 89 | Expo: 0.86 | id_str: US11133T1034
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.2, infrastr 0.8, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: Annual reports (2018, 2021, 2022, 2023), Q1 FY2022 and Q4 FY2021 earnings call transcripts

Firm Overview

Broadridge Financial Solutions, Inc. is a U.S.-listed fintech and market-infrastructure company serving broker-dealers, asset managers, banks, funds, and corporate issuers through investor communications, governance, trading, and post-trade technology. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, its crypto-adjacent exposure appears through blockchain and distributed-ledger-based market infrastructure rather than through direct crypto asset ownership, mining, or retail crypto intermediation.

Exposure Channels Identified

The clearest exposure channel is infrastr. Management repeatedly described Broadridge’s blockchain-enabled Distributed Ledger Repo (DLR) platform as a production solution for agreeing, executing, and settling repo transactions, with disclosed transaction volumes rising from about $35 billion average daily volume in fiscal 2021 to over $50 billion per day in fiscal 2022 and over $1 trillion monthly volume in fiscal 2023. These statements indicate that Broadridge supplies capital-markets infrastructure built on distributed ledger technology rather than directly handling cryptocurrencies. (fool.com)

A smaller bizserv weight is also appropriate because Broadridge at times framed blockchain as part of a broader suite of next-generation technology solutions and client enablement services, including blockchain-based proxy voting and support for clients adopting new technologies. However, the evidence is still centered on operational and settlement infrastructure, so bizserv is secondary rather than primary. holders, miners, and finserv do not apply: no retrieved source showed treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, or direct exchange/brokerage/custody/payment services in digital assets. (s203.q4cdn.com)

Weight Assignment

A split of infrastr = 0.8 and bizserv = 0.2 best fits the evidence. The dominant discussion across calls and annual reports concerns Broadridge’s blockchain-enabled DLR platform as a core market-technology utility, which squarely matches infrastructure provision. A modest bizserv component reflects management’s broader presentation of blockchain solutions and transformation support for clients, including proxy-voting applications, but these appear adjacent to the infrastructure role rather than separate crypto-financial intermediation. RevShare is set to 1 because blockchain-related activity is clearly commercialized yet appears small relative to Broadridge’s multi-billion-dollar total revenue base; the company disclosed operating metrics and volumes, but not a large standalone crypto/blockchain revenue segment. AssetShare is 0 because no evidence indicates material crypto or token holdings on the balance sheet. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is definitional: Broadridge’s exposure is to blockchain / distributed-ledger infrastructure in traditional capital markets, not clearly to cryptocurrencies themselves. Under the project rules, that still supports classification where there is explicit evidence of infrastructure directed at blockchain-based market workflows, but it is less direct than exposure through exchanges, custody, or balance-sheet crypto holdings. There was no evidence that Broadridge touched digital assets as principal, so finserv was excluded. No evidence of crypto-focused shareholder-base commentary or proxy-stock trading was found, so invbase = 0. None identified beyond that boundary issue. (s203.q4cdn.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • November 3, 2021, Q1 FY2022 earnings call transcript: “We're delivering blockchain solutions to the repo market” — The Motley Fool
  • August 12, 2021, Q4 FY2021 earnings call transcript: “We also recently launched our Digital Ledger Repo platform and are averaging $35 billion worth of transactions daily” — The Motley Fool

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2018 annual report: “Broadridge is making blockchain a reality in proxy voting” and had “an end-to-end solution built on distributed ledger technology for the U.S. proxy market” — Broadridge 2018 Annual Report
  • 2021 annual report: DLR “provides a single platform where market participants can agree, execute and settle repo transactions” and had “executed $35 billion in average daily volume” — Broadridge 2021 Annual Report
  • 2022 annual report: Broadridge said its blockchain-enabled DLR solution had “increased to over $50 billion per day” — Broadridge 2022 Annual Report
  • 2023 annual report: clients executed cross-border intraday repo on the DLR network, and the platform had “over $1 trillion in monthly volume” — Broadridge 2023 Annual Report

Financial news / other:

  • 2023, Forbes: Broadridge was reported to conduct roughly $70 billion of blockchain repo trades per day, corroborating external market characterization of the firm’s blockchain infrastructure role. — Forbes
  • Broadridge article: the company described DLR as a “blockchain-enabled Distributed Ledger Repo (DLR) platform” used for repo processing and settlement. — Broadridge

CMC MARKETS

  • Rank: 90 | Expo: 0.86 | id_str: GB00B14SKR37
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: company press releases / results statements (2018, 2024), company product pages, Reuters coverage

Firm Overview

CMC Markets plc is a London-listed online trading and investing firm whose core activities include CFDs, spread betting, stockbroking, and institutional trading technology. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, its crypto exposure came through offering cryptocurrency-linked spread bets and CFDs to professional and retail clients rather than through owning crypto on balance sheet or operating crypto infrastructure.

Exposure Channels Identified

The applicable channel is finserv. CMC explicitly launched cryptocurrency spread bets and CFDs for professional clients and then extended them to its retail client base in 2018, with management stating that clients could take positions in bitcoin and ethereum without buying the currencies outright. That is direct crypto-related financial intermediation via derivative trading products, which fits the project’s finserv definition. (CMC Markets; Reuters via Investing.com)

The evidence does not support holders, miners, bizserv, or infrastr during 2017–2024. CMC’s own crypto pages stress that clients speculate on price movements through CFDs “without owning the underlying asset,” and the FY2024 results only note the “rollout of cryptocurrencies for Invest Australia” as a product expansion within the broker platform. No retrieved 2017–2024 filing or results statement showed treasury crypto holdings, mining activity, or crypto-focused hardware / hosting infrastructure. (CMC Markets; CMC Markets)

Weight Assignment

A full finserv = 1.0 is the best fit. Across the retrieved sample-period sources, crypto appears as a tradable financial product offered on CMC’s platform, not as a treasury asset, mining business, or infrastructure line. RevShare is set to 1 because crypto products were clearly commercialized and revenue-generating, but the available results materials do not indicate that crypto constituted a large share of total group revenue relative to CMC’s much broader multi-asset trading business. AssetShare is 0 because no evidence was found of material crypto assets on the balance sheet in the 2017–2024 period. (CMC Markets; CMC Markets)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is transcript availability: accessible full earnings-call transcripts for CMC Markets within 2017–2024 were limited in open web sources, so the classification is anchored partly by management remarks in official results statements and press releases rather than a richer set of third-party transcript archives. That said, at least one management quote was retrievable from official investor materials, and the business model is consistent across corroborating sources. No explicit evidence was found that CMC was treated by financial media as a primary crypto-proxy stock during 2017–2024, so invbase = 0. (CMC Markets; Reuters via Investing.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • November 21, 2024, H1 FY2025 interim results statement / management remarks: “our diversified product offering, including the expansion of cash equities and options offerings” and ongoing technology-led product expansion; official results material confirms the investor-presentation anchor for classification, though without a detailed crypto revenue breakdown — CMC Markets H1 FY2025 Results

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • January 11, 2018, company press release: “CMC Markets will offer leveraged trading on bitcoin and ethereum” and clients could trade crypto price movements “without having to buy the currency outright” — CMC Markets Press Release
  • June 20, 2024, FY2024 final results: operational highlights included the “rollout of cryptocurrencies for Invest Australia” — CMC Markets FY2024 Results
  • Current product documentation describing the same mechanism: crypto CFDs let traders speculate “without owning the underlying asset” — CMC Markets Crypto CFDs

Financial news / other:

  • January 11, 2018, Reuters: CMC “had launched contracts for differences (CFDs) and spreadbetting offerings for cryptocurrencies following significant interest from its users” — Reuters via Investing.com
  • January 2018, external trade press: CMC expanded its cryptocurrency spread-bet and CFD offering to retail clients, corroborating commercialization of crypto-linked trading products — Finance Magnates

NCSOFT

  • Rank: 91 | Expo: 0.85 | id_str: KR7036570000
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: Q2 2022 earnings call transcript, 2023 Q3 earnings call coverage, 2024 earnings release materials, annual-report archive

Firm Overview

NCSOFT is a South Korea-listed video game developer and publisher best known for MMORPG franchises such as Lineage, Blade & Soul, Aion, and Guild Wars. Across the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, the retrieved materials show a core business centered on game development and publishing, with only exploratory or cautionary discussion of blockchain integration rather than a documented crypto business line.

Exposure Channels Identified

No project category is sufficiently supported, so the appropriate classification is unclass. The strongest crypto-adjacent evidence retrieved is management commentary acknowledging that NCSOFT had not abandoned blockchain as a topic, while also stating in the November 9, 2023 Q3 earnings conference call that integrating blockchain games was “premature” and that blockchain games should not be equated with P2E. That indicates consideration of the topic, but not substantive, operational crypto exposure. (Asiae)

The evidence does not support holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr for 2017–2024. Retrieved earnings materials and annual-report archives show NCSOFT reporting game revenues and conventional publishing operations, while the accessible 4Q 2024 earnings release contains no blockchain or NFT disclosure, and no retrieved filing or source showed treasury crypto holdings, mining activity, crypto custody/trading, or crypto-directed infrastructure sales/services. (NCSOFT IR archive; NCSOFT 4Q 2024 earnings release; NCSOFT annual report archive via DART)

Weight Assignment

unclass = 1.0 is the best fit. While NCSOFT management discussed blockchain in at least one earnings-call setting, the retrieved evidence points to evaluation and caution rather than a launched crypto-financial product, blockchain-service revenue stream, mining operation, or balance-sheet crypto position. Because the threshold for a non-zero mechanism weight requires substantive exposure rather than passing mention, all mechanism categories remain at 0.0 and both materiality fields are NA. (Asiae; NCSOFT 4Q 2024 earnings release)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is source accessibility. Open-web access to full NCSOFT transcript text within 2017–2024 was limited; one accessible transcript page for Q2 2022 was only partially visible before paywall truncation, and the DART annual report viewer encountered a fetch error in this environment even though the filing archive entry was identifiable. A transcript quote was retrievable, but it supports non-classification because it describes blockchain integration as premature rather than evidencing an active crypto business. No explicit evidence was found that NCSOFT was treated in financial media as a primary crypto-proxy stock during 2017–2024, so invbase = 0. (GuruFocus transcript page; Asiae; NCSOFT annual report archive via DART)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • August 12, 2022, Q2 2022 earnings call transcript: “Thank you very much for joining the company's second quarter 2022 earnings presentation.” The accessible transcript confirms the earnings-call anchor, but the visible portion contains operating discussion rather than crypto activity — GuruFocus Q2 2022 Transcript
  • November 9, 2023, Q3 2023 earnings conference call coverage: CFO Hong Won-jun said “We have never officially said that we are discontinuing blockchain,” but also that integrating blockchain games is “premature” and blockchain games are “absolutely not P2E” — Asiae

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • IR archive covering 2017–2024: NCSOFT maintains earnings-release, presentation, and audit-report archives for the sample period, supporting review of official investor materials but not showing a crypto-specific product line in the retrieved index pages — NCSOFT IR Archive
  • February 12, 2025 release for FY2024 / 4Q 2024 results: the accessible English earnings deck reports sales, operating profit, and game-level breakdowns, with no blockchain or NFT disclosure in the retrieved text — NCSOFT 4Q 2024 Earnings Release
  • March 21, 2023 annual report archive entry: official DART archive confirms a 2022 annual report exists for NCSOFT, but the viewer could not be fully scraped in this environment — NCSOFT Annual Report Archive

Financial news / other:

  • November 9, 2023, Smartkarma summary of the same earnings-call theme: external market commentary characterized blockchain/NFT game launch talk as a conference-call highlight, but this was not sufficient on its own to evidence realized crypto exposure — Smartkarma
  • July 25, 2023, company sustainability-report press release: NCSOFT’s ESG disclosure emphasized governance, technology, and sustainable management, with no retrieved indication of a crypto revenue segment or digital-asset treasury position — NCSOFT ESG PLAYBOOK 2022

NETMARBLE

  • Rank: 92 | Expo: 0.83 | id_str: KR7251270005
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.7, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.3, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings-call presentations (2021, 2023–2024), Netmarble/MARBLEX official documentation, quarterly report disclosures, third-party securities coverage

Firm Overview

Netmarble Corporation is a South Korea-listed mobile and online game publisher. Across the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, the retrieved materials show its core business remained game development and publishing, but from 2021 onward it also built a blockchain-gaming ecosystem through MARBLEX, including token, wallet, NFT marketplace, and blockchain-enabled game services. (sgimage.netmarble.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports primarily finserv exposure because Netmarble’s MARBLEX ecosystem directly handles crypto-like digital assets through token management, wallet services, swaps/station functions, and NFT marketplace activity rather than merely providing ancillary services. Official MARBLEX documentation states that the ecosystem was established by Netmarble to build a blockchain ecosystem for games, and its wallet documentation says the MARBLEX Wallet lets users authenticate and manage tokens, while the marketplace documentation confirms NFT trading functionality. (docs.marblex.io)

A secondary infrastr weight is also warranted. Netmarble was not just issuing one-off NFT features; it was building and operating a broader blockchain game platform and token infrastructure for its own and partner games. A March 18, 2022 Mirae Asset securities report described Netmarble as operating the MBX/ITAM Cube blockchain gaming platforms and noted MBX/ITAM on Klaytn/BSC, while Netmarble’s own 4Q 2021 earnings materials said six blockchain games were planned for 2022. (securities.miraeasset.com)

The evidence does not support holders, miners, or bizserv. No retrieved filing, earnings material, or official product documentation showed treasury crypto accumulation, proof-of-work mining, or purely non-financial service provision to third-party crypto firms. The accessible quarterly-report extract showed conventional balance-sheet line items without identified crypto assets, and the main crypto activity appeared operationally tied to blockchain gaming products and platforms. (financialreports.eu)

Weight Assignment

finserv = 0.7 and infrastr = 0.3 best fits the evidence. The larger weight goes to finserv because the clearest retrieved evidence is that Netmarble, via MARBLEX, offered token- and NFT-facing services such as wallet management and marketplace functions, which directly involve handling digital assets. infrastr receives a meaningful but smaller weight because Netmarble also built the underlying blockchain gaming platform and ecosystem rails for games, but the sources retrieved did not show infrastructure revenue to be more economically important than the asset-facing service layer. Given the absence of evidence for mining, treasury holdings, or a support-services-only model, all other mechanism weights remain 0.0. (docs.marblex.io)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is transcript accessibility. Open-web access to full verbatim earnings-call transcripts for Netmarble was patchy; one retrieved “Q2 2023 earnings call” PDF was misattributed to another company, and the accessible MarketScreener transcript page for February 7, 2024 was paywalled after the opening speaker line. That still provides an earnings-call anchor, but the strongest substantive evidence came from investor presentations and official MARBLEX documentation rather than a long transcript passage. No explicit evidence was retrieved that financial media treated Netmarble stock itself as a primary crypto-proxy equity, so invbase = 0. Because crypto appears to have been a side initiative within a much larger gaming business rather than a dominant reported segment, RevShare is coded 1 and AssetShare 0. (in.marketscreener.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 7, 2024, Q4 2023 earnings call transcript page: “Good afternoon. This is [indiscernible], Head of Finance Planning at Netmarble. I thank the” — accessible opening line confirms the earnings-call event, though the transcript body is paywalled and does not provide visible crypto detail here — MarketScreener Q4 2023 Transcript (in.marketscreener.com)
  • February 9, 2022, 4Q 2021 & FY 2021 earnings results: “In 2022, 6 blockchain games, including A3: Still Alive (Mar.) will be launched” — Netmarble 4Q 2021 & FY 2021 Earnings Results (sgimage.netmarble.com)
  • February 7, 2024, 4Q 2023 & FY 2023 earnings results: retrieved official earnings deck reports revenue, EBITDA, and game mix, but no visible blockchain/NFT disclosure in the fetched text, suggesting crypto was not a separately highlighted earnings line by then — Netmarble 4Q 2023 & FY 2023 Earnings Results (sgimage.netmarble.com)

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2024 quarterly report extract: the filing text includes a glossary defining blockchain, NFT, and P2E game concepts, but the financial statement extract shows no clearly identified crypto-asset balance-sheet line — Netmarble 2024 Quarterly Report (financialreports.eu)
  • Company history page: Netmarble’s corporate history notes its blockchain-specialized subsidiary MARBLEX and the opening of the MBX 2.0 ecosystem — Netmarble Company History (company.netmarble.com)
  • 2022 ESG report search result: Netmarble’s 2022 ESG report identifies MARBLEX Corp. among major subsidiaries subject to consolidation — Netmarble 2022 ESG Report (sgimage.netmarble.com)

Financial news / other:

  • March 18, 2022, Mirae Asset securities coverage: the report says Netmarble is “focusing on adding blockchain elements to its existing games” and describes MBX/ITAM platforms plus a Binance partnership for global user reach — Mirae Asset Securities Initiation Report (securities.miraeasset.com)
  • MARBLEX overview page: official documentation says MARBLEX was established by Netmarble to build “a blockchain game ecosystem” specialized for games — MARBLEX Overview (docs.marblex.io)
  • MARBLEX Wallet documentation: official documentation says the wallet is “the official wallet app for MBX” and allows users to “authenticate and manage tokens” — MARBLEX Wallet (docs.marblex.io)
  • MBX Marketplace documentation: official documentation states the marketplace was closed on November 30, 2024, and that users could still trade ecosystem NFTs through linked venues — MBX Marketplace (docs.marblex.io)

ENSURGE MICROPOWER

  • Rank: 93 | Expo: 0.80 | id_str: NO0013186460
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: company annual reports (2019, 2022, 2023), quarterly reports/presentations (Q3 2022, Q4 2022), investor webcast/presentation archive

Firm Overview

Ensurge Micropower ASA is a Norway-listed battery technology company with operating headquarters in San Jose, California, focused on solid-state lithium microbatteries for wearables, hearables, medical devices, and IoT sensors. Across the retrieved reports and investor materials for 2017–2024, the company is described as a microbattery developer and manufacturer rather than a crypto-related business. (assets.milestoneinternet.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto exposure was identified in the retrieved source set. The company’s filings and presentations consistently discuss solid-state lithium battery technology, customer sampling, manufacturing scale-up, licensing, strategic partners, and capital raising, but not cryptocurrency holdings, mining, exchanges, custody, blockchain services, or crypto-directed infrastructure. (assets.milestoneinternet.com)

Searches of the 2022 and 2023 annual reports returned no matches for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” or “blockchain,” and additional targeted web searches did not surface credible financial-news evidence tying the firm to crypto markets or describing the stock as a crypto proxy. On that basis, the firm is classified as unclass = 1.0. (assets.milestoneinternet.com)

Weight Assignment

All mechanism weights are set to 0.0 and unclass is set to 1.0 because the retrieved evidence supports only a conventional industrial technology narrative centered on microbattery R&D and commercialization. The available earnings-call/presentation materials likewise contain no substantive crypto discussion, so there is no basis to allocate weight to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. (assets.milestoneinternet.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A transcript-style earnings-call anchor was available only indirectly through investor presentation and transcript index pages rather than a fully open verbatim call transcript for multiple periods. However, this limitation does not affect the classification outcome materially because the accessible company filings and presentation materials are extensive and consistently non-crypto. No explicit evidence was retrieved that management discussed crypto-focused shareholders or that financial media treated Ensurge Micropower as a crypto proxy, so invbase = 0. (marketscreener.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • November 16, 2022, Q3 2022 results presentation: “Transforming the microbattery industry by delivering 1st mAh capacity solid-state microbattery” — Ensurge Q3 2022 Preliminary Report (assets.milestoneinternet.com)
  • February 27, 2023, Q4 2022 results presentation: “First production order for 150,000 Ensurge batteries in December 2022” — Ensurge Q4 2022 Presentation (assets.milestoneinternet.com)
  • MarketScreener transcript archive page lists calls for 2021–2022 but the accessible retrieved materials point to battery commercialization themes rather than crypto activity — MarketScreener Transcript Archive (marketscreener.com)

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2022 annual report: “Ensurge’s revenue and other income amounted to USD 0 in 2022 and 2021” and the report describes SSLB development/commercialization rather than any crypto line of business — 2022 Annual Report (assets.milestoneinternet.com)
  • 2022 annual report text search: no matches for bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain — 2022 Annual Report Search Results (assets.milestoneinternet.com)
  • 2023 annual report text search: no matches for bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain — 2023 Annual Report (assets.milestoneinternet.com)
  • 2019 annual report: retrieved historical filing for predecessor-period operations; no blockchain match found in search — 2019 Annual Report (assets.milestoneinternet.com)

Financial news / other:

  • Company investor archive shows a sequence of presentations and webcasts from 2022–2024 focused on corporate updates, not crypto initiatives — Ensurge Webcasts & Presentations (ensurge.com)
  • Company website describes patented solid-state microbattery technology for sensors and other devices, with no crypto-related positioning — Ensurge Company Overview (ensurge.com)
  • Company financial reports archive provides annual and interim reports from 2019–2024 used for cross-checking; none retrieved here indicated crypto exposure — Interim & Annual Reports Archive (ensurge.com)

NTT DATA GROUP DEAD - DELIST.26/09/25

  • Rank: 94 | Expo: 0.76 | id_str: JP3165700000
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: company briefing materials (2019, 2023, 2024), company blockchain-services announcement (2023), integrated-report press release (2024)

Firm Overview

NTT DATA Group Corporation was a large Japan-listed global IT services and consulting company within the NTT group, with businesses spanning consulting, applications, infrastructure, data centers, and industry solutions across more than 50 countries. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, the retrieved evidence indicates that crypto-related exposure fit mainly through enterprise blockchain advisory and solution delivery rather than through direct cryptocurrency ownership, mining, exchange activity, or crypto-asset custody. (nttdata.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports classification in bizserv rather than finserv or holders. NTT DATA’s investor materials repeatedly frame blockchain as one of its digital capability areas and centers of excellence, indicating it provided blockchain design, development, and commercialization support to clients as part of its broader IT-services portfolio. (nttdata.com)

The retrieved sources do not show that NTT DATA held bitcoin or other crypto assets on its balance sheet, mined crypto, operated an exchange or brokerage, or took custody of client crypto assets. A 2024 third-party article notes participation as a validator on a permissioned tokenization network, but the stronger primary-source record still characterizes the firm’s role as enterprise technology and consulting support for blockchain use cases rather than direct crypto financial intermediation. (nttdata.com)

Weight Assignment

A full weight of bizserv = 1.0 is assigned because the crypto-adjacent activity evidenced in the sample is entirely service-based: blockchain consulting, development capabilities, and enterprise solutions for clients. No credible evidence was retrieved for treasury holdings, mining economics, custodial/trading revenues, or crypto-directed infrastructure sales, so holders, miners, finserv, and infrastr are all set to 0.0. The activity appears peripheral to the group’s overall multitrillion-yen IT-services business, so RevShare is coded 1 and AssetShare is coded 0. (nttdata.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

An open verbatim earnings-call transcript from Seeking Alpha was not accessible in the tool environment, so the earnings-call anchor is taken from company briefing-session PDFs hosted on NTT DATA’s investor site rather than a sell-side transcript page. The 2024 SWIAT validator article suggests some deeper participation in tokenization infrastructure, but because the strongest accessible primary evidence presents blockchain as a client-service capability within a broad consulting/IT offering, the classification remains bizserv rather than infrastr or finserv. No explicit evidence was retrieved that financial media treated the stock as a crypto proxy or that management discussed crypto-focused shareholders, so invbase = 0. (nttdata.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • December 10, 2019, company briefing session: “Expand CoE Blockchain CoE Established team of 300+ professionals from 24 countries and regions” — NTT DATA FY2019 Business Briefing (nttdata.com)
  • December 10, 2019, company briefing session: “World’s 1st commercialized blockchain technology” — NTT DATA FY2019 Business Briefing (nttdata.com)
  • December 14, 2023, company briefing session: “Blockchain technology improves the digitalization and efficiency of trade operations” — NTT DATA Company Briefing Session 2023 (nttdata.com)

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • November 29, 2024, integrated-report announcement: NTT DATA described itself as a “$30+ billion trusted global innovator of business and technology services” focused on consulting, applications, infrastructure and connectivity, with no indication of balance-sheet crypto holdings or crypto-financial products — Integrated Report 2024 Announcement (nttdata.com)
  • December 16, 2024, company briefing session: document text search returned no matches for “blockchain,” “crypto,” or “bitcoin,” consistent with crypto not being a major reporting line in the latest investor presentation — NTT DATA Group Company Briefing Session 2024 (nttdata.com)

Financial news / other:

  • 2023, company news release: NTT DATA said it was named a leader in Everest Group’s blockchain services assessment and listed proprietary blockchain offerings such as ioTrace, Blockchain Reconciliation Platform, BlockTrace, StampChain, and KayTrust — NTT DATA Named a Leader in Everest Group’s Blockchain PEAK Matrix 2023 Report (gr.nttdata.com)
  • February 27, 2024, Ledger Insights: NTT DATA joined SWIAT’s permissioned blockchain network as a validator and planned to run tenant nodes for clients, indicating enterprise blockchain participation but not direct crypto-asset treasury exposure — Ledger Insights: NTT Data joins SWIAT tokenization platform (ledgerinsights.com)
  • Investor relations archive: NTT DATA’s briefing materials from FY2019–FY2024 were used to cross-check management discussion of blockchain capabilities over time — NTT DATA Business Briefings Archive (nttdata.com)

LESAKA TECHNOLOGIES

  • Rank: 95 | Expo: 0.76 | id_str: US64107N2062
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: earnings-call transcripts (FY2022–FY2024), 10-K filings (2021–2024), company investor relations materials

Firm Overview

Lesaka Technologies, Inc. is a South Africa-focused fintech group listed on Nasdaq that provides banking, lending, insurance, payment processing, supplier payments, software, and cash-to-digital financial services to consumers and merchants in Southern Africa. Across the sampled 2021–2024 earnings-call transcripts and 2021–2024 annual filings retrieved from its investor relations site, the company is consistently described as a conventional payments and fintech platform rather than a crypto-focused business. (ir.lesakatech.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive crypto exposure was identified within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period. Searches of multiple Lesaka earnings-call transcripts and annual reports returned no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain,” and the accessible transcript content focused on merchant acquiring, digital wallets, lending, insurance, supplier payments, and card acceptance rather than digital assets. (ir.lesakatech.com)

The 2024 Form 10-K describes Lesaka as using “banking and payment technologies” to deliver financial services solutions and software in Southern Africa, with product lines including transactional accounts, lending, insurance, cash management, card acceptance, supplier payments, software services and bill payments. That profile supports traditional fintech/payments exposure, but not any of the six crypto-mechanism categories defined in the coding scheme. (ir.lesakatech.com)

Weight Assignment

A full weight of unclass = 1.0 is assigned because no credible evidence was retrieved of treasury crypto holdings, mining activity, crypto brokerage/custody/payment settlement involving digital assets, crypto-focused business services, or crypto-directed infrastructure sales. The evidence instead shows a fiat payments and financial-services platform, so all mechanism categories other than unclass are set to 0.0, with invbase = 0 because no retrieved source indicated that investors treated Lesaka as a crypto proxy. (ir.lesakatech.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

At least one earnings-call transcript was retrieved, satisfying the search requirement, but no crypto-related passage could be identified in the accessible transcripts; accordingly, there is no positive crypto quote to anchor a non-zero mechanism classification. A 2026 news result about Lesaka and a stablecoin was excluded from the classification because it falls outside the required sample period ending December 2024. (ir.lesakatech.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • September 12, 2024, company transcript: “FY2024 affirms our position as the leading independent fintech in Southern Africa” — Lesaka FY2024 Q4 Earnings Transcript (ir.lesakatech.com)
  • February 7, 2024, company transcript: transcript text search returned no matches for “crypto” — Lesaka FY2024 Q2 Earnings Transcript (ir.lesakatech.com)
  • September 13, 2023, company transcript: transcript text search returned no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain” — Lesaka FY2023 Q4 Earnings Transcript (ir.lesakatech.com)
  • September 8, 2022, company transcript: transcript text search returned no matches for “crypto” — Lesaka FY2022 Q4 Earnings Transcript (ir.lesakatech.com)

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • September 11, 2024, Form 10-K: Lesaka said it “utilizes its proprietary banking and payment technologies to deliver financial services solutions and software to consumers and merchants in Southern Africa” — Lesaka 2024 Form 10-K (ir.lesakatech.com)
  • September 11, 2024, Form 10-K: text searches returned no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain” — Lesaka 2024 Form 10-K (ir.lesakatech.com)
  • September 2023, Form 10-K: text searches returned no matches for “crypto” or “bitcoin” — Lesaka 2023 Form 10-K (ir.lesakatech.com)
  • September 2022 and 2021, Form 10-K filings: text searches returned no matches for “crypto” — Lesaka 2022 Form 10-K, Net1 2021 Form 10-K (ir.lesakatech.com)

Financial news / other:

  • Company IR archive: Lesaka’s quarterly results page was used to retrieve transcripts and annual reports spanning FY2021–FY2025 — Lesaka Quarterly Results Archive (ir.lesakatech.com)
  • November 14, 2023, company-hosted financial news item: Lesaka was described as “a cash to digital FinTech in the emerging market of South Africa,” emphasizing payments infrastructure rather than crypto activities — NASDAQ: LSAK - A Cash to Digital FinTech in the Emerging Market of South Africa (lesakatech.com)
  • February 24, 2026, Business Day: a stablecoin-related article exists but was excluded because it is outside the January 2017–December 2024 sample window — Lesaka looks to cross-border payments via Zaru stablecoin (businessday.co.za)

RAS TECHNOLOGY HOLDINGS

  • Rank: 96 | Expo: 0.76 | id_str: AU0000186496
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: ASX filings (FY2022, FY2024), half-year report / investor materials (1H FY22), FY24 results announcement

Firm Overview

RAS Technology Holdings Ltd is an Australian-listed software and data company operating through its Racing and Sports business, supplying premium racing data, enhanced content and SaaS solutions to global racing and wagering operators. Across the retrieved 2017–2024 materials, the company was described as a wagering-technology and content provider rather than a crypto business. (announcements.asx.com.au)

Exposure Channels Identified

No qualifying crypto exposure channel was identified. The strongest crypto-adjacent reference retrieved was a FY24 results statement that Stake.com, described there as a large “crypto wagering and casino” operator, was a customer of RAS; however, that indicates customer exposure to a gambling operator that accepts crypto, not that RAS itself holds crypto, mines it, provides crypto financial services, sells crypto-focused infrastructure, or provides non-financial services directly to the crypto ecosystem under the coding rules. (announcements.asx.com.au)

The broader filings and half-year materials instead consistently describe RAS as a provider of racing data, content, and SaaS to wagering markets. Text searches of the retrieved FY2022 annual report and 1H FY22 half-year / investor materials returned no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain,” which supports a non-crypto classification. (asx.com.au)

Weight Assignment

A full weight of unclass = 1.0 is assigned because the retrieved evidence shows a racing-data and wagering-technology vendor, with no credible evidence during January 2017–December 2024 of treasury crypto holdings, mining activity, crypto exchange/custody/payment functions, crypto-focused business services, or crypto-directed infrastructure provision. invbase = 0 because no retrieved source indicated that management or financial media treated RAS shares as a crypto proxy. (announcements.asx.com.au)

Ambiguities / Limitations

At least one earnings-call transcript was not directly retrievable from a primary transcript source within the sample window despite targeted searching; accessible evidence therefore relied more heavily on ASX filings and investor presentation-style materials, and this would cap certainty at 2 if a positive classification were being made. The FY24 announcement’s reference to Stake.com as a crypto-enabled wagering client was treated as insufficient for any non-zero crypto mechanism weight because the company’s role remained wagering software/data supply rather than direct participation in crypto activities. (marketscreener.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 21, 2022, half-year investor materials: “RAS Technology Holdings Limited is the holding company of Racing and Sports, a leading provider of fully integrated premium data, enhanced content, and SaaS solutions to the global racing and wagering industries.” — RAS Technology 1H FY22 Half-Year Report (announcements.asx.com.au)
  • February 21, 2022, investor presentation / half-year materials: text searches returned no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain” — RAS Technology 1H FY22 Investor Presentation / Materials (announcements.asx.com.au)
  • MarketScreener events archive indicates webinar/call dates in 2023–2024, but an accessible primary transcript was not directly retrieved in the search process — RAS Technology Holdings Events Calendar (marketscreener.com)

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • August 29, 2022, FY2022 annual report: RAS described itself through Racing and Sports operations and text searches returned no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain” — RAS Technology FY2022 Annual Report (asx.com.au)
  • February 21, 2022, half-year report: principal activities were providing “premium data, enhanced content, and SaaS solutions to the global racing and wagering industries” — RAS Technology 1H FY22 Half-Year Report (announcements.asx.com.au)
  • October 22, 2024, AGM notice: shareholders were directed to the FY2024 annual report archive; text search of the accessible AGM notice itself returned no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain” — RAS Technology 2024 AGM Notice (announcements.asx.com.au)

Financial news / other:

  • August 22, 2024, ASX results announcement: management said, “we have signed landmark deals with Stake.com, who are one of the largest crypto wagering and casino operators in the world” — RTH Delivers Before Tax Profit in FY24 (announcements.asx.com.au)
  • August 22, 2024, ASX results announcement: the same filing stated Stake.com “supports fiat and cryptocurrencies for betting,” but the disclosure concerned a customer partnership rather than RAS’s own crypto operations — RTH Delivers Before Tax Profit in FY24 (announcements.asx.com.au)
  • MarketScreener company profile/calendar summary described the customer base as racing bodies, wagering operators, media and digital organizations, and retail/private clients, consistent with a non-crypto core business — RAS Technology Holdings Calendar / Profile (uk.marketscreener.com)

TAIWAN SEMICON.MNFG.

  • Rank: 97 | Expo: 0.74 | id_str: TW0002330008
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: TSMC annual reports (2017–2018), TSMC Q3 2024 earnings call transcript, Reuters/CNBC coverage of 2018 crypto-mining demand

Firm Overview

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited is a Taiwan-based pure-play semiconductor foundry and one of the world’s largest contract chip manufacturers, serving a broad global customer base across mobile, high-performance computing, networking and other end markets. Within the sample period, the retrieved evidence ties its crypto relevance to supplying manufacturing capacity for chips used in cryptocurrency-mining hardware rather than to holding crypto, mining coins, or providing crypto financial services. (investor.tsmc.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports infrastr exposure because TSMC manufactures semiconductors used by customers building crypto-mining hardware, making it an upstream infrastructure supplier to the crypto ecosystem rather than a direct participant. In the 2017 annual report, management explicitly listed “cryptocurrency” among the product areas driving tape-out activity for its 16-nanometer technology. (investor.tsmc.com)

This interpretation is reinforced by 2018 financial-news coverage of TSMC management commentary linking revenue guidance and demand conditions to cryptocurrency mining demand. CNBC, citing TSMC’s earnings discussion, reported both strong first-quarter mining demand and later uncertainty or softening in crypto-mining demand affecting outlook, which indicates real but cyclical exposure through chip demand. (cnbc.com)

No evidence was found that TSMC held crypto on its balance sheet, mined crypto itself, operated exchanges/custody/payments, or provided non-financial advisory or software services directly to crypto firms. Searches of the retrieved 2018 annual report and Q3 2024 earnings transcript returned no matches for “cryptocurrency,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain” in those later materials, suggesting crypto exposure was indirect and not a persistent separately disclosed business line. (investor.tsmc.com)

Weight Assignment

A full weight of infrastr = 1.0 is assigned because the only clearly evidenced crypto channel is TSMC’s role as an upstream fabrication provider for chips used in cryptocurrency-mining equipment. The company does not itself transact in digital assets or provide crypto-native services under the coding rules, so no weight is assigned to holders, miners, finserv, or bizserv. invbase = 0 because the retrieved sources describe operating exposure via end-demand from mining chips, not a stock-market identity as a crypto proxy. (investor.tsmc.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The classification relies on a mix of one directly retrieved earnings-call transcript and supporting annual-report / financial-news evidence, but the accessible transcript anchor from 2024 does not itself contain crypto discussion. The clearest crypto-specific management evidence in the sample came from the 2017 annual report and Reuters/CNBC reporting on 2018 earnings commentary, so the exposure appears episodic and indirect rather than a stable disclosed segment. Revenue materiality is therefore coded conservatively as minor. (investor.tsmc.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • October 17, 2024, TSMC earnings call transcript: “Good afternoon, everyone and welcome to TSMC’s third-quarter 2024 earnings conference call.” Accessible transcript retrieved, but text searches returned no matches for “cryptocurrency,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain” — TSMC Q3 2024 Earnings Call Transcript (investor.tsmc.com)

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2017 annual report, Letter to Shareholders / technology discussion: “Strong tape-out activities covered a variety of mainstream smartphones, cryptocurrency, AI, GPU and RF products.” — TSMC 2017 Annual Report (investor.tsmc.com)
  • 2018 annual report, company description: TSMC described itself as the world’s largest semiconductor foundry with 481 customers and broad end-market exposure; text searches of the retrieved annual-report site returned no matches for “cryptocurrency,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain” — TSMC 2018 Annual Report (investor.tsmc.com)
  • January 16, 2025 press release for 4Q24 results: TSMC said it “supports a thriving ecosystem of global customers and partners,” consistent with an upstream manufacturing role rather than direct crypto operations — TSMC Reports Fourth Quarter EPS of NT$14.45 (pr.tsmc.com)

Financial news / other:

  • April 19, 2018, CNBC citing TSMC earnings commentary: “We forecast TSMC's 2018 revenue in U.S. dollar will be about 10 percent ... due to the smartphone weakness and the uncertainty in cryptocurrency mining demand.” — CNBC (cnbc.com)
  • July 19, 2018, CNBC/Reuters coverage: management said business would benefit from new launches while “cryptocurrency mining demand will decrease,” tying crypto exposure to foundry demand rather than direct coin activity — CNBC (cnbc.com)
  • January 17, 2018, Bloomberg summary: crypto-mining could account for as much as a tenth of TSMC revenue in 2018, indicating potentially meaningful but still customer-driven infrastructure exposure — Bloomberg summary (bloomberg.com)

VINCO VENTURES

  • Rank: 98 | Expo: 0.74 | id_str: US9273302094
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.6, infrastr 0.4, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 2
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: 2021–2022 Form 10 / 10-K filings, 2021 crypto JV press release, limited earnings-call availability

Firm Overview

Vinco Ventures, Inc. was a U.S.-listed digital media and content technologies company whose main disclosed businesses during the sample period centered on Lomotif, digital advertising/media assets, and a set of blockchain-related operations housed in subsidiary Cryptyde. Its crypto exposure fit mainly through NFT/Web3 consumer products and a smaller but explicit Bitcoin-mining-equipment venture that was being separated from Vinco through the planned Cryptyde spin-off. (materials.proxyvote.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The strongest evidenced channel is bizserv because Vinco, through Cryptyde and EVNT/Emmersive, described a streaming music NFT platform and broader Web3 products using blockchain technologies in consumer-facing industries such as music, digital art, ticketing, event services, and gaming. Those activities are crypto-adjacent digital products rather than exchange, custody, payments, or balance-sheet token investing. (sec.gov)

A secondary infrastr channel is also present because Vinco disclosed a controlled interest in CW Machines, LLC and separately announced a Cryptyde/Wattum joint venture focused on BTC-mining equipment and turnkey mining solutions, including an initial order of 2,000 Bitmain Antminer S19 units. However, the accessible evidence frames this more as enabling consumer access to mining equipment than as Vinco itself operating a large mining fleet or recognizing substantial mining revenue. (materials.proxyvote.com)

No evidence was found that Vinco itself pursued a treasury crypto holding strategy, directly operated a crypto exchange/broker/custodian, or mined Bitcoin on its own balance sheet in a way that would justify holders, finserv, or miners. The materials instead repeatedly describe the relevant crypto operations as subsidiaries or spin-off businesses under Cryptyde. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

A mixed assignment of bizserv = 0.6 and infrastr = 0.4 best fits the evidence. The larger weight goes to bizserv because Vinco’s 2021–2022 filings give more sustained discussion to Web3, blockchain consumer products, and the E‑NFT platform than to mining hardware, while infrastr receives a meaningful but smaller weight because CW Machines and BTC-mining-equipment activity were explicit yet appear narrower and housed within the planned spin-off structure. invbase = 0 because the retrieved sources did not explicitly characterize BBIG’s shareholder base as crypto-focused or its stock as a crypto proxy, even though it had meme-stock attention. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Accessible earnings-call pages were found for April 12, 2021, November 23, 2021, and May 23, 2022, but the retrieved call pages stated “Transcript not yet available,” so a crypto-specific earnings-call quote could not be recovered despite search efforts. Under the coding rules, that caps certainty at 2; the classification therefore relies primarily on SEC filings and company press releases rather than a transcript quote directly discussing crypto exposure. The main borderline issue is whether the NFT/Web3 activity should be treated as finserv, but the retrieved descriptions point to consumer-facing blockchain products and content platforms rather than direct asset trading/custody, so bizserv is more appropriate. (news.alphastreet.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • November 23, 2021, AlphaStreet event page: “Transcript not yet available.” Earnings-call page confirms a BBIG Q3 2021 conference call existed, but no usable transcript quote was retrievable — AlphaStreet BBIG Q3 2021 Earnings Conference Call (news.alphastreet.com)
  • April 12, 2021, AlphaStreet event page: “Transcript not yet available.” Accessible Q4 2020 call page located, but no transcript text could be scraped for crypto discussion — AlphaStreet BBIG Q4 2020 Earnings Call (news.alphastreet.com)

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 31, 2022, Form 10-K: “Cryptyde – Our wholly-owned subsidiary… plans to offer three initial business lines, Web3 (decentralized internet) products, Bitcoin mining services, and consumer packaging” — Vinco Ventures 2021 Form 10-K (sec.gov)
  • March 31, 2022, Form 10-K: EVNT/Emmersive was described as a “Streaming Music Non-Fungible Token (‘NFT’) Platform” and the filing discussed risks tied to NFT and cryptocurrency transactions — Vinco Ventures 2021 Form 10-K (sec.gov)
  • March 2023 proxy-hosted 10-K PDF for 2021 operations: Vinco said it was focused on digital media and content technologies and listed Cryptyde, EVNT, BlockHiro, and a 51% voting interest in CW Machines among subsidiaries/consolidated entities — Vinco Ventures 2022 Form 10-K PDF (materials.proxyvote.com)
  • November 8, 2021, company press release: Cryptyde’s operations were described as including “E-NFT.com, a Streaming Music NFT Platform, and CW Machines LLC, a crypto mining ecosystem” — Vinco Ventures Announces Filing of Form 10 (d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net)

Financial news / other:

  • November 1, 2021, GlobeNewswire/company release: Cryptyde announced CW Machines with Wattum, “focused on simplifying consumer ownership of BTC Mining equipment through the integration of smart contract technology,” with an initial order of 2,000 Bitmain Antminer S19s — GlobeNewswire (globenewswire.com)
  • August 30, 2021, Bloomberg: Vinco Ventures was described as part of meme-stock trading fueled by retail chatrooms, but the retrieved item did not tie that investor base directly to crypto sentiment — Bloomberg (bloomberg.com)

ASUSTEK COMPUTER

  • Rank: 99 | Expo: 0.72 | id_str: TW0002357001
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.3, infrastr 0.7, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: ASUS investor-conference transcripts/presentations (2022), ASUS annual-report / quarterly investor materials, financial news

Firm Overview

ASUSTeK Computer Inc. is a Taiwan-listed hardware company best known for PCs, gaming products, motherboards, and graphics cards. Within the sample period, its crypto relevance appears to come mainly from supplying graphics cards and related hardware into crypto-mining demand, with a smaller adjacent role through blockchain/Web3 platform activity rather than direct crypto custody, trading, or mining. (asus.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The strongest evidence supports infrastr because management explicitly said graphics-card demand had been “boosted by cryptocurrency by mining demand,” and discussed the subsequent normalization as that mining demand faded. That places ASUS in the crypto-infrastructure channel as a hardware supplier to mining workloads rather than as a miner or balance-sheet holder. (alphaspread.com)

A smaller bizserv weight is also warranted because ASUS’s 3Q 2022 investor materials described a first move into “Web 3.0” via an NFT platform and metaverse initiative “powered by” cloud computing, AI, and blockchain. Those materials suggest a non-financial service/application layer exposure, but not direct exchange, brokerage, custody, or crypto-product issuance, so finserv is not appropriate. (asus.com)

There is no evidence that ASUS mined crypto on its own account, held crypto as treasury, or served as a crypto exchange/custodian/broker during January 2017 to December 2024. The retrieved sources instead frame crypto exposure as end-market demand for components and a limited Web3/NFT initiative. (alphaspread.com)

Weight Assignment

A mixed assignment of infrastr = 0.7 and bizserv = 0.3 best fits the evidence. The larger weight goes to infrastr because the clearest and most economically grounded crypto linkage in both the earnings-call transcript and news coverage is GPU demand from cryptocurrency miners; the smaller bizserv weight reflects ASUS Metaverse / NFT-platform activity disclosed in 2022 investor materials, which appears real but secondary to the hardware channel. invbase = 0 because the retrieved materials did not explicitly describe ASUS shares as a crypto proxy or identify a crypto-focused shareholder base. (alphaspread.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The key limitation is that ASUS’s crypto exposure appears cyclical and mostly indirect: management discussed miners as customers for graphics cards, but the retrieved materials do not quantify how much of total company revenue came from crypto-related demand. The Web3/NFT evidence is confined to 2022 investor-presentation materials and does not clearly show material revenue contribution, so bizserv receives only a minority weight. No filing or transcript evidence was found for direct crypto holdings, mining output, or custody activity. (alphaspread.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • May 11, 2022, Q1 2022 investor conference transcript: “In the past 2 years, the demand of graphics cards were boosted by cryptocurrency by mining demand.” — Alpha Spread ASUSTeK Q1 2022 Earnings Call (alphaspread.com)
  • May 11, 2022, Q1 2022 investor conference transcript: “What we see right now is that demand from mining is disappearing.” — Alpha Spread ASUSTeK Q1 2022 Earnings Call (alphaspread.com)
  • Marketscreener transcript index confirms broad investor-conference coverage across 2019–2024, including Q1 2022, Q3 2022, Q4 2022, and 2023–2024 calls used to assess whether crypto remained a recurring management topic — MarketScreener ASUSTeK Call Transcripts (marketscreener.com)

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 3Q 2022 investor presentation: “ASUS announced its first move into Web 3.0 with the launch of ‘Art Black hole’, a new NFT platform” — ASUSTEK 3Q 2022 Investor Conference PDF (asus.com)
  • 3Q 2022 investor presentation: ASUS said the metaverse initiative was “powered by the technological expertise of ASUS, especially cloud computing, AI, and the blockchain.” — ASUSTEK 3Q 2022 Investor Conference PDF (asus.com)
  • ASUS investor-relations annual-report page provides the firm’s official annual reports for 2017–2024; no retrieved annual-report evidence indicated treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, or crypto-financial services — ASUS Annual Reports (asus.com)

Financial news / other:

  • May 13, 2022, The Register summarized ASUS’s earnings-call comments as: demand for GPUs used to mine cryptocurrency was “disappearing,” linking ASUS to miner demand through hardware sales rather than direct mining — The Register (theregister.com)
  • ASUS company profile / IR pages identify ASUSTeK as the listed operating company and support firm disambiguation for the Taiwan hardware manufacturer — ASUS Investor Profile (asus.com)

REKLAIM

  • Rank: 100 | Expo: 0.71 | id_str: CA7594021007
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: company financials / MD&A index (2021–2024), 2023 earnings-call materials, company help-center / product pages

Firm Overview

Reklaim Ltd. is a Canadian-listed consumer-data privacy and monetization company operating the Reklaim app, through which users can view, control, and monetize personal data. The retrieved 2021–2024 materials consistently describe a privacy-compliant data platform rather than a crypto operating business. (investors.reklaimyours.com; prnewswire.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive evidence supports any of the six positive crypto-exposure mechanisms during January 2017 to December 2024. The strongest potentially relevant evidence is that Reklaim allowed users to redeem points for cryptocurrency, but the retrieved product materials frame this as a payout option within a consumer rewards app rather than exchange, brokerage, custody, treasury holding, mining, or crypto-focused infrastructure. (help.reklaimyours.com; reklaimyours.com; reklaimyours.com)

The available earnings-call anchor also points away from a crypto classification. In Reklaim’s November 9, 2023 earnings-call announcement, management described the business as benefiting from “demand for privacy-compliant data,” and the same release said platform revenue came from compliant zero-party data sold to brands, platforms, and data companies, with no mention of crypto products or digital-asset handling. (prnewswire.com)

Although one SEDAR Plus annual-information-form search result surfaced patent language containing the word “blockchain,” that result appears to have pulled text from another issuer’s filing rather than Reklaim-specific operating disclosures, so it is not treated as valid evidence for classifying Reklaim. Given the absence of credible firm-specific evidence for holdings, mining, financial services, business services to crypto firms, or crypto infrastructure, unclass = 1.0 is the appropriate assignment. (sedarplus.ca)

Weight Assignment

unclass = 1.0 is the best fit because the retrieved 2021–2024 evidence describes Reklaim as a privacy-compliant consumer-data platform, and the only crypto-adjacent references found were redemption options for app users rather than a direct crypto business line. There is no credible evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, exchange/brokerage/custody activity, material service revenue from crypto clients, or mining/blockchain infrastructure supply. invbase = 0 because no retrieved source described Reklaim shares as a crypto proxy or documented crypto-driven retail trading in the stock. (investors.reklaimyours.com; prnewswire.com; help.reklaimyours.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is transcript access: transcript indexes indicate some 2020–2023 coverage, but a full non-paywalled earnings-call transcript was not directly retrievable in the browsing session. Still, the accessible November 9, 2023 earnings-call announcement provides an earnings-call anchor and contains no substantive crypto discussion. Another limitation is that product pages mention cryptocurrency as a redemption option for users, which is crypto-adjacent, but under the project’s rules that is not enough to classify the firm as finserv without evidence that Reklaim directly handles, custodies, issues, trades, or earns material fees from digital assets. (gurufocus.com; prnewswire.com; reklaimyours.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:
