  • Nov. 9, 2023, Q3 2023 earnings-call announcement: “we've witnessed an extraordinary surge in demand for privacy-compliant data” — PR Newswire Q3 2023 release / earnings-call notice
  • Nov. 9, 2023, Q3 2023 earnings-call announcement: Reklaim said it offers “compliant, zero-party data to Fortune 500 brands, platforms, and data companies” — PR Newswire Q3 2023 release / earnings-call notice
  • Transcript index covering 2020–2023 confirms company call/event coverage was searched, including Q2 2023, Q3 2023, and 2021 investor-event transcripts — GuruFocus Reklaim transcript index

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2021–2024 financials page lists annual quarterly financial statements and MD&A materials used to assess whether crypto appeared in official disclosures; no retrieved filing index evidence showed treasury crypto, mining, or crypto-financial services — Reklaim Financials / MD&A index
  • Nov. 2023 results release: platform revenue represented 74% of total revenue in Q3 2023, with business described around privacy-compliant data rather than crypto — PR Newswire Q3 2023 results

Financial news / other:

  • Help-center article explains users earn points through data orders and claims; no crypto operating segment is described — How do I earn with Reklaim?
  • Product update says redemption options include “Cryptocurrency, gift cards, and charity donations,” indicating crypto only as a user payout option — Redemption Now on Web
  • Consumer-sharing page tells users to redeem points for “crypto, gift cards, or PayPal cash,” again suggesting incidental redemption functionality rather than a crypto business model — Share Your Success

SHIFT4 PAYMENTS A

  • Rank: 101 | Expo: 0.70 | id_str: US82452J1097
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings-call transcript (Q2 2021), shareholder letter / 8-K (Q4 2021), 2023–2024 company releases

Firm Overview

Shift4 Payments, Inc. is a U.S.-listed integrated payments and commerce-technology company serving merchants across hospitality, restaurants, entertainment, ecommerce, gaming, and nonprofit verticals. Within the 2017–2024 sample, its crypto exposure comes from building crypto-acceptance and settlement capabilities into its payments stack, especially after acquiring The Giving Block and later launching crypto on-ramp and merchant-payment products. (fool.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports finserv exposure. In a Q2 2021 earnings-call transcript, management said Shift4 was pursuing “risk management tools and capabilities like crypto acceptance” for Shift4Shop, indicating direct work on crypto-enabled payment functionality. (fool.com)

That exposure became more explicit in 2022 when management told investors that The Giving Block added “cryptocurrency acceptance and settlement capabilities” and would let Shift4 bundle crypto donations with its end-to-end nonprofit offering. By 2023, Shift4 said it had built a fiat-to-crypto on-ramp for web3 merchants using Zero Hash infrastructure, and in October 2024 it announced Pay with Crypto for ecommerce and POS merchants with automatic USD conversion and settlement. These are direct crypto payment and transaction-enablement activities, which fit finserv rather than bizserv or infrastr. (investors.shift4.com)

Weight Assignment

finserv = 1.0 is the best fit because the retrieved evidence consistently shows Shift4 directly enabling crypto-related payment flows, donation acceptance, on-ramping, and merchant settlement rather than merely advising crypto firms or supplying generic infrastructure. There is no evidence of treasury crypto accumulation (holders) or mining (miners). bizserv is not appropriate because Shift4 is not just supporting crypto businesses from the sidelines; it is directly involved in payments and acceptance rails. infrastr is also not the main mechanism because the crypto capabilities described are payment services embedded in Shift4’s merchant stack, even where third-party infrastructure partners help power them. invbase = 0 because the retrieved sources did not show management or financial media describing FOUR primarily as a crypto-proxy stock driven by crypto-focused investor flows. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is that full access to multiple historical earnings-call transcripts was uneven in the browsing session; however, at least one accessible transcript quote was retrieved and the classification is reinforced by a detailed 2022 shareholder letter and later company disclosures. Crypto appears to be an adjunct capability within a much larger payments business rather than the dominant business line, so the classification concerns mechanism rather than overall firm identity. No credible evidence was found that crypto assets were material on the balance sheet, so AssetShare is coded 0. (fool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Aug. 5, 2021, Q2 2021 earnings call transcript: “risk management tools and capabilities like crypto acceptance that we have recently released” — Motley Fool Q2 2021 earnings call transcript
  • Aug. 5, 2021, Q2 2021 earnings call transcript: management said Shift4Shop partnerships would accelerate capabilities including crypto acceptance — Motley Fool Q2 2021 earnings call transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Mar. 1, 2022, Q4 2021 shareholder letter / 8-K: Shift4 said The Giving Block added “key cryptocurrency acceptance and settlement capabilities” for the organization — Shift4 Q4 2021 shareholder letter / 8-K
  • Mar. 1, 2022, Q4 2021 shareholder letter / 8-K: management said it intended to “bundle crypto-donation with our end-to-end solution” for nonprofits — Shift4 Q4 2021 shareholder letter / 8-K
  • 2023 Form 10-K: Shift4 disclosed The Giving Block acquisition details and earnout tied to revenue earned by the business, supporting that the unit was an operating payments/service asset rather than treasury holdings — Shift4 2023 Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • Oct. 25, 2023, GlobeNewswire: Shift4 said it used Zero Hash APIs to build a “fiat-to-crypto on-ramp for web3 merchants” — Zero Hash / GlobeNewswire announcement
  • Oct. 25, 2023, GlobeNewswire: Shift4’s director of crypto said Zero Hash infrastructure enables Shift4 to power crypto merchants globally — Zero Hash / GlobeNewswire announcement
  • Oct. 28, 2024, Business Wire: Shift4 announced “Pay with Crypto,” allowing ecommerce and POS merchants to accept cryptocurrencies with automatic conversion and settlement to U.S. dollars — Shift4 Unveils Global Crypto Payment Capabilities

ENVIROSUITE SUSP - SUSP.22/08/25

  • Rank: 102 | Expo: 0.69 | id_str: AU000000EVS3
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: investor results releases and annual-report materials (FY22–FY24), Reuters briefs, company investor relations pages

Firm Overview

Envirosuite Ltd is an Australian-listed environmental-intelligence software company serving aviation, mining, waste, water, and industrial customers with monitoring, modelling, and compliance tools. Across the retrieved 2017–2024 company materials, its disclosed business focus is environmental intelligence, ESG, air/noise/water monitoring, carbon-emissions modules, and related enterprise software rather than cryptocurrency activity.

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive evidence was found for any of the crypto-exposure mechanisms. The retrieved Envirosuite materials describe SaaS products for environmental monitoring and operational optimisation, including EVS Aviation, EVS Industrial, EVS Water, and carbon-emissions functionality, but do not show crypto treasury holdings, mining activity, crypto financial services, crypto-focused business services, or crypto-specific infrastructure.

A transcript-style investor-results source was retrieved, but its content discussed regional growth, environmental products, mining customers in the traditional extractive-industry sense, and strategic alliances unrelated to digital assets. Reuters and company news results likewise concerned licensing, management changes, partnerships, and the 2025 takeover process, with no credible crypto linkage within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period.

Weight Assignment

unclass = 1.0 is the appropriate assignment because the retrieved evidence supports only Envirosuite’s core environmental software business and provides no meaningful or credible indication of crypto exposure through holdings, mining, financial services, non-financial crypto services, or infrastructure. invbase = 0 because no source indicated that EVS traded as a crypto proxy or that management discussed crypto-driven investor flows.

Ambiguities / Limitations

A clear crypto-related earnings-call quote could not be retrieved because the accessible investor-presentation and results materials located in this session contained no crypto discussion at all. That limitation does not affect the outcome here because the absence of crypto references was consistent across the company materials, investor pages, and Reuters items reviewed. One search hit mentioned “Bitcoin Corporation Holdings” only as a shareholder line in an ASX announcement context, which is not evidence of Envirosuite having crypto business exposure and was therefore excluded.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Feb. 2023, 1H FY23 results update: “promote, market, and sell bundled services that combine SGS’s testing, inspection, and certification services with the EVS Omnis environmental intelligence platform” — Envirosuite 1H FY23 Results
  • Feb. 2024, 1H FY24 results presentation: company highlighted growth in EVS Aviation, EVS Industrial, and EVS Water, with no crypto-related activity disclosed — ASX 1H FY24 Results Presentation

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Aug. 2024, FY24 results / annual-report page: revenue, ARR, cash, and product discussion focused on environmental intelligence, mining, waste, aviation, and carbon emissions, with no crypto holdings or services disclosed — Envirosuite FY24 Annual Report page
  • Aug. 2022, FY22 annual-report summary: management described Envirosuite as an “Environmental Intelligence” technology platform with recurring software revenue and customer-site growth, with no crypto references — Envirosuite FY22 Annual Report summary
  • Investor relations page, accessed 2026: company overview states it helps customers “harness the power of environmental intelligence” across airports, cities, construction, mining, waste and water — Envirosuite Investor Relations

Financial news / other:

  • Dec. 2, 2019, Reuters brief: coverage concerned an exclusive global IP licence agreement with UniQuest for SeweX, not crypto activity — Reuters brief via Investing.com
  • 2024 company news: Envirosuite announced a strategic alliance with SGS around testing, inspection, certification, and the EVS Omnis platform, again unrelated to crypto — Envirosuite and SGS strategic alliance

ATEAM HOLDINGS

  • Rank: 103 | Expo: 0.68 | id_str: JP3160890004
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.7, bizserv 0.3, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: company IR/news releases (2024–2025), FY2025 Q2 business-report transcript-style summary, FY2025 annual-report filing

Firm Overview

Ateam Holdings Co., Ltd. is a Japan-listed internet and technology group whose core businesses are digital marketing, media, and entertainment. Within the sample period, its crypto exposure emerged late in 2024 through the acquisition and consolidation of Paddle, an operator of point apps whose user rewards can be redeemed into crypto assets.

Exposure Channels Identified

The strongest identified exposure is finserv because Ateam, via Paddle, operates consumer-facing apps such as Bit Start and Bit Walk that grant points redeemable for crypto assets, and the group explicitly states it holds crypto assets for that business. A FY2025 Q2 management report said new accounting procedures were needed because Paddle operates a “crypto asset-related business,” with provisions tied to points redeemable for crypto assets and profit effects linked to crypto-asset valuation. (ssl4.eir-parts.net)

A smaller bizserv component is also warranted because Ateam’s acquisition rationale emphasized Paddle’s advertising-operation and point-app operating know-how, and Ateam framed the deal partly as a digital-marketing/customer-acquisition expansion rather than a pure financial-asset business. However, there is no evidence of mining or crypto-specific infrastructure. (a-tm.co.jp)

Weight Assignment

finserv = 0.7 is the dominant weight because the direct mechanism is a crypto-related consumer service in which users earn points exchangeable for crypto assets and the group holds crypto assets operationally for that offering; this fits the financial-services channel more closely than treasury holding. bizserv = 0.3 captures the ancillary digital-marketing and app-operation capabilities that management highlighted as part of the Paddle acquisition. holders = 0.0 because the annual report says the crypto assets are held mainly for Paddle’s point-app operations rather than as a standalone treasury allocation. invbase = 0 because no sourced evidence showed that Ateam traded primarily as a crypto proxy or that management discussed crypto-driven shareholder flows. (a-tm.co.jp)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The firm’s meaningful crypto exposure appears only at the end of the sample period, mainly after the May 28, 2024 Paddle acquisition decision and November 2024 consolidation, so earlier 2017–2023 materials are much less informative. An accessible transcript-style earnings-call anchor was found for March 14, 2025, but older call transcripts with crypto discussion were not retrievable because the exposure itself seems newly added. The classification therefore relies heavily on late-period company disclosures and the annual report, but those sources are explicit enough to support a confident classification. (ssl4.eir-parts.net)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Mar. 14, 2025, FY2025 Q2 business-report summary: “Paddle Inc. ... operates a crypto asset-related business” and user points are “redeemable for crypto assets” such as in BitWalk and Bit Start — Ateam FY2025 Q2 Business Report (Summary)
  • Mar. 14, 2025, FY2025 Q2 business-report summary: management said adjusted EBITDA excludes “expenses related to crypto assets associated with the start of consolidation of Paddle” — Ateam FY2025 Q2 Business Report (Summary)

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2025 annual report: Ateam stated it holds crypto assets mainly “for the operation of point apps exchangeable for crypto assets at Paddle,” indicating operational rather than treasury holdings — Ateam Holdings Annual Report 2025
  • 2025 annual report: balance-sheet carrying amount of crypto assets was 1,236 million yen as of July 31, 2025, including 59.1667 BTC, XRP, and DOGE — Ateam Holdings Annual Report 2025
  • May 28, 2024, company IR release: Ateam announced acquisition of 67% of Paddle, which operates the crypto-redeemable points app Bit Start, with planned eventual 100% ownership — Ateam acquisition of Paddle

Financial news / other:

  • Sep. 6, 2024, company medium-term-plan release: Ateam described its strategic transformation toward a sales-support/digital-marketing company, providing context for the non-financial-services component of the Paddle deal — Ateam Medium-Term Business Plan FY2025–FY2028
  • Oct. 2025 retrospective news coverage: Jakota reported FY2025 profit was boosted by crypto valuation gains, corroborating that crypto prices had become relevant to reported earnings after Paddle consolidation — JAKOTA News on Ateam profits and crypto gains

RECORD

  • Rank: 104 | Expo: 0.68 | id_str: GB00B28ZPS36
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.4, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.6
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 2
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: annual reports (2023–2024), interim-results statement for 30 September 2023, company results/publications pages

Firm Overview

Record plc is a UK-listed specialist currency and asset manager whose core business is institutional currency hedging, derivative solutions, and related asset-management services. Within the sample period, its crypto-adjacent activity appeared through Record Digital Asset Ventures, an internal initiative and investment vehicle aimed at learning about and partnering with digital-asset businesses rather than operating a large direct crypto business itself.

Exposure Channels Identified

A partial bizserv classification is warranted because Record explicitly described Record Digital as a vehicle to identify opportunities in the digital-asset sector, invested in Block Scholes, and discussed building products and services around digital-asset analytics and fund functionality. In the 2023 annual report, management said Record Digital existed “to identify future opportunities for growth and diversification in the digital asset sector,” and highlighted Block Scholes as providing institutional-grade analytics and research for digital-asset clients. Record Annual Report 2023

However, the evidence does not support holders, miners, or infrastr, and only weakly supports finserv. The available sources show small venture-style investments and exploratory fund-building around digital assets, but no clear evidence that Record itself materially held crypto as treasury, mined crypto, or directly operated an exchange, custody platform, or other core crypto financial service at scale during 2017–2024. Record Annual Report 2023 Record Annual Report 2024

Weight Assignment

bizserv = 0.4 reflects substantive but still limited crypto exposure through advisory/partnership/incubation activity around digital-asset analytics, research, and related product development rather than direct handling of crypto assets. unclass = 0.6 remains the largest weight because the evidence points to exploratory, early-stage and partly disposed activity, with no strong indication that crypto-related business became a material operating segment by December 2024. finserv = 0.0 is kept at zero because references to Luxembourg funds “embedding” digital-asset capabilities were still developmental and did not clearly establish a direct, operating crypto financial-service business by Record itself. invbase = 0 because no sourced evidence showed that Record traded as a crypto proxy or that management discussed crypto-driven shareholder flows.

Ambiguities / Limitations

A transcript-style anchor was retrievable only through Record’s published interim-results statement rather than a conventional analyst-call transcript, so certainty is capped at 2 under the project rules. The company used broad “digital assets” language, but much of the disclosed activity was exploratory, venture-oriented, or partnership-based, and the 2024 annual report also states that Record Digital Asset Ventures was sold during the year, which weakens the case for a larger ongoing crypto classification. The evidence therefore supports a modest bizserv allocation rather than a pure unclass outcome, but the case is not strong enough for high certainty.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Nov. 17, 2023, interim-results statement for six months ended Sept. 30, 2023: “Record Digital was set up as a separate group entity ... to track, learn and identify opportunities for future diversification and growth in this sector” and had committed capital to “digital asset companies” — Record Interim Announcement, six months ended 30 September 2023
  • Nov. 17, 2023, interim-results statement for six months ended Sept. 30, 2023: management said it was “building out a suite of Luxembourg funds” embedding capabilities from digital-asset specialists, with Block Scholes as a service provider for data, analytics, and research — Record Interim Announcement, six months ended 30 September 2023

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • June 2023, annual report: Record said it had set up Record Digital “to identify future opportunities for growth and diversification in the digital asset sector” — Record Annual Report 2023
  • June 2023, annual report: Record disclosed that about 75% of an initially £2 million capital allocation had been committed to “small direct investments, and investment funds investing in start-up and early stage technology and digital asset companies” — Record Annual Report 2023
  • June 2023, annual report: Record highlighted Block Scholes as aiming to be the “Bloomberg of Digital Assets,” and said Record had invested in and become a client/partner of that business — Record Annual Report 2023
  • June 2024, annual report: Record stated that Record Digital Asset Ventures Limited was a “UK company investing in opportunities linked to innovation and research surrounding digital assets – sold during the period” — Record Annual Report 2024

Financial news / other:

  • Feb. 9, 2023, capital markets presentation: Record identified Block Scholes ties and digital-asset expertise among personnel, corroborating that the initiative focused on partnerships and sector learning rather than mining or treasury crypto ownership — Record Capital Markets Presentation
  • Record results/publications archive: company history pages show recurring annual/interim reports across 2017–2024, with crypto-related references only appearing in the later Record Digital materials rather than as a longstanding core segment — Record Results and Publications

ESPORTS ENTERTAINMENT GROUP

  • Rank: 105 | Expo: 0.68 | id_str: US29667K6038
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2021, 2023), earnings call transcripts index, company press/archive materials

Firm Overview

Esports Entertainment Group, Inc. was a Malta-based, U.S.-listed online gambling and esports company operating iGaming, sportsbook, and esports-related platform businesses during the 2017–2024 sample period. The retrieved sources describe the firm primarily as an online wagering and esports operator rather than a crypto-focused company. (esportsentertainmentgroup.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence does not support any non-zero allocation to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. Searches of the company’s 2021 and 2023 Form 10-K filings returned no references to “bitcoin” or “crypto,” and the only blockchain-related references were to broad investment-fund language around gaming, data, blockchain, and metaverse-related service arrangements rather than direct crypto operations, treasury holdings, mining, custody, exchange activity, or infrastructure provision. (sec.gov)

A conventional earnings-call transcript history is retrievable for the company, but the available search evidence did not surface any crypto-specific management discussion. Because the sourced record points to incidental, non-operational mentions of blockchain/metaverse themes rather than substantive crypto exposure, the firm is best classified as unclass = 1.0. (seekingalpha.com)

Weight Assignment

unclass = 1.0 is appropriate because the retrieved evidence shows no meaningful, clearly evidenced crypto-exposure mechanism under the project definitions. The blockchain references in filings relate to a planned game fund and a services agreement with Metaverse Partners, but these are too general and indirect to justify finserv, bizserv, or infrastr, and there is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining activity, or a crypto-linked investor-base narrative. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A transcript index was retrievable, satisfying the search step for earnings-call availability, but a crypto-related transcript quote could not be identified because the evidence gathered did not show substantive crypto discussion by management. The only potentially relevant references were broad “blockchain” or “metaverse” mentions in filing footnotes and agreement descriptions, which were excluded under the project’s threshold principle because they did not establish material crypto business activity. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Seeking Alpha transcript archive, covering 2020–2022 calls: transcript history is available for GMBL, but the retrieved archive snippet did not indicate crypto-focused calls or recurring digital-asset discussion — Seeking Alpha GMBL Earnings Call Transcripts
  • Feb. 22, 2022 / Nov. 15, 2021 / Oct. 13, 2021 / May 17, 2021 / Nov. 20, 2020 / Nov. 16, 2020, transcript archive listing: used to verify call coverage during the sample period rather than as evidence of crypto exposure — Seeking Alpha GMBL Earnings Call Transcripts

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Sept. 28, 2021, Form 10-K: the filing’s only relevant passage says the company signed a partnership agreement with Game Fund Partners for projects in “gaming, data, blockchain, online gaming, and joint casino hotel investments,” but also states no shares had yet been contributed to the fund as of October 13, 2021 — SEC Form 10-K (2021)
  • Sept. 28, 2021, Form 10-K: the company also disclosed a services agreement with Metaverse Partners tied to esports gaming services, not direct crypto operations — SEC Form 10-K (2021)
  • Sept. 28, 2023, Form 10-K: searching the filing returned no matches for “bitcoin” or “crypto,” while a blockchain mention again appeared only in planned game-fund language; as of June 30, 2023 the company had not contributed any common stock to the fund — SEC Form 10-K (2023)
  • Sept. 28, 2023, Form 10-K: no evidence was found of mining revenue, crypto assets on the balance sheet, custody/trading activity, or crypto infrastructure sales — SEC Form 10-K (2023)

Financial news / other:

  • Company about page: Esports Entertainment Group describes itself as focused on esports and online gaming innovation, with no retrieved reference to cryptocurrency as a core business line — Esports Entertainment Group About Page
  • Company press-release archive: recent and archived releases emphasize iGaming, esports content, wagering, restructuring, and acquisitions rather than crypto exposure — Esports Entertainment Group Press Releases

MEDICENNA THERP.

  • Rank: 106 | Expo: 0.67 | id_str: CA58490H1073
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: company annual/quarterly reports archive (2017–2024), earnings call transcript archive/index, company financial-result releases

Firm Overview

Medicenna Therapeutics Corp. is a Canada-based clinical-stage immunotherapy company developing cytokine-based cancer therapies, including its IL-2 Superkine MDNA11 and bizaxofusp/MDNA55, during the 2017–2024 sample period. The retrieved investor-relations materials and earnings-call archive describe a biotechnology issuer focused on oncology drug development rather than any crypto-related business line. (medicennatherapeutics.gcs-web.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence does not support any non-zero allocation to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. Searches of the company’s report archive for 2017–2024 surfaced annual reports, MD&A documents, and financial statements, but the retrieved materials describe financing, clinical trials, and drug-development operations rather than treasury crypto holdings, mining activity, exchange/brokerage services, custody, payments, blockchain consulting, or crypto-focused infrastructure. (medicennatherapeutics.gcs-web.com)

An earnings-call transcript archive is retrievable for Medicenna, satisfying the transcript search step, but the surfaced transcript/index evidence did not indicate crypto-specific management discussion. Because the available record points to a conventional clinical-stage biotech profile with no substantive digital-asset exposure, the firm is best classified as unclass = 1.0. (seekingalpha.com)

Weight Assignment

unclass = 1.0 is appropriate because the collected sources consistently characterize Medicenna as a clinical-stage cancer-therapy developer and do not provide credible evidence for any project-defined crypto mechanism. There is no retrieved indication of deliberate crypto treasury holdings, mined coin exposure, direct crypto financial services, non-financial crypto servicing, crypto-targeted infrastructure sales, or a stock-market narrative framing the company as a crypto proxy. (ir.medicenna.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A transcript archive/index was retrievable, but no crypto-related transcript quote could be identified because the search results did not surface substantive management discussion of bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain. Some investor-relations pages and downloadable filings were partially inaccessible to direct page fetch, so the classification relies on the archived report listings and available result releases; however, the retrieved evidence was consistently non-crypto and therefore sufficient for an unclass assignment. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Seeking Alpha transcript archive: transcript history is available for Medicenna/MDNAF, used to verify earnings-call coverage during the sample period; no crypto-focused transcript evidence was surfaced in the archive result — Seeking Alpha MDNAF Earnings Call Transcripts
  • Aug. 15, 2022, Q1 fiscal 2023 earnings call listing: company IR page confirms the existence of a Medicenna earnings call event, used to anchor transcript availability rather than crypto exposure — Medicenna First Quarter Fiscal 2023 Earnings Call
  • MarketScreener transcript archive: lists multiple Medicenna call transcripts across 2021–2022, but the surfaced archive snippet does not indicate digital-asset discussion — Medicenna Transcript Archive

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Company financial-reports archive, 2017–2024: lists annual reports, annual information forms, MD&A, and quarterly financial statements across the sample period, indicating a standard biotech reporting set rather than crypto-specific disclosures — Medicenna Financial Reports
  • 2024 reports index: lists 2024 annual financial statements, annual MD&A, and annual information form; retrieved archive description contains no bitcoin/crypto/blockchain business disclosure — Medicenna 2024 Report Archive
  • Financial reports page snapshot: 2021–2024 archives include annual and quarterly MD&A/financial statements, supporting review of the firm as a conventional development-stage therapeutic company — Medicenna Financial Reports (IR)

Financial news / other:

  • June 27, 2024, company results release: Medicenna reported fiscal 2024 financial results and operational highlights centered on MDNA11, bizaxofusp, cash runway, and a private placement by RA Capital, with no crypto-related operating line — Medicenna Reports Fiscal Year 2024 Financial Results
  • Investor relations homepage: describes Medicenna through corporate presentation, financials, and oncology pipeline updates rather than digital-asset activities — Medicenna Investor Relations
  • Nov. 25, 2024, company release: presentation of preclinical oncology data at SNO 2024 further supports classification as a therapeutics developer, not a crypto-exposed firm — Medicenna SNO 2024 Release

NASDAQ

  • Rank: 107 | Expo: 0.67 | id_str: US6311031081
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.7, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.3, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings-call reporting on Q2 2023, Nasdaq annual-report / investor-relations materials for 2023–2024, and financial news on Nasdaq Digital Assets

Firm Overview

Nasdaq, Inc. is a U.S.-listed exchange operator and financial-technology company whose core businesses span trading venues, market technology, indexes, data, and workflow solutions. During the 2017–2024 sample period, the retrieved evidence shows crypto exposure not through treasury holdings or mining, but through digital-asset custody initiatives, crypto index products, ETF-related market infrastructure, and software capabilities aimed at digital-asset markets. (seekingalpha.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The strongest supported channel is finserv. Nasdaq publicly pursued a digital-assets custody business and management stated on the July 19, 2023 earnings call that it had decided to halt the U.S. custodian launch while continuing to build digital-asset capabilities; that is direct evidence of an intended custody/financial-services exposure rather than mere commentary on blockchain. (cnbc.com)

A secondary channel is infrastr. Even after pausing the U.S. custody launch, management said Nasdaq would keep advancing the custody solution “as a technology platform,” and Nasdaq’s investor-relations / reporting materials describe activity in blockchain, digital assets, analytics, and crypto-linked index / ETP infrastructure. That supports a meaningful infrastructure allocation alongside the primary financial-services classification. (reddit.com)

There is no credible evidence in the retrieved 2017–2024 record that Nasdaq was a crypto holder, miner, or a non-financial crypto services provider under the project definitions. The evidence instead points to exchange, custody, index, and technology-enablement roles around digital assets. (cnbc.com)

Weight Assignment

finserv = 0.7 and infrastr = 0.3 best fits the evidence. The custody initiative, crypto index products, and ETF/listing ecosystem role place Nasdaq mainly in crypto financial services, while the retained software/platform emphasis after the custody pause supports a smaller but real infrastructure component. holders, miners, and bizserv remain at 0.0 because no substantive balance-sheet crypto investment, mining operations, or purely non-financial crypto servicing was evidenced. RevShare is coded 1 because crypto appears peripheral relative to Nasdaq’s much larger diversified exchange and fintech revenues, while AssetShare is 0 because no material crypto assets on balance sheet were evidenced. (cnbc.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Direct fetching of several primary IR and transcript pages was partially blocked, so the classification relies on searchable snippets, investor-relations result pages, and contemporaneous financial-news coverage. The key transcript anchor is available through reporting of management’s July 19, 2023 earnings-call remarks rather than a fully scraped transcript page. That is sufficient for a non-unclass assignment, but it limits precision on how large digital-asset revenues were inside Nasdaq’s broader reporting segments. (cnbc.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • July 19, 2023, Q2 2023 earnings call reporting: CEO Adena Friedman said Nasdaq had “made the decision to halt” the U.S. digital-assets custodian launch because of the regulatory environment — CNBC report on Q2 2023 earnings-call remarks
  • July 19, 2023, earnings-call quote surfaced in search: management added that Nasdaq would continue building “technology capabilities” and advance its custody solution “as a technology platform” for the broader market — Search-visible transcript quotation reference
  • NDAQ earnings transcript archive, 2017–2024 coverage: Seeking Alpha lists a multi-year earnings transcript archive for Nasdaq, confirming transcript availability over the sample period — Seeking Alpha NDAQ Earnings Call Transcripts

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Feb. 2025, full-year 2024 results PDF: Nasdaq reported period-end ETP assets under management tracking in its index business, supporting ongoing crypto/ETP-adjacent financial-market exposure rather than treasury holding exposure — Nasdaq Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
  • 2023 annual-report / IR search result: Nasdaq investor-relations materials explicitly reference “blockchain and digital assets” among its technology and analytics activities — Nasdaq annual-report / IR materials snippet
  • Annual reports archive: Nasdaq maintains an annual-report archive used to review 2017–2024 disclosures for any evidence of crypto holdings or mining; none was surfaced — Nasdaq Annual Reports Archive

Financial news / other:

  • July 19, 2023, CoinDesk: Nasdaq halted its crypto custody plan due to U.S. regulatory conditions, corroborating direct financial-services exposure via attempted custody operations — CoinDesk on Nasdaq halting custody plan
  • July 19, 2023, Bloomberg: reported Nasdaq halted the launch of a U.S. crypto-custodian business, reinforcing classification in finserv rather than generic tech commentary — Bloomberg on halted crypto-custodian launch
  • Dec. 13, 2023, Business Standard: after curtailing custody plans, Nasdaq intended to repurpose the technology and continue services related to tokenized assets, supporting a residual infrastr component — Business Standard on repurposing crypto technology

CBOE GLOBAL MARKETS(BTS)

  • Rank: 108 | Expo: 0.67 | id_str: US12503M1080
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.9, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.1, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings-call transcripts (2021, 2023), 10-K / annual reports (2022–2024), company press releases (2021–2024)

Firm Overview

Cboe Global Markets, Inc. is a U.S.-listed exchange operator whose core businesses span options, equities, futures, FX, data and access services, and clearing. During the 2017–2024 sample period, its crypto exposure came mainly through Cboe Digital, a digital-asset spot, futures, and clearing platform built via the ErisX acquisition and later refocused toward derivatives and clearing. (ir.cboe.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The dominant channel is finserv. Management explicitly described Cboe Digital as serving demand for “institutional and retail trading solutions,” and the company’s 2022 acquisition release said ErisX operated a U.S.-based digital-asset spot market, a regulated futures exchange, and a regulated clearinghouse. (ir.cboe.com)

A smaller infrastr component is also supported. Cboe said it intended to develop and distribute digital-asset data products, joined Pyth Network to bring market data to blockchain, and by 2024 was refocusing the digital business around derivatives, clearing, technology, and product innovation rather than spot trading. (ir.cboe.com)

There is no evidence of miners. There is also no basis for holders as a treasury-investment category: the annual report states Cboe Digital maintained its own digital assets only to facilitate customer trading and did not use customer digital assets for its own corporate or business purposes, which excludes a strategic balance-sheet crypto-holder classification. (ir.cboe.com)

Weight Assignment

finserv = 0.9 and infrastr = 0.1 best reflects the evidence. The core documented crypto mechanism was direct operation of crypto spot trading, futures, clearing, and custody-like safeguarding functions through Cboe Digital, which squarely fits crypto financial services. A modest infrastructure weight is warranted because Cboe also emphasized digital-asset data products and blockchain data distribution, but those appear secondary to the exchange/clearing function. RevShare = 1 because crypto was clearly peripheral within Cboe’s much larger diversified exchange group, while AssetShare = 0 because the disclosed digital assets were primarily safeguarded customer assets and facilitation balances rather than material proprietary crypto assets. (ir.cboe.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Some transcript sources were partially blocked, so the transcript anchor relies on an investor-relations-hosted Q3 2021 transcript PDF and a searchable Q2 2023 transcript mirror rather than a full set of directly scraped Seeking Alpha pages. Cboe did hold small digital-asset balances to facilitate customer transactions, but the 2023 annual report explicitly frames these as operational rather than treasury holdings, so they were not coded as holders. No explicit evidence was found that Cboe’s shareholder base traded the stock as a primary crypto proxy, so invbase remains 0. (ir.cboe.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Nov. 5, 2021, Q3 2021 earnings call: “bring the knowledge, structure and transparency of our trusted markets to the digital asset space” and meet demand for “institutional and retail trading solutions” through Cboe Digital — Cboe Q3 2021 earnings-call transcript
  • Aug. 4, 2023, Q2 2023 earnings call: management said hiring had slowed in the digital business “in part due to ongoing regulatory uncertainty” while remaining “incredibly committed to the digital business” — Insider Monkey Q2 2023 transcript mirror
  • Feb. 9, 2018, Q4 2017 earnings call: management said, “Our bitcoin futures launch put demand on our market data in our futures product” — The Motley Fool Q4 2017 earnings-call transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • May 2, 2022, acquisition release: Cboe completed the ErisX acquisition, describing it as a U.S.-based digital-asset spot market, regulated futures exchange, and regulated clearinghouse — Cboe acquisition press release
  • Mar. 31, 2023, 2022 annual report: Cboe said its trading network spans equities, derivatives, FX “and digital assets” across regions — 2022 Annual Report
  • 2023 annual report, note disclosure: Cboe recorded digital-assets safeguarded assets/liabilities for customer holdings and stated it maintained its own digital assets only “to facilitate customer trading” — 2023 Annual Report
  • Apr. 25, 2024, strategy update: Cboe said it would wind down the spot digital-asset trading platform and focus on derivatives and clearing — Cboe digital realignment release

Financial news / other:

  • Oct. 5, 2022, company release: Cboe joined Pyth Network to bring market data to blockchain and said it was expanding in the digital-asset space — Cboe joins Pyth Network
  • Apr. 25, 2024, Reuters-republished report: Cboe said it would integrate digital-asset derivatives into its derivatives and clearing arm and wind down the spot platform — Investing.com republication of Reuters report
  • 2024 annual-report snippet / SEC filing: Cboe said it halted spot-market trading in May 2024 and refocused on derivatives, technology, and product innovation — 2024 Form 10-K filing

BLACKBOXSTOCKS

  • Rank: 109 | Expo: 0.66 | id_str: US09229E3036
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2022 Q3 earnings call transcript, 2022–2024 10-K filings, 2022 investor presentation

Firm Overview

Blackboxstocks Inc. is a Dallas-based financial-technology company whose core business is a subscription software platform for stock and options traders, combining market scanners, analytics, news, and a social-media/community interface. Across its filings and investor materials, the company is described as a SaaS platform focused on equities and options trading rather than an operating crypto business. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

No mechanism category is supported strongly enough for a non-zero crypto weight. The only crypto-related evidence found is management’s November 15, 2022 earnings-call statement that it had put its “crypto initiative” on hold and still viewed crypto as part of the product offering long term, plus a September 2022 investor presentation slide describing a planned “Crypto Currency Platform” combining quotation/execution with the BLBX social platform. (insidermonkey.com)

However, the company’s 2022 and 2024 10-Ks describe the actual business as stock-and-options analytics, social media, and education, with no bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain activity disclosed in the business description or searchable filing text. There is no evidence that Blackboxstocks operated a crypto exchange or brokerage, held crypto on balance sheet, mined crypto, provided crypto-focused infrastructure, or generated identifiable crypto revenue during 2017–2024. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

unclass = 1.0 is the best fit. The transcript anchor confirms only a delayed, still-unlaunched crypto initiative, while filings consistently frame Blackboxstocks as a stock/options SaaS platform and do not document substantive crypto operations or material exposure. Under the threshold principle, a planned or exploratory product without evidence of launch, revenue, custody, holdings, mining, or infrastructure support is insufficient for a non-zero mechanism weight. (insidermonkey.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is that management and investor materials did discuss a prospective crypto product in 2022. But the available 10-K evidence through fiscal 2024 does not show that this initiative became an operating business, and no filing-based crypto revenues, assets, or segment disclosures were found. No evidence was found that BLBX traded as a primary crypto proxy stock, so invbase remains 0. (insidermonkey.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Nov. 15, 2022, Q3 2022 earnings call: “We have also load or put on hold our crypto initiative for now … we believe that crypto is here to stay … But for now, we believe Stock Nanny and Black Box Pro take precedence over the crypto platform.” — Insider Monkey Q3 2022 earnings-call transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Mar. 21, 2025, 2024 Form 10-K: company described itself as a “financial technology and social media hybrid platform” for “stock and options traders,” with no searchable bitcoin/crypto/blockchain disclosure in the filing text — SEC 2024 Form 10-K
  • Apr. 14, 2023, 2022 Form 10-K: no searchable references to bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain were found in the filing — SEC 2022 Form 10-K
  • Nov. 15, 2021, Nasdaq uplisting release: Blackboxstocks described itself as a platform offering real-time analytics for stock and options traders — SEC-exhibit press release

Financial news / other:

  • Sept. 2022, investor presentation: planned “Crypto Currency Platform” that “combines a quotation and execution system with the BLBX social media platform” and targets a “technology focused investor base” — Blackboxstocks investor presentation

SANRIO

  • Rank: 110 | Expo: 0.66 | id_str: JP3343200006
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: FY3/2024 and FY3/2025 investor materials / integrated-report disclosures, NTT Digital press release, 2024 earnings-call materials

Firm Overview

Sanrio Co., Ltd. is a Japan-listed entertainment and consumer-products company centered on character IP such as Hello Kitty, with businesses spanning licensing, merchandise, digital content, and theme-park / experiential offerings. Across its investor materials, the company frames growth around IP monetization, digital experiences, games, and fan communities rather than operating as a crypto-native business.

Exposure Channels Identified

No mechanism category is supported strongly enough for a non-zero crypto weight. Sanrio did make explicit web3 / NFT-related statements during 2021–2024, including a Hello Kitty NFT partnership in November 2021 and a May 30, 2024 agreement with NTT Digital to introduce wallet infrastructure for a next-generation community service in which users could create wallets and receive / use NFTs. Those references show experimentation with blockchain-enabled fan engagement, not evidence that Sanrio directly operated a crypto exchange, brokerage, custody platform, mining business, treasury-crypto strategy, or crypto-focused infrastructure business.

The closest plausible channel would be finserv, because the 2024 service contemplates wallets and NFTs, but the available evidence indicates Sanrio was using a third-party wallet provider and presenting NFTs as part of digital fandom and marketing rather than as a financial-service line. Its 2025 management materials also discuss Web 3.0 and target “10 million registered users for Web3 services,” yet these statements sit inside a broader digital / IP strategy and do not show identifiable crypto revenue, on-balance-sheet crypto assets, or direct handling of customer digital assets as a core business.

Weight Assignment

unclass = 1.0 is the best fit. The evidence shows brand-extension and community experiments involving NFTs / web3, but under the threshold principle this remains too incidental and too weakly tied to the six mechanism channels to justify a positive weight. There is no evidence of deliberate crypto holdings, mining, direct crypto financial intermediation, non-financial service provision to third-party crypto firms, or crypto-specific hardware / datacenter infrastructure.

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is that Sanrio’s digital strategy did become more explicit by 2024–2025, including references to web3 services and NFTs. However, the retrieved materials do not show that these activities were financially material during the January 2017–December 2024 sample period, and the accessible “earnings call” materials were slide decks rather than text transcripts with management Q&A discussing crypto in detail. Because the final classification is unclass = 1.0, no certainty score applies; invbase remains 0 because no evidence was found that SANRIO traded as a primary crypto proxy stock.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Jan. 26, 2024, 4Q FY2023 earnings-call materials: searchable text in the accessible presentation showed no references to NFT, blockchain, or web3, indicating crypto was not a featured earnings-call topic in that briefing — Seeking Alpha-hosted 4Q 2023 earnings-call presentation
  • May 2, 2024, Q1 2024 earnings-call materials: searchable text in the accessible presentation showed no references to NFT, blockchain, or web3 — Seeking Alpha-hosted Q1 2024 earnings-call presentation

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • May 30, 2024, partner press release: Sanrio planned a next-generation community service using “web3 and generative AI technology,” with NTT Digital’s wallet service enabling users to “create wallets, receive and utilize NFTs” — NTT Digital press release
  • Oct. 30, 2024, integrated-report release: Sanrio said its first integrated report covered business performance, management policies, and strategy for FY3/2025–FY3/2027 — Sanrio integrated-report release
  • 2025 integrated-report / management-update materials discussing FY3/2025 results: Sanrio identified Web 3.0 as a long-term opportunity, noted Southeast Asia’s market for “blockchain games,” and listed a digital target of “10 million registered users for Web3 services” — Sanrio long-term vision / medium-term management update

Financial news / other:

  • Nov. 18, 2021, License Global: Sanrio’s Hello Kitty brand partnered with RECUR to let fans “buy, collect and trade Hello Kitty NFTs,” which Sanrio described as exploring an innovative business challenge — License Global report on Hello Kitty NFT partnership

COOTEK 1:650 ADR

  • Rank: 111 | Expo: 0.65 | id_str: US21718L2016
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2021 strategic press release, 2021–2024 Form 20-F filings, 2018–2022 earnings-call transcript index

Firm Overview

CooTek (Cayman) Inc. is a China-focused, Cayman-incorporated mobile internet company whose core businesses during the sample period were online literature, mobile games, and mobile advertising. Its SEC filings through fiscal 2024 describe the company as a pan-entertainment mobile-app operator rather than a crypto-native or crypto-financial business. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

No mechanism category is supported strongly enough for a non-zero crypto weight. The strongest crypto-adjacent item located was a December 2, 2021 company press release saying CooTek was focusing on “Metaverse” products and an NFT-based digital-content copyright system built on blockchain, but this was framed as a strategic announcement rather than evidence of operating a crypto exchange, brokerage, custody platform, mining business, treasury-crypto strategy, or crypto infrastructure business. (nasdaq.com)

The company’s accessible annual reports for fiscal 2021, 2023, and 2024 contain no matches for “bitcoin” or “blockchain,” while describing revenue drivers instead around mobile apps and advertising. That absence of recurring crypto disclosure in the filings weighs against assigning finserv, bizserv, or infrastr based on a single NFT/metaverse announcement. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

unclass = 1.0 is the best fit. Although CooTek publicly referenced NFT and metaverse ambitions in December 2021, the retrieved evidence does not show substantive direct handling of crypto assets, custody, trading, mining, treasury holdings, or crypto-specific infrastructure, nor does it show identifiable crypto revenue or crypto assets on the balance sheet during January 2017 through December 2024. Under the threshold principle, the NFT / blockchain language appears too episodic and too weakly evidenced in subsequent filings to justify a positive mechanism weight. (nasdaq.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is whether the December 2, 2021 NFT/metaverse strategy announcement ever developed into a material operating line. The transcript index confirms that multiple earnings-call transcripts exist for 2018–2022, but the underlying transcript pages were not retrievable through the browsing tool, so no earnings-call quote could be extracted directly; because the final classification is unclass = 1.0, no certainty score applies. No evidence was found that CooTek’s stock traded as a primary crypto proxy, so invbase remains 0. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Mar. 15, 2022 / Dec. 8, 2021 / Mar. 25, 2021, transcript index: Seeking Alpha’s transcript archive lists CooTek earnings-call transcripts across 2018–2022, but no retrievable transcript text discussing crypto was accessible through the tool — Seeking Alpha transcript index

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Apr. 29, 2022, Form 20-F for FY2021: CooTek described itself as a mobile internet company and said CooTek Ads accounted for approximately 8%, 45%, and 13% of total revenue in 2019, 2020, and 2021, respectively — SEC FY2021 Form 20-F
  • Apr. 30, 2024, Form 20-F for FY2023: searchable filing text showed no matches for “bitcoin” or “blockchain,” while describing the business as online literature and mobile games — SEC FY2023 Form 20-F
  • Apr. 30, 2025, Form 20-F for FY2024: searchable filing text again showed no matches for “bitcoin” or “blockchain,” and continued to describe CooTek as a mobile internet company offering mobile applications — SEC FY2024 Form 20-F

Financial news / other:

  • Dec. 2, 2021, company press release carried by Nasdaq: CooTek said it was developing immersive digital content and interactive game products related to metaverse and was building a digital content copyright system based on NFT “cryptographic assets on blockchain” — Nasdaq press release
  • May 20, 2022, SEC Form 6-K: CooTek disclosed NYSE delisting proceedings, useful for confirming listing history but with no crypto-related business disclosure — SEC Form 6-K delisting notice

EMEMORY TECHNOLOGY

  • Rank: 112 | Expo: 0.64 | id_str: TW0003529004
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2021–2024 annual reports, 2021–2023 investor conference transcripts, company investor-relations event archive

Firm Overview

eMemory Technology Inc. is a Taiwan-listed semiconductor intellectual-property company focused on embedded non-volatile memory and hardware-security IP, including OTP, PUF, and related security subsystems for foundries, chip designers, and system applications. During the 2017–2024 sample period, the retrieved company materials describe eMemory primarily as a semiconductor IP and chip-security supplier rather than a crypto-native, mining, treasury-crypto, or crypto-financial firm.

Exposure Channels Identified

No mechanism category is supported strongly enough for a non-zero crypto weight. The clearest crypto-adjacent evidence comes from earnings-call language in 2021–2022 that eMemory’s PUF-based security solutions were being marketed for applications including “Blockchain,” and from a 1Q22 transcript discussing metaverse security, NFTs, and attacks on cryptocurrency exchanges. However, those references describe end-market use cases for security IP rather than eMemory directly handling digital assets, operating crypto financial services, mining, or providing infrastructure purpose-built for miners.

The exclusion rules matter here. Although eMemory mentioned “Blockchain” as one of several application areas for its hardware-security products, the evidence does not show a dedicated crypto business line, identified crypto customers, crypto-related revenue disclosure, or material crypto-directed infrastructure. The annual reports for 2021, 2022, 2023, and 2024 also showed no matches for “bitcoin” or “blockchain,” which weighs against assigning bizserv or infrastr on the basis of scattered transcript remarks alone.

Weight Assignment

unclass = 1.0 is the best fit. The company’s transcript evidence shows only incidental crypto-adjacent references within broader semiconductor-security presentations, not substantive exposure through treasury holdings, mining operations, exchange/custody/payment activity, crypto-specific services, or infrastructure materially directed at crypto workloads. Under the threshold principle, the scattered “Blockchain,” NFT, and cryptocurrency-exchange references are too peripheral and too weakly tied to revenue or assets to justify a positive mechanism weight.

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main borderline issue is whether semiconductor security IP used in blockchain-related applications could qualify as bizserv or infrastr. Here, the exclusion rules point against that classification because the retrieved sources frame blockchain as one among many application verticals for general security IP, without evidence that crypto is a material standalone business channel. Earnings-call transcripts were retrievable, so the classification is anchored in transcript evidence, but no transcript or filing located any explicit crypto revenue, crypto assets, or investor-base commentary linking the stock to crypto sentiment. None identified beyond that borderline application-language issue.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • May 12, 2021, 1Q21 investor conference transcript: eMemory said “Business activities of PUF-based security solutions are in progress in applications of IoT, industrial IoT, AI, Blockchain, FPGA, Data Processor Unit (DPU), Mobile Storage (UFS), and Automotive” — eMemory 1Q21 Earnings Call Transcript
  • Nov. 10, 2021, 3Q21 investor conference transcript: management said PUF-based solutions were in progress in “IoT, Industrial IoT, AI, Blockchain” and other applications, and described PUFcc as a “crypto co-processor IP” — eMemory Q3 2021 Results Earnings Call Transcript
  • May 11, 2022, 1Q22 investor conference transcript: management’s metaverse-security presentation said “Digital assets, such as NFTs, for example, exist on a Blockchain” and noted attacks where “cryptocurrency exchanges” caused individuals to lose crypto coins — eMemory 1Q22 Earnings Call Transcript
  • Feb. 8, 2023, 4Q22 investor conference transcript: eMemory discussed security revenue growth and said its “security subsystem SoC IP” included a “crypto engine,” but the discussion was about chip-security architecture rather than crypto-asset services — eMemory 4Q22 Earnings Call Transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • May 17, 2023, 2022 annual report: searchable PDF returned no matches for “bitcoin” or “blockchain,” while the report described eMemory’s business through licensing and royalty revenue from embedded memory and security IP — eMemory 2022 Annual Report
  • May 28, 2024, 2023 annual report: searchable PDF again returned no matches for “bitcoin” or “blockchain,” with business discussion centered on semiconductor IP and security solutions — eMemory 2023 Annual Report
  • May 21, 2025, 2024 annual report: searchable PDF returned no matches for “bitcoin” or “blockchain,” continuing to characterize the company as an embedded-memory and security-IP supplier — eMemory 2024 Annual Report
  • May 24, 2022, 2021 annual report: the shareholder letter reported NT$2,363.82 million of 2021 revenue from license fees and royalties tied to embedded memory and PUF-based security IP, without crypto-asset or crypto-service disclosure — eMemory 2021 Annual Report

Financial news / other:

  • Investor-relations archive covering 2017–2024: eMemory’s IR page lists numerous English-language quarterly investor conference transcripts across the sample period, supporting review of management commentary over time — eMemory IR Events archive
  • 2022 investor conference presentation: eMemory’s presentation included metaverse layers and listed “Blockchains” within decentralization infrastructure, but as a technology-context slide rather than a disclosure of direct crypto operations — 2022 Investor Conference Presentation

MARQETA A

  • Rank: 113 | Expo: 0.64 | id_str: US57142B1044
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.6, bizserv 0.4, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings-call transcripts (2021–2023), SEC 10-K filings (2022–2024), company press/blog disclosures on crypto card programs

Firm Overview

Marqeta, Inc. is a U.S.-listed card-issuing and payment-processing platform focused on modern card programs, embedded finance, and API-based payment infrastructure for customers launching debit, prepaid, and credit products. Within that broader payments business, the retrieved sources show a recurring crypto-related niche tied to powering crypto-linked card programs for customers such as Coinbase, Blockchain.com, and Fold. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

finserv applies because Marqeta directly enables crypto-linked payment card programs in which cardholders spend against crypto balances and transactions are funded in fiat at the point of sale; management specifically used Coinbase as an example where Marqeta sends an authorization to check the user’s crypto balance, and company materials describe Blockchain.com and Coinbase cards that convert crypto for purchases. (fool.com)

bizserv also applies because Marqeta is fundamentally a card-issuing technology and processing platform serving crypto firms rather than itself operating a crypto exchange, brokerage, custody platform, or proprietary crypto fund. Its 10-Ks describe the company as a cloud-based payment card platform and do not show bitcoin, crypto, blockchain, or digital-asset holdings/disclosures on the balance sheet, which argues against holders and supports a service-provider role around the ecosystem. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

The split of finserv 0.6 and bizserv 0.4 best reflects the evidence. Crypto exposure is real and repeatedly documented, but it appears as a minority use case within a much larger non-crypto payments platform. The heavier finserv weight is justified because Marqeta’s role goes beyond generic consulting or software: it powers live crypto-linked card/payment functionality where crypto balances are checked and converted for spend, which is closer to direct crypto-payment intermediation than to a purely peripheral business service. At the same time, the company is not primarily a crypto-native financial institution, so a meaningful bizserv share remains appropriate. (investors.marqeta.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The key borderline issue is whether Marqeta should be coded entirely as finserv or split with bizserv. The exclusion rules cut both ways: Marqeta does directly enable crypto-linked card spending, but the sources do not show Marqeta itself custodying customer crypto or running a crypto exchange. The transcript anchor is available, but crypto is discussed as an illustrative customer use case rather than a separately disclosed segment, so exact crypto revenue share is not determinable and is therefore coded conservatively. No evidence located tied the stock’s investor base directly to crypto sentiment, so invbase = 0. (fool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Mar. 9, 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: management said, “Coinbase users can swipe a Marqeta-powered card at the point of sale, and we send an authorization for notification to Coinbase to check the user's crypto balance” — Motley Fool transcript snippet in search result (fool.com)
  • Aug. 8, 2023, Q2 2023 earnings call transcript: management described Marqeta’s growth as concentrated in financial services, BNPL, and on-demand delivery, without separate crypto segment disclosure — Marqeta Q2 2023 Earnings Call Transcript (fool.com)
  • Feb. 28, 2023, Q4 2022 earnings call transcript: management discussed TPV growth, Powered by Marqeta, and value-added services, but did not break out crypto as a core reporting segment — Marqeta Q4 2022 Earnings Call Transcript (fool.com)
  • Aug. 7, 2024, Q2 2024 earnings call transcript: management emphasized cash, short-term investments, and broader platform growth with no evidence of treasury crypto holdings — Marqeta Q2 2024 Earnings Call Transcript (fool.com)

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Feb. 28, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K: Marqeta described itself as providing customizable payment card programs via open APIs and cloud-based payment infrastructure — Marqeta 2023 Form 10-K (sec.gov)
  • Feb. 28, 2024, 2023 annual report PDF: searchable filing returned no matches for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” “blockchain,” or “digital asset” — Marqeta 2023 Annual Report PDF (sec.gov)
  • Feb. 28, 2023, 2022 Form 10-K: audited annual filing supports Marqeta’s identity as a payments/card platform rather than a crypto holder or miner — Marqeta 2022 Form 10-K (sec.gov)
  • Feb. 26, 2025, 2024 Form 10-K: the filing continued the same payments-platform framing and did not surface crypto-asset balance-sheet disclosure in the reviewed materials — Marqeta 2024 Form 10-K (sec.gov)

Financial news / other:

  • 2020 blog post, Marqeta: Marqeta said it was chosen by Coinbase to power the U.S. Coinbase Card, which allows purchases with cryptocurrency converted to U.S. dollars and uses Marqeta’s Just-in-Time funding — Marqeta to power U.S. launch of Coinbase Card (marqeta.com)
  • 2022 blog post, Marqeta: Marqeta said it was working with Blockchain.com to power the Blockchain.com Visa Card, enabling cardholders to pay from crypto or cash balances and earn crypto rewards — Marqeta teams up with Blockchain.com to power their Visa Card (marqeta.com)
  • 2022 blog post, Marqeta: Marqeta described partner Fold’s debit card as enabling users to earn Bitcoin on purchases and referenced “additional crypto partners” — Meet Fold: On a mission to bring crypto to the masses (marqeta.com)
  • Nov. 9, 2022, company earnings release: Marqeta highlighted the new Blockchain.com Visa card as a program where users utilize available cryptocurrency balance to fund purchases in fiat currency — Marqeta Reports Third Quarter Net Revenue Increase of 46 Percent Year Over Year (investors.marqeta.com)

ONE UNITED PROPERTIES

  • Rank: 114 | Expo: 0.64 | id_str: ROJ8YZPDHWW8
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: company investor relations materials (2021–2024), annual reports (2022–2024), financial news

Firm Overview

One United Properties S.A. is a Romanian listed real estate developer and investor focused on residential, mixed-use, office, and restoration projects in Bucharest. Across the reviewed 2021–2024 investor relations materials and annual reports, the company is consistently presented as a green real estate developer rather than a crypto-facing operating business. (bvb.ro)

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto exposure was identified within the 2017–2024 sample period. The company’s investor relations archive provides multiple analyst-call recordings and reports from 2021 onward, but the available web-accessible materials reviewed here did not yield a transcript containing substantive discussion of bitcoin, crypto, blockchain, digital assets, mining, custody, exchanges, or crypto-related services. (one.ro)

The strongest documentary evidence points the other way: the 2024 annual report contains no matches for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” “blockchain,” “digital asset,” or “token,” while describing a conventional real estate development and rental business with real estate assets, residential sales, offices, and property management activities. A 2022 Reuters item likewise characterizes One United Properties as a green investor and developer of residential, mixed-use, and commercial real estate projects in Bucharest, with no crypto angle. (bvb.ro)

Weight Assignment

unclass = 1.0 is the appropriate coding because the searched evidence does not support any of the five substantive crypto-exposure mechanisms. There is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining activity, crypto financial services, non-financial services to crypto firms, or crypto-specific infrastructure provision during 2017–2024; accordingly, all mechanism weights are set to 0.0 and invbase = 0. (bvb.ro)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that One United Properties’ website primarily provides call recordings rather than text transcripts, and several recording files were not directly retrievable in this environment, so no verbatim earnings-call quote could be extracted despite clear evidence that calls exist in the IR archive. There are later web results about real-estate tokenization involving One United Properties in 2025, but those fall outside the required January 2017 to December 2024 sample period and were therefore excluded from the classification. Because the in-sample filings and IR materials reviewed do not show substantive crypto activity, the firm remains conservatively classified as unclassified. (one.ro)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 2021–2024 IR archive: the company lists multiple analyst call recordings for Q3 2021, 2021P, Q1 2022, H1 2022, Q3 2022, 2022P, Q1 2023, and H1 2023, but no web-accessible text transcript was retrieved in this session — One United Properties Investor Relations archive (one.ro)
  • Mar. 2, 2023, 2022P results call recording: recording file existed in the IR archive but direct fetch timed out in this environment — 2022P results call recording reference ()
  • May 16, 2023, Q1 2023 results call recording: recording file existed in the IR archive but direct fetch timed out in this environment — Q1 2023 results call recording reference ()

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Apr. 29, 2025, 2024 annual report covering FY2024: the report describes residential, office, retail, restoration, and property management activities and returned no matches for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” “blockchain,” “digital asset,” or “token” — 2024 Annual Report (bvb.ro)
  • Apr. 25, 2023, 2022 annual report listed in IR archive: annual-report materials are provided under the 2022 results folder, supporting the firm’s identity as a conventional real estate issuer rather than a disclosed crypto operator — 2022 Annual Results folder (one.ro)
  • Apr. 26, 2022, 2021 annual report listed in IR archive: archived investor materials again center on property development and financial reporting, with no surfaced crypto framing in the reviewed archive structure — 2021 Annual Results folder (one.ro)

Financial news / other:

  • Oct. 27, 2022, Reuters/SeeNews: One United Properties was described as “a green investor and developer of residential, mixed-use and commercial real estate projects in Bucharest” in coverage of a new residential complex investment — Reuters/SeeNews coverage (tradingview.com)
  • Mar. 2026 crawl of company IR page: the current archive shows the continuity of the firm’s reporting focus on financial results, conference calls, sustainability, and investor presentations, not crypto products or crypto markets — One United Properties Investor Relations (one.ro)

PEARLABYSS

  • Rank: 115 | Expo: 0.63 | id_str: KR7263750002
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: company IR earnings materials (2021–2024), company financial statements, company IR notices

Firm Overview

Pearl Abyss Corp. is a South Korean video game developer and publisher best known for Black Desert and, through subsidiary CCP Games, EVE-related titles. Across the reviewed 2021–2024 investor materials and financial disclosures, the company is presented as a conventional game publisher focused on live-service game operations, new IP launches, and investment activity through Pearl Abyss Capital rather than as a crypto-facing operating business.

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto exposure was identified within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period. Pearl Abyss’s investor-relations archive provides multiple earnings webcast notices and presentation decks, but the accessible materials reviewed here do not show substantive discussion of bitcoin holdings, mining, crypto trading, custody, payment services, token issuance, or crypto-directed infrastructure.

The closest in-sample reference is in the 3Q22 earnings presentation, which lists “CCP Games : Web3 / Blockchain biz” under growth strategy. However, this is only a brief strategic mention tied to a subsidiary initiative and is not accompanied in the reviewed materials by disclosed crypto revenue, digital-asset holdings, mining activity, exchange/custody operations, or any clearly material crypto service line. Under the project rules, that is insufficient to assign a non-zero mechanism weight.

Weight Assignment

unclass = 1.0 is the appropriate coding because the searched evidence does not support any of the five substantive crypto-exposure mechanisms. There is no evidence in the reviewed 2017–2024 materials of treasury crypto holdings, mining, crypto financial services, non-financial service provision to crypto firms at material scale, or crypto-specific infrastructure provision; accordingly, all mechanism weights are set to 0.0 and invbase = 0.

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that Pearl Abyss’s IR site mainly provides webcast links and slide decks rather than full text call transcripts, so no fuller management Q&A transcript was retrievable in this session. One earnings-slide reference to “Web3 / Blockchain biz” at CCP Games indicates some exploratory or developmental interest, but the available in-sample evidence does not show that this translated into disclosed, material crypto exposure by Pearl Abyss itself during 2017–2024. Because the threshold requires substantive and clearly evidenced exposure, the firm is conservatively classified as unclassified.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Nov. 9, 2022, 3Q22 earnings presentation: “CCP Games : Web3 / Blockchain biz” appears under growth strategy, but without disclosed crypto revenue, holdings, or operating segment detail — 3Q22 Earnings Presentation
  • Feb. 15, 2024, 4Q23 earnings presentation page: the IR page provides a conference-call webcast and slide deck, but no web-accessible full transcript was retrieved in this session — 4Q23 Earnings Presentation
  • Feb. 11, 2025, 4Q24 earnings presentation covering FY2024: the deck discusses live services, new IP, and “Others : PAC (Pearl Abyss Capital)” in revenue breakdown, with no surfaced crypto terminology in the deck text — 4Q24 Earnings Presentation

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • FY2022–FY2024 financial information page: the company reports total assets, revenues, profits, and cash flows, but reviewed web text returned no matches for “crypto,” “digital asset,” or “bitcoin” — Pearl Abyss Financial Info
  • Apr. 2024, 1Q24 earnings presentation: the deck focuses on Black Desert, EVE, and new IP development, with no surfaced blockchain or crypto references in the reviewed text — 1Q24 Earnings Presentation
  • Nov. 18, 2024, IR release: the company’s IR book for November 2024 was posted through the IR archive, supporting the firm’s identity as a mainstream game publisher and investor-relations issuer rather than a disclosed crypto operator — IR Presentation (Nov. 2024)

Financial news / other:

  • Feb. 15, 2022, CNBC: Pearl Abyss was mentioned as a constituent in South Korean metaverse-themed ETFs, reflecting metaverse/gaming positioning rather than direct crypto-business exposure — CNBC metaverse ETF coverage
  • Company IR notices archive, accessed March 2026: the archive shows regular webcast notices for quarterly earnings presentations in 2024 and 2025, but no standalone crypto-focused IR framing was surfaced in the reviewed notices — Pearl Abyss IR Notices

BNY MELLON STRATEGIC MUNICIPALS

  • Rank: 116 | Expo: 0.62 | id_str: US05588W1080
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: fund annual reports / SEC N-CSR filings (2024–2025), BNY Investments fund overview page

Firm Overview

BNY Mellon Strategic Municipals, Inc. is a NYSE-listed closed-end municipal bond fund managed within BNY’s investment platform, with ticker LEO and a mandate centered on tax-exempt municipal securities. Across the reviewed fund materials, it is presented as a conventional municipal fixed-income vehicle rather than a crypto-facing operating company or digital-asset fund. (bny.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto exposure was identified for this specific issuer within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period. The fund overview states that it seeks monthly federal tax-exempt income by investing primarily in high quality and high yielding municipal bonds, and the annual report states that the fund invests primarily in municipal securities. (bny.com)

The reviewed SEC and fund-report text returned no matches for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” or “digital asset,” and the annual report’s fair-value disclosures categorize the portfolio as municipal securities rather than crypto-related holdings or services.
