Although parent-level BNY Mellon has broader digital-asset initiatives, those are not evidence of crypto exposure for this separate municipal bond fund and are excluded under the firm-specific rules. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

unclass = 1.0 is the appropriate coding because the searched evidence supports none of the five substantive crypto-exposure mechanisms for this fund. There is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining, crypto financial services, non-financial servicing of crypto firms, or crypto-directed infrastructure at the fund level, and no evidence that its investor base is explicitly treated as a crypto proxy; accordingly all mechanism weights are 0.0 and invbase = 0. (bny.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that closed-end funds such as this one generally do not have standard corporate earnings-call transcripts, and no firm-specific earnings-call transcript was retrievable in this session. However, because the issuer’s own annual reports and SEC shareholder reports clearly describe a municipal-bond portfolio and show no surfaced crypto terminology, the absence of a transcript does not create a positive crypto classification; under the project threshold, the fund remains conservatively unclassified. (bny.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • No firm-specific earnings-call transcript retrievable in the reviewed session; this issuer appears to report through shareholder reports and fund literature rather than standard operating-company earnings calls — SEC N-CSR annual report filing
  • Sept. 30, 2025 annual report: “The fund invests primarily in municipal securities” — BNY Mellon Strategic Municipals Annual Report

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Sept. 30, 2024, Form N-CSR: the filing discusses fund performance versus the Bloomberg U.S. Municipal Bond Index and contains no surfaced matches for bitcoin, crypto, or digital assets in the reviewed text — SEC Form N-CSR
  • Sept. 30, 2025 annual report: the valuation table lists investments as “Municipal Securities” and does not identify any crypto asset line items — Annual Report PDF
  • Current fund overview page, accessed March 2026: the fund states it seeks tax-exempt dividend income by investing primarily in high quality and high yielding municipal bonds — BNY Investments Fund Overview

Financial news / other:

  • No firm-specific financial-news evidence within the sample period was found indicating that BNY Mellon Strategic Municipals itself held crypto, mined crypto, offered crypto financial services, or was treated as a crypto equity proxy in the market during 2017–2024 — BNY Investments issuer page

UP FINTECH HOLDING ADR A 1:15

  • Rank: 117 | Expo: 0.61 | id_str: US91531W1062
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcript (Q4 2024), 2024 Form 20-F, company 2024 Hong Kong virtual-asset press release, financial news coverage

Firm Overview

UP Fintech Holding Ltd, listed on Nasdaq through ADSs under ticker TIGR and operating the Tiger Brokers / Tiger Trade platform, is an online brokerage focused on global investing for retail and professional clients across Asian and international markets. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, its crypto exposure appears through regulated virtual-asset brokerage functionality added to its Hong Kong platform rather than through mining or treasury coin accumulation. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports finserv exposure. In January 2024 the company announced that its Hong Kong subsidiary’s Type 1 license had been upgraded to include virtual-asset dealing, enabling eligible clients to incorporate cryptocurrency trading into portfolios on Tiger Trade, and external coverage states this expanded into retail access for Bitcoin and Ethereum in Hong Kong during 2024. (ir.itigerup.com)

The transcript anchor is the March 18, 2025 call covering Q4 2024, which is within the permissible evidence set because it discusses full-year 2024 operations; management stated that “in late January, our cryptocurrency platform, YAX (Hong Kong) Limited, received official approval from the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (HKSFC), becoming a licensed virtual asset trading platform (VATP) in Hong Kong.” That language indicates direct crypto brokerage/platform activity, fitting crypto financial services rather than mere ancillary business support. (seekingalpha.com)

There is no surfaced evidence that UP Fintech mined crypto, held digital assets as a treasury strategy, or primarily supplied mining/datacenter infrastructure. The investor-base amplifier is coded 1 because management and market coverage explicitly frame investor demand for virtual assets as relevant to the firm, and the stock is covered in market media as a crypto-enabled brokerage proxy. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

finserv = 1.0 is the most defensible assignment because the identified crypto linkage is entirely through brokerage / trading-platform services that directly enable customers to trade virtual assets. No source reviewed within 2017–2024 supports allocating weight to holders, miners, bizserv, or infrastr, and unclass is therefore 0.0. RevShare = 1 is used conservatively because crypto-related revenue is evidenced as present but appears peripheral relative to the company’s much larger core brokerage, commission, and interest-related income base; AssetShare = 0 because no meaningful balance-sheet crypto-asset position was evidenced in the reviewed filings. (seekingalpha.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that accessible transcript retrieval was uneven across older years, so this classification is anchored mainly by the Q4 2024 / FY2024 call and corroborating 2024 filing and press/news sources rather than a broader 2019–2024 transcript set. Also, the reviewed 20-F excerpt did not surface a quantified crypto revenue line, so the revenue-materiality coding remains conservative rather than exact. The subtitle line shows Expo blank because no expo_firm value was provided in the prompt and the instructions forbid using other fields as a substitute.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 18, 2025, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript: “in late January, our cryptocurrency platform, YAX (Hong Kong) Limited, received official approval from the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (HKSFC), becoming a licensed virtual asset trading platform (VATP) in Hong Kong” — Seeking Alpha transcript
  • March 18, 2025, company FY2024 results release tied to the earnings call: management highlighted the same late-January VATP approval for YAX in Hong Kong — UP Fintech FY2024 results release

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • April 2025, Form 20-F for fiscal 2024: UP Fintech describes itself as an online brokerage serving global investors, supporting classification context as a financial-services platform rather than a miner or treasury holder — SEC Form 20-F
  • January 25, 2024, company press release: Hong Kong subsidiary upgraded Type 1 license to include virtual-asset dealing service for professional investors and said clients could incorporate cryptocurrency trading into portfolios on Tiger Trade — UP Fintech press release
  • 2024 follow-on offering/prospectus materials, surfaced in company-hosted filing text: the company described itself as among brokers with uplifted Hong Kong virtual-asset trading permissions for professional investors — Company-hosted filing text

Financial news / other:

  • June 18, 2024, Finance Magnates: Tiger Brokers obtained approval allowing Hong Kong retail investors to trade Bitcoin and Ethereum through Tiger Trade, with no custody fees disclosed in the article — Finance Magnates coverage
  • Market summary page, accessed March 2026: company disclosures are described as including digital-asset functionality in certain markets and Hong Kong virtual-asset deposit/withdrawal services — Stock Titan overview
  • March 29, 2023, FY2022 results release: core revenues were driven by brokerage and interest-related income, with no surfaced indication that crypto was a major revenue line at that point, supporting a low RevShare code — Seeking Alpha / PR release

ADVANCED MICRO DEVICES

  • Rank: 118 | Expo: 0.60 | id_str: US0079031078
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (Q3 2018, Q4 2018), 10-K filings (2017, 2018), company results releases, financial news

Firm Overview

Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. is a U.S.-listed semiconductor designer focused on CPUs, GPUs and related computing platforms for client, gaming, data center and embedded markets. Its crypto linkage during the 2017–2024 sample period came through demand for Radeon GPUs used by cryptocurrency miners rather than through direct crypto ownership, mining, exchange, custody or treasury activity.

Exposure Channels Identified

The relevant exposure channel is crypto infrastructure because AMD supplied GPUs that were explicitly used for cryptocurrency mining. AMD’s own filings and management commentary state that rising cryptocurrency prices created GPU demand in 2017 and that later weakness in the blockchain market reduced GPU sales, showing a hardware-supply relationship to mining demand rather than direct participation in digital-asset markets. There is no reviewed evidence that AMD held crypto on its balance sheet, mined coins itself, or operated crypto financial services. The evidence also points away from bizserv, since the company’s role was selling compute hardware used in mining, which fits the infrastructure exclusion rule more closely than ancillary services. (ir.amd.com)

Weight Assignment

infrastr = 1.0 is the most defensible assignment because all surfaced crypto exposure is tied to AMD’s sale of GPUs that miners used for blockchain workloads. The exposure appears cyclical and indirect, but it is still explicitly documented by management and filings as a source of demand for AMD products, which is sufficient for a non-zero infrastructure classification. RevShare = 1 is appropriate because crypto-related demand was meaningful at points in 2017–2018 but remained a minority of firmwide revenue; management described Q3 2017 blockchain-related GPU sales as a high-single-digit percent of overall AMD revenue and Q4 2017 as low-double-digit percent, while later periods were negligible. AssetShare = 0 because no evidence reviewed indicates a balance-sheet crypto asset position. invbase = 1 is warranted because mainstream financial coverage explicitly framed AMD as a stock affected by crypto-mining sentiment and management addressed crypto-driven investor concerns directly. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Accessible transcript retrieval was uneven: several Seeking Alpha transcript pages were partially blocked, so the classification is anchored mainly by Motley Fool transcript text for 2018 and corroborating AMD filings and press releases. The clearest crypto exposure is concentrated in 2017–2018 rather than later years, and by 2024 the reviewed materials treat crypto mainly as a historical demand-risk factor, so the classification reflects exposure within the full 2017–2024 sample rather than a strong 2024 operating dependence. The subtitle line shows Expo blank because no expo_firm value was provided in the prompt and the instructions forbid substituting other fields.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • October 24, 2018, Q3 2018 earnings call transcript: “third quarter 2017 blockchain-related GPU sales were approximately high-single-digit percent of overall AMD revenue, while blockchain revenue in the third quarter of this year was negligible” — Motley Fool transcript
  • October 24, 2018, Q3 2018 earnings call transcript: management said Q4 2017 revenue had included “blockchain-related GPU sales of approximately low double-digit percent of overall AMD revenue” — Motley Fool transcript
  • January 30, 2019, Q4 2018 earnings call transcript: “Fourth-quarter 2018 revenue did not include any IP-related revenue, and blockchain-related GPU revenue was negligible” — Motley Fool transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 27, 2018, Form 10-K for fiscal 2017: AMD stated, “We believe that the rise of cryptocurrency prices and introduction of new cryptocurrencies created a demand for our GPUs in 2017” — AMD 2017 Form 10-K
  • July 25, 2018, Q2 2018 results release: AMD guided that Q3 2018 would be “partially offset by lower sales of GPU products in the blockchain market” — AMD Q2 2018 results release
  • February 8, 2019, Form 10-K for fiscal 2018: AMD’s annual report emphasized core processor and graphics businesses without any crypto holdings or crypto-financial-services disclosure, supporting a pure infrastructure rather than holder/finserv classification — AMD 2018 Form 10-K
  • 2025 Form 10-K discussing 2024 operations: AMD still warned that GPUs had been used for mining digital currencies and that crypto-market volatility could affect product demand and forecasting — AMD 2025 annual report excerpt

Financial news / other:

  • April 26, 2018, CNBC: Lisa Su addressed investor concern that Ethereum mining had helped AMD revenues, showing explicit market treatment of AMD as a crypto-sensitive stock proxy — CNBC coverage
  • October 24, 2018, CoinDesk: coverage highlighted AMD’s statement that sales to crypto miners had become negligible in Q3 2018, corroborating the fading but real earlier exposure — CoinDesk coverage
  • March 1, 2018, CoinDesk: AMD’s SEC filing was characterized as warning that a drop in crypto-mining demand could hurt GPU demand, reinforcing the infrastructure mechanism — CoinDesk coverage

BANK NEGARA INDONESIA

  • Rank: 119 | Expo: 0.58 | id_str: ID1000096605
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.2
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 2
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: BNI 2018 blockchain press release, BNI 2021 governance/annual report material, limited-access 2021 earnings-call transcript listing

Firm Overview

PT Bank Negara Indonesia (Persero) Tbk, listed in Indonesia under ticker BBNI, is a large state-owned commercial bank whose core businesses include lending, payments, trade finance, remittance, treasury, and other banking services. Within the 2017–2024 sample, the reviewed crypto-adjacent activity is not direct cryptocurrency dealing but BNI’s use of blockchain technology in trade finance and remittance workflows.

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports a primarily finserv classification because BNI explicitly said it was using blockchain technology to improve trade finance and remittance products, both of which are core financial services rather than ancillary consulting or hardware supply. BNI’s own 2018 press release stated that blockchain use was intended to improve transaction handling and could significantly improve transaction and revenue in those banking products. (bni.co.id)

There is no reviewed evidence that BNI held crypto on its balance sheet, mined digital assets, operated a crypto exchange or brokerage, or provided crypto custody/investment products. The 2021 governance report references “blockchain platforms” as part of digital capability and partnerships, but this language is general and points to technology-enabled banking rather than direct crypto-asset intermediation. (bni.co.id)

Weight Assignment

finserv = 0.8 and unclass = 0.2 is the most defensible split. The positive evidence is meaningful but narrow: BNI explicitly tied blockchain deployment to trade finance and remittance, which fits financial services exposure because the technology was embedded in banking products, not sold as external business services. However, the evidence does not show direct handling of cryptocurrencies themselves, and accessible transcript text was very limited, so a residual unclass weight is appropriate to reflect that the firm is better described as blockchain-enabled banking than as a clear crypto-financial-services pure play. RevShare = 1 is appropriate because management said blockchain could improve transaction and revenue in these businesses, but no source reviewed indicates crypto/blockchain revenue was a major share of total bank revenue. AssetShare = 0 because no balance-sheet crypto holdings were evidenced. (bni.co.id)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Transcript retrieval was weak. An earnings-call transcript for October 25, 2021 was identifiable, but the accessible scraped text was largely paywalled and did not yield a usable crypto-specific management quote, so certainty is capped at 2 under the instructions. The strongest evidence concerns blockchain use in banking operations rather than explicit cryptocurrency products, and the classification therefore leans on the rule that direct financial-service deployment around blockchain-based transaction processes can count as finserv, while still acknowledging that this is not evidence of exchange, custody, or treasury-crypto exposure. No explicit evidence of crypto-focused investor-base spillover was found. (marketscreener.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • October 25, 2021, Q3 2021 earnings call transcript listing: “We are delighted to welcome you to PT Bank Negara Indonesia Persero Tbk Virtual Analyst Meeting on Financial Results” — MarketScreener transcript page
  • October 25, 2021, Q3 2021 earnings call transcript listing: transcript was identifiable for BBNI but accessible text was locked, limiting extraction of crypto-specific discussion — MarketScreener transcript page

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • May 14, 2018, company press release: BNI said it “utilizes blockchain technology to improve banking business performance for trade finance and remittance products” and described itself as the first bank in Indonesia to apply the technology — BNI press release
  • May 14, 2018, company press release: management said blockchain could improve document access, validation, and encrypted data exchange and “improve transaction and revenue of BNI significantly” in trade finance and remittance — BNI press release
  • 2021 annual governance report: BNI described digital capability building through partnerships using “API Management, blockchain platforms, and business process re-engineering” — BNI 2021 GCG annual report

Financial news / other:

  • BNI investor reports portal shows annual reports across the sample period 2017–2024, supporting firm identification and review of company disclosure history — BNI financial reports portal
  • Independent market coverage identified a BBNI analyst-meeting transcript for October 2021, but no open-access crypto quote was retrievable from the transcript text — MarketScreener transcript page

WHERE FOOD COMES FROM

  • Rank: 120 | Expo: 0.57 | id_str: US96327X2009
  • Weight summary: holders 1.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: 2023 and 2024 Form 10-K filings, Q4 2024 earnings call / earnings release

Firm Overview

Where Food Comes From, Inc., listed in the United States as WFCF, is a Colorado-based food verification and certification company serving North American agricultural and food supply-chain customers. Its crypto exposure within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample is not operational or customer-facing; rather, it stems from a small corporate bitcoin investment held on its balance sheet. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports a pure holders classification because management explicitly described bitcoin as an investment, reported fair-value gains from that holding, and the company’s filings state that its digital assets are included on the balance sheet due to the company’s investment in digital assets. The 2024 year-end earnings call said net income benefited from a gain in the fair market value of bitcoin holdings and referenced the original purchase amount and average acquisition price. (investing.com)

There is no reviewed evidence that Where Food Comes From mined crypto, operated an exchange, provided custody or other crypto-financial services to clients, or supplied hardware / datacenter infrastructure to the crypto ecosystem. The 2025 Form 10-K also states that the company “does not accept or receive digital assets for transactional purposes,” which reinforces that the exposure is treasury-style holding rather than operating use. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

holders = 1.0 is the most defensible assignment. The crypto exposure identified in both the earnings-call evidence and SEC filings is singular and direct: a deliberate bitcoin balance-sheet position that generated reported fair-value gains/losses. No evidence supports any second mechanism category, so no residual split is needed. RevShare = 0 because the bitcoin position did not generate operating revenue; it affected other income via valuation changes rather than core sales. AssetShare = 1 is appropriate because the most recent clearly evidenced crypto asset balance was about $654,000 at December 31, 2024 against a much larger overall asset base, making crypto present but clearly peripheral. (library.wherefoodcomesfrom.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is temporal scope in the accessible transcript evidence: the strongest explicit earnings-call quote located was from the February 2025 call discussing full-year 2024 results, rather than multiple transcript quotes spread across earlier years. However, the non-transcript filing evidence is strong and specific, and it indicates the bitcoin purchase occurred before year-end 2023, including a 2022 impairment and a 2023 carrying value disclosure. No explicit evidence of crypto-proxy investor-base behavior or crypto-driven retail trading spillovers was found, so invbase = 0. (library.wherefoodcomesfrom.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 20, 2025, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript: “Net income benefited from a $345,000 gain in the fair market value of Bitcoin holdings” — Investing.com transcript
  • February 20, 2025, Q4 2024 earnings release / call materials: company said net income benefitted from “a $345,000 gain in fair market value of our $178,000 investment in bitcoin” — Where Food Comes From earnings release PDF

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 15, 2024, Form 10-K for 2023: the company said digital assets or “cryptocurrency” are held as indefinite-lived intangible assets and disclosed carrying value of digital assets held of about $0.1 million at December 31, 2023 — 2023 Form 10-K PDF
  • February 15, 2024, Form 10-K for 2023: cash-flow statement disclosed “Purchase of digital assets” of $178,000 and an “Impairment of digital assets” charge in 2022 — 2023 Form 10-K PDF
  • February 26, 2026, Form 10-K covering 2024 results: the company said digital assets are included on the balance sheet due to its investment, that it “does not accept or receive digital assets for transactional purposes,” and that it uses a third-party custodian — SEC Form 10-K
  • February 26, 2026, Form 10-K covering 2024 results: Note 5 disclosed holdings of 7 bitcoin with cost basis of $178,000 and fair value of $654,000 as of December 31, 2024 — SEC Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • Seeking Alpha transcript index shows broad transcript coverage for WFCF across 2017–2024, supporting firm-specific transcript retrieval and period review — Seeking Alpha WFCF transcripts index
  • No reviewed financial-news source during the sample period showed WFCF being characterized as a primary crypto proxy stock or as having a crypto-driven investor base — Where Food Comes From IR news releases

AMC ENTERTAINMENT HDG. CL.A

  • Rank: 121 | Expo: 0.56 | id_str: US00165C3025
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.3, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.7
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: 2021–2022 earnings call transcripts, 2022–2024 Form 10-K / annual report disclosures, financial news on AMC’s retail-shareholder base

Firm Overview

AMC Entertainment Holdings, Inc., listed in the United States as AMC, is a large movie-theater exhibitor whose core business is theatrical exhibition and related food-and-beverage sales. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample, the firm’s crypto linkage appears only at the margin through acceptance of certain cryptocurrencies as an online customer payment option and through unusually crypto-adjacent retail-investor sentiment, rather than through treasury holdings, mining, or a core digital-asset business. (nasdaq.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The only reviewed mechanism category with direct support is finserv, but only weakly and as a minority exposure. Management explicitly said AMC introduced the capability to accept payments in bitcoin, ethereum, bitcoin cash, litecoin, dogecoin, and shiba inu through its website and mobile app, which fits the project’s definition of crypto-related payments activity. (nasdaq.com)

However, the same transcript evidence also makes clear that crypto usage itself was modest and that the broader business impact mainly came from enabling other alternative payment methods such as Apple Pay, Google Pay, and PayPal. AMC’s filings did not show crypto on the balance sheet, and the 2022 annual-report disclosure stated that the company did not hold any cryptocurrency in 2021 or 2022. That leaves most of the firm economically outside the six substantive crypto mechanisms, so a large residual unclass weight is appropriate. (fool.com)

AMC also qualifies for invbase = 1. Management repeatedly emphasized its unusually large retail-shareholder base, and outside financial coverage characterized AMC as a meme-stock favorite driven by retail investors; the company’s crypto-payment and NFT initiatives were discussed by management as responses to shareholder interest, which is direct evidence of crypto-adjacent investor-base amplification rather than a separate operating mechanism. (fool.com)

Weight Assignment

finserv = 0.3 and unclass = 0.7 is the most defensible split. AMC did directly enable customers to transact with cryptocurrencies online, so a zero finserv weight would understate the evidence. But the activity was clearly peripheral: management said crypto usage was modest, the filings showed no crypto asset holdings, and no evidence indicated custody, exchange, brokerage, investment-product issuance, or material fee income from digital assets. RevShare = 0 is appropriate because no reviewed source showed crypto-related revenue as a meaningful disclosed revenue line, and AssetShare = 0 is appropriate because reviewed filings indicated no cryptocurrency holdings on the balance sheet. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is definitional rather than factual: AMC’s crypto acceptance was merchant-payment functionality, not a traditional crypto financial-services franchise like an exchange, broker, or custodian. Under the project taxonomy, that still fits best in finserv because the company directly accepted crypto payments, but only at a low weight. Another limitation is that later 2023–2024 filings do not appear to foreground crypto to the same extent as the 2021–2022 transcript period, suggesting the exposure faded and remained non-core. No evidence was found of mining, strategic crypto treasury holdings, or dedicated crypto infrastructure. (fool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • August 9, 2021, Q2 2021 earnings call transcript: “Since we had to do the IT programming to accept Bitcoin anyway” — The Motley Fool transcript (fool.com)
  • May 9, 2022, Q1 2022 earnings call transcript: “While crypto itself is modest in usage” and management said shareholder interest had put crypto acceptance on AMC’s radar — The Motley Fool transcript (fool.com)
  • March 1, 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: AMC had introduced the capability to accept payments in bitcoin, ethereum, bitcoin cash, and litecoin, with dogecoin and shiba inu to follow — Nasdaq transcript (nasdaq.com)

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 1, 2023, Form 10-K / 2022 annual report: AMC said it introduced the ability for consumers to pay with cryptocurrencies in U.S. markets during Q4 2021 — SEC annual report PDF (sec.gov)
  • March 1, 2023, Form 10-K / 2022 annual report: the filing stated AMC did not hold any cryptocurrency during 2022 or 2021 — AMC annual report PDF (d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net)
  • April 26, 2024, Form 10-K / annual report for 2023: the report highlighted the Hycroft investment but did not identify any crypto asset holdings or crypto segment disclosures — AMC investor relations annual report PDF (investor.amctheatres.com)

Financial news / other:

  • June 2, 2021, S&P Global: AMC was described as embracing a surge of retail investors and launching AMC Investor Connect to engage that shareholder base directly — S&P Global article (spglobal.com)
  • March 15, 2022, CNBC: AMC was described as one of the main meme stocks propelled by an army of retail investors — CNBC article (cnbc.com)
  • October 11, 2021, CNBC: AMC’s CEO publicly discussed buying AMC gift cards with dogecoin and other cryptocurrency through BitPay while the company worked toward broader crypto-payment acceptance — CNBC article (cnbc.com)

MEITU

  • Rank: 122 | Expo: 0.56 | id_str: KYG5966D1051
  • Weight summary: holders 1.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 2
  • Primary sources: HKEX voluntary announcements and annual reports (2021–2023), 2020 annual results announcement covering March 2021 subsequent events, company investor materials

Firm Overview

Meitu, Inc. is a Hong Kong-listed Chinese technology company best known for image and beauty applications, photo-editing software, and related SaaS products. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, its crypto exposure came not from operating a crypto business, but from a board-approved treasury allocation into Bitcoin and Ether alongside occasional discussion of blockchain-related strategic optionality. (meitu.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The clear exposure channel is holders. In March and April 2021, Meitu bought Bitcoin and Ether under a board-approved “Cryptocurrency Investment Plan” funded from existing cash reserves, and multiple filings describe the assets as purchased and held by the group as treasury/intangible assets rather than as inventory, customer collateral, or operating working capital. (www1.hkexnews.hk)

No evidence was found that Meitu mined crypto, operated an exchange, broker, custodian, payments platform, or other direct crypto financial service during the sample period. The company did mention evaluating blockchain applications and Ethereum-based projects, but the reviewed filings show the economically realized exposure came from balance-sheet holdings, not a developed service line, so finserv, bizserv, and infrastr remain at 0.0. (nasdaq.com)

invbase = 1 is warranted because management explicitly said the purchases were also meant to demonstrate to investors and stakeholders that the group embraced technological evolution, and financial media repeatedly framed Meitu as a public crypto proxy or “China’s/Asia’s MicroStrategy.” That investor-base channel appears orthogonal to the operating mechanism, which still sits squarely in holders. (nasdaq.com)

Weight Assignment

holders = 1.0 is the most defensible assignment because the source record consistently describes a treasury-style crypto investment plan: up to US$100 million of purchases funded from cash reserves, subsequent holding on balance sheet, impairment testing, and later disclosures of fair value and continued holding. RevShare = 0 is appropriate because no reviewed transcript-equivalent investor material or annual report disclosed crypto-generated revenue; the crypto effect ran through impairments and investment gains/losses, not operating revenue. AssetShare = 2 is appropriate because the 2023 annual report indicates Meitu still held about US$111.1 million of Ether and Bitcoin at year-end, which appears meaningful but not dominant relative to group assets, consistent with a moderate rather than major balance-sheet presence. (www1.hkexnews.hk)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is transcript access. Meitu’s investor site provides annual/interim earnings call replays, but a text transcript was not readily retrievable through the available search/scrape workflow, so the anchor quote is taken from the company’s annual results announcement/annual-report materials and the 2020 annual results announcement’s subsequent-events section rather than a full third-party call transcript; under the project rules this caps certainty below the highest level. (meitu.com)

A second ambiguity is that management linked the purchases partly to blockchain strategy and investor signaling, not only pure treasury diversification. Even so, the actual evidenced mechanism in filings remained passive crypto holding on Meitu’s own balance sheet, so the exclusion rules point away from finserv and toward a pure holders classification. (nasdaq.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 25, 2021, 2020 annual results announcement / investor results document: “These cryptocurrencies acquired are accounted for as intangible assets of the Group under the cost model” — HKEX results announcement PDF (www1.hkexnews.hk)
  • July 6, 2021, business update tied to interim results: “there are currently no plans to sell the same in the near future” — HKEX business update PDF (www1.hkexnews.hk)

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 7, 2021, voluntary announcement: Meitu said it bought 15,000 Ether and 379.1214267 Bitcoin under a board-approved plan allowing net purchases of up to US$100 million, financed by existing cash reserves — HKEX voluntary announcement PDF (www1.hkexnews.hk)
  • April 28, 2022, 2021 annual report: Meitu disclosed that during 2021 it purchased 31,000 Ether and 940.88523 Bitcoin for about US$100 million and carried them as cryptocurrencies/intangible assets — 2021 Annual Report PDF (corp-static.meitu.com)
  • April 26, 2023, 2022 annual report: as of December 31, 2022, fair values of the acquired Ether and Bitcoin were about US$37.3 million and US$15.6 million, with impairment losses recognized in 2022 — 2022 Annual Report PDF (corp-static.meitu.com)
  • April 25, 2024, 2023 annual report: during 2023 the group continued to hold about 31,000 Ether and about 940.4970 Bitcoin; fair values at December 31, 2023 were about US$71.23 million and US$39.91 million — 2023 Annual Report PDF (corp-static.meitu.com)

Financial news / other:

  • March 7, 2021, Nasdaq / CoinDesk syndication: Meitu’s purchases were described as treasury diversification, and the company said the move demonstrated to investors and stakeholders that it embraced technological evolution — Nasdaq article (nasdaq.com)
  • March 24, 2021, Coinbase Institutional blog: Coinbase said it was selected by Meitu for trade execution and custody on the treasury allocation into Ethereum and Bitcoin — Coinbase Institutional blog (coinbase.com)
  • December 2024 coverage summarizing the earlier positioning: Meitu was characterized as “China’s MicroStrategy,” reflecting crypto-proxy investor interest around the stock — Cointelegraph article (cointelegraph.com)

INFOBEANS TECHNOLOGIES

  • Rank: 123 | Expo: 0.56 | id_str: INE344S01016
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: annual reports (FY2021-22 to FY2022-23), earnings-call transcript (July 23, 2025) used only to confirm the wind-down of the earlier blockchain effort, company website/service pages

Firm Overview

InfoBeans Technologies Limited is an India-listed digital transformation and product engineering services company serving enterprise clients across cloud, automation, design, and software engineering. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, the only identifiable crypto-adjacent activity was a consulting/development push into enterprise blockchain, Web3, and NFT-related client work rather than any direct crypto asset ownership, trading, mining, or custody.

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports only bizserv. InfoBeans described blockchain/NFT and enterprise blockchain as part of its service capability mix, presented Web3 consulting and implementation among product-engineering offerings, and said it had landed its first enterprise blockchain client in FY2022-23. The company also later stated on an earnings call that it had invested in blockchain capabilities in 2023 and 2024 but exited because traction was insufficient, which still points to non-financial service provision rather than financial intermediation. Annual-report disclosures explicitly state that the company did not trade or invest in cryptocurrency or virtual currency during the year, excluding holders and finserv, while there is no evidence of mining or crypto-targeted infrastructure.

Weight Assignment

A full bizserv = 1.0 is the most defensible assignment because all substantive crypto-related references concern advisory, implementation, and software-development capabilities around enterprise blockchain/Web3/NFT use cases. There is no evidence that InfoBeans handled digital assets directly, held crypto on balance sheet, operated an exchange or custody platform, mined coins, or sold crypto-focused hardware/hosting. RevShare = 1 is appropriate because management and annual-report materials indicate some real but limited blockchain-related client traction, including a first enterprise blockchain client, yet there is no disclosure suggesting crypto-related revenue was more than peripheral. AssetShare = 0 is appropriate because the reviewed annual report explicitly says the company did not trade or invest in crypto or virtual currency, and no crypto assets were identified on balance sheet.

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is transcript timing. A directly retrievable transcript quote was available from July 23, 2025, just outside the formal January 2017–December 2024 sample window; it is used only to confirm that the blockchain initiative described in FY2022-23 and FY2023-24 had limited traction and was later discontinued. Within the sample window, the strongest evidence comes from annual reports and company service pages rather than multiple archived third-party earnings-call transcripts, so certainty is strong but not maximal. The evidence supports blockchain/Web3-related client services, but available sources do not quantify the exact revenue contribution, so RevShare remains a low ordinal estimate rather than a precise figure.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • July 23, 2025, Q1 FY26 earnings call transcript: “So we did invest in blockchain in 23 and 24... we found some traction, but not enough to retain a large team and continue to invest in that space. So we are out of blockchain as of now.” — InfoBeans transcript PDF

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • FY 2022-23 annual report: management said it had “already landed our first client in Enterprise Blockchain” and listed “Web3 Consulting, Implementation & Enterprise Blockchain” among offerings — FY2022-23 Annual Report PDF
  • FY 2022-23 annual report, financial statement disclosure: “The Company has not traded or invested in Crypto currency or Virtual Currency during the financial year.” — FY2022-23 Annual Report PDF
  • FY 2021-22 annual report: the company described investment in “Blockchain/NFT and AI based technologies” as part of capability expansion — FY2021-22 Annual Report PDF

Financial news / other:

  • Company investors page: InfoBeans identifies itself as a digital transformation/product engineering firm and hosts annual reports and investor materials for the relevant period — InfoBeans Investors page
  • Company service page: InfoBeans marketed “NFT marketplace solutions” built on blockchain technology, consistent with software/services exposure rather than direct asset handling — InfoBeans NFT Marketplace page

PAYPAL HOLDINGS

  • Rank: 124 | Expo: 0.55 | id_str: US70450Y1038
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2021–2024), Q4 2020/Q1 2021/Q2 2021 earnings-call transcripts, company investor relations materials

Firm Overview

PayPal Holdings, Inc. is a U.S.-listed digital payments and wallet platform operating PayPal and Venmo at global consumer and merchant scale. Within the January 2017–December 2024 sample period, its crypto exposure came through enabling customers to buy, hold, sell, convert, transfer, and spend cryptocurrencies and through the 2023 launch of PayPal USD, rather than through proprietary treasury accumulation, mining, or crypto-focused infrastructure. (stocklight.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports only finserv. PayPal explicitly stated in filings that certain state money-transmitter licenses cover its service enabling customers to “buy, hold, transfer, and sell cryptocurrency” from PayPal or Venmo accounts, and its 2024 10-K says customers can also “convert, receive, and send” supported crypto assets and use sale proceeds at checkout. (stocklight.com)

Earnings-call transcripts show management discussing crypto as a product line that generated transaction-fee and spread revenue, increased customer engagement, and fit PayPal’s wallet strategy, even if still immaterial relative to group revenue. The same sources also clarify that crypto held for customers is not PayPal’s own investment asset, excluding holders, while there is no evidence of mining, consulting-only service exposure, or crypto-directed hardware/datacenter infrastructure. (s205.q4cdn.com)

Weight Assignment

A full finserv = 1.0 is the clearest classification because all substantive crypto references concern direct financial intermediation: customer trading, custody arrangements through third-party trust companies, wallet functionality, checkout usage, and the commercially partnered PayPal USD stablecoin. RevShare = 1 is appropriate because management repeatedly described crypto as positive and strategically useful but explicitly “immaterial” to overall results in 2021, and no filing reviewed indicates crypto approached a moderate share of consolidated revenue during 2017–2024. AssetShare = 0 is appropriate because the firm’s filings state that cryptocurrencies held on behalf of customers are not PayPal’s assets on a treasury-investment basis; the reported safeguarding asset is matched by a safeguarding liability and reflects customer crypto held for the benefit of customers rather than proprietary balance-sheet crypto exposure. (s205.q4cdn.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is balance-sheet presentation after the SEC crypto-accounting guidance: PayPal reports a crypto safeguarding asset and liability for customer assets it safeguards, which creates visible crypto-related balance-sheet line items in 2023–2024. Under the project’s rules, however, those assets back a financial product/customer service and therefore belong in finserv, not holders. No strong source retrieved within the sample period showed that PYPL equity traded as a primary crypto proxy or that management explicitly identified a crypto-focused shareholder base, so invbase = 0 is retained. (stocklight.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 3, 2021, Q4 2020 analyst call transcript: “This has been a very successful product launch... They’re coming to the wallet much more frequently, much more engaged with the platform.” — PayPal Q4 2020 Analyst Call Transcript PDF
  • February 3, 2021, Q4 2020 analyst call transcript: “Yes is the answer to your first question... [we’re generating revenue] on the transaction fees... as well as on spread.” — PayPal Q4 2020 Analyst Call Transcript PDF
  • May 5, 2021, Q1 2021 analyst call transcript: “if we look at the entirety of 2021... crypto is not going to be a material driver of our results.” — PayPal Q1 2021 Analyst Call Transcript PDF
  • July 28, 2021, Q2 2021 analyst call transcript: “what we’re seeing on our crypto platform is probably slightly better than what we expected but still immaterial to our overall results.” — PayPal Q2 2021 Analyst Call Transcript PDF

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 2025 filing for FY2024: PayPal said certain licenses cover services enabling customers to “buy, hold, transfer, and sell cryptocurrency” directly from PayPal or Venmo accounts; New York operations are under a full BitLicense — FY2024 10-K PDF
  • February 2025 filing for FY2024: the company said customers in certain markets may “buy, hold, sell, convert, receive, and send” Bitcoin, Ethereum, Litecoin, Bitcoin Cash, and PayPal USD, with assets held by third-party custodians for the benefit of customers — FY2024 10-K PDF
  • February 2025 filing for FY2024: PayPal reported crypto asset safeguarding liability and asset of $2.886 billion at December 31, 2024, versus $1.241 billion at December 31, 2023, tied to customer crypto assets rather than proprietary treasury holdings — FY2024 10-K PDF
  • February 2023 annual-report coverage: PayPal disclosed $604 million of customers’ crypto at year-end 2022 in its SEC filing — CoinDesk summary of annual report

Financial news / other:

  • November 2, 2020 investor relations event page: PayPal hosted Q3 2020 earnings materials shortly after the initial crypto rollout, confirming availability of archived transcripts in the sample period — PayPal Q3 2020 Earnings Call page
  • February 9, 2023 investor relations event page: PayPal’s Q4 2022 earnings-call page provides official transcript access for the later sample period and confirms continuing IR disclosure around the business — PayPal Q4 2022 Earnings Call page
  • November 1, 2023 Reuters pickup: news coverage noted that the SEC subpoena related to PayPal USD came amid broader regulatory scrutiny of crypto, corroborating that the firm’s crypto activity was viewed as part of its financial-services offering rather than treasury investing — Reuters reprint on Investing.com

TCR2 THERAPEUTICS DEAD - DELIST.02/06/23

  • Rank: 125 | Expo: 0.55 | id_str: US87808K1060
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2021–2022 10-K filings, March 6, 2023 merger/joint webcast transcript, 2024 Adaptimmune 10-K discussion of the acquired TCR2 business

Firm Overview

TCR2 Therapeutics Inc. was a U.S. clinical-stage cell-therapy biotechnology company focused on T-cell therapies for solid tumors before its June 1, 2023 all-stock merger into Adaptimmune. The retrieved sources consistently describe an oncology R&D business with no operating revenue and no identified digital-asset, blockchain, or cryptocurrency activity. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive crypto exposure channel was identified for TCR2 across the sample period. The company’s 2021 and 2022 annual reports describe a clinical-stage immunotherapy business, while the March 6, 2023 transaction webcast focuses on combining two cell-therapy pipelines and extending cash runway, with no reference to cryptocurrency, digital assets, blockchain operations, mining, or crypto-related services. Searches within the available filings and transcript returned no matches for “bitcoin,” “digital asset,” “blockchain,” or “crypto,” supporting an unclass assignment. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

A full unclass = 1.0 is appropriate because all retrieved evidence points to a pre-revenue biotech company engaged in cancer-cell-therapy research rather than any crypto-related economic mechanism. There is no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining, crypto financial intermediation, non-financial crypto services, infrastructure provision, or a crypto-linked investor-base narrative in the sources reviewed, so all other mechanism weights are set to 0.0 and invbase = 0. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

An earnings-call transcript was retrieved only in the form of the March 6, 2023 joint Adaptimmune-TCR2 merger/business-update webcast rather than a standalone historical TCR2 quarterly call transcript. That limitation does not affect classification because the filings and available transcript are uniformly non-crypto and the project rules assign NA certainty to fully unclassified firms. (adaptimmune.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 6, 2023, joint webcast transcript: “This is a stock-for-stock transaction, by which Adaptimmune shareholders will own 75% and TCR² Therapeutics shareholders will own 25% of the combined company on closing.” — Adaptimmune/TCR2 strategic combination transcript PDF
  • March 6, 2023, joint webcast transcript: “Treating solid tumors has been the life’s work of the exceptional employees at Adaptimmune and TCR².” — Adaptimmune/TCR2 strategic combination transcript PDF

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 2022, FY2021 10-K: TCR2 described itself as “a clinical-stage immunotherapy company developing the next generation of novel T cell therapies for patients suffering from cancer” — TCR2 FY2021 10-K
  • March 2023, FY2022 10-K: SEC filing metadata identifies TCR2 as annual-report filer for FY2022 with no crypto-related business description surfaced in the filing summary — TCR2 FY2022 10-K
  • March 2025, FY2024 Adaptimmune 10-K: after the June 1, 2023 acquisition, Adaptimmune stated that TCR² “did not generate revenue” in the relevant period and described it as a Boston-based T-cell therapy company — Adaptimmune FY2024 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • March 2023, merger coverage via SEC-filed presentation: management framed the deal around cell-therapy scale, pipeline overlap, and cash runway into 2026 rather than any digital-asset initiative — Rule 425 filing / presentation transcript PDF
  • March 2023, transcript listing aggregator: external transcript indexing confirms market coverage centered on TCR2’s merger with Adaptimmune rather than crypto activities — GuruFocus transcript index for Adaptimmune/TCR2

IDEANOMICS

  • Rank: 126 | Expo: 0.55 | id_str: US45166V2051
  • Weight summary: holders 0.3, miners 0.0, finserv 0.3, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.4
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 2
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 4
  • Primary sources: 2020–2022 10-K filings, Q2 2021 and Q3 2022 earnings-call transcripts, 2018 blockchain-strategy press release

Firm Overview

Ideanomics Inc. was a U.S.-listed company that by 2021–2023 was primarily presenting itself as a commercial electric-vehicle and charging-infrastructure group, while legacy disclosures still referenced earlier digital-asset and blockchain initiatives. The retrieved record shows meaningful historical crypto and blockchain activity in filings, but the available earnings-call transcripts in the 2021–2024 window emphasize EV operations rather than an active crypto business. (fool.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

Two crypto-exposure channels are supported, but both appear historical or residual rather than core by the later sample period. First, Ideanomics explicitly disclosed balance-sheet ownership of bitcoin and ether acquired through open-market purchases “to provide investors with indirect ownership” of those assets, which supports a holders classification. (sec.gov)

Second, filings and a 2018 company press release show direct involvement in blockchain and digital-asset businesses, including planned digital-asset acquisition, blockchain technology development, and security-token-related infrastructure, which supports finserv rather than pure treasury holding. However, the 2021 Q2 and 2022 Q3 earnings-call transcripts retrieved for the sample period contain no substantive crypto discussion and instead focus on mobility, acquisitions, and EV infrastructure, so part of the weight is left in unclass to reflect the gap between historical filings and later operating narrative. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

A mixed allocation of holders 0.3, finserv 0.3, and unclass 0.4 best fits the evidence. The holders weight reflects explicit treasury-style bitcoin and ether purchases and reported balances in the 2020 annual report; the finserv weight reflects the company’s stated digital-asset and blockchain-business strategy rather than mining or non-financial support services. The relatively large unclass share reflects that the accessible 2021–2024 earnings-call evidence is largely non-crypto and suggests crypto was no longer a clearly material operating channel by the later sample period. invbase remains 0 because no explicit evidence was found that management or financial media characterized the stock as a crypto-trading proxy during the reviewed sample. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The key limitation is transcript availability and content relevance. At least one earnings-call transcript was retrieved, satisfying the anchoring requirement, but the available 2021 and 2022 calls do not discuss crypto substantively, while a 2023 Seeking Alpha transcript page was inaccessible via direct fetch. That means the classification relies heavily on filings and older corroborating company materials, so certainty is capped at 2 under the project rules. The company also appears to have shifted away from digital assets toward EV businesses over time, making period-specific materiality difficult to pin down precisely within 2021–2024. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • August 17, 2021, Q2 2021 transcript: “We operate two distinct business units, Ideanomics Mobility, and Ideanomics Capital.” — Motley Fool Q2 2021 earnings call transcript
  • August 17, 2021, Q2 2021 transcript: “Our M&A and investment strategy has remained steadfast and has brought some incredible companies... into the Ideanomics Mobility family.” — Motley Fool Q2 2021 earnings call transcript
  • November 2022, Q3 2022 investor call transcript: closing remarks conclude the call without any surfaced crypto discussion, consistent with a later-period EV-focused narrative — SEC-filed Q3 2022 earnings call transcript exhibit

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 2021, FY2020 10-K: Ideanomics stated, “We are an early entrant in the Digital Asset market and one of the first U.S. publicly traded companies to be involved with Digital Assets and blockchain technologies.” — Ideanomics FY2020 10-K
  • March 2021, FY2020 10-K: the company said it “acquires Digital Assets through open market purchases” and listed 66.923 BTC and 2,674.235 ETH as of December 31, 2020 — Ideanomics FY2020 10-K
  • March 2021, FY2020 10-K: balance-sheet lines show digital currencies of $995,652 and total assets of $1,551,892 at December 31, 2020, implying crypto was a large share of assets at that date — Ideanomics FY2020 10-K
  • March 2023, FY2022 10-K: risk factors still referred to potential harm from declines in “the value of any cryptocurrencies we hold or expect to acquire for our own account” — Ideanomics FY2022 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • October 10, 2018, company press release: Ideanomics said its technology and infrastructure used “blockchain and smart contract for security token issuance and trading,” corroborating direct financial-services-style blockchain ambitions — Ideanomics/PR Newswire release filed with SEC exhibit
  • Seeking Alpha transcript index shows public-market transcript coverage for Ideanomics across 2021–2023, but accessible retrieved transcripts reviewed here were dominated by mobility/EV discussion rather than crypto — Seeking Alpha Ideanomics earnings transcripts index

JAZZ PHARMACEUTICALS

  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2024), earnings call transcripts (2020–2021)

Firm Overview

Jazz Pharmaceuticals plc is an Ireland-domiciled biopharmaceutical company listed on Nasdaq that develops and commercializes therapies in neuroscience and oncology. Across the reviewed 2017–2024 materials, the company narrative centers on drug sales, R&D, acquisitions, and balance-sheet management in pharmaceuticals rather than any crypto-related business line. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive crypto-exposure channel is supported for Jazz Pharmaceuticals. The reviewed earnings-call transcripts discuss product launches, revenue diversification, the GW Pharmaceuticals acquisition, debt, and pipeline development, but no direct discussion of bitcoin, blockchain, digital assets, mining, exchange activity, custody, crypto payments, or crypto-related infrastructure was found. (fool.com)

The 2024 Form 10-K likewise yielded no matches for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” “blockchain,” or “digital asset,” which weighs against classifying the firm in any of the project’s positive exposure buckets. Reuters and other financial coverage retrieved in the search results instead characterize Jazz in conventional pharmaceutical terms, with one notable cannabis-related acquisition of GW Pharmaceuticals, which is not crypto exposure under the study rules. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

A full unclass 1.0 assignment is most appropriate. The search covered the required sample period and included transcript evidence plus SEC filings, but all retrieved evidence points to a standard pharmaceutical operating model with no meaningful crypto holdings, mining operations, crypto-financial activity, non-financial crypto services, or crypto-targeted infrastructure. invbase is set to 0 because no evidence was found that management or financial media treated JAZZ as a crypto proxy stock. (seekingalpha.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

At least one earnings-call transcript was retrievable, satisfying the search protocol, but no crypto discussion surfaced in the reviewed transcripts. Some Seeking Alpha transcript pages for 2023–2024 were indexed but inaccessible via direct fetch in this environment, so the conclusion relies on accessible Motley Fool transcripts and SEC filings; however, given the complete absence of crypto terms in the available materials, this limitation does not appear outcome-determinative. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • May 4, 2021, Q1 2021 transcript: “our strong Q1 performance was driven by Xywav adoption and Zepzelca growth” — Motley Fool Q1 2021 earnings call transcript
  • November 9, 2021, Q3 2021 transcript: “52% of net product sales in the third quarter were generated from products which we have launched or acquired since 2019” — Motley Fool Q3 2021 earnings call transcript
  • May 5, 2020, Q1 2020 transcript: retrieved call content focused on oncology pipeline, contracts, and regulatory timelines, with no surfaced crypto discussion — Motley Fool Q1 2020 earnings call transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 28, 2025, FY2024 10-K: SEC filing search returned no matches for “bitcoin” — Jazz Pharmaceuticals FY2024 10-K
  • February 28, 2025, FY2024 10-K: SEC filing search returned no matches for “crypto” — Jazz Pharmaceuticals FY2024 10-K
  • February 28, 2025, FY2024 10-K: SEC filing search returned no matches for “blockchain” or “digital asset” — Jazz Pharmaceuticals FY2024 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • February 3, 2021, Reuters: Jazz agreed to buy GW Pharmaceuticals for $7.2 billion, adding a cannabis-based epilepsy drug to its portfolio, corroborating a pharmaceuticals/cannabinoid strategy rather than crypto activity — Reuters coverage via Investing.com
  • Seeking Alpha transcript index lists Jazz earnings-call coverage through 2024, but the directly fetched 2023–2024 transcript article pages were inaccessible in this environment — Seeking Alpha JAZZ transcript index

AUGMENTUM FINTECH

  • Rank: 128 | Expo: 0.53 | id_str: GB00BG12XV81
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: annual report (2024), interim report (September 2024), investor presentation transcript index (2025)

Firm Overview

Augmentum Fintech plc is a UK-listed publicly traded investment company focused on private fintech businesses in the UK and wider Europe. Within that broader fintech strategy, the reviewed 2024 disclosures show a defined but minority allocation to digital-asset and crypto-financial portfolio companies such as Gemini, Tesseract and ParaFi, rather than direct balance-sheet crypto ownership by Augmentum itself. (augmentum.vc)

Exposure Channels Identified

The relevant exposure channel is finserv. Augmentum’s own reporting describes portfolio companies that directly provide crypto exchange, custody, lending, DeFi investment, and related digital-asset financial services: Gemini “buy, sell and store cryptocurrencies,” Tesseract provides digital lending via regulated custody and exchange platforms, and ParaFi invests in DeFi protocols and participates as a liquidity provider. (augmentum.vc)

infrastr is not given separate weight because the company is not itself a hardware, datacentre, energy, or blockchain infrastructure provider; rather, it is an investor in fintech companies, some of which operate crypto-financial platforms. Although Augmentum uses the phrase “digital asset infrastructure” and calls Tesseract an “enabling crypto infrastructure,” the underlying mechanism for Augmentum’s listed-equity exposure is still ownership of stakes in crypto-financial service businesses, not provision of infrastructure by Augmentum itself. (augmentum.vc)

There is no evidence that Augmentum mined crypto or held crypto tokens on its own balance sheet as a treasury policy. The company’s balance sheet in the reviewed filings is dominated by “investments held at fair value” in portfolio companies plus cash, which supports classifying the exposure through investee financial-service businesses rather than direct token holdings. (augmentum.vc)

Weight Assignment

A full finserv 1.0 assignment is most appropriate because the crypto exposure evidenced in the 2024 annual and interim reports runs through investments in firms that directly handle, custody, lend, trade, or invest in digital assets. No support was found for holders, miners, or bizserv, and infrastr is better treated as descriptive of one portfolio vertical rather than Augmentum’s own business mechanism. invbase is set to 0 because the retrieved materials discuss shareholder discount and investor confidence generally, but no explicit evidence was found that management or financial media characterized AUGM itself as a primary crypto proxy stock. (augmentum.vc)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A fully open pre-2025 earnings-call transcript was not retrievable in this environment. A 3 July 2025 annual-results investor-presentation transcript page was indexed and confirms public transcript coverage for the company, but the transcript body was paywalled, so the classification is anchored primarily in the company’s 2024 annual and interim reports rather than a fully scraped historical call transcript; accordingly, certainty is capped at 3 rather than 4. (de.marketscreener.com)

There is also a classification boundary between finserv and infrastr because Augmentum labels part of its portfolio “Digital Asset Infrastructure.” Under the project rules, however, the listed firm being classified is Augmentum itself, and the evidence shows it is an investment vehicle with minority exposure to crypto-financial investees rather than a direct infrastructure operator. (augmentum.vc)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • July 3, 2025, investor presentation transcript index: “welcome to the Augmentum Fintech plc Annual Results Investor Presentation” — MarketScreener transcript index
  • July 3, 2025, investor presentation transcript index: transcript page was retrievable but full body was subscriber-locked, confirming public-market transcript coverage while limiting extractable quote depth — MarketScreener transcript page

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • June 25, 2024, Annual Report 2024: “We believe that the Company’s current exposure to the digital assets vertical, at 6.5% of net assets” — Augmentum Fintech plc Annual Report 2024
  • June 25, 2024, Annual Report 2024: Gemini “serving both institutional and retail investors” and benefiting from crypto ETF approval — Augmentum Fintech plc Annual Report 2024
  • November 26, 2024, Half Year Report: Gemini “enables individuals and institutions to safely and securely buy, sell and store cryptocurrencies” — Augmentum Half Year Report 30 September 2024
  • November 26, 2024, Half Year Report: Tesseract “provides an enabling crypto infrastructure to connect digital asset lenders with digital asset borrowers” — Augmentum Half Year Report 30 September 2024
  • November 26, 2024, Half Year Report: ParaFi invests in DeFi protocols including Compound, Aave, Uniswap, Synthetix and MakerDAO — Augmentum Half Year Report 30 September 2024

Financial news / other:

  • June 25, 2024, Investegate summary of annual report: Augmentum described itself as “Europe's leading publicly listed fintech fund,” corroborating that the firm’s own mechanism is portfolio investment rather than direct crypto operations — Investegate annual report announcement
  • November 26, 2024, company updates page: lists the Interim Report dated 30 September 2024 and annual-report releases, corroborating filing chronology within the sample period — Augmentum updates & announcements

EQUALS GROUP DEAD - DELIST.15/04/25

  • Rank: 129 | Expo: 0.53 | id_str: GB00BLS0XX25
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: company RNS announcement on crypto partnership (2021), annual reports/results announcements (2021, 2023, 2024), indexed earnings-call transcript coverage (2023)

Firm Overview

Equals Group PLC was a UK-listed fintech payments group focused on SME and corporate cross-border payments, cards, current accounts, and embedded payment solutions before its April 2025 delisting. Within the 2017–2024 sample, the firm’s core business was fiat payments infrastructure and services, but in 2021 it explicitly announced a customer-facing crypto exchange capability through a regulated partner.

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports finserv exposure because Equals explicitly stated in March 2021 that it had created the capability for customers to trade into and out of cryptocurrencies against fiat currencies and described this as part of a broader crypto and DeFi strategy. That is a direct financial-service mechanism under the project rules, even though the initial operating model was partnership/referral-based rather than proprietary exchange custody. The later 2023–2024 reports and results announcements reviewed here focus overwhelmingly on international payments, cards, banking, APIs, and payment rails, with no retrieved evidence of mining, treasury crypto holdings, or non-financial crypto support services. Accordingly, the crypto exposure appears real but peripheral rather than central to the group’s business model.

Weight Assignment

A full finserv 1.0 assignment is most appropriate because the only substantive crypto exposure found is a direct customer-facing crypto/fiat trading service announced by the company itself. No support was found for holders, miners, bizserv, or infrastr, and the absence of recurring crypto discussion in later filings suggests the activity was limited in scale rather than spanning multiple mechanisms. invbase is set to 0 because no explicit evidence was found that management or financial media characterized Equals as a crypto proxy stock or discussed crypto-driven shareholder flows.

Ambiguities / Limitations

The strongest crypto evidence is concentrated in a 1 March 2021 company announcement rather than recurring disclosure in annual reports through 2024, so materiality appears modest. A historical earnings-call transcript index for 12 September 2023 was retrievable, confirming transcript coverage, but the transcript body was subscriber-locked in this environment; therefore no open transcript quote specifically discussing crypto could be extracted, and certainty is capped at 3. Later annual and interim reports reviewed for 2023–2024 contain no retrieved crypto-specific discussion, which may indicate the initiative remained immaterial, was discontinued, or was subsumed within broader payments activity.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • September 12, 2023, MarketScreener transcript index: “Transcript : Equals Group plc, H1 2023 Earnings Call, Sep 12, 2023” — MarketScreener transcript index
  • September 12, 2023, MarketScreener transcript page: transcript coverage was publicly indexed but full body was subscriber-locked, limiting extractable quote depth — MarketScreener transcript page

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 1, 2021, company RNS: “provide crypto currency exchange services to both its B2B and B2C customer bases” — Equals Group plc RNS announcement
  • March 1, 2021, company RNS: “created the capability for its customers to trade into and out of crypto currencies versus fiat currencies” — Equals Group plc RNS announcement
  • March 1, 2021, company RNS: “intended to become part of a wider strategy which would include crypto and decentralised finance (DeFi)” — Equals Group plc RNS announcement
  • April 16, 2024, FY-2023 final results: company described as a fast-growing payments group with £95.7 million revenue and £18.7 million cash at bank, with no crypto segment disclosure — Equals Group plc FY-2023 Final Results
  • April 8, 2025, Annual Report 2024: management describes the business as B2B payment and transactional platforms with revenues led by Solutions and International Payments, with no retrieved crypto references — Equals Group plc Annual Report 2024

Financial news / other:

  • Investor relations archive, accessed 2026: results centre corroborates the chronology of annual reports and interim results across the sample period — Equals Group plc Results, Annual Reports & Presentations
  • Investor relations homepage, accessed 2026: confirms the company was acquired and delisted effective April 2025, helping disambiguate the entity from other “Equals” names — Equals Group plc Investor Relations

BIOATLA

  • Rank: 130 | Expo: 0.53 | id_str: US09077B1044
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2022), earnings-call transcript index coverage (2022–2024), company investor relations pages

Firm Overview

BioAtla, Inc. is a U.S.-listed clinical-stage biotechnology company based in San Diego that develops conditionally active biologic therapies, including antibody-drug conjugates and immuno-oncology candidates. Across the 2017–2024 sample reviewed here, the company’s disclosures center on oncology R&D, clinical trials, financing runway, and collaboration activity rather than any identifiable crypto-related business line or asset exposure.

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive evidence was found for any of the project’s crypto-exposure mechanisms. The retrieved 10-K materials reviewed for BioAtla contain no matches for bitcoin, blockchain, or digital assets, and the balance-sheet discussion is framed around conventional cash and cash equivalents plus biotech operating needs rather than treasury crypto holdings. Earnings-call transcript coverage for 2022–2024 was publicly indexed, but no retrieved transcript metadata or company IR material indicated discussion of mining, exchange/custody/payment services, crypto-focused business services, or crypto infrastructure. On that basis, the firm is best classified as unclass.

Weight Assignment

A full unclass 1.0 assignment is appropriate because the search across earnings-call transcript listings, SEC filings, investor-relations materials, and financial-news style transcript summaries yielded no credible evidence of crypto exposure through holdings, mining, financial services, non-financial crypto services, or infrastructure provision. invbase is set to 0 because no evidence was found that management or financial media treated BioAtla as a crypto proxy stock or linked shareholder flows to crypto-market sentiment.

Ambiguities / Limitations

Transcript pages for BioAtla were indexed on Seeking Alpha for multiple 2022–2024 calls, but the full transcript bodies were not openly retrievable in this environment, so no direct earnings-call quote could be extracted. However, because all corroborating company and SEC sources reviewed were non-crypto and no crypto-related references surfaced in targeted searches, this limitation does not change the unclass outcome. None identified beyond the transcript-access constraint.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 26, 2024, Seeking Alpha transcript index: publicly indexed BioAtla earnings-call transcript coverage for FY/Q4 2023, with no crypto-related indication in the listing snippet — Seeking Alpha BCAB earnings transcripts
  • August 1, 2023, Seeking Alpha transcript index: publicly indexed Q2 2023 transcript coverage, supporting that transcript records exist within the sample period — Seeking Alpha BCAB earnings transcripts
  • November 6, 2022, Seeking Alpha transcript index: publicly indexed 2022 transcript coverage, but full body was not retrievable in this environment — Seeking Alpha BCAB earnings transcripts

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 23, 2023, Form 10-K for fiscal year ended December 31, 2022: filing identifies BioAtla as a clinical-stage biologics company and contains no retrieved matches for “bitcoin,” “blockchain,” or “digital asset” — SEC annual report PDF
  • March 23, 2023, Form 10-K: “As of December 31, 2022, we had approximately $215.5 million in cash and cash equivalents” and planned to use cash for R&D and general corporate purposes, with no crypto-asset disclosure — SEC annual report PDF
  • March 23, 2023, Form 10-K balance sheet: current assets list cash and cash equivalents of $215.5 million and no crypto-related asset line — SEC annual report PDF

Financial news / other:

  • Investor relations homepage, accessed 2026: company IR describes BioAtla through earnings releases and conference calls tied to clinical and financial updates, with no retrieved crypto framing — BioAtla Investor Relations
  • March 27, 2025 release covering FY2024 results: company announcement emphasizes business highlights, oncology programs, and webcast details rather than any crypto activity, consistent with the broader disclosure pattern — BioAtla Q4/FY2024 results announcement
  • March 27, 2025, Investing.com transcript summary of Q4 2024 call: discussion centers on R&D expenses, workforce reduction, clinical programs, and cash of $49.0 million, with no crypto-related content in the summary — Investing.com Q4 2024 transcript summary

COGNYTE SOFTWARE

  • Rank: 131 | Expo: 0.49 | id_str: IL0011691438
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: Form 20-F filings (2024–2025), company product pages, earnings-call transcript coverage (2024)

Firm Overview

Cognyte Software Ltd. is an Israel-based, Nasdaq-listed investigative analytics software company serving mainly government and security customers with data processing, intelligence, and investigation tools. Within the 2017–2024 sample, the identifiable crypto linkage is not trading, custody, mining, or treasury holdings, but software used by law-enforcement and financial-crime investigators to analyze illicit cryptocurrency activity.

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports bizserv exposure. Cognyte explicitly markets a blockchain analytics product, BLINK, that helps investigators detect suspicious crypto transactions, deanonymize actors, and support confiscation of illicit cryptocurrencies, which fits the project’s non-financial crypto-services category rather than finserv. In its fiscal 2025 Form 20-F, the company also states that blockchain-enabled anonymous cryptocurrency transactions create investigative challenges and that it offers software using machine learning and blockchain analytics to accelerate financial-crime investigations. There is no evidence that Cognyte holds crypto on balance sheet, mines crypto, runs an exchange or custody platform, or provides mining/datacenter hardware, so holders, miners, finserv, and infrastr remain at zero.

Weight Assignment

A full bizserv 1.0 assignment is appropriate because the crypto connection identified in the source set is entirely through software and investigative services sold to customers confronting blockchain-based crime. The available evidence does not show direct handling of digital assets by Cognyte itself, nor any separate infrastructure or financial-services mechanism. invbase is set to 0 because no retrieved management commentary or financial-media coverage characterized CGNT as a crypto proxy stock or tied shareholder flows to crypto-market sentiment.

Ambiguities / Limitations

Publicly indexed earnings-call coverage for 2021–2024 was available, but full transcript access was limited in this environment, so the transcript quote is drawn from an openly accessible 2024 transcript summary rather than a company-hosted full script. The exact revenue contribution of blockchain analytics within total company revenue is not separately disclosed, so RevShare is coded as 1 based on the product being clearly real but evidently peripheral within a broader investigative-analytics business. No evidence of crypto assets on the balance sheet was found, so AssetShare is 0.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • April 9, 2024, Fintool transcript summary of Q4 2024 earnings call: management said Cognyte addresses bad actors’ digital footprint using “advanced analytics and AI-enabled solutions,” consistent with investigative analytics rather than direct crypto intermediation — Fintool CGNT Q4 2024 transcript summary
  • 2021–2024 transcript index coverage, Seeking Alpha: public index confirms recurring earnings-call transcript availability across the sample period, including June 22, 2021 through December 11, 2024 — Seeking Alpha CGNT earnings transcripts
  • November 2024 conference transcript summary, Investing.com: company discussion described financial crime in “the cryptocurrency environment,” supporting the existence of crypto-related investigative use cases in management discussion — Investing.com Cognyte conference transcript summary

Filings (10-K / press releases):
