  • April 2, 2025, Form 20-F: company states that “Blockchain... has presented a valuable opportunity for dark actors to make countless types of anonymous cryptocurrency transactions” and that it offers software using “machine learning algorithms and blockchain analytics” to accelerate financial-crime investigations — SEC Form 20-F
  • April 2, 2025, Form 20-F: Cognyte says it generates the substantial majority of revenue from government-agency contracts, indicating the crypto-related activity is part of a broader investigative software business rather than a dedicated crypto segment — SEC Form 20-F
  • April 2024 earnings release: company describes itself as a “global leader in investigative analytics software,” with no digital-asset holdings or crypto-financial-services disclosure in the release materials — Cognyte Q4/FYE24 earnings release exhibit

Financial news / other:

  • Company product page, accessed 2026: BLINK is described as a “blockchain analytics” solution that helps detect illicit crypto transactions and reveal identities behind them — Cognyte Blockchain Analytics product page
  • Company investors page, accessed 2026: investor materials describe Cognyte broadly as a software-driven investigative analytics company, with no indication of crypto treasury, exchange, custody, or mining operations — Cognyte Investor Relations
  • Company product page, accessed 2026: BLINK is framed as helping law enforcement independently of third parties such as cryptocurrency exchanges, reinforcing classification as non-financial service provision to the crypto ecosystem — Cognyte Blockchain Analytics product page

AUTHID

  • Rank: 132 | Expo: 0.49 | id_str: US46264C3051
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2022–2024 Form 10-K filings, 2024 earnings-call transcript coverage, company investor relations materials

Firm Overview

authID Inc. (formerly Ipsidy Inc.) is a U.S.-listed identity-verification software company focused on biometric authentication, fraud prevention, and digital identity solutions for enterprise and fintech customers. Across the 2017–2024 sample reviewed, the company’s discussed business activity centers on identity and transaction security rather than direct cryptocurrency operations.

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto-exposure mechanism was substantiated for authID within the project sample. The company’s SEC filings describe biometric identification, identity management, authentication, and electronic transaction processing, but retrieved 2022–2024 Form 10-K materials did not show evidence of crypto treasury holdings, mining, exchange/custody activity, or crypto-targeted infrastructure. A 2024 earnings-call transcript references “enterprise encryption and crypto” only in the generic cryptography sense and mentions one blockchain-based privacy integration, but that is too incidental and non-material to support a non-zero mechanism weight under the project rules. Accordingly, the firm is classified as unclass rather than bizserv or finserv.

Weight Assignment

A full unclass 1.0 assignment is appropriate because the source set does not document substantive crypto-related revenues, assets, products, or management emphasis tied to digital assets or blockchain markets. The limited references retrieved are generic to encryption/identity architecture and do not establish a clear exposure channel through holdings, mining, financial services, business services to the crypto ecosystem, or crypto-specific infrastructure.

Ambiguities / Limitations

A transcript quote was retrievable, but it did not anchor a substantive crypto classification because the “crypto” language referred primarily to cryptographic/public-key technology rather than cryptocurrency activity. The company may have adjacent technology relevance for privacy or digital trust, yet the retrieved evidence did not show material servicing of the cryptocurrency ecosystem as such. No evidence was found that authID was treated by management or financial media as a crypto proxy stock, so invbase remains 0.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 13, 2025, Investing.com transcript of Q4 2024 call: management said PrivacyKey leverages “public key technology, the baseline for all enterprise encryption and crypto,” a generic cryptography reference rather than evidence of cryptocurrency operations — Investing.com authID Q4 2024 transcript
  • November 2024, Fintool transcript summary of Q3 2024 call: management discussed biometric identity products, fraud prevention, and privacy features without identifying crypto-asset, mining, exchange, or custody exposure — Fintool authID Q3 2024 transcript
  • May 2024, Fintool transcript summary of Q1 2024 call: management described identity verification use cases across financial services, fintech, social commerce, and workforce authentication, but not cryptocurrency business lines — Fintool authID Q1 2024 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 30, 2023, Form 10-K for 2022: authID describes itself as an identity-verification company and the retrieved filing text contains no matched references to “blockchain,” “cryptocurrency,” or “bitcoin” — SEC 2022 Form 10-K
  • March 20, 2024, Form 10-K for 2023: filing materials reviewed similarly did not reveal crypto-asset holdings, mining, exchange, or custody disclosures — SEC 2023 Form 10-K
  • March 13, 2025, Form 10-K index for 2024: annual report filing confirms the company remained a software issuer with no obvious crypto-specific exhibit or filing-level disclosure in the retrieved materials — SEC 2024 Form 10-K filing index

Financial news / other:

  • July 18, 2022, Form 8-K: company changed its name from Ipsidy Inc. to authID Inc., confirming entity continuity for the sample-period review — SEC name-change 8-K
  • March 27, 2025, investor relations press release: authID describes itself as a provider of biometric identity verification and authentication solutions, with no indication of crypto-market operations — authID investor relations release
  • Investor relations site, accessed 2026: company materials emphasize identity verification, authentication, and fraud prevention rather than digital-asset products or services — authID Investor Relations

MIXI

  • Rank: 133 | Expo: 0.49 | id_str: JP3882750007
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.2, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.8, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: company news releases (2021–2023), FY2024 integrated report / financial statements, FY2024 Q3 earnings-call Q&A summary

Firm Overview

MIXI, Inc. is a Japan-listed consumer internet and entertainment company whose core businesses span social networking, mobile games, sports, and related digital services. Within the 2017–2024 sample, its crypto-linked activity appears as an adjacent growth initiative in blockchain gaming and NFT-based digital-content services rather than as a core operating segment.

Exposure Channels Identified

MIXI showed substantive crypto-adjacent activity through two explicit channels. First, it operated crypto-related infrastructure by joining the Oasys gaming blockchain as a transaction-validating “validator,” which is direct participation in blockchain network operation and fits infrastr. Second, it developed and operated the sports-focused NFT marketplace DAZN MOMENTS and disclosed plans to provide NFT services in Japan using Flow with Dapper Labs support, which supports a smaller finserv weight because the firm was directly involved in launching and operating NFT/digital-asset marketplace services. There was no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining, or a crypto-proxy investor-base narrative.

Weight Assignment

The 0.8 infrastr / 0.2 finserv split reflects that the clearest and most explicit company-stated activity was infrastructure-like participation as an Oasys blockchain validator, while the NFT marketplace activity appears real but more limited, project-specific, and not prominent in the FY2024 integrated report or financial statements. Crypto appears peripheral to the group’s much larger gaming, social, and sports operations, so no category merits a dominant pure-play assignment and RevShare is kept at 1.

Ambiguities / Limitations

A transcript-style investor Q&A source was retrievable for February 9, 2024, but it did not discuss crypto, NFT, or blockchain activities; the crypto classification therefore relies mainly on company press releases plus filings/reporting silence on materiality. The strongest evidence is from company announcements in 2021–2023 rather than repeated management emphasis across multiple earnings calls, which keeps certainty at 3 rather than 4. The NFT activities could also be viewed as borderline bizserv, but because MIXI described operating/developing NFT marketplace services and blockchain-based products rather than merely supporting third parties, finserv is the better fit under the project rules.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 9, 2024, FY2024 Q3 earnings-call Q&A summary: management discussion focused on game titles, costs, and MAU trends, with no crypto/NFT discussion, indicating such activity was not a highlighted earnings-call driver by this point — MIXI FY2024 Q3 earnings-call Q&A summary

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • January 14, 2025, consolidated financial results for six months ended September 30, 2024: MIXI reported total assets of ¥213,259 million and provided no identified crypto-asset line or crypto-segment disclosure in the retrieved statements, supporting low asset materiality — FY2025 2Q consolidated financial results
  • 2024 integrated report for fiscal year ended March 31, 2024: the retrieved report describes the group’s main businesses and IR activity without identified NFT/blockchain segment emphasis, suggesting crypto was peripheral rather than core by FY2024 — MIXI Group Integrated Report 2024
  • April 7, 2023, company news release: MIXI said it would join game-focused public blockchain project Oasys “as a validator” verifying transactions on the blockchain — MIXI joins Oasys as validator

Financial news / other:

  • February 4, 2022, company news release: MIXI and DAZN said MIXI would handle service development and operation for sports-focused NFT marketplace “DAZN MOMENTS,” with plans for marketplace functions enabling users to trade NFT content — DAZN MOMENTS NFT marketplace announcement
  • November 5, 2021, company news release: MIXI said it signed a basic agreement with Dapper Labs to create new businesses using the Flow blockchain and provide NFT services in Japan — MIXI-Dapper Labs Flow partnership
  • November 5, 2021, PR Newswire coverage quoting MIXI president: management said it hoped to transform entertainment and sports content into digital assets for the Japanese NFT market — PR Newswire on MIXI and Flow

PLUS500

  • Rank: 134 | Expo: 0.47 | id_str: IL0011284465
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: annual reports (2021, 2023, 2024), H1 2019 investor presentation transcript, Plus500 product/support disclosures

Firm Overview

Plus500 Ltd is an Israel-founded, London-listed online trading platform operator whose core business is offering CFDs and other trading products to retail and professional clients across multiple jurisdictions. Within the 2017–2024 sample, its crypto linkage comes through offering cryptocurrency-linked derivative products to clients rather than through mining, treasury holdings, or crypto infrastructure. (investors.plus500.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The relevant exposure channel is finserv. Plus500’s business model is to operate trading platforms and earn spreads and overnight funding charges from client trading activity, and its public customer-facing materials explicitly state that it offered cryptocurrency CFDs in at least some jurisdictions, while also noting regulatory restrictions for UK retail clients. (cdn.plus500.com)

There is no evidence in the retrieved materials that Plus500 mined crypto, held crypto on its balance sheet as a treasury asset, or supplied crypto-specific hardware/compute infrastructure. The crypto connection is therefore best classified as direct provision of crypto-linked financial trading products, which fits finserv under the project rules. (annualreports.com)

Weight Assignment

A full finserv weight of 1.0 is appropriate because the only substantive crypto exposure evidenced in the sample is Plus500’s role as a platform offering cryptocurrency CFDs, i.e. a financial product handled within its brokerage/trading service. The evidence does not support splitting weight into holders, miners, bizserv, or infrastr, and the annual reports do not indicate crypto assets on the balance sheet, so AssetShare is set at 0; because crypto appears as one instrument set within a much broader multi-asset platform rather than a dominant disclosed segment, RevShare is kept at 1. (plus500.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The strongest transcript evidence retrievable in the sample was the August 13, 2019 H1 2019 investor presentation transcript, which describes Plus500’s platform and business model but does not itself contain an explicit crypto quote; the explicit crypto evidence comes mainly from annual-report text in 2021 and customer-facing product/regulatory disclosures. Because repeated earnings-call discussion specifically quantifying crypto revenue was not retrieved, certainty is 3 rather than 4. The available filings also do not separately disclose cryptocurrency-CFD revenue, so materiality is inferred as peripheral relative to the broader CFD business rather than precisely measured. (cdn.plus500.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • August 13, 2019, H1 2019 investor presentation transcript: “Plus500 is no different to our peers, in that we act as a principal to the customer trade” and the business is “primarily designed to generate revenue through collecting dealing spreads and overnight funding charges from clients,” anchoring the firm as a direct trading-platform financial intermediary — Plus500 H1 2019 investor presentation transcript
  • August 13, 2019, H1 2019 investor presentation transcript: management said it had “added over 100 new CFDs to the platform,” consistent with a multi-asset derivative-product model into which crypto CFDs fit — Plus500 H1 2019 investor presentation transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2021 annual report: management stated that “crypto currency continued to move into the mainstream within the industry,” citing CME Ether and Micro-Bitcoin futures as part of the expanding market opportunity around futures and options products — Plus500 Annual Report 2021
  • 2021 annual report: the report describes Plus500 as a “multi-asset fintech group” and notes over 83% of group revenue was CFD-related, supporting classification as financial-services exposure rather than treasury/infrastructure exposure — Plus500 Annual Report 2021
  • March 24, 2025 posted FY 2024 annual report for fiscal 2024: no retrieved mention of crypto, bitcoin, or digital assets in the annual report text, supporting the view that crypto was not a separately disclosed balance-sheet asset or major standalone segment by the end of the sample — FY 2024 Plus500 Annual Report

Financial news / other:

  • Plus500 support/contact page, accessed 2026: the site states “Cryptocurrency CFDs are not available to UK Retail Clients,” which directly evidences that cryptocurrency CFDs were part of Plus500’s product perimeter subject to jurisdictional restrictions — Plus500 online support / contact page
  • Plus500 terms page, accessed 2026: the company again states “Cryptocurrency CFDs are not available to Retail Clients” for the UK entity, reinforcing direct provision of crypto-linked trading products rather than merely ancillary services — Plus500 terms & agreements
  • Plus500 Australia product disclosure statement, accessed 2026: the disclosure refers to the possibility that “a Cryptocurrency CFD trade” moves in the client’s favour, corroborating that crypto CFDs were offered as part of the group’s CFD product suite during the relevant period or its aftermath — Plus500AU Product Disclosure Statement

TATA CONSULTANCY SVS.

  • Rank: 135 | Expo: 0.46 | id_str: INE467B01029
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: TCS earnings-call transcript (Q2 FY2024-25), TCS press releases/product materials, integrated annual report metadata

Firm Overview

Tata Consultancy Services Ltd. is a large India-listed multinational IT services, consulting, and business solutions company. Within the 2017–2024 sample, its crypto-related exposure appears through enterprise blockchain and tokenization solutions sold to clients, rather than through direct crypto trading, custody, mining, or treasury holdings.

Exposure Channels Identified

The relevant exposure channel is bizserv. The retrieved materials show TCS offering blockchain-based software, consulting, and implementation services through its Quartz platform, including tokenization, digital identity, and other enterprise use cases, which fits non-financial support services to the crypto/blockchain ecosystem rather than direct handling of cryptoassets. (tcs.com)

The October 10, 2024 earnings-call transcript provides the required transcript anchor: management said that the “Quartz blockchain platform had 2 wins this quarter,” indicating active commercial deployment of blockchain-related products within TCS’s broader software and platform portfolio. The same transcript does not indicate mining, bitcoin holdings, exchange activity, custody, or crypto-backed financial products. (tcs.com)

Weight Assignment

A full bizserv weight of 1.0 is appropriate because the evidence consistently points to TCS as a provider of enterprise blockchain solutions and associated consulting/implementation services, not as a direct financial intermediary in cryptoassets and not as a hardware/compute infrastructure supplier. RevShare is set at 1 because blockchain/Quartz is clearly commercialized but appears to be a small part of a very large diversified IT-services revenue base; AssetShare is 0 because the retrieved sources do not show material cryptoassets on TCS’s balance sheet. (tcs.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is that the accessible annual-report PDFs could be found in search results but failed to open through the browsing tool, so balance-sheet confirmation relied mainly on the earnings-call transcript and TCS press materials rather than scraped annual-report text. In addition, some TCS blockchain offerings touch financial-services use cases such as tokenized securities and digital guarantees, but the retrieved evidence characterizes TCS as the technology/service provider rather than the entity directly issuing, custodying, or trading cryptoassets; accordingly, bizserv is preferred over finserv. Certainty is therefore 3 rather than 4. ()

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • October 10, 2024, Q2 FY2024-25 earnings conference call: “Quartz blockchain platform had 2 wins this quarter.” — TCS Q2 FY2024-25 earnings call transcript
  • October 10, 2024, Q2 FY2024-25 earnings conference call: management grouped Quartz within TCS’s broader “products and platform” portfolio, supporting classification as a software/services offering rather than direct asset exposure — TCS Q2 FY2024-25 earnings call transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 14, 2019, company press release: TCS said it was “developing solutions to drive scaled adoption of blockchain across industries” using Microsoft and R3 technology — TCS press release on blockchain solutions
  • March 14, 2019, company press release: TCS said it was developing blockchain platforms including “digital identity, track and trace, assets in common, asset monetization, and tokenization” — TCS press release on blockchain solutions
  • August 14, 2023, company press release citing Everest Group: TCS described Quartz as its “flagship IP solution in the blockchain space,” indicating a productized services/software offering rather than treasury or mining activity — TCS named leader in enterprise blockchain services

Financial news / other:

  • January 19, 2022, company press release citing Everest Group: Quartz blockchain solutions were said to be deployed across use cases including digitized bank guarantees and supply-chain tokenization — TCS leader in enterprise blockchain services
  • April 20, 2023, company press release citing Everest Group: TCS highlighted investments in “TCS Quartz for tokenized securities” and a “Smart Bond with blockchain trade processing model,” reinforcing a technology-provider role to financial-sector clients — TCS recognized as a leader in capital markets operations
  • 2024 Quartz product/award page, accessed via search: Quartz is described as providing technology and tools that harness DLT for organizations across industries, again supporting bizserv rather than direct crypto-financial intermediation — Quartz technology overview

DARLING INGREDIENTS

  • Rank: 136 | Expo: 0.46 | id_str: US2372661015
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (2022 Q4, 2023 Q4, 2024 Q3), 2023 10-K filing, company investor materials

Firm Overview

Darling Ingredients Inc. is a U.S.-listed consumer-staples-adjacent industrial processor that collects and repurposes animal by-products and used cooking oil into feed ingredients, food ingredients, and renewable-fuel products. Across the retrieved sources, its discussed business exposure is to rendering, collagen, used cooking oil, renewable diesel, and sustainable aviation fuel rather than to cryptocurrencies or blockchain-related activities. (darlingii.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto exposure channel was identified for Darling Ingredients in the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period. The accessible earnings-call transcripts reviewed from 2022 Q4, 2023 Q4, and 2024 Q3 discuss renewable diesel, feedstocks, collagen, and fuel-market regulation, but contain no references to bitcoin, crypto, blockchain, digital assets, mining, custody, exchanges, or tokenized products. (darl-p-001.sitecorecontenthub.cloud)

The 2023 Form 10-K likewise shows no matches for bitcoin, blockchain, crypto, or digital assets, and instead reports conventional operating segments of Feed Ingredients, Food Ingredients, and Fuel Ingredients. Company investor pages and quarterly-results archives also frame the business around circularity, renewable energy, and specialty ingredients, not around crypto markets or crypto-linked investor positioning. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

A full unclass weight of 1.0 is appropriate because the searched transcript, filing, and investor-relations evidence produced no substantive crypto references at all. None of the mechanism categories are supported, and there is no direct evidence that Darling Ingredients functions as a crypto holder, miner, financial intermediary, crypto-service provider, infrastructure supplier, or crypto-proxy stock. (edge.sitecorecloud.io)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A transcript anchor was successfully retrieved, but it did not contain crypto-related discussion; under the coding rules, that supports an unclass outcome rather than a positive classification. The review focused on accessible company-hosted transcripts and the 2023 SEC filing, and no contrary evidence appeared in targeted web searches of Reuters-style news queries or company materials. (edge.sitecorecloud.io)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • October 24, 2024, Q3 2024 earnings call: “our fuel segment, DGD margins remain challenged”; transcript discussion centers on renewable diesel and feedstocks, not crypto — Darling Ingredients Q3 2024 earnings call transcript
  • February 28, 2024, Q4 2023 earnings call transcript retrieved from investor archive; searches within the transcript found no matches for bitcoin, blockchain, or crypto — Darling Ingredients Q4 2023 transcript
  • February 28, 2023, Q4 2022 earnings call transcript retrieved from investor archive; searches within the transcript found no matches for bitcoin, blockchain, or crypto — Darling Ingredients Q4 2022 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 2024, 2023 Form 10-K: operating segments reported as Feed Ingredients, Food Ingredients, and Fuel Ingredients; no matches for bitcoin, blockchain, crypto, or digital assets — Darling Ingredients 2023 Form 10-K
  • Quarterly results archive page lists 2022–2024 releases, presentations, and transcripts focused on earnings and operations, with no crypto framing — Darling Ingredients quarterly results archive
  • Company overview describes the firm as transforming animal-agriculture and food-industry materials into feed, food, and fuel products — Darling Ingredients company overview

Financial news / other:

  • Search review of public earnings-transcript index shows extensive DAR earnings-call coverage across 2020–2025, supporting transcript availability but not revealing crypto-related topics — Seeking Alpha DAR earnings transcripts index
  • Company investor page presents Darling as a circularity and renewable-energy business, not a crypto-related issuer — Darling Ingredients investor page

INTERNATIONAL BUS.MCHS.

  • Rank: 137 | Expo: 0.46 | id_str: US4592001014
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.6, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.4
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call prepared remarks (2018 Q1, 2023 Q4, 2024 Q2), 2024 Form 10-K filing, IBM press releases/news

Firm Overview

International Business Machines Corporation is a large U.S.-listed enterprise technology company focused on software, consulting, infrastructure, hybrid cloud, and AI. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, the retrieved evidence shows IBM engaged with blockchain mainly as an enterprise software and consulting offering rather than as a crypto holder, miner, exchange, or token-asset balance-sheet business. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The strongest supported exposure channel is bizserv. IBM management explicitly discussed blockchain networks on earnings calls in 2018, and IBM later continued to market blockchain-related consulting and enterprise solutions such as Food Trust and a 2024 IBM Consulting/Casper Labs governance offering. These activities fit non-financial crypto-adjacent services because the evidence points to enterprise software, consulting, and traceability/auditability solutions rather than direct custody, exchange operation, token issuance, mining, or treasury holdings. (fool.com)

No evidence was found that IBM held bitcoin or other cryptoassets on its balance sheet, mined crypto, or operated a crypto brokerage/custody platform during the sample period. The 2024 Form 10-K contains no matches for “blockchain,” “bitcoin,” or “digital asset,” and the more recent 2023 Q4 and 2024 Q2 earnings materials reviewed are centered on AI, software, consulting, and infrastructure without crypto discussion. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

A bizserv weight of 0.6 is appropriate because IBM has explicit, but not core-to-firm, blockchain-related enterprise service activity documented in both earnings-call and company news sources. The remaining 0.4 is assigned to unclass because the evidence also shows that by 2023–2024 blockchain was not a prominent topic in reviewed earnings materials or the 2024 10-K, implying that any crypto-adjacent exposure was peripheral to IBM’s much larger software, consulting, AI, and infrastructure businesses. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

IBM’s exposure is to blockchain-for-business activity, not clearly to cryptocurrency economics in the narrower sense. Some blockchain products appear to have been scaled back or de-emphasized over time, including IBM Blockchain Platform support ending in April 2023 and the later earnings materials lacking blockchain discussion, so the classification is best treated as moderate peripheral bizserv exposure rather than a core crypto classification. There was no evidence of crypto-focused investor-base commentary, so invbase remains 0. (ibm.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • April 18, 2018, Q1 2018 earnings call transcript: “We’ve grown to over 50 active blockchain networks since the release of our IBM Blockchain platform” — The Motley Fool IBM Q1 2018 earnings call transcript
  • October 16, 2018, Q3 2018 earnings call transcript: “in the emerging area of blockchain, this quarter, our IBM Food Trust network for food safety went live” — Nasdaq IBM Q3 2018 earnings call transcript
  • January 24, 2024, Q4 2023 prepared remarks: management highlighted AI, hybrid cloud, consulting, and software growth; no retrieved match for blockchain in the remarks — IBM 4Q23 earnings prepared remarks
  • July 24, 2024, Q2 2024 prepared remarks: management discussed software, consulting, infrastructure, and AI; no retrieved match for blockchain or crypto in the remarks — IBM 2Q24 earnings prepared remarks

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 2025, 2024 Form 10-K: no retrieved matches for blockchain, bitcoin, or digital assets in the filing text reviewed — IBM 2024 Form 10-K
  • November 13, 2018, product announcement: IBM said its blockchain platform could be deployed on IBM Cloud, other clouds, or on-premises data centers — IBM Blockchain Platform now runs in multiple environments
  • April 2023 support notice: IBM stated IBM Blockchain Platform software would reach end of support on April 30, 2023 — IBM support notice on IBM Blockchain Platform

Financial news / other:

  • March 20, 2017, CNBC: IBM launched an enterprise-ready blockchain service aimed at business use cases, indicating ecosystem-service rather than token-holding exposure — CNBC on IBM blockchain service launch
  • January 11, 2024, IBM Newsroom: IBM Consulting and Casper Labs announced work on a blockchain-powered AI governance solution for clients — Casper Labs and IBM Consulting announcement
  • November 11, 2020, IBM Newsroom: IBM Food Trust was described as using IBM Blockchain technology and IBM Cloud for traceability and quality assurance — IBM Food Trust olive-oil announcement

FUTU HOLDINGS ADR 1:8

  • Rank: 138 | Expo: 0.45 | id_str: US36118L1061
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.2
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcript (Q2 2024), 2024 Form 20-F / annual report materials, company crypto product announcements

Firm Overview

Futu Holdings Ltd is a Hong Kong-based, Nasdaq-listed digital brokerage and wealth-management platform that operates the Futubull and moomoo trading apps. During the 2017–2024 sample period, its crypto relevance emerged late and primarily through adding regulated virtual-asset trading services for brokerage clients in Hong Kong and Singapore.

Exposure Channels Identified

The strongest supported exposure channel is finserv. Management explicitly discussed the launch of cryptocurrency trading in the Q2 2024 earnings call, framing it as a new product line within Futu’s one-stop investment platform rather than a treasury investment or infrastructure business. (fintool.com)

Company product announcements corroborate that Futu directly enabled client trading in spot BTC and ETH pairs through its brokerage interface and marketed the service as licensed crypto trading for retail investors in Hong Kong. That fits crypto financial services because Futu is providing brokerage/trading access to digital assets, while no evidence was found in the reviewed materials that it mined crypto, held bitcoin as a strategic treasury reserve, or supplied crypto-specific hardware or datacenter infrastructure. (futuhk.com; futuhk.com)

Weight Assignment

A finserv weight of 0.8 is appropriate because the evidence shows direct participation in crypto brokerage/trading by 2024, including management discussion on earnings call and formal product launches for end clients. The remaining 0.2 is assigned to unclass because crypto appears to be a newly introduced and still relatively small business line within a much larger multi-asset brokerage and wealth-management platform; management said crypto trading volume and client assets were “pretty small” versus the whole business. (fintool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Transcript access was limited by paywall/403 restrictions on some primary transcript hosts, so the classification relies on one successfully retrieved earnings-call transcript plus corroborating company materials. The reviewed SEC filing search output did not show bitcoin/digital-asset treasury holdings, but a full line-by-line scrape of the 2024 Form 20-F was not retrievable in the tool, so the asset-side assessment is based on the absence of matched balance-sheet crypto evidence in accessible materials. No explicit evidence was found that FUTU’s shareholder base traded the stock mainly as a crypto proxy, so invbase remains 0.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • August 20, 2024, Q2 2024 earnings call transcript: “One major product update is our recent launch of cryptocurrency trading in Hong Kong and Singapore.” — Fintool FUTU Q2 2024 earnings call transcript
  • August 20, 2024, Q2 2024 earnings call transcript: “clients trading volume of cryptocurrency and client assets are both pretty small in comparison to the scale of the whole business.” — Fintool FUTU Q2 2024 earnings call transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • April 14, 2025, Form 20-F filing notice for fiscal year 2024: annual report filed for year ended December 31, 2024; accessible search output did not surface bitcoin or digital-asset balance-sheet references in retrieved matches — Futu annual report filing notice
  • 2024 annual report page: Futu’s investor-relations site lists the 2024 Annual Report as part of its official reporting archive — Futu annual reports page

Financial news / other:

  • July 31, 2024, company announcement: Futu said it would launch licensed zero-commission crypto trading in Hong Kong on August 1, with BTC/USD, BTC/HKD, ETH/USD, and ETH/HKD pairs — Futu crypto trading launch announcement
  • Product page retrieved during review: Futu states that it offers virtual-asset trading through its Hong Kong licensed arrangement and is working to expand crypto offerings — Futu Hong Kong crypto product page
  • March 13, 2025, FY2024 results summary: company said the average deposit into crypto accounts in Q4 2024 nearly doubled quarter over quarter, indicating an emerging but still ancillary product line — Futu FY2024 financial results summary

MADISON SQUARE GARDEN SPORTS A

  • Rank: 139 | Expo: 0.44 | id_str: US55825T1034
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (Q4 2020, Q2 2022, Q4 2021–2022), 2022–2024 Form 10-K filings, company investor-relations materials

Firm Overview

Madison Square Garden Sports Corp. is a New York-based, NYSE-listed sports holding company whose principal assets are the New York Knicks and New York Rangers, along with related development teams and a training center. Across the 2017–2024 sample period, the reviewed materials show a business centered on ticketing, sponsorship, media rights, suites, merchandise, and team operations rather than cryptocurrency-related activities.

Exposure Channels Identified

No supported crypto exposure channel was identified. The reviewed earnings call transcripts focused on attendance, ticket demand, sponsorships, media rights, playoffs, balance-sheet flexibility, and, in some periods, sports-betting-related marketing opportunities, but did not show Madison Square Garden Sports holding crypto, mining crypto, providing crypto financial services, offering non-financial crypto services, or supplying crypto infrastructure. (fool.com; fool.com; fool.com; fool.com; fool.com)

The company’s annual reports for fiscal 2022, fiscal 2023, and fiscal 2024 likewise did not surface matches for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” or “blockchain” in the retrieved filings, reinforcing that crypto was not a disclosed operating or treasury theme in accessible primary filings during the sample period. (MSG Sports annual reports page; 2024 Form 10-K PDF; 2023 Form 10-K PDF; 2022 Form 10-K PDF)

Weight Assignment

unclass is assigned a weight of 1.0 because the evidence search across transcripts, investor-relations filings, and targeted news/search queries found no meaningful or credible crypto exposure under the project’s mechanism definitions. Mentions of sports betting and esports do not qualify as crypto exposure under the coding rules, and no evidence was found of crypto-linked investor-base behavior specific to MSGS.

Ambiguities / Limitations

A transcript quote was successfully retrieved, but it did not contain crypto-related content; instead, it helped confirm that management discussion centered on sports operations and sports-betting commercialization rather than digital assets. Seeking Alpha transcript pages were blocked by 403 restrictions in the tool, so transcript evidence relies on Motley Fool-accessible calls plus company filings. No credible financial-news evidence was found characterizing MSGS as a crypto proxy stock.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 3, 2021, Q4 2020 earnings call transcript: “there's lots of ways to make -- to -- to everybody -- both -- both companies would benefit if there was a sportsbook” — Motley Fool MSGS Q4 2020 transcript
  • August 19, 2021, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: management discussed New York sports-betting competition and marketing demand, with no crypto business described — Motley Fool MSGS Q4 2021 transcript
  • February 3, 2022, Q2 2022 earnings call transcript: management highlighted ticketing, sponsorship, media-rights growth and debt paydown, with no crypto references — Motley Fool MSGS Q2 2022 transcript
  • May 5, 2022, Q3 2022 earnings call transcript: management focused on premium revenues, sponsorship upside and media-rights outlook, with no crypto references — Motley Fool MSGS Q3 2022 transcript
  • August 18, 2022, Q4 2022 earnings call transcript: fiscal 2022 results were presented around team demand and playoff run effects, with no crypto exposure described — Motley Fool MSGS Q4 2022 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2024 annual reports archive page: investor-relations site lists annual reports for 2017–2025, including the 2024, 2023 and 2022 Form 10-K PDFs used in review — MSG Sports annual reports page
  • August 19, 2024, Form 10-K for fiscal year ended June 30, 2024: business overview describes ownership of the Knicks and Rangers and related sports assets; retrieved text search found no matches for bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain — 2024 Form 10-K PDF
  • August 18, 2023, Form 10-K for fiscal year ended June 30, 2023: reviewed filing did not surface bitcoin or crypto references in retrieved search — 2023 Form 10-K PDF
  • August 19, 2022, Form 10-K for fiscal year ended June 30, 2022: reviewed filing did not surface bitcoin or crypto references in retrieved search — 2022 Form 10-K PDF

Financial news / other:

  • No credible 2017–2024 financial-news source retrieved in the search set identified Madison Square Garden Sports as a crypto holder, miner, crypto financial-service provider, crypto business-service provider, or crypto infrastructure supplier — Google-style web search results captured via tool

AVANZA BANK HOLDING

  • Rank: 140 | Expo: 0.43 | id_str: SE0012454072
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: company investor-relations reports pages (2021–2024), company press release on Safello crypto-asset LOI (2022), Reuters/Bloomberg Law coverage of the same

Firm Overview

Avanza Bank Holding AB is a Swedish listed digital savings and investment platform offering brokerage, funds, savings accounts, mortgages, and pension products. Across the reviewed 2017–2024 investor-relations materials, Avanza appears primarily as a mainstream retail savings and brokerage platform rather than a disclosed crypto-focused operating business.

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence does not support a non-zero crypto-exposure mechanism weight under the project definitions. The strongest crypto-related item found was a March–June 2022 exploratory process with Safello to examine whether Safello’s crypto-asset services could potentially be distributed via Avanza’s platform, but Avanza explicitly canceled that examination before launch and said it wanted to wait for clearer regulation. That aborted initiative is not sufficient to code finserv, because the rules require actual direct handling, issuance, trading, custody, or management of crypto assets rather than a shelved possibility. No evidence was found that Avanza held crypto on balance sheet, mined crypto, provided non-financial crypto services, or supplied crypto infrastructure during the sample period.

Weight Assignment

unclass is assigned a weight of 1.0 because the retrieved source set shows only a canceled exploratory partnership discussion around possible crypto trading, not an implemented crypto business line or treasury position. Company reports reviewed for 2021–2023 did not surface bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain references in the retrieved text, and no evidence was found of mining, custody, exchange operations, crypto-backed products, or crypto-focused investor-base commentary specific enough to code invbase = 1.

Ambiguities / Limitations

Avanza’s investor-relations site has a transcripts page, but the accessible page in the tool did not expose the underlying Modular Finance transcript links, so a direct earnings-call transcript quote could not be retrieved despite targeted searching. Because no implemented crypto activity was evidenced and the firm is classified as unclass, this does not affect the final coding, but it does limit transcript-level corroboration. A 2022 crypto exploration with Safello clearly existed, yet the company’s own press release states the process was canceled pending clearer regulation, so the exclusion rule against coding unimplemented possibilities was applied.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Avanza investor-relations transcripts page: the company states that presentations are held with each interim and year-end report and that transcripts are available via Modular Finance, but accessible transcript links were not exposed in the tool during retrieval — Avanza transcripts page

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 25, 2022, annual report release: Avanza described itself as a digital savings and investment platform with services in shares, funds, savings accounts, mortgages, and pensions; retrieved page search found no matches for bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain — Avanza publishes the Annual and Sustainability Report for 2021
  • January 20, 2023, preliminary financial statement: retrieved page search found no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain” — Avanza Bank Holding AB (publ): Preliminary Financial Statement 2022
  • January 19, 2024, preliminary financial statement: retrieved page search found no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain” — Avanza Bank Holding AB (publ): Preliminary Financial Statement 2023
  • June 16, 2022, company press release: “Avanza has decided to currently cancel the process” after examining a potential partnership with Safello for crypto-asset trading, and said it wanted to wait for clearer regulation — Avanza cancels the examination of the potential partnership with Safello regarding crypto assets

Financial news / other:

  • March 14, 2022, Reuters item: reported that Avanza Bank and Safello signed an LOI to examine a potential partnership for distribution of Safello’s crypto-asset services via Avanza’s platform — TradingView / Reuters: Avanza Bank Signs LoI With Safello For Partnership In Crypto Assets
  • June 16, 2022, Bloomberg Law: reported that Avanza canceled the potential Safello crypto-asset trading offer because the market was not sufficiently regulated — Bloomberg Law: Avanza to Cancel Potential Safello Crypto Asset Trading

SOFI TECHNOLOGIES

  • Rank: 141 | Expo: 0.43 | id_str: US83406F1021
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: company earnings-call transcripts (2022–2024), SEC filings / annual reports (2023–2024), company crypto-service update and Bloomberg/Reuters-style financial news on the 2023 wind-down

Firm Overview

SoFi Technologies, Inc. is a U.S.-listed digital financial-services platform offering lending, banking, brokerage/investing, credit cards, and technology-platform services. Within the 2017–2024 sample, its crypto relevance came from SoFi Invest’s retail digital-asset trading product and the associated customer accounts and safeguarded crypto assets, before the company wound those activities down in late 2023 and into early 2024.

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports finserv exposure because SoFi directly offered retail cryptocurrency trading through SoFi Invest / SoFi Digital Assets, handled customer digital-asset accounts, and disclosed digital-asset safeguarding assets and liabilities in SEC filings. Management continued to reference the impact of the crypto exit on product counts in 2024 earnings calls, confirming that crypto had been part of the firm’s financial-services offering rather than a mere exploratory initiative. The evidence does not support holders, because the relevant crypto assets were tied to customer-facing platform operations rather than a discretionary treasury accumulation strategy. No evidence was found of mining, crypto-focused non-financial services, or crypto infrastructure supply.

Weight Assignment

finserv receives 1.0 because the retrieved source set shows direct crypto financial intermediation: SoFi offered customers the ability to buy, sell, and hold cryptocurrencies, maintained digital-asset accounts as part of SoFi Invest, disclosed safeguarded digital assets on its balance-sheet-style disclosures, and later transferred those accounts to Blockchain.com when exiting the activity. The crypto business appears economically peripheral relative to SoFi’s much larger lending, banking, and broader financial-services platform, so the mechanism is clear but the materiality is modest.

Ambiguities / Limitations

The classification is straightforward on mechanism but less precise on materiality. Available filings and investor materials show that crypto was a real operating line within SoFi Invest, yet they do not provide a clean stand-alone revenue line for crypto trading, so RevShare is estimated conservatively at 1 rather than more precisely quantified. AssetShare is also coded 1 because disclosed safeguarded digital assets were present and measurable, but relative to SoFi’s total assets they appear small. No explicit evidence was found that SoFi’s shareholder base traded the stock primarily as a crypto proxy during the sample period, so invbase remains 0.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • August 2, 2022, SoFi Q2 2022 earnings conference call page: company IR page provides a transcript for the quarter, anchoring management-era coverage of SoFi’s operating businesses including Invest and digital-asset activity — SoFi Q2 2022 Earnings Conference Call
  • July 31, 2023, SoFi Q2 2023 earnings conference call page: company IR page provides a transcript during the period when SoFi still operated digital-asset accounts within SoFi Invest — SoFi Q2 2023 Earnings Conference Call
  • July 30, 2024, company transcript: management said products were up 43% year over year, “adjusting for our exit from crypto last year” — SOFI 2Q 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
  • October 29, 2024, company transcript: management said products were up 31% year over year, or 37% “when excluding digital asset accounts related to our transfer of crypto services in 2023” — SOFI Q3 2024 Earnings Conference Call transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • September 30, 2023, Form 10-Q excerpt: SoFi disclosed “Digital assets safeguarding liability and corresponding safeguarding asset” of $139.359 million and noted 17 digital assets as of September 30, 2023 — SoFi 10-Q for quarter ended September 30, 2023
  • January 29, 2024, Q4/FY 2023 investor presentation: SoFi stated, “We began to wind-down our crypto-related activities in November 2023” and that members could no longer create new digital-assets accounts while existing accounts were being closed — Q4 & FY 2023 Investor Presentation
  • 2024 annual report filing for fiscal 2024: SoFi stated that in the fourth quarter of 2023 it “transferred the crypto services provided by SoFi Digital Assets, LLC, and began closing existing digital assets accounts” — SoFi Technologies 2024 Annual Report
  • November 29, 2023, company update: SoFi said it had “made the decision to discontinue our crypto services by the end of this year” and offered members either migration to Blockchain.com or liquidation, while noting SoFi would no longer open new crypto trading accounts — An Update on SoFi’s Crypto Services

Financial news / other:

  • November 29, 2023, Bloomberg: reported SoFi was exiting crypto as banking regulators increased scrutiny and that customers could liquidate or move to Blockchain.com — Bloomberg: SoFi Is Exiting Crypto With Banking Regulators Stepping Up Scrutiny
  • November 29, 2023, PR Newswire / Blockchain.com announcement: described Blockchain.com as SoFi’s digital-asset partner for migration of SoFi crypto accounts — SoFi selects Blockchain.com as its first digital asset partner

DEUTSCHE BOERSE

  • Rank: 142 | Expo: 0.43 | id_str: DE0005810055
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: annual reports and consolidated financial statements (2021, 2023), analyst and investor conference-call presentations (2021, 2023), company press releases (2021, 2024)

Firm Overview

Deutsche Börse AG is a German exchange and post-trade market-infrastructure group whose core businesses span trading, clearing, custody, fund services, data, and analytics. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, its crypto exposure emerged through regulated institutional digital-asset trading, custody, and investment services, especially after acquiring Crypto Finance in late 2021 and launching Deutsche Börse Digital Exchange in 2024.

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports finserv exposure because Deutsche Börse, through Crypto Finance, Clearstream, and later Deutsche Börse Digital Exchange, directly offered institutional clients trading, custody, settlement, wallet, and investment access to digital assets rather than merely servicing crypto firms from the sidelines. Management materials explicitly framed Crypto Finance as a new asset-class expansion and as the basis for a regulated digital-assets ecosystem, while filings describe Crypto Finance as a provider of trading, custody, and investment services for digital assets. The evidence does not support holders, because the retrieved sources do not show a discretionary treasury crypto strategy by Deutsche Börse itself. No evidence was found of mining or of Deutsche Börse acting primarily as a crypto hardware, energy, or compute infrastructure supplier under the project’s infrastr definition.

Weight Assignment

finserv receives 1.0 because the crypto mechanism is direct financial intermediation throughout the evidence set: Deutsche Börse acquired a majority stake in Crypto Finance to provide trading, custody, and investment in digital assets; conference-call materials incorporated Crypto Finance into growth and M&A commentary; and by March 2024 the group had launched a regulated crypto spot platform for institutional clients. Although crypto is strategically meaningful, the filings indicate it remained economically small relative to the group’s overall multi-billion-euro revenue base, so the mechanism is clear while materiality is modest.

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity concerns scale, not mechanism. Deutsche Börse clearly operated crypto financial-services activities, but the available filings do not provide a clean standalone recurring revenue line for the crypto business across the full sample, so RevShare is coded conservatively at 1 based on disclosed acquisition-era and planning references rather than a precise 2023 or 2024 segment share. AssetShare is coded 0 because the retrieved sources show no meaningful group-level balance-sheet crypto holdings as an investment asset; references are instead to acquired businesses, goodwill, and service operations. No direct evidence was found that Deutsche Börse’s equity was widely traded as a retail crypto proxy, so invbase remains 0.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 10, 2022, Q4/FY 2021 analyst and investor conference-call presentation: management cited the “Acquisition of a majority stake in Crypto Finance, laying the foundation for building an independent, transparent, and highly scalable regulated ecosystem for digital assets” — Q4 and FY/2021 Preliminary Results Presentation
  • July 28, 2021, Q2 2021 analyst and investor call presentation: Deutsche Börse said Crypto Finance offered institutional clients brokerage, storage and asset-management services and that the deal would give the group exposure to digital assets, with expected 2021 net revenue of about €20 million — Q2/2021 Results Presentation
  • February 9, 2023, Q4/FY 2022 analyst and investor conference-call presentation: Deutsche Börse highlighted “Further expansion into new asset classes (e.g. Crypto Finance, 360X)” and listed Crypto Finance among M&A contributors to growth — Q4 and FY/2022 Preliminary Results Presentation

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 2022, Annual Report 2021: Deutsche Börse stated, “Deutsche Börse has a direct entry point for investments in digital assets, including post-trading and custody services,” and described Crypto Finance as a FINMA-regulated provider of trading, custody and investment services for digital assets — Deutsche Börse Group Annual Report 2021
  • March 2022, Annual Report 2021: the filing said full consolidation of Crypto Finance would have added €18.3 million of net revenue in 2021 if consolidated from January 1, providing a scale anchor for modest crypto revenue materiality — Deutsche Börse Group Annual Report 2021
  • March 2024, Annual Report 2023 / consolidated financial statements: Deutsche Börse disclosed an impairment test for Crypto Finance and separately identified a “Crypto Finance / Digital Assets” planning unit, confirming crypto remained a recognized operating activity within the group — Deutsche Börse Group Annual Report 2023
  • March 2024, Combined Management Report 2023: Deutsche Börse described D7 as “our contribution to a European ecosystem for digital assets” and said it would enable settlement and management of digital assets on an institutionalised platform — Combined Management Report 2023

Financial news / other:

  • June 29, 2021, company press release: Deutsche Börse announced the majority acquisition of Crypto Finance, which offered institutional and professional clients trading, custody, and investment in digital assets — Deutsche Börse acquires majority stake in Crypto Finance AG
  • March 5, 2024, company press release: Deutsche Börse launched Deutsche Börse Digital Exchange, describing it as a regulated crypto spot platform for institutional clients, with Crypto Finance Deutschland providing trading, settlement, and custody services — Deutsche Börse launches regulated spot platform for crypto assets
  • March 12, 2024, company press release: Deutsche Börse said the first DBDX transactions had been completed and that the platform initially offered Bitcoin and Ether trading for institutional users — First transactions on spot platform for crypto assets DBDX successfully concluded
  • March 5, 2024, Bloomberg Law: reported Deutsche Börse had launched a crypto spot platform targeted at institutional clients, corroborating the company’s positioning of crypto as a financial-service offering rather than an internal treasury activity — Deutsche Boerse Starts Regulated Spot Platform for Crypto Assets

SIDECHANNEL

  • Rank: 143 | Expo: 0.43 | id_str: US17253H1005
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.1, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.9
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 2
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings-call transcripts (2023–2024), 2024 Form 10-K, company investor materials

Firm Overview

SideChannel, Inc. is a U.S.-listed cybersecurity company providing virtual CISO services, security engineering, and its proprietary Enclave network-segmentation software to mid-market and emerging companies. Across the retrieved 2023–2024 company materials, its business is described as cybersecurity services and software, with only limited evidence of crypto-adjacent exposure through serving Bitcoin-mining clients rather than through direct digital-asset activity. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence does not support classification as a holder, miner, or crypto financial-services firm. No retrieved filing shows balance-sheet crypto holdings, mining operations, exchange/brokerage/custody activities, or crypto-backed products, and the 2024 Form 10-K contains no meaningful Bitcoin/blockchain disclosure beyond generic references to protecting clients’ “digital assets,” which in context refers broadly to cybersecurity assets and networks rather than cryptocurrency. (sec.gov)

There is, however, a narrow crypto-adjacent service signal in the May 7, 2024 earnings-call transcript: management stated that the company had previously worked with one of the largest Bitcoin mining operations in the world and had “just acquired as a new client” another large Bitcoin-mining operation, emphasizing advisory and governance services to help protect those clients’ digital assets and infrastructure. That points to a small bizserv exposure because SideChannel appears to provide non-financial cybersecurity services to firms in the crypto ecosystem without itself transacting in or custodying crypto assets. (irp.cdn-website.com)

Weight Assignment

A conservative split of bizserv 0.1 and unclass 0.9 best fits the evidence. The crypto linkage appears only as a limited client vertical within a broader cybersecurity business, while all higher-weight crypto mechanisms are unsupported in filings and transcripts. Because the crypto-related discussion is incidental rather than a recurring disclosed segment or material revenue line, the firm remains predominantly unclassified rather than a substantive crypto-exposed name. (irp.cdn-website.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is materiality. A transcript quote clearly shows service work for Bitcoin-mining clients, but the filings do not isolate crypto-client revenue, identify those clients by name, or describe a dedicated crypto business line. That makes bizserv support real but weak in scale, so certainty is capped at 2. No evidence was found that SideChannel’s stock traded as a crypto proxy or that management discussed crypto-driven investor flows, so invbase remains 0. (irp.cdn-website.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • May 9, 2023, earnings call transcript: management discussed growth in cybersecurity services and software, with no retrieved crypto-specific operating line or treasury activity — SideChannel Earnings Call Transcript
  • May 7, 2024, Fiscal Year 2024 Q2 financial results update: management said, “We previously had worked at one of the largest Bitcoin mining operations in the world. We actually just acquired as a new client, one of the other largest Bitcoin mining operations in the world.” — SideChannel FY2024 Q2 Transcript
  • February 7, 2024, Fiscal Year 2024 Q1 financial results update: retrieved transcript showed operating discussion around cash flow, Enclave, and cybersecurity offerings, with no located Bitcoin/blockchain references — SideChannel FY2024 Q1 Transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • December 2024, Form 10-K for fiscal year ended September 30, 2024: SideChannel described itself as a provider of vCISO services and cybersecurity software/services, including Enclave, with revenue reported in only two cybersecurity categories — 2024 Form 10-K
  • December 2024, Form 10-K: the filing reported total revenue of $7.4 million in FY2024 and no disclosed crypto holdings, mining revenue, or crypto financial-service activity — 2024 Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • Investor relations events archive, accessed 2026: SideChannel’s IR site provides transcripts for 2023–2025 results calls, helping confirm that the relevant crypto-adjacent evidence comes from regular company earnings communications rather than third-party commentary — SideChannel Events & Presentations
  • Company product page, accessed 2026: SideChannel markets Polymorphic Encryption Core as a cybersecurity product, reinforcing the firm’s identity as a security/software provider rather than a direct crypto operator — Polymorphic Encryption Core (PEC)

LOGIQ

  • Rank: 144 | Expo: 0.42 | id_str: US5414401035
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.1, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.9
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 2
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings-call press release/transcript materials (2022), SEC filings and investor materials (2021–2024)

Firm Overview

LOGIQ, Inc. is a U.S.-listed technology company whose disclosed businesses during the sample period centered on digital marketing, consumer data, e-commerce enablement, and, before a separation, mobile-commerce and fintech app services. Across the retrieved 2021–2024 materials, crypto does not appear as a direct operating line; the only clear crypto-related signal is management’s discussion of cryptocurrency as a potential regulated vertical for its marketing technology tools rather than as an asset or core business. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence does not support classification as a holder, miner, infrastructure provider, or crypto financial-services firm. No retrieved filing or investor material shows balance-sheet crypto holdings, mining operations, exchange/custody activity, crypto-backed products, or material blockchain infrastructure revenue. (sec.gov)

There is, however, a limited crypto-adjacent service signal in the May 16, 2022 first-quarter results communication. Management stated that it was evaluating entry into “regulated verticals” including cryptocurrency and said its ad-tech and mar-tech tools could be repurposed across such industries; that points, at most, to a small bizserv exposure because the contemplated role was marketing/business-enablement support around crypto businesses rather than direct handling of digital assets. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

A conservative split of bizserv 0.1 and unclass 0.9 best fits the record. The company’s retrieved disclosures overwhelmingly describe a general digital marketing / e-commerce / consumer-data business, while the crypto linkage appears only as a passing management reference to cryptocurrency as a possible client vertical for existing marketing tools. Because there is no evidence that LOGIQ actually operated a material crypto business, generated disclosed crypto revenue, or held crypto assets, the firm remains predominantly unclassified. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The key limitation is that accessible transcript evidence was sparse; the strongest earnings-call anchor available through SEC-hosted earnings materials is a results press release for May 16, 2022 rather than a full call transcript, so certainty is capped at 2. In addition, the 2022 reference to cryptocurrency concerns strategic interest in a vertical market, not confirmed operating exposure or disclosed revenue, so the bizserv weight is intentionally minimal. No evidence was found that LOGIQ traded as a crypto proxy or that management discussed crypto-driven shareholder flows, so invbase remains 0. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • May 16, 2022, Q1 2022 results / earnings-call announcement: management said it was considering “regulated verticals” such as “cannabis, cryptocurrency, online wagering, and pharmaceutical/medtech,” adding that its ad-tech and mar-tech tools were reusable across those verticals — Q1 2022 Results Press Release
  • Seeking Alpha transcript archive, accessed 2026: archive shows limited available LGIQ earnings transcript coverage, with listed transcripts concentrated in 2020–2022 rather than a broad multi-year set through 2024 — Seeking Alpha LGIQ Transcript Archive

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 30, 2021, acquisition press release filed with SEC: LOGIQ described itself as a provider of eCommerce, mCommerce, and fintech business-enablement solutions, and said Rebel AI added “blockchain-based security” to its marketing platform — Rebel AI Acquisition Release
  • November 2021 investor presentation filed with SEC: PayLogiq was presented as an e-wallet / mobile-payments product alongside AtozPay and food-delivery services, with no disclosed cryptocurrency handling or digital-asset activity — Investor Presentation
  • May 2024, Form 10-K for year ended December 31, 2023: LOGIQ’s filing described operating segments around digital consumer acquisition solutions and contained no retrieved evidence of crypto holdings, mining, custody, or exchange activity — 2023 Form 10-K
  • January 2025, Form 10-Q for quarter ended September 30, 2024: the filing focused on DLQ/DataLogiq operations and corporate restructuring, with no located disclosure of cryptocurrency assets or crypto-service revenue — Q3 2024 Form 10-Q

Financial news / other:

  • Strike.market earnings-transcript page, accessed 2026: third-party earnings archive corroborates that publicly indexed LGIQ transcript coverage is limited and centered on 2022 reporting periods — LGIQ Earnings Call Transcripts
  • Google Finance company profile, accessed 2026: market profile identifies Logiq as a listed operating company rather than a market-recognized crypto proxy stock — Google Finance LGIQ

CURRENCY EXCHANGE INTL.

  • Rank: 145 | Expo: 0.42 | id_str: US23131B3078
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: annual reports (2023–2024), investor relations materials, earnings transcript archive listings

Firm Overview

Currency Exchange International, Corp. is a North American foreign-exchange and payments company that provides foreign banknote services, international payments, foreign check clearing, and related FX technology to banks, credit unions, businesses, and retail travelers. Its retrieved corporate materials describe a traditional fiat foreign-exchange and cross-border payments business rather than a cryptocurrency business. (ceifx.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The retrieved evidence does not support classification as a crypto holder, miner, crypto financial-services firm, crypto business-services provider, or crypto infrastructure provider. Across the company website, investor-relations materials, and 2023–2024 annual reports, CXI is described as providing foreign banknotes, international wire payments, global EFTs, foreign check clearing, drafts, and proprietary FX software for banks and travelers, with no located disclosure of cryptocurrency trading, custody, exchange services, mining, treasury holdings, or blockchain infrastructure activity. (ceifx.com)

Accessible transcript evidence was limited to archive listings rather than scraped full transcript text, but those archives confirm regular earnings-call coverage over 2020–2025. Because the directly retrievable filings and company materials contain no crypto references, and text search within the 2023 and 2024 annual reports returned no matches for “crypt,” “bitcoin,” or “digital asset,” the record supports an unclassified designation. (marketscreener.com)

Weight Assignment

unclass 1.0 is the appropriate assignment. The company is plainly exposed to foreign exchange and payments, but under the project’s mechanism definitions that does not qualify as crypto exposure absent evidence of direct digital-asset handling, crypto-linked revenue, crypto treasury holdings, mining activity, or infrastructure/services specifically tied to the crypto ecosystem. No such evidence was found in the sampled 2017–2024 materials. (ceifx.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is transcript access: public search results clearly show that earnings-call transcripts exist for multiple years, but the directly accessible transcript pages were blocked, so no compliant earnings-call quote could be extracted from full transcript text. Under the coding rules, that prevents a non-unclassified assignment from being anchored to a transcript quote; here, however, the absence of crypto evidence across filings and company materials still supports unclass = 1.0. No evidence was found that the stock traded as a crypto proxy or that management discussed crypto-driven investor flows, so invbase remains 0. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 2020–2025 transcript archive listing, MarketScreener: archive shows recurring CXI earnings calls and shareholder/analyst calls, but no directly retrievable transcript text indicating cryptocurrency activity was available in the accessible search results — MarketScreener Transcript Archive
  • Seeking Alpha transcript archive, accessed 2026: archive lists CURN earnings call transcripts across multiple years, corroborating that transcript coverage exists even though direct page access was blocked in retrieval — Seeking Alpha CURN Transcript Archive

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • January 2025, 2024 Annual Report: annual report describes CXI as a foreign-exchange and payments business; text searches found no matches for crypto-related terms in the report — 2024 Annual Report
  • January 2024, 2023 Annual Report: annual report covers foreign-exchange operations and contained no located cryptocurrency references — 2023 Annual Report
  • January 22, 2025, FY2024 results press release: company said payments revenue grew 12% and banknotes revenue grew 2%, framing operations around fiat payments and foreign banknotes rather than digital assets — FY2024 Results Press Release
  • 2025 investor-relations financial statements page: company states it releases MD&A and consolidated financial statements quarterly, with no crypto-specific product line disclosed on the page — Financial Statements

Financial news / other:

  • Company profile page, accessed 2026: CXI describes itself as a “full-service foreign exchange technology and service provider” serving financial institutions and travelers — Company Profile
  • About page, accessed 2026: CXI says it provides foreign currency exchange, international payments, foreign check clearing, and foreign drafts for financial institutions — About CXI
  • Investor-relations page, accessed 2026: investor materials link annual reports and conference calls, but the page characterizes the business as currency exchange and FX technology rather than cryptocurrency — Investor Relations

ALKAMI TECHNOLOGY

  • Rank: 146 | Expo: 0.42 | id_str: US01644J1088
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2023–2024 annual reports, investor relations earnings releases, earnings transcript archive/event listings

Firm Overview

Alkami Technology, Inc. is a U.S.-listed financial-technology company that provides cloud-based digital banking software to banks and credit unions. The retrieved 2023–2024 filings and investor-relations materials describe a business focused on digital banking, account opening, payment security, data, and marketing solutions for financial institutions, rather than cryptocurrency activities.

Exposure Channels Identified

The retrieved evidence does not support classification as a crypto holder, miner, crypto financial-services firm, crypto business-services provider, or crypto infrastructure provider. In its 2024 Form 10-K, Alkami describes itself as a digital banking solutions provider and discusses client growth, deposits, loans, money movement, and product cross-sell, but no located references to bitcoin, blockchain, crypto, or digital assets were found in the filing.

Investor-relations earnings materials for 2024 likewise frame the business around digital banking users, annual recurring revenue, onboarding, payment security, and analytics for financial institutions. Accessible earnings-call evidence was limited to archive/event pages and earnings-release text rather than full transcript text, but the available materials contained no indication that Alkami directly handles digital assets or provides crypto-specific services.

Weight Assignment

unclass 1.0 is the appropriate assignment. Although Alkami serves financial institutions and offers software related to digital banking and payments, the project’s definitions require explicit evidence of substantive crypto exposure; across the sampled 2017–2024 accessible materials, no such evidence was located for treasury crypto holdings, mining, exchange/custody activity, crypto-focused business services, or crypto-directed infrastructure.

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is transcript accessibility. Public IR event pages and transcript archive listings confirm that Alkami held earnings calls, including a Q4 2023 financial results call on February 28, 2024, but a full compliant transcript quote discussing operations could not be retrieved from accessible sources. Under the coding rules, that prevents anchoring any non-unclassified assignment to a transcript quote; here, however, the absence of crypto references across filings and company materials supports unclass = 1.0. No direct evidence was found that the stock traded as a crypto proxy or that management discussed crypto-driven investor flows, so invbase remains 0.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 28, 2024, investor relations event page: Q4 2023 earnings call listing confirms public earnings-call coverage, though no full transcript text discussing cryptocurrency was accessible — Q4 2023 Financial Results Conference Call
  • Accessed 2026, Seeking Alpha archive listing: archive lists ALKT earnings call transcripts, corroborating transcript coverage even though direct transcript retrieval was blocked — Seeking Alpha ALKT Transcript Archive

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 28, 2025, 2024 Form 10-K: filing describes Alkami as a digital banking platform provider for financial institutions; text searches returned no matches for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” “blockchain,” or “digital asset” — 2024 Form 10-K
  • February 29, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K: accessible filing record for the prior annual report provides the company’s annual-report source for 2023, with no located indication of crypto operations in retrieved search snippets — 2023 Form 10-K PDF
  • February 27, 2025, Q4 2024 results release: company said it is a “leading cloud-based digital banking solutions provider” and highlighted revenue growth, digital banking users, and ARR rather than digital-asset activity — Q4 2024 Results Release
  • February 28, 2024, Q4 2023 results release: earnings release reported revenue and margin growth for Alkami’s digital banking platform with no crypto-specific product or revenue disclosure — Q4 2023 Results Release

Financial news / other:

  • Accessed 2026, investor relations overview/events archive: IR materials consistently categorize Alkami around digital banking, data and marketing, and treasury-management solutions for banks and credit unions — Investor Relations
  • September 19, 2024, company news release: Alkami was described as a digital banking solutions provider and included in IDC’s FinTech rankings, with no mention of cryptocurrency business lines — IDC FinTech Rankings Release

KAKAO

  • Rank: 147 | Expo: 0.42 | id_str: KR7035720002
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.2, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.8, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: 2021 ESG report, 2022–2024 IR earnings materials, Q4 2021 earnings-call transcript snippet

Firm Overview

Kakao Corp. is a South Korea-listed internet platform company centered on KakaoTalk, advertising, commerce, mobility, content, and related digital services. Within that broader platform portfolio, retrieved company materials show that Kakao developed and operated blockchain-related businesses through subsidiaries such as Ground X / Krust, including the Klaytn public blockchain and Klip digital-asset wallet. (kakaocorp.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports classification primarily as a crypto infrastructure provider. Kakao’s 2021 ESG report states that “Klaytn is the global public blockchain platform independently developed by Kakao,” and describes Klaytn as infrastructure for DApp development, metaverse/Web3 applications, and blockchain-based services; this fits the project definition of infrastr. (kakaocorp.com)

A smaller finserv weight is also warranted because the same report says Klaytn provides “Klip, a digital asset wallet” for KakaoTalk users and describes Klip Drops as enabling trading of digital art and NFTs, which means Kakao-group entities directly handled digital-asset wallet and marketplace functions rather than merely supplying back-end software. (kakaocorp.com)

The earnings-call anchor is limited but usable: in Kakao’s February 11, 2022 Q4 2021 earnings-call transcript, management said, “Also, the Klaytn blockchain led by Krust, we plan to kick-start changes towards building a metaverse platform,” confirming blockchain activity was discussed by management on an earnings call. Public 2022–2024 IR decks also continued to present “blockchain” as part of “platform other,” indicating an ongoing but non-core business line. (ae.marketscreener.com)

Weight Assignment
