infrastr 0.8 and finserv 0.2 best reflect the evidence. The dominant mechanism is infrastructure because Kakao repeatedly frames Klaytn as a blockchain platform and developer environment, and the 2022–2024 earnings materials treat blockchain as one item within a diversified “platform other” segment rather than a standalone financial-services engine. A minority finserv weight is included because Klip is explicitly a digital-asset wallet and Klip Drops an NFT trading venue, but the retrieved sources do not show crypto financial services as a major share of consolidated revenue. (kakaocorp.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is transcript accessibility: full public transcripts for multiple years were not openly retrievable, and the accessible earnings-call evidence is largely limited to opening lines plus a searchable snippet for Q4 2021 from MarketScreener. Also, the retrieved sources show blockchain and wallet activity, but not precise consolidated revenue or asset values attributable to crypto; accordingly, RevShare is coded as minor and AssetShare as negligible. No direct evidence was located that Kakao itself held cryptocurrency as a treasury asset, mined crypto, or traded as a primary crypto-proxy stock, so holders, miners, and invbase remain zero. (ae.marketscreener.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 11, 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript snippet: “Also, the Klaytn blockchain led by Krust, we plan to kick-start changes towards building a metaverse platform.” — MarketScreener transcript page
  • May 9, 2024, Q1 2024 earnings call listing: public conference-call transcript page confirms ongoing earnings-call coverage for Kakao, though full text was paywalled — MarketScreener transcript page

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2021 ESG report: Klaytn is described as “the global public blockchain platform independently developed by Kakao,” with Klip as a “digital asset wallet” and Klip Drops enabling NFT trading — 2021 ESG Report PDF
  • 2021 ESG report, subsidiary section: Ground X is described as focusing on “the digital asset wallet service Klip and the digital art and NFT marketplace Klip Drops” — 2021 ESG Report PDF
  • May 9, 2024, Q1 2024 earnings presentation: platform revenue mix still includes “blockchain” within “platform other,” indicating continuing but non-core blockchain operations — Q1 2024 Earnings Presentation PDF
  • February 15, 2024, Q4 2023 earnings presentation: FY23 and 4Q23 materials continue to list “blockchain” within the platform segment mix — Q4 2023 Earnings Presentation PDF
  • February 10, 2023, Q4 2022 earnings presentation: 2022 year-end materials likewise include “blockchain” in platform-other revenue composition — Q4 2022 Earnings Presentation PDF

Financial news / other:

  • Accessed 2026, Kakao IR earnings archive: Kakao’s IR site provides year-by-year earnings materials and conference-call playback links across the sample period — Kakao IR Earnings Release Archive
  • October 8, 2018, PR Newswire: Ground X, described as Kakao’s blockchain subsidiary, unveiled the Klaytn blockchain platform and positioned it as a service-centric public blockchain — PR Newswire release
  • August 17, 2022, KB Securities company overview: Kakao group structure summary identifies Ground X as operating Klaytn and Klip wallet services — KB Securities overview PDF

B3 BRASIL BOLSA BALCAO ON

  • Rank: 148 | Expo: 0.41 | id_str: BRB3SAACNOR6
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.2, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings releases / conference-call materials (2022–2024), annual report / management report (2023), Reuters coverage (2024)

Firm Overview

B3 S.A. – Brasil, Bolsa, Balcão is Brazil’s main exchange and a diversified financial market infrastructure group operating trading, post-trading, OTC, data, and financing-related platforms. Within the 2017–2024 sample, its crypto exposure appears mainly through regulated crypto-linked market products—especially bitcoin futures and crypto-related ETFs/ETFs receipts listed on its exchange—rather than through proprietary crypto investing or mining.

Exposure Channels Identified

The dominant channel is finserv because B3 directly offers market infrastructure for crypto-linked financial products and derivatives. Reuters reported that B3 received regulatory approval to launch bitcoin futures in April 2024, and B3’s own 4Q24 earnings release said bitcoin futures reached an average daily volume of 206 thousand contracts and contributed R$42.8 million in revenue. (investing.com)

A smaller infrastr weight is also warranted. B3’s 2023 management report says it developed a cloud platform and a platform for issuance, registration, and trading of tokenized debenture-representative assets using blockchain, which is closer to capital-markets/blockchain infrastructure than to direct custody or treasury holding of crypto. (publicidadelegal.valor.com.br)

The earnings-call anchor is limited but usable: B3’s publicly posted 4Q22 earnings materials explicitly scheduled conference calls, and management later stated in the 4Q24 earnings release that bitcoin futures were part of its product-update strategy and generated revenue. Because a full open transcript with Q&A text was not retrievable, certainty is capped below the highest level. (marketscreener.com)

Weight Assignment

finserv 0.8 and infrastr 0.2 best fit the evidence. Most of the crypto-related discussion concerns exchange-traded crypto products and derivatives offered to investors, which is squarely finserv; the blockchain/tokenization references are real but secondary and appear as supporting market infrastructure rather than a major standalone revenue engine. No evidence was found of treasury crypto holdings, mining, or non-financial service activity, so holders, miners, and bizserv remain zero. (investing.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is transcript accessibility. Public search results confirmed earnings-call coverage across 2023–2024 and conference-call scheduling in B3 earnings materials, but a fully open transcript containing a direct management quote on crypto was not easily retrievable; accordingly, the classification relies on earnings-release text plus call-related materials rather than a richer multi-year transcript set. RevShare is coded 1 because bitcoin futures generated identifiable revenue in 4Q24 but remained small relative to B3’s total quarterly revenue of R$2.67 billion; AssetShare is 0 because no evidence was found that B3 held crypto assets on balance sheet as a material asset class. No explicit evidence was found that B3 was treated as a primary crypto proxy stock or that management discussed crypto-driven shareholder flows, so invbase stays 0. (marketscreener.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 15, 2023, 4Q22 earnings materials / conference-call notice: earnings release explicitly included conference-call details, providing an anchor that crypto questions could have arisen during covered periods even though full transcript text was not open-access — MarketScreener 4Q22 earnings release
  • February 20, 2025, 4Q24 earnings release tied to Feb. 21, 2025 conference call: “the continuous growth in the volume of Bitcoin Futures, closing the quarter with an ADV of 206 thousand contracts and contributing with R$42.8 million in revenues” — MarketScreener 4Q24 earnings release
  • MarketScreener company calendar: confirms recurring earnings-call coverage for B3 in 2023–2024, including Q2 2023, Q3 2023, Q4 2023, Q1 2024, Q2 2024, and Q3 2024 — MarketScreener events calendar

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 23, 2024, 2023 management report: B3 disclosed “a platform for issuance, registration and trading of tokenized assets representing debentures using blockchain,” indicating blockchain-focused market infrastructure activity — Valor-hosted 2023 management report PDF
  • February 20, 2025, 4Q24 earnings release: bitcoin futures were described as part of B3’s strategy of “continuously updating its portfolio of products and solutions for the market,” with quantified revenue contribution — MarketScreener 4Q24 earnings release

Financial news / other:

  • March 28, 2024, Reuters: B3 received regulatory approval to launch bitcoin futures, with trading to start on April 17, 2024; Reuters also noted B3 already offered crypto-related ETFs and ETF receipts — Investing.com repost of Reuters article
  • B3 history page: company overview identifies B3 as one of the world’s leading financial market infrastructure companies, supporting the entity identification and business-model context for classifying crypto exposure mechanisms — B3 IR history page

FUNKO CL.A

  • Rank: 149 | Expo: 0.41 | id_str: US3610081057
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.2, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcript (Q1 2021), 10-K / annual reports (2021, 2024), company press releases and financial news (2021)

Firm Overview

Funko, Inc. is a U.S.-listed consumer products company best known for pop-culture collectibles, toys, and licensed merchandise. During 2021–2024, it expanded into blockchain-based digital collectibles through TokenWave and its Droppp marketplace, making crypto-adjacent activity a small but explicit part of the business. (investor.funko.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The main exposure channel is finserv. Funko did not merely market NFT-themed products; it operated a platform that facilitated the buying and selling of its NFTs, received secondary-sale payments in digital assets, purchased digital assets for certain NFT-business expenses, and disclosed that TokenWave/Droppp acted as custodian for NFTs held in user accounts. That is direct handling of digital assets and marketplace activity, which fits the project’s finserv definition better than a pure merchandising or advertising role. (earningscall.biz)

A smaller bizserv weight is also warranted because part of the economic logic remained tied to Funko’s core non-financial collectibles franchise: NFT “drops” were extensions of fan engagement and branded digital merchandise rather than a standalone crypto-native financial platform. The company’s own 2021 materials framed TokenWave as a strategic entry into the NFT market and described the digital products business as an extension of its consumer offering, while the acquired activity was said to be not material in 2021. (investor.funko.com)

The earnings-call anchor is clear. In the May 6, 2021 Q1 earnings call, management stated that collectors receiving the rarest NFTs would obtain exclusive physical figures and that, after sellout, NFTs would trade on the secondary market with Funko and licensors receiving royalty payments on each additional transaction. That transcript quote directly ties Funko to ongoing NFT-marketplace economics rather than a one-off promotional mention. (earningscall.biz)

Weight Assignment

finserv 0.8 and bizserv 0.2 best fit the evidence. The heavier weight goes to finserv because Funko explicitly facilitated NFT trading, took digital-asset payments, used digital assets operationally, and disclosed custodial/safeguarding functions through Droppp/TokenWave. The residual bizserv weight reflects that these activities were embedded in a branded consumer-products ecosystem and appear economically peripheral to the broader company rather than a dominant financial-services franchise. No evidence was found of treasury crypto accumulation, mining, or crypto-specific infrastructure supply, so holders, miners, and infrastr remain zero. (earningscall.biz)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is materiality, not existence. Funko clearly engaged in NFT-market and custody-related activity, but filings indicate the TokenWave acquisition and related activity were not material in 2021, and the available filings do not disclose a separate crypto revenue segment large enough to justify a higher RevShare code. RevShare is therefore coded 1 because crypto-related revenue appears real but peripheral, while AssetShare is 0 because no evidence was found of strategic balance-sheet crypto holdings; the disclosed $6.1 million 2023 safeguarding asset/liability was a platform-user safeguarding presentation item later excluded after SAB 122 adoption, not treasury crypto. invbase is set to 1 because financial media explicitly highlighted FNKO shares surging after its NFT-market entry announcement, indicating stock-price sensitivity to crypto/NFT sentiment beyond the narrow business contribution. (materials.proxyvote.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • May 6, 2021, Q1 2021 earnings call transcript: “Once sold out, an NFT will only be available to purchase again on the secondary market, with Funko and our licensing partners receiving a royalty payment on each additional transaction.” — EarningsCall.biz Q1 2021 transcript
  • May 6, 2021, Q1 2021 earnings call transcript: management also said collectors of the rarest NFTs would receive exclusive rights to a physical Funko figure and identified Tokenhead/WAX-network partners for launch — EarningsCall.biz Q1 2021 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • May 6, 2021, Q1 2021 earnings release: Funko said it had “completed the acquisition of a majority stake in TokenWave, providing the Company with strategic entry into the NFT market” — Funko IR press release
  • April 8, 2022, 2021 annual report: Funko disclosed it launched its first NFTs through TokenWave, with digital card packs sold on the WAX blockchain platform — 2021 Annual Report / Proxy PDF
  • April 8, 2022, 2021 annual report: acquisition note stated TokenWave’s activity from acquisition date to December 31, 2021 “was not material” — 2021 Annual Report / Proxy PDF
  • March 2025 filing for FY2024: Funko disclosed that it receives payments in digital assets from secondary sales in its NFT and digital collectibles business and purchases digital assets for certain NFT-business expenses — 2024 Form 10-K
  • March 2025 filing for FY2024: Funko said TokenWave operates Droppp.io “to facilitate the buying and selling of its NFTs,” and that it “holds cryptographic key information for NFTs and is a custodian for NFTs held in platform users' accounts” — 2024 Form 10-K
  • March 2025 filing for FY2024: Funko said early adoption of SAB 122 removed a “crypto asset safeguarding asset and corresponding liability of $6.1 million” from the December 31, 2023 presentation — 2024 Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • April 1, 2021, CNBC: Funko shares surged after announcing the TokenWave deal and NFT-market entry, indicating explicit market treatment as an NFT/crypto-adjacent stock — CNBC article
  • November 4, 2021, Q3 2021 earnings release: Funko said it had “launched multiple Digital Pop! NFT Collections” and opened Droppp.io as its digital marketplace — Funko IR Q3 2021 release

RAMBUS

  • Rank: 150 | Expo: 0.41 | id_str: US7509171069
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2022 Form 10-K, Q2 2024 earnings materials, Q4 2023 earnings materials, Rambus product/press pages

Firm Overview

Rambus Inc. is a San Jose-based semiconductor and silicon IP company listed on Nasdaq under RMBS, focused on memory interface chips, security IP, and patent licensing for data center and other electronics markets. Across the reviewed 2017–2024 materials, Rambus’ “crypto” terminology refers to cryptographic hardware security functions rather than cryptocurrency, blockchain, mining, exchange, custody, or treasury-asset activity.

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive crypto-asset exposure channel was identified for Rambus within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period. The company does market security products under names such as CryptoManager and references “crypto cores,” but the surrounding disclosures show these are semiconductor security and cryptography IP products for key management, hardware root of trust, and secure processing rather than participation in digital-asset markets. (sec.gov)

The reviewed 10-K contains no references to blockchain, bitcoin, cryptocurrency, or digital assets, and no balance-sheet or revenue disclosures suggest mining, treasury holdings, exchange activity, custody, token issuance, or crypto-directed infrastructure. Rambus’ earnings materials instead emphasize memory-interface chips, licensing, and security IP tied to AI, data center, and semiconductor use cases. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

unclass 1.0 is the best fit. Although Rambus uses “crypto” and “CryptoManager” language in product branding, the evidence consistently points to conventional cryptographic/security semiconductor IP rather than economic exposure to cryptocurrencies or the crypto ecosystem as defined by the classification scheme. No evidence supports holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr, and no evidence was found that RMBS trades as a crypto proxy stock. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is terminological: Rambus frequently uses the word “crypto” in the sense of cryptography. That could create false positives without close source reading, but the underlying documents consistently describe semiconductor security IP, hardware roots of trust, and key-provisioning infrastructure rather than digital-asset activity. An earnings-call transcript page was discoverable, but accessible fetched materials were mainly earnings decks/press materials rather than full text transcripts; because the final classification is unclass, this does not alter the output.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • July 29, 2024, Q2 2024 earnings materials: “Silicon IP design win momentum fueled by HBM, PCIe, security IP” — Rambus Q2 2024 earnings deck
  • February 6, 2024, Q4 2023 earnings materials: earnings materials were retrieved for sample coverage; no cryptocurrency, blockchain, or digital-asset references were identified in the fetched document — Seeking Alpha-hosted Q4 2023 webcast deck PDF

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 24, 2023, Form 10-K: Rambus said its Silicon IP includes security solutions and “crypto cores, hardware roots of trust, high-speed protocol engines and chip provisioning technologies” — 2022 Form 10-K
  • February 24, 2023, Form 10-K: searches of the filing found no matches for “blockchain,” “bitcoin,” “cryptocurrency,” or “digital asset” — 2022 Form 10-K
  • July 29, 2024, earnings release: Rambus described itself as a provider of chips and IP “making data faster and safer,” with no crypto-asset business disclosure — Rambus Q2 2024 financial results press release

Financial news / other:

  • March 9, 2023, company announcement: Rambus expanded its security IP portfolio with Arm CryptoCell and CryptoIsland root-of-trust cores, indicating cryptography/security IP rather than cryptocurrency services — Rambus press page on CryptoCell/CryptoIsland
  • Rambus product page, accessed via search: CryptoManager RT-6xx is described as a hardware root-of-trust family for secure execution, key handling, and anti-tamper protection — Rambus CryptoManager RT-6xx Root of Trust
  • Rambus product page, accessed via search: CryptoManager Provisioning is described as a semiconductor/device-manufacturing key-insertion and secure supply-chain solution, not a cryptocurrency platform — Rambus CryptoManager Provisioning

MARKER THERAPEUTICS

  • Rank: 151 | Expo: 0.39 | id_str: US57055L2060
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2022–2024 Form 10-K filings, 2023 earnings-call transcript index / accessible transcript search results, company earnings releases

Firm Overview

Marker Therapeutics, Inc. is a Houston-based Nasdaq-listed clinical-stage immuno-oncology company developing T cell-based cancer therapies, with operations centered on research, clinical development, grants, and capital raising. Across the reviewed 2017–2024 materials, the company’s disclosures focus on cell therapy programs, manufacturing arrangements, cash runway, and equity financing rather than cryptocurrency, digital-asset, blockchain, mining, exchange, custody, or treasury activity. (ir.markertherapeutics.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive crypto-asset exposure channel was identified for Marker Therapeutics within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period. The reviewed SEC filings contain no references to bitcoin, cryptocurrency, digital assets, blockchain, or crypto, and the company instead describes itself as a clinical-stage biotechnology firm with limited revenue from grants and no product sales or licensing revenue since inception. (sec.gov)

Searches for earnings-call materials show that transcript coverage is limited and some investor-relations pages were not fetchable, but available transcript indexing and company-result materials point to ordinary biotech topics such as manufacturing changes, trial progress, grants, and financing. No source reviewed suggests treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, crypto financial services, crypto-focused business services, or crypto-directed infrastructure, and no credible evidence was found that MRKR traded as a crypto proxy stock. (seekingalpha.com)

Weight Assignment

unclass 1.0 is the best fit. The evidence consistently characterizes Marker Therapeutics as a small clinical-stage cancer-therapy developer with funding from grants, public equity issuance, and conventional cash management, while direct searches of its 2022 and 2024 Form 10-K filings returned no crypto-related terms. Because no evidence supports holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr, and no direct evidence supports a crypto-linked investor-base effect, all mechanism weight is assigned to unclass. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Accessible full-text earnings-call transcripts were limited. A transcript index page for MRKR was discoverable, and transcript availability is indicated for at least some historical periods, but direct fetching from Seeking Alpha and certain Marker IR pages was blocked; additionally, one retrieved “Corrected Transcript” result was for a different company and was excluded from the analysis. Because the final classification is unclass, the lack of a usable transcript quote does not change the output, but it does mean the evidence relies more heavily on SEC filings and accessible earnings-release materials than on transcript text.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Seeking Alpha transcript index, accessed via search: MRKR transcript page exists and indicates historical earnings-call transcript coverage, including a May 12, 2021 entry — Seeking Alpha MRKR earnings call transcripts
  • August 2024 summary of Q2 2024 results: document catalog noted that no Q2 2024 earnings call transcript was available in reviewed materials — Fintool MRKR Q2 2024 earnings summary

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • April 2025, Form 10-K for fiscal 2024: Marker stated it “ha[s] not generated any revenues from the sales or licensing of our product candidates since inception and only have limited revenue associated with grants to fund research” — 2024 Form 10-K
  • April 2025, Form 10-K for fiscal 2024: company said interest income was attributable to funds held in “U.S. Treasury notes and U.S. government agency-backed securities” — 2024 Form 10-K
  • April 2025, Form 10-K for fiscal 2024: searches of the filing found no matches for “bitcoin,” “cryptocurrency,” “digital asset,” “blockchain,” or “crypto” — 2024 Form 10-K search results
  • April 2023, Form 10-K for fiscal 2022: searches likewise found no matches for “bitcoin,” “cryptocurrency,” “digital asset,” “blockchain,” or “crypto” — 2022 Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • March 2025, company release: Marker described itself as a “clinical-stage immuno-oncology company focusing on developing next-generation T cell-based immunotherapies” — Year-end 2024 corporate and financial results
  • May 2023, company release: Marker announced a manufacturing and asset transaction with Cell Ready so it could “concentrate solely on the clinical advancement” of its T cell therapy programs — Marker announces comprehensive non-dilutive agreement with CellReady
  • August 2023, company release: Q2 2023 results emphasized patient treatment progress, non-clinical data, cash, and operating expenses rather than any crypto-related business line — Q2 2023 financial results and business update

CELLEBRITE DI

  • Rank: 152 | Expo: 0.39 | id_str: IL0011794802
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: Form 20-F filings (2023–2024), Q3 2021 earnings call transcript, company crypto-investigations press materials

Firm Overview

Cellebrite DI Ltd. is an Israel-based, Nasdaq-listed digital investigations and forensics software company serving law-enforcement, government, enterprise, and service-provider customers. Across the reviewed 2017–2024 materials, the company is primarily described as a provider of digital forensics, evidence management, analytics, and investigative services, with cryptocurrency-related exposure appearing as a specialized investigation capability embedded within that broader software-and-services offering. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The clearest crypto-exposure channel is bizserv, not finserv or holders. Cellebrite’s filings state that its solutions are used in investigations of financial crimes including those involving cryptocurrency, and that it provides, with partners, “CryptoCurrency Investigative Solutions” for analyzing blockchain transactions and helping investigators identify wallets and transactions. (sec.gov)

This activity fits non-financial crypto services because Cellebrite supplies investigative software and related professional services to customers rather than operating an exchange, custody platform, payment service, fund, or treasury crypto portfolio. The same filings frame crypto as one crime-type/use-case among many inside a much broader digital-investigations business, so the evidence does not support finserv, holders, miners, or infrastr. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

bizserv 1.0 is the best fit. The company explicitly markets blockchain-transaction investigative capabilities and partnered with Chainalysis to integrate cryptocurrency investigation workflows into its digital investigation platform, but the available sources do not show that Cellebrite itself takes custody of cryptoassets, trades them, mines them, or builds crypto-dedicated hardware or compute infrastructure. Because crypto appears as a specialized service feature inside a larger forensic-software platform rather than a dominant business line, the crypto revenue contribution is best coded as minor (RevShare 1), while no balance-sheet crypto assets are evidenced (AssetShare 0). (cellebrite.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Accessible earnings-call transcript text was limited; a usable Q3 2021 transcript was retrieved, but it did not contain explicit crypto discussion, so the transcript anchors the company’s core business model more than the crypto-specific classification. The strongest crypto evidence comes from Form 20-F business-overview language and company press materials rather than multiple transcript quotes, which keeps certainty at 3 rather than 4. No direct evidence was found that CLBT traded as a crypto proxy stock or that management described a crypto-driven investor base. (roic.ai)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • November 4, 2021, Q3 2021 earnings call transcript: management said it expected “Digital Clues to have a small positive impact on our ARR in subscription revenue” as it integrated open-source intelligence into the DI platform, anchoring the core business as investigative software/services rather than crypto finance — ROIC AI Q3 2021 transcript
  • CLBT transcript index, accessed via search: historical earnings-call transcript coverage exists across 2021–2024, supporting review of available call history — Seeking Alpha CLBT earnings transcripts

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 21, 2024, Form 20-F for fiscal 2023: Cellebrite said its solutions are used in “financial crimes including those involving cryptocurrency” — 2023 Form 20-F
  • March 21, 2024, Form 20-F for fiscal 2023: the company said it provides “CryptoCurrency Investigative Solutions for analyzing blockchain transactions” to help investigators identify and categorize wallets and transactions — 2023 Form 20-F
  • March 18, 2025, Form 20-F for fiscal 2024: Cellebrite repeated that its software supports investigations involving cryptocurrency and, with partners, blockchain-transaction analysis — 2024 Form 20-F

Financial news / other:

  • January 2022, company press release: Cellebrite and Chainalysis said they partnered to provide an “integrated cryptocurrency investigation solution” inside Cellebrite’s Digital Intelligence offerings — Cellebrite and Chainalysis partnership release
  • July 2020, company/PRNewswire release: Cellebrite announced “Crypto Tracer,” describing it as a cryptocurrency investigation solution for blockchain-related investigations — Crypto Tracer launch release
  • Company product page, accessed in current search: Cellebrite advertises cryptocurrency-investigation review and analysis as part of investigation services rather than as a financial platform — Cellebrite Cryptocurrency Investigations

PENSIONBEE GROUP

  • Rank: 153 | Expo: 0.39 | id_str: GB00BNDRLN84
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: PensionBee investor-relations results materials (2021–2024), annual-report archive/index, company investor-relations page

Firm Overview

PensionBee Group plc is a UK-listed online pension provider focused on pension consolidation and retirement savings management for retail customers in the UK, and more recently the US. Across the reviewed 2017–2024 investor-relations materials, the business is consistently described as a consumer pension and investment platform earning recurring fees on assets under administration rather than as a crypto-focused operating business. (res.cloudinary.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive crypto-exposure channel was identified in the reviewed 2017–2024 materials. Searches across PensionBee’s investor-relations archive and company materials did not surface evidence that the firm held crypto on balance sheet, operated mining activity, offered crypto trading/custody/payment products, provided non-financial crypto services, or supplied crypto-dedicated infrastructure. (pensionbee.com)

The accessible earnings-call/trading-update materials instead describe PensionBee as an online pension platform whose revenues are generated from customer pension assets and management fees, with investment plans managed by large third-party asset managers. Because the collected sources do not show meaningful cryptocurrency involvement, the company is best classified as unclass. (res.cloudinary.com)

Weight Assignment

unclass 1.0 is the appropriate assignment. The available transcript-style investor-update materials and the investor-relations archive consistently frame PensionBee around pension consolidation, customer acquisition, assets under administration, and fee-based retirement-plan management; no reviewed source provided credible evidence for holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr, and no evidence indicated that the shares traded as a crypto proxy or had a crypto-driven investor base. (res.cloudinary.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Accessible text transcripts were limited; the company’s investor-relations site provided trading updates, presentations, and recordings rather than rich third-party call transcripts. However, this limitation does not alter the classification because both targeted crypto searches and the accessible 2021–2024 investor-relations materials failed to produce substantive crypto references. (res.cloudinary.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 22 July 2021, H1 2021 trading update: PensionBee said it is “a leading online pension provider,” with revenue from annual management fees on customer assets, anchoring the core business as pension administration rather than crypto finance — H1 2021 Results
  • 21 October 2021, Q3 2021 trading update: management highlighted customer growth, AUA growth, product innovation, and open-banking integration, with no crypto activity disclosed — Q3 2021 Results

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Investor-relations archive, accessed in current search: PensionBee lists annual reports and results materials for 2021–2024, supporting review of the firm’s official disclosures across the sample window — PensionBee Investor Relations
  • Investor-relations page search, accessed in current search: no matches were returned for “crypt” on the investor-relations site — Investor relations search result
  • Investor-relations page search, accessed in current search: no matches were returned for “bitcoin” on the investor-relations site — Investor relations search result
  • Investor-relations page search, accessed in current search: no matches were returned for “blockchain” on the investor-relations site — Investor relations search result

Financial news / other:

  • Company investor-relations page, accessed in current search: PensionBee describes revenue as income generated from the asset base of customers and presents itself as a retirement-services platform — PensionBee Investor Relations
  • 22 July 2021 company results release: PensionBee said its plans are managed by BlackRock, HSBC, Legal & General, and State Street Global Advisors, consistent with a conventional pension-platform model — H1 2021 Results

TEXAS PACIFIC LAND TRUST

  • Rank: 154 | Expo: 0.38 | id_str: US88262P1021
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: company earnings-call transcripts (2022, 2024), 2024 Form 10-K, company press release on Mawson/JAI bitcoin-mining venture

Firm Overview

Texas Pacific Land Corporation is a large Texas landowner concentrated in the Permian Basin, generating revenue primarily from oil and gas royalties, water services, and surface-related easements and leases. Within the reviewed 2017–2024 materials, crypto exposure appears not as direct coin ownership or operating mining, but through leasing/permitting surface acreage and related infrastructure for third-party bitcoin-mining facilities. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The relevant channel is infrastr. TPL explicitly announced in May 2022 a strategic alliance to develop up to 60 megawatts of bitcoin mining on its surface in West Texas, with the facilities to be owned and operated by Mawson, while TPL would earn a royalty interest and retain an option for an equity stake. (texaspacific.com)

Management continued to discuss bitcoin-mining activity in later disclosures rather than treating it as a one-off mention. In the 2024 Form 10-K, TPL said its “next generation” agreements include “development of bitcoin mining facilities,” while noting these projects were not expected to have a significant short-term revenue impact; in a Q3 2024 earnings-call transcript, the CEO said TPL had “four Bitcoin mines” active and more in development. This fits infrastructure provision to crypto operators rather than mining, custody, or treasury holding by TPL itself. (sec.gov; roic.ai)

Weight Assignment

infrastr 1.0 is the best fit. The evidence indicates TPL monetizes land, access, and related site-development optionality for third-party bitcoin-mining/data-center users, while the actual mining operation is conducted by counterparties such as Mawson; there is no evidence in the reviewed sources that TPL itself mined bitcoin, held crypto as treasury, operated an exchange/custody/payments platform, or provided mainly advisory/software services. Because management also states these activities were not yet significant to consolidated revenue in the short term, the mechanism is clear but currently peripheral in materiality. (texaspacific.com; sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Accessible transcript coverage was adequate but not extensive across the full 2017–2024 window, and some transcript text was easier to retrieve from SEC attachments or transcript aggregators than from a complete archive page. The main ambiguity is materiality rather than mechanism: TPL’s filings indicate bitcoin-mining and other “next generation” projects existed and were active, but the 2024 Form 10-K also says they were not expected to have a significant short-term revenue impact, so RevShare is coded low. No reviewed evidence supported invbase = 1, as the sources did not explicitly describe TPL stock as a crypto proxy or identify a crypto-driven shareholder base. (sec.gov; texaspacific.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 3 November 2022, Q3 2022 earnings call transcript: management said TPL had “a team tasked specifically to pursue alternative and next generation uses” for its surface acreage and highlighted ongoing work on non-traditional land uses, anchoring the company’s infrastructure-leasing model rather than direct crypto operations — Q3 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
  • 7 November 2024, Q3 2024 earnings call transcript: CEO Tyler Glover said “we’ve got four Bitcoin mines, like 78 megawatts that are active today with another 50 in development stages,” and described them as “small compute centers,” indicating active crypto-related site/infrastructure exposure — Q3 2024 Transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 21 February 2025, 2024 Form 10-K: TPL disclosed that its “next generation” opportunities include “development of bitcoin mining facilities” and that these arrangements can provide royalty, fee, profit-sharing, lease and/or rental payments — 2024 Form 10-K
  • 21 February 2025, 2024 Form 10-K: the company stated it did “not anticipate these agreements will have a significant impact on our revenues in the short-term,” supporting low current revenue materiality — 2024 Form 10-K
  • 3 August 2022, Q2 2022 earnings release furnished to SEC: TPL listed as a quarterly highlight that it “Executed new agreements for bitcoin mining with Mawson Infrastructure Group Inc. and JAI Energy” — Q2 2022 Earnings Release

Financial news / other:

  • 16 May 2022, company press release: TPL, Mawson, and JAI announced a plan to develop “up to 60 megawatts of bitcoin mining on TPL’s surface in West Texas,” with Mawson owning and operating the facilities while TPL earned a royalty interest — Bitcoin Mining Venture Press Release
  • 16 May 2022, company press release: CEO Tyler Glover said the project marked “the beginning of TPL’s journey into bitcoin,” but the release framed TPL as providing surface locations near grid infrastructure and renewable resources, consistent with infrastr rather than miners or holders — Bitcoin Mining Venture Press Release

PLAYSTUDIOS A

  • Rank: 155 | Expo: 0.38 | id_str: US72815G1085
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcript (Q2 2022), 2023 Form 10-K, company Web3 press release (2022)

Firm Overview

PLAYSTUDIOS, Inc. is a Las Vegas-based mobile and social-game publisher listed on Nasdaq as MYPS, best known for free-to-play gaming titles and its playAWARDS loyalty platform. Within the 2017–2024 sample, the company’s crypto-adjacent activity appeared in 2022–2023 through a blockchain/Web3 initiative tied to rewards, loyalty, and game-platform features rather than mining, treasury crypto holding, or crypto financial intermediation.

Exposure Channels Identified

The relevant channel is bizserv. In the reviewed sources, PLAYSTUDIOS described a blockchain division, playBLOCKS, meant to add Web3 features to its loyalty and rewards ecosystem, including tokenization, gifting/exchange functionality, and a marketplace concept anchored in its gaming platform rather than direct exchange, custody, mining, or treasury accumulation. The strongest management evidence comes from the August 9, 2022 earnings call, where the CEO said the company was “working on tokenizing our playAWARDS loyalty program” and described playBLOCKS as supporting the company’s broader loyalty-and-rewards vision. The 2023 Form 10-K corroborates this by describing the WonderBlocks acquisition as providing tools for a play-to-earn loyalty platform on Ethereum and saying the deal would enhance playAWARDS with “new Web3 features and capabilities.” This fits non-financial crypto-related services best: PLAYSTUDIOS was building blockchain-enabled product features around its gaming and loyalty ecosystem, but the reviewed evidence does not show that it directly handled customer crypto assets as an exchange, broker, custodian, or issuer of crypto financial products. (gurufocus.com)

Weight Assignment

bizserv 1.0 is the best fit. The evidence supports a single mechanism: PLAYSTUDIOS pursued blockchain-enabled loyalty, marketplace, and game-platform functionality as a product/service extension to its entertainment ecosystem, which is closer to non-financial service provision than to finserv or infrastr. There is no reviewed evidence that the company mined crypto, held bitcoin or other tokens as a treasury strategy, operated a crypto exchange/custody/payments platform, or supplied hardware/compute/energy infrastructure to crypto operators. Because the blockchain effort appears developmental and ancillary to its core gaming business, RevShare and AssetShare are both coded 0. (gurufocus.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is boundary-setting between a Web3-enabled product initiative and true crypto-financial activity. Management used blockchain and Web3 language explicitly in 2022, but the reviewed sources do not show meaningful disclosed crypto revenue, balance-sheet crypto assets, or direct customer handling of digital assets, so finserv was excluded. Transcript access across the full 2017–2024 window was not equally rich; however, at least one anchored transcript quote was retrievable, and the 2023 Form 10-K corroborated the mechanism. No reviewed source explicitly characterized MYPS as a crypto proxy stock or identified a crypto-driven shareholder base, so invbase = 0. (gurufocus.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 9 August 2022, Q2 2022 earnings call transcript: CEO Andrew Pascal said, “We advanced our Web3 strategy, consummating key strategic agreements and seeding a variety of opportunities that align with our rewarded play vision” — Q2 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
  • 9 August 2022, Q2 2022 earnings call transcript: management said it was “working on tokenizing our playAWARDS loyalty program” to let players “gift and exchange rewards” and sell them in “a new myVIP rewards marketplace” — Q2 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
  • 9 August 2022, Q2 2022 earnings call transcript: CEO said the Forte relationship, WonderBlocks investment/acquisition, and Kryptomon investment were “the underpinnings of playBLOCKS, our Blockchain division” — Q2 2022 Earnings Call Transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 12 March 2024, 2023 Form 10-K: PLAYSTUDIOS disclosed that WonderBlocks “provides tools for the development of a play-to-earn loyalty platform for digital entertainment on the Ethereum blockchain” — 2023 Form 10-K
  • 12 March 2024, 2023 Form 10-K: the company said the WonderBlocks acquisition would “enhance our playAWARDS model with new Web3 features and capabilities” — 2023 Form 10-K
  • 8 November 2022, Q3 2022 earnings release furnished on Form 8-K: the company listed as a quarterly highlight that it had “Added head count and continued to make progress in our Web3.0 strategy” — Q3 2022 8-K / Earnings Release

Financial news / other:

  • 3 August 2022, company press release: PLAYSTUDIOS announced a new blockchain division and said its Future Fund would partner with Web3 companies at the intersection of “gaming, loyalty marketing, and blockchain rewards” — Web3 Blockchain Division Press Release
  • 3 August 2022, company press release: PLAYSTUDIOS said investments in Forte and Kryptomon would give it access to Web3 technology, marketing, and community-building expertise as it deepened capabilities in the space — Web3 Blockchain Division Press Release
  • 3 August 2022, TechCrunch: coverage described PLAYSTUDIOS as launching a blockchain gaming division and a $10 million Web3-focused fund built on WonderBlocks and a strategic alliance with Forte, corroborating that the initiative was product/platform oriented rather than mining or treasury holding — TechCrunch Coverage

TEL AVIV STOCK EXCHANGE

  • Rank: 156 | Expo: 0.38 | id_str: IL0011590291
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.7, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.3, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: investor presentation / annual meeting materials (2021–2024), 2021 periodic report, 2018 blockchain lending coverage

Firm Overview

The Tel-Aviv Stock Exchange Ltd. is Israel’s primary securities exchange and operates related trading, clearing, settlement, custody, and depository services through its exchange and clearing-house subsidiaries. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, the reviewed sources show that crypto- and blockchain-related activity was pursued as an extension of its market-infrastructure and exchange-services strategy rather than as treasury holding or mining activity. (mayafiles.tase.co.il)

Exposure Channels Identified

The strongest evidence supports finserv: TASE explicitly presented “creating a platform for digital assets,” alongside a “crypto services (trading/clearing/custody) value proposition,” indicating an intended role in handling trading, clearing, and custody around digital assets rather than merely advising the ecosystem. (mayafiles.tase.co.il)

A secondary infrastr weight is also warranted because the firm repeatedly framed blockchain as part of its exchange technology stack and had already developed and launched a blockchain-based securities-lending product, while later participating in a blockchain platform for a digital government bond proof of concept. Those activities are infrastructure-like because they concern market plumbing and post-trade systems rather than direct balance-sheet crypto investment. (mayafiles.tase.co.il)

Weight Assignment

finserv 0.7 and infrastr 0.3 is the best fit. The higher finserv weight reflects management’s explicit positioning of crypto through exchange-like functions—trading, clearing, and custody for digital assets—which maps directly to crypto financial services. The smaller infrastr weight captures the blockchain-based lending platform and tokenized-bond platform development as enabling market infrastructure. There is no reviewed evidence of proprietary crypto treasury accumulation (holders), mining (miners), or non-financial support services that would justify bizserv. Because the reviewed materials do not show meaningful realized crypto revenue or crypto assets on the balance sheet through December 2024, RevShare and AssetShare are both coded 0. (mayafiles.tase.co.il)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is transcript coverage within the sample window. A post-sample earnings-call transcript was retrievable, but it did not contain crypto discussion, so the classification is anchored instead by an investor-presentation/management materials quote and corroborating filing and news sources; accordingly, certainty is capped at 3 rather than 4. The evidence also leans more toward strategic development and platform building than toward clearly monetized crypto operations by December 31, 2024, so the finserv weight reflects explicit stated direction rather than disclosed material crypto revenues. No reviewed source explicitly identified TASE’s stock as a crypto proxy or documented a crypto-driven investor base, so invbase = 0. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • No in-window 2017–2024 earnings-call transcript with usable crypto passage was retrievable in the reviewed sources; a post-sample 5 March 2026 transcript was checked and did not show crypto language in the accessible text — Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript
  • 2023 investor presentation / management materials: TASE listed “Creating a platform for digital assets and implementing blockchain technology” and “Crypto services (trading/clearing/custody) value proposition” under strategic initiatives — TASE Investor Presentation

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2021 periodic report / annual meeting materials: the company said it had worked to “develop and launch a Blockchain-based product” enabling clearing-house members to conduct securities lending automatically; development cost was stated at NIS 16.3 million and the product was launched on 2 November 2020 — Immediate Report / Annual Meeting Materials
  • 2023 annual-meeting materials: TASE said a May 2023 live test included a mock issuance of “a digital government bond (security token) over a Blockchain platform developed by TASE” — Annual Meeting Materials
  • 2021 periodic report: TASE described itself as engaged in “managing a securities stock exchange and in related activities,” with clearing and depository subsidiaries, supporting the financial-services context for the digital-asset initiative — Immediate Report / Annual Meeting Materials

Financial news / other:

  • 17 May 2018, CoinDesk: TASE was reported to be teaming with Accenture and The Floor to build a Hyperledger-based securities-lending platform aimed at direct lending of financial instruments — CoinDesk Coverage
  • 27 February 2023, CoinDesk search result summary: TASE planned to let customers of non-bank members trade crypto, corroborating direct financial-services ambition in crypto markets — CoinDesk Search Result
  • 6 June 2023, Globes search result summary: TASE, Israel’s Finance Ministry, and technology partners completed a proof of concept for a blockchain-based digital government bond under Project Eden — Globes Coverage

TMX GROUP

  • Rank: 157 | Expo: 0.36 | id_str: CA87262K1057
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 1.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: 2017 annual report and Q4 2017 earnings call transcript, corroborated by TMX press releases and later annual reports

Firm Overview

TMX Group Ltd. is a Canadian market-infrastructure operator whose businesses include stock exchanges, clearing, depository, trust, data, and related technology services. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, the reviewed crypto- and blockchain-related activity fits best as exchange and post-trade technology development rather than crypto trading, custody, mining, or treasury investment. (s21.q4cdn.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The strongest evidence supports infrastr. In its April 6, 2017 press release, TMX said it launched a Distributed Ledger Technology prototype for electronic shareholder voting as part of a strategy to improve efficiencies across its business areas, and its 2017 annual report described that blockchain-based system as a public-company solution for TSX Trust. (investors.tmx.com)

The in-window earnings-call anchor points the same way. On the Q4 2017 analyst call, management said TMX had taken a “leadership position in blockchain initiatives” and was “developing new data and analytics solutions and multi-asset class trading capabilities” using blockchain and other technologies, which indicates infrastructure and market-plumbing experimentation rather than direct crypto-asset intermediation. (alphaspread.com)

No reviewed source within 2017–2024 showed TMX operating a crypto exchange or brokerage, providing direct crypto custody, holding crypto on its balance sheet, or mining digital assets. Later annual reports reviewed for 2023 and 2024 did not surface crypto or digital-asset disclosures, which suggests the blockchain activity remained non-material to reported revenue and assets by December 31, 2024. (investors.tmx.com)

Weight Assignment

infrastr 1.0 is the best fit. The evidence consistently describes TMX using blockchain as part of exchange- and issuer-services technology development, especially shareholder voting and broader trading/data capability exploration, which maps directly to crypto infrastructure under the coding rules. There is not enough evidence for finserv because the reviewed sources do not show TMX directly handling, custodying, issuing, or trading crypto assets, and there is no support for holders, miners, or bizserv. Because no reviewed filing showed meaningful crypto-related revenue or crypto assets on the balance sheet through December 31, 2024, RevShare = 0 and AssetShare = 0. (investors.tmx.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is that the retrieved transcript evidence is concentrated in the earlier part of the sample, especially the Q4 2017 call; later accessible filings reviewed for 2023 and 2024 did not contain crypto-related text. That still permits a non-unclassified assignment because an in-window earnings-call quote was retrievable and is corroborated by company reporting, but certainty remains 3 rather than 4 because the activity appears exploratory and infrastructure-oriented rather than a clearly monetized, recurring crypto business line. No reviewed source explicitly identified TMX shares as a crypto proxy or documented crypto-driven shareholder flows, so invbase = 0. (alphaspread.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • February 13, 2018, Q4 2017 analyst call: “We received the leadership position in blockchain initiatives that can revolutionize and invigorate our existing businesses and the exchange industry” — Alpha Spread transcript
  • February 13, 2018, Q4 2017 analyst call: “We're developing new data and analytics solutions and multi-asset class trading capabilities that deploy breakthrough technologies, including blockchain” — Alpha Spread transcript
  • May 10, 2019, Q1 2019 analyst call: management discussion focused on derivatives, Trayport, analytics, and AI/data science, with no accessible crypto-operating disclosure — The Motley Fool transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 30, 2018, 2017 annual report: TMX said it had “announced a new Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT) prototype” and developed a shareholder-voting system on blockchain with Accenture — 2017 Annual Report
  • March 30, 2018, 2017 annual report: TMX said the blockchain-based voting system aimed to improve voting efficiency and security and increase governance engagement — 2017 Annual Report
  • April 6, 2017, company press release: TMX announced its “first blockchain-based prototype” for electronic shareholder voting through TSX Trust — TMX press release
  • February 5, 2025, 2024 annual report: reviewed later-period annual report did not show crypto or digital-asset disclosures material to 2024 results — 2024 Annual Report

Financial news / other:

  • May 22, 2017, CoinDesk: TMX’s NGX natural-gas exchange was reported to be piloting a blockchain beta with Nuco, corroborating experimentation in market technology rather than direct crypto finance — CoinDesk coverage
  • April 7, 2017, CoinDesk: TMX’s voting prototype was reported as using Hyperledger for shareholder-voting use cases — CoinDesk coverage

IZEA WORLDWIDE

  • Rank: 158 | Expo: 0.36 | id_str: US46604H2040
  • Weight summary: holders 0.6, miners 0.1, finserv 0.3, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2017, 2023, 2024), Q4 2024 earnings call, company crypto press releases

Firm Overview

IZEA Worldwide, Inc. is a U.S.-listed influencer marketing and creator-economy company that provides managed services, marketplaces, and software connecting brands with creators. Within 2017–2024, its crypto exposure appeared as a side initiative around cryptocurrency mining, treasury holdings of Bitcoin and Ethereum, and limited acceptance of crypto payments for campaigns rather than as a core operating segment. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

holders is the largest channel because IZEA explicitly disclosed purchased Bitcoin and Ethereum on its balance sheet and recognized gains, losses, and impairment/fair-value adjustments on those digital assets. The 2023 10-K states that the company purchased 2.29 Bitcoin and 29.01 Ethereum on December 31, 2021 and still held $162,905 of digital assets at December 31, 2023; the 2024 10-K then says it sold all digital assets in September 2024. (sec.gov)

There is also evidence for finserv, but only as a secondary channel, because IZEA announced in January 2022 that it would accept Bitcoin and Ethereum as payment for influencer marketing services and would pay select influencers in cryptocurrency when marketers paid in crypto. That activity involved direct receipt and onward transfer of digital assets, but reviewed filings suggest it was limited and later discontinued; IZEA said in a December 11, 2024 notice that due to limited interest it had ended the program and did not currently support crypto payments. (globenewswire.com)

A small miners weight is warranted because the 2017 annual report said IZEA maintained a small cryptocurrency mining operation and had developed CPU mining software, while the 2023 10-K later stated that mining operations had ceased in 2019. That makes mining real but historically small and non-enduring by the latter part of the sample. The accessible in-window earnings-call anchor is the March 27, 2025 Q4 2024 call, which discussed the December 31, 2024 balance sheet without any crypto business line, consistent with the 2024 filing showing the company had exited its residual digital-asset position by September 2024. (annualreports.com)

Weight Assignment

A mixed classification best fits the evidence: holders 0.6, finserv 0.3, and miners 0.1. Holdings receive the highest weight because the balance-sheet evidence is the clearest and persists into 2023, with explicit BTC and ETH amounts and accounting treatment. Financial-services exposure is meaningful but smaller because accepting crypto payments and paying select creators in crypto touched digital assets directly, yet appears experimental and was later discontinued. Mining receives only a residual weight because management/filing evidence describes it as a small historical activity that had already ceased by 2019. RevShare = 1 because any crypto-related revenue appears peripheral to the firm’s main influencer-marketing business, and AssetShare = 1 because digital assets were present but small relative to the company’s broader asset base. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is transcript evidence. An accessible in-window earnings-call transcript was retrieved for Q4 2024, but it did not discuss crypto directly; the crypto classification is therefore anchored by an in-window earnings call mainly as a late-period absence check and relies more heavily on 10-Ks and company press releases for the substantive crypto evidence. Certainty is therefore 3 rather than 4. No reviewed source explicitly identified IZEA shares as a crypto proxy or documented crypto-driven shareholder flows, so invbase = 0. (alphaspread.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 27, 2025, Q4 2024 earnings call: “As of December 31, 2024, we had $51.1 million in cash and investments” with no crypto business line discussed on the call, consistent with later-period exit from residual digital-asset exposure — Alpha Spread transcript
  • March 27, 2025, Q4 2024 earnings call: “For a more detailed discussion of full year results, please refer to our Form 10-K” — Alpha Spread transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 28, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K: “Historically, we mined digital assets (mining operations ceased in 2019) and purchased digital assets on exchanges” — SEC 2023 10-K
  • March 28, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K: “As of December 31, 2023, the Company held $96,722 of Bitcoin [and] $66,183 of Ethereum with total holdings in digital assets of $162,905” — SEC 2023 10-K
  • March 28, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K: “The Company purchased 2.29 Bitcoin and 29.01 Ethereum on December 31, 2021” — SEC 2023 10-K
  • March 31, 2025, 2024 Form 10-K: “In September 2024, the Company sold all its digital assets for total proceeds of $0.2 million” — SEC 2024 10-K
  • March 30, 2018, 2017 annual report: “IZEA maintains a small cryptocurrency mining operation” — 2017 Annual Report
  • January 11, 2022, company press release: IZEA said it “now accepts Bitcoin and Ethereum as payments for its influencer marketing services” and would pay select influencers in crypto where marketers paid in crypto — GlobeNewswire press release
  • January 16, 2018, company press release: IZEA announced “IZEA Crypto™,” a blockchain and cryptocurrency initiative for the creator economy — IZEA press release

Financial news / other:

  • December 11, 2024, company blog / notice: IZEA said that after a 2022 launch of crypto payment options, “due to limited interest, we discontinued this program” and “currently, IZEA does not support cryptocurrency payments” — IZEA notice
  • March 28, 2023/2024 archive summary: annual-report archive reproduced that IZEA sold $33,921 of digital assets in 2021 and held crypto-related line items in subsequent filings, corroborating non-core but real exposure — AnnualReports archive

CARECLOUD

  • Rank: 159 | Expo: 0.36 | id_str: US14167R1005
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2022–2024 earnings call transcripts, 2022–2024 SEC filings

Firm Overview

CareCloud, Inc. is a U.S.-listed healthcare technology company that provides revenue-cycle management, cloud software, digital health, staffing, and related business services to medical practices and health systems. Across the reviewed 2017–2024 materials, the company presents itself as a healthcare IT and services business rather than a crypto-related company. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto exposure was identified in the reviewed source set. Searches of the company’s 2023 Form 10-K produced no references to bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain, and the accessible earnings-call transcripts reviewed for Q4 2022, Q4 2023, and Q4 2024 likewise contained no bitcoin-related discussion. (sec.gov)

Because the classification framework requires substantive, firm-specific evidence of holdings, mining, financial services, non-financial crypto services, infrastructure provision, or crypto-linked investor-base dynamics, the absence of such evidence supports an unclass assignment. Nothing in the reviewed transcripts or filings indicates direct handling of digital assets, mining operations, blockchain infrastructure supply, or crypto-specific service revenue. (ir.carecloud.com)

Weight Assignment

The weight is assigned entirely to unclass 1.0 because the evidence review did not uncover any substantive crypto-related activity during the January 2017 to December 2024 sample window. The accessible earnings-call anchor is the March 2, 2023 Q4 2022 conference-call transcript, which discusses healthcare IT growth initiatives and the ticker change to CCLD but contains no crypto content; later accessible Q4 2023 and Q4 2024 transcripts are consistent with that absence. RevShare and AssetShare are therefore NA under the project rules for unclassified firms. (ir.carecloud.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is that some Seeking Alpha transcript pages were inaccessible due to fetch restrictions, so the transcript review relied on an investor-relations PDF for Q4 2022 and accessible transcript mirrors/search snippets for Q4 2023 and Q4 2024. However, because the reviewed filings and transcript searches were uniformly negative for bitcoin/crypto/blockchain terms, this limitation does not appear to affect the classification outcome. invbase = 0 because no reviewed source described CareCloud shares as a crypto proxy or documented crypto-driven shareholder flows. ()

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 2, 2023, Q4 2022 earnings call transcript: “Thank you, Kim, and thanks to all of you for joining us for our Fourth Quarter and Full Year Earnings Call”; reviewed transcript discusses healthcare operations and contains no bitcoin discussion — CareCloud IR transcript PDF
  • March 2, 2023, Q4 2022 earnings call transcript: “We changed our common stock ticker symbol, from MTBC to CCLD, to better align with our corporate brand” — CareCloud IR transcript PDF
  • March 21, 2024, Q4 2023 earnings call transcript: “Thank you, Chantelle, and thanks to all of you for joining our fourth quarter and full-year earnings call”; reviewed accessible transcript excerpt showed no crypto-related discussion — ROIC AI transcript
  • March 13, 2025, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript: “Thank you, Kristen, and thanks, everyone, for joining us today on the fourth quarter 2024 earnings call”; reviewed accessible transcript excerpt showed no crypto-related discussion — ROIC AI transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 28, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K: CareCloud described itself as a provider of technology-enabled healthcare revenue cycle management, software, digital health, staffing, and practice-management services, with no crypto business line disclosed — SEC 2023 Form 10-K
  • March 28, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K search check: no matches for “bitcoin” — SEC 2023 Form 10-K
  • March 28, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K search check: no matches for “crypto” — SEC 2023 Form 10-K
  • March 28, 2024, 2023 Form 10-K search check: no matches for “blockchain” — SEC 2023 Form 10-K
  • March 31, 2025, 2024 Form 10-K: 2024 filing continued to describe CareCloud as a healthcare SaaS and services company, with no crypto-related segment disclosure in the retrieved filing text — SEC 2024 Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • February 2024, Investing.com earnings-call coverage: summary focused on profitability, free cash flow, healthcare technology, and operations, with no crypto angle identified — Investing.com coverage
  • November 2024, Investing.com earnings-call coverage: summary focused on AI, RPM, and growth initiatives, with no crypto-related characterization — Investing.com coverage
  • Reuters preview item surfaced in search results covered ordinary earnings expectations rather than any crypto-proxy framing — Reuters/TradingView item

ENTHUSIAST GAMING HOLDINGS

  • Rank: 160 | Expo: 0.35 | id_str: CA29385B1094
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.6, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.4
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings-call transcripts (Q4 2021, Q3 2022, Q1 2023), company press releases (March 2022), MD&A / annual financial statements (2022–2024)

Firm Overview

Enthusiast Gaming Holdings Inc. is a Canadian-listed gaming media and entertainment company focused on digital media, esports, subscriptions, creators, and gaming-related experiences rather than core crypto operations. Across the reviewed 2021–2024 materials, crypto exposure appeared only through limited Web3/NFT-related game integrations and sponsorship partnerships around EV.IO and Luminosity, while the company’s main revenue base remained advertising, media/content, esports, and subscriptions. (enthusiastgaming.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The strongest evidence points to bizserv exposure, not finserv, holders, or miners. Management explicitly discussed “exploring NFT opportunities,” a partnership with Hut 8 Mining, an NFT mint via Fractal, and “Web 3.0 opportunities,” but these activities were framed as fan engagement, sponsorship activation, game content, and new purchasing experiences inside EV.IO rather than direct custody, exchange, brokerage, or treasury ownership of crypto assets. (roic.ai)

This remains a borderline case because the company did touch blockchain/NFT-related products in 2022, including thousands of NFTs minted and integrated into EV.IO. However, the later 2024 MD&A and financial statements show ordinary gaming-media revenue categories and no disclosed bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain assets on the balance sheet, suggesting crypto was peripheral and not a sustained core line of business. (nasdaq.com)

Weight Assignment

A majority weight is assigned to bizserv 0.6 because the firm clearly provided crypto-adjacent, non-financial services and content experiences through sponsorship, NFT integration, and Web3-themed game activations in 2022, all centered on engagement and marketing rather than handling digital assets as a financial intermediary. unclass 0.4 remains appropriate because the exposure looks episodic and economically peripheral relative to the company’s main business, and later 2023–2024 disclosures do not show a material crypto segment, mining operation, or crypto treasury. RevShare is coded 1 because any crypto-related revenue appears minor relative to total company revenue; AssetShare is 0 because no crypto asset position was evidenced in the reviewed filings. (roic.ai)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is whether NFT-enabled EV.IO activity should be treated as meaningful crypto exposure or as a short-lived marketing/product experiment. The evidence supports some non-financial crypto adjacency in 2022, but the absence of corresponding 2023–2024 balance-sheet or segment disclosures limits confidence about durability and materiality. invbase = 0 because no reviewed source described Enthusiast Gaming shares as a crypto proxy or documented crypto-driven shareholder flows. (globenewswire.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • March 28, 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: “Last quarter, I spoke about exploring NFT opportunities” — ROIC AI transcript
  • March 28, 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: “we recently announced that we are releasing custom NFTs via a partnership with the Fractal marketplace” — ROIC AI transcript
  • March 28, 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: “We’re plugging into Web 3.0 opportunities with the right partners at the right time” — ROIC AI transcript
  • November 14, 2022, Q3 2022 earnings call script: “Today, Enthusiast Gaming owns some of the largest fan communities in the world”; reviewed script discusses media, games, and advertising with no sustained crypto business line — Enthusiast Gaming Q3 2022 script
  • May 15, 2023, Q1 2023 earnings call script: “Community commerce is an area of strategic focus”; reviewed script contains no bitcoin discussion and emphasizes gaming monetization rather than crypto finance — Enthusiast Gaming Q1 2023 script

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 2, 2022, company press release: Enthusiast and Hut 8 said they would “collaborate on new experiences and content within mobile and blockchain gaming, Web 3.0, NFTs, and cryptocurrency” — GlobeNewswire press release
  • March 15, 2022, company press release: Fractal partnership was described as the company’s first NFT marketplace partnership, adding play-to-earn capabilities to EV.IO — Enthusiast Gaming press release
  • August 15, 2022, Q2 2022 results release: the company said the Fractal activation resulted in over 3,500 NFTs being minted and integrated into EV.IO — GlobeNewswire Q2 2022 results
  • March 27, 2023, year-end 2022 results: the company said the Fractal partnership resulted in over 6,000 NFTs being minted and integrated into EV.IO — Nasdaq-hosted 2022 results release
  • November 2024, Q3 2024 MD&A: revenue was broken out into media and content, esports and entertainment, and subscriptions, with no matches for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” or “blockchain” in the retrieved document — Q3 2024 MD&A
  • March 2025, FY2024 financial statements: subsidiaries and assets were listed in ordinary operating categories, with no crypto asset disclosure identified in the retrieved statements — FY2024 financial statements

Financial news / other:

  • March 2, 2022, company/GlobeNewswire coverage emphasized that this was Enthusiast Gaming’s first partnership with a digital asset miner and included data-centre hosting support from Hut 8 — GlobeNewswire partnership release
  • 2024–2025 investor materials and results pages continued to frame the firm as a gaming media and entertainment company rather than a crypto company — Enthusiast Gaming investors page
  • March 31, 2025, Q4/FY2024 results press release highlighted audience expansion, monetization, EBITDA, and gaming properties, with no crypto framing in the retrieved summary — Enthusiast Gaming FY2024 results

MONEYLION A DEAD - DELIST.17/04/25

  • Rank: 161 | Expo: 0.35 | id_str: US60938K3041
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: 2024 10-K filing, 2022 Investor Day transcript, Q1 2023 earnings call transcript

Firm Overview

MoneyLion Inc. was a U.S.-listed consumer fintech platform offering banking, borrowing, investing, financial content, and marketplace products before being acquired by Gen Digital on April 17, 2025. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, its crypto exposure came through MoneyLion Crypto, a customer-facing digital-asset offering enabled through Zero Hash rather than through proprietary mining or treasury holdings. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports finserv exposure because MoneyLion offered crypto access to retail customers through its app and earned a share of transaction-related fees from those activities. The 2024 10-K states that under its agreement with Zero Hash, MoneyLion received “a share of the fees” earned from customer digital-currency transactions and that Bitcoin, Bitcoin Cash, Ether, and Litecoin were available through MoneyLion Crypto. (sec.gov) Transcript evidence is consistent with this classification: at MoneyLion’s 2022 Investor Day, the CFO explicitly included “crypto commissions” within consumer revenue, and the product presentation discussed users with indicated interest in crypto and knowledge of their holdings. (moneylion.com, moneylion.com) There is no evidence that MoneyLion mined crypto, held crypto as a treasury asset, or provided crypto infrastructure; the 10-K instead says it was not directly involved in custody, trading, or pricing, indicating platform/intermediary financial-service exposure rather than holder or infrastructure exposure. (sec.gov, moneylion.com)

Weight Assignment

A full weight is assigned to finserv 1.0 because the firm’s crypto activity was clearly a financial-service channel: it enabled customer crypto transactions, monetized those through fee sharing and commissions, and marketed the service as part of its consumer finance app. No offsetting weight is given to holders, miners, bizserv, or infrastr because the reviewed sources show no strategic balance-sheet crypto position, no mining operation, no non-financial crypto-services line, and no hardware/hosting role. RevShare is coded 1 because crypto commissions were mentioned as a component of consumer revenue but no evidence suggests they were more than a minor share of total revenue. AssetShare is coded 0 because the reviewed evidence indicates customer crypto exposure was facilitated via Zero Hash and does not show a meaningful MoneyLion-owned crypto asset position. (moneylion.com, sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is that readily retrievable quarterly earnings-call transcripts from 2023–2024 did not surface detailed crypto discussion, so the strongest transcript anchor comes from the December 2022 Investor Day transcript rather than a standard quarter-end call. That still satisfies the transcript requirement and is corroborated by the 2024 10-K and product materials, but it limits precision on how material crypto commissions remained by late 2024. invbase = 0 because no reviewed transcript, filing, or financial-news source described MoneyLion shares as a crypto proxy or documented crypto-driven shareholder flows. (moneylion.com, sec.gov, d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • December 2022, MoneyLion Investor Day financial update transcript: “we also make money from tips and make money from interest income and crypto commissions” — MoneyLion Investor Day Financial Update transcript
  • December 2022, MoneyLion Investor Day consumer products transcript: “Our algorithms know that this kind of content is right up my alley not only because of indicated interest in crypto but also because it knows my holdings as well” — MoneyLion Investor Day Consumer Products transcript
  • May 9, 2023, Q1 2023 earnings call transcript: reviewed transcript emphasizes consumer and enterprise revenue mix, lending, marketplace, and content, with no retrieved direct crypto discussion in the call text — FactSet/CallStreet transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 2025, 2024 Form 10-K: under the Zero Hash agreement, MoneyLion said Zero Hash pays it a share of the fees earned from customer digital-currency transactions — 2024 Form 10-K
  • February 2025, 2024 Form 10-K: “As of December 31, 2024, the only digital assets available through MoneyLion Crypto were Bitcoin, Bitcoin Cash, Ether and Litecoin” — 2024 Form 10-K
  • February 2025, 2024 Form 10-K: MoneyLion said it was “not directly involved in the custody, trading or pricing of any digital assets” and instead enabled Zero Hash to offer the service — 2024 Form 10-K
  • March 2022, fourth-quarter 2021 results release: the company described itself as providing banking, borrowing, and investing solutions tailored to customers’ needs — Q4 2021 results release

Financial news / other:

  • November 2021, MoneyLion product page: “Neither MoneyLion, nor Pathward, is involved in the purchase, sale, exchange of fiat funds for cryptocurrency, or custody of the cryptocurrencies” — MoneyLion Crypto product explainer
  • 2022 Investor Day page: MoneyLion published dedicated product and financial transcripts showing crypto-related product and revenue discussion as part of the platform offering — MoneyLion Investor Day page
  • April 17, 2025, Gen Digital press coverage on the post-sample acquisition confirms the entity identification as MoneyLion, the consumer-finance app later acquired by Gen — Gen / MoneyLion corporate page

COMSOVEREIGN HOLDING

  • Rank: 162 | Expo: 0.35 | id_str: US2056504010
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
