  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2020–2022), Q2 2022 Form 10-Q, company materials and financial news

Firm Overview

COMSovereign Holding Corp. was a U.S.-listed telecommunications equipment and solutions company focused on 4G LTE, 5G, edge-compute, network radios, batteries, and related communications infrastructure rather than digital-asset activities. Across the reviewed 2020–2022 SEC filings and company materials retrieved for the January 2017 to December 2024 sample window, the firm was presented as a telecom and wireless-network business undergoing financial distress and restructuring, not as a crypto-exposed company. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto exposure channel was identified. The reviewed annual and quarterly SEC filings did not surface references to bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain, and the business description centered on telecom equipment, wireless transport, small cells, edge computing, and power systems. (sec.gov)

The evidence also does not support classification as infrastr, because although the company supplied communications and network infrastructure, the retrieved sources did not document hardware, compute, hosting, or energy capacity targeted at mining or blockchain workloads. There was likewise no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, exchange/brokerage/custody activity, or non-financial service provision to crypto firms. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

A full weight is assigned to unclass 1.0 because the reviewed source set shows COMSovereign as a telecom-equipment and network-solutions company with no substantive, credible crypto references during the sample period. No weight is given to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr, since neither filings nor other retrieved materials show balance-sheet crypto, mining revenue, crypto financial intermediation, service work for crypto clients, or crypto-directed infrastructure demand. invbase = 0 because no reviewed source described COMS as a crypto proxy or linked its shareholder flows to crypto sentiment. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A clear earnings-call transcript with crypto discussion was not retrievable, and the searches did not surface a standard transcript evidencing any digital-asset activity. That limitation does not affect the unclass outcome here, because the stronger issue is the absence of any crypto reference in the reviewed filings and business descriptions. (sec.gov)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Earnings-call transcript search across 2021–2024 did not retrieve a COMS transcript containing bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain discussion; no transcript anchor for non-unclassified exposure was found — Seeking Alpha COMS page
  • Retrieved search results for COMS were company-page and filing references rather than substantive transcript evidence of any crypto activity — Seeking Alpha COMS SEC filings page

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 30, 2021, 2020 Form 10-K: COMSovereign described itself as a provider of “technologically-advanced telecom solutions” focused on 4G LTE, 5G, backhaul radios, small cells, and edge-compute capabilities — 2020 Form 10-K
  • February 2023 filing of June 30, 2022 Form 10-Q: the quarterly filing discusses delayed reports, Nasdaq compliance, telecom operations, asset sales, and impairments, with no retrieved crypto terminology — Q2 2022 Form 10-Q
  • Text search of the 2020 Form 10-K returned no matches for “blockchain,” “bitcoin,” or “crypto” — 2020 Form 10-K text search results
  • Text search of the Q2 2022 Form 10-Q returned no matches for “blockchain,” “bitcoin,” or “crypto” — Q2 2022 Form 10-Q text search results

Financial news / other:

  • 2024 financial news coverage characterized COMSovereign as a failed “made in USA” 5G telecom story, reinforcing telecom rather than crypto exposure — Light Reading article
  • 2021 company investor materials presented COMSovereign around 4G, 5G, IoT, deployable LTE, and telecom radios, with no retrieved crypto focus — Company investor presentation filing

GLOBANT

  • Rank: 163 | Expo: 0.35 | id_str: LU0974299876
  • Weight summary: holders 0.1, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.9, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings-call transcripts (Q2 2021, Q2 2023), 20-F / annual-report disclosures (2020–2024), company blockchain materials

Firm Overview

Globant S.A. is a Luxembourg-domiciled, NYSE-listed digital engineering and IT-services company that helps enterprises with software, design, cloud, AI, and related transformation work across multiple industries. Within the 2017–2024 sample window, the retrieved materials show crypto exposure as a small adjacent activity through its Blockchain Studio and related consulting/development services, rather than as a core line of business or a crypto-native operating model. (investors.globant.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports a primary bizserv classification. In the Q2 2021 earnings call, management described a dedicated blockchain studio and said that “the rise of NFTs, cryptocurrency adoption and use of private and semiprivate blockchains has created a surge in demand,” which indicates service work for crypto/blockchain-related client needs rather than exchange, custody, or principal investing activity. (fool.com)

The 2020 Form 20-F and company blockchain pages corroborate that Globant offers blockchain consulting, tokenization, smart-contract, DeFi, and platform-development services for clients. Those activities fit the project definition of non-financial crypto services because the retrieved sources frame Globant as a technology integrator and developer, not as a direct handler of customer crypto assets. (sec.gov)

A small holders weight is also warranted because public disclosures and contemporaneous coverage indicate that Globant purchased Bitcoin in 2021 as a balance-sheet asset. However, the retrieved evidence does not suggest that treasury crypto became a major or recurring strategic pillar of the company relative to its much larger software-services business. (investing.com)

Weight Assignment

bizserv receives 0.9 because the dominant, repeatedly evidenced mechanism is enterprise blockchain-related consulting and software development through Globant’s Blockchain Studio and related offerings, including tokenization, smart contracts, and DeFi implementation for clients. holders receives 0.1 because there is evidence of a modest Bitcoin treasury purchase in 2021, but the available sources do not indicate that balance-sheet crypto was economically material or central to management messaging during 2017–2024. No weight is assigned to finserv, miners, or infrastr: the reviewed sources do not show Globant operating an exchange, brokerage, custody platform, mining operation, or crypto-directed hardware/hosting infrastructure. invbase = 0 because the retrieved sources do not describe GLOB as a market-traded crypto proxy or document shareholder flows tied directly to crypto sentiment. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The SEC filing containing the 2021 Bitcoin disclosure was surfaced in search results and secondary reporting, but direct opening of the archived filing failed in the browsing tool, so the Bitcoin-holder evidence is corroborated here with secondary-but-contemporaneous coverage and treasury tracking rather than a directly scraped SEC passage. That limitation keeps the holders weight modest and prevents a higher certainty score. The retrieved transcript evidence is stronger for bizserv than for holders, and no transcript located in the sample period emphasized crypto as a material revenue segment. ()

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • August 12, 2021, Q2 2021 earnings call transcript: “the rise of NFTs, cryptocurrency adoption and use of private and semiprivate blockchains has created a surge in demand” — Motley Fool Q2 2021 transcript
  • August 12, 2021, Q2 2021 earnings call transcript: management introduced “Globant's blockchain studio” and discussed adoption across industries — Motley Fool Q2 2021 transcript
  • August 17, 2023, Q2 2023 earnings call transcript: management said product evolution was “accelerated by AI and blockchain,” indicating ongoing but secondary blockchain relevance in the platform portfolio — Seeking Alpha Q2 2023 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • March 2021, 2020 Form 20-F: Globant listed “Blockchain” among its studio capabilities, describing solutions that harness blockchain technology for client business needs — 2020 Form 20-F search result
  • February 28, 2025, annual-report notice covering FY2024: Globant described itself as a “digitally native technology services company,” consistent with a services-led rather than crypto-native model — Globant Files Annual Report for 2024
  • 2018 integrated report / 2017 Form 20-F extract: Globant stated that it provided research and development services over multiple blockchains including Ethereum, Bitcoin, and Hyperledger — 2017 integrated report extract

Financial news / other:

  • 2022 company blockchain page: Globant said it designs decentralized solutions and offers digital tokenization, smart contracts, DeFi, and blockchain platform consulting/development — Globant Blockchain Studio
  • October 21, 2022, company news: Globant said “The value of blockchain is not only found in crypto asset management or new ways of interacting with money,” while presenting its Blockchain Studio as a client-service offering — Globant launches new Blockchain Sentinel Report
  • May 2021, market coverage: Investing.com reported that Globant disclosed a roughly $500,000 Bitcoin purchase in Q1 2021 — Investing.com coverage
  • 2025 treasury tracking summarizing public disclosures: CoinTreasuries noted no concrete later update beyond the initial 2021 Bitcoin purchase — CoinTreasuries Globant entry

SPROTT

  • Rank: 164 | Expo: 0.35 | id_str: CA8520662088
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2024 annual report, investor relations materials, 2023 strategy commentary, earnings-call archive search

Firm Overview

Sprott Inc. is a Canada-based publicly listed asset manager whose investor materials describe the firm as focused on precious metals and critical materials investments, including physical bullion trusts, mining ETFs, managed equities and private strategies. The retrieved company and filing sources consistently frame Sprott’s business around metals, mining and critical materials rather than around cryptocurrencies or digital-asset financial products.

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto exposure channel was identified for Sprott during the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period. The strongest company disclosures reviewed describe exchange-listed products and private strategies tied to precious metals and critical materials, while the 2024 annual report contains no retrieved references to bitcoin, crypto, blockchain, ethereum or digital assets.

A Sprott-authored 2023 gold strategy commentary does discuss Bitcoin and cryptocurrencies, but only as macro comparison material in an argument for gold as a safer asset. That does not meet the project threshold for any non-zero mechanism weight because it does not evidence treasury holdings, mining, crypto financial services, crypto-focused business services, or crypto-targeted infrastructure.

Weight Assignment

unclass receives 1.0 because the retrieved evidence does not show Sprott holding crypto on balance sheet, mining crypto, operating crypto exchanges/custody/investment products, providing non-financial services to crypto firms, or supplying crypto-directed infrastructure. The firm’s identified business model is commodity and precious-metals asset management, and the isolated crypto references found were commentary-level comparisons rather than evidence of substantive operating exposure. invbase = 0 because the reviewed sources do not describe Sprott as a crypto proxy stock or document shareholder flows tied to crypto sentiment.

Ambiguities / Limitations

A dedicated earnings-call transcript quote specifically about crypto could not be retrieved from the available sources searched; however, because the classification is unclass = 1.0, the transcript-anchor requirement for non-unclassified assignments does not alter the coding outcome. The browsing results did surface Sprott commentary pieces mentioning Bitcoin, but these were used as exclusion evidence only: they show Sprott discussing crypto as a comparison point in market commentary, not participating in the crypto economy through any project-defined mechanism. None identified beyond that.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • Transcript archive search, Seeking Alpha transcript index: Sprott has a broad earnings-call record across 2020–2024, but the retrieved archive/search results did not surface crypto-focused transcript evidence for the sample-period classification — Seeking Alpha SII transcript archive
  • Investor relations webcast archive, company site: Sprott’s webcast history centers on quarterly and annual earnings calls for the asset-management business, with no retrieved crypto product framing in the archive page — Sprott webcast archive

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • February 2025, 2024 Annual Report: Sprott described its investment solutions as covering “precious metals and critical materials” and its businesses as exchange-listed products, managed equities and private strategies — Sprott 2024 Annual Report
  • 2024 Annual Report text search: no matches were found in the annual report for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” “digital asset,” “blockchain,” or “ethereum,” supporting a no-exposure classification — Sprott 2024 Annual Report
  • Investor relations page: Sprott presents itself to shareholders as an NYSE/TSX-listed asset manager and co-investor in its own products, without any retrieved digital-asset product line — Sprott Investor Relations

Financial news / other:

  • Company “About Us” page: Sprott states that it is a global asset manager focused on precious metals and critical materials, with offerings including physical bullion trusts, mining ETFs, and private equity and debt strategies — About Sprott
  • April 3, 2023, Sprott Gold Equities Strategy commentary: Sprott wrote that “Cryptocurrencies were considered to be safe” but argued they had performed poorly relative to gold; this is market commentary rather than evidence of crypto business activity — Sprott Gold Equities Strategy Q1 2023 Commentary
  • Sprott special report “Is My Money Safe?”: the firm compared Bitcoin and gold in a macro/safe-haven discussion, again indicating commentary usage rather than a crypto exposure mechanism — Sprott special report

DBS GROUP HOLDINGS

  • Rank: 165 | Expo: 0.34 | id_str: SG1L01001701
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: analyst conference call transcripts (2021–2022), annual reports (2020, 2023, 2024), company digital-exchange press release, financial news coverage

Firm Overview

DBS Group Holdings Ltd is a Singapore-listed banking group and one of Asia’s largest financial institutions, with core activities in consumer banking, institutional banking, wealth management and treasury markets. Within that broader banking franchise, the retrieved 2020–2024 sources show DBS building a regulated digital-asset platform spanning trading, custody and related structured products for institutional, accredited and selected wealth clients.

Exposure Channels Identified

finserv is the clear exposure channel. DBS explicitly launched DBS Digital Exchange to provide tokenisation, trading and custody services for digital assets, and later disclosed growth in bitcoin trading activity, custody balances, crypto derivatives activity and crypto-linked structured products. The evidence fits the project’s crypto financial services definition because DBS directly handles trading access, custody and product issuance around digital assets rather than merely supplying supporting software or infrastructure.

No evidence from the retrieved sample-period sources supports holders, miners, bizserv, or infrastr. The reviewed materials describe customer-facing digital-asset financial intermediation, not treasury accumulation of crypto for DBS’s own balance sheet, mining operations, non-financial advisory services, or crypto-targeted hardware/compute provision.

Weight Assignment

finserv receives 1.0 because DBS’s crypto exposure is consistently described through direct financial services: a bank-backed digital exchange, institutional-grade custody, tokenisation, crypto derivatives activity and crypto-linked structured notes. Although crypto appears to be a small part of the group relative to DBS’s very large conventional banking revenues and assets, it is still a substantive and repeatedly documented business line, so unclass is not appropriate. invbase = 0 because the retrieved evidence discusses client demand for digital assets, but does not directly document DBS shares trading as a crypto proxy or management commentary about a crypto-driven shareholder base.

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is materiality, not mechanism. Retrieved sources clearly establish crypto financial services exposure, but they do not disclose a large standalone crypto revenue line for the listed group, so RevShare is best coded as minor rather than higher. There was also no retrieved evidence that DBS held crypto as a discretionary treasury asset; mentions of custody balances and product backing were treated under finserv, consistent with the exclusion rules. None identified beyond that.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 14 Feb 2022, analyst conference call transcript: “we started doing the work on seeing how we can in a sensible way take it out and expand it beyond the greater investor base” — DBS 4Q21 analyst conference call transcript
  • 13 Feb 2023, analyst conference call transcript: Piyush Gupta said, “Another is our digital asset ecosystem… tokenised assets do have a role to play in the future and clients are keen to participate in these kinds of opportunities.” — DBS 4Q22 analyst conference call transcript
  • Transcript archive spanning 2017–2024: DBS has a broad earnings transcript history, with sample-period analyst-call materials available through the investor relations archive and 2021–2024 transcript listings on Seeking Alpha — Seeking Alpha DBS transcript archive
  • Investor relations quarterly archive: DBS hosts analyst call transcripts for 2019–2024, which were used to test whether crypto discussion was recurring rather than incidental — DBS quarterly financials archive

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2020 annual report: DBS disclosed that in January 2021 it launched an “in-house digital exchange” focused on security token offerings, digital currency exchange and digital custody for institutional clients — DBS Annual Report 2020
  • 2023 annual report: DBS wrote that transaction value on its digital exchange “continued to grow and crossed SGD 1 billion” despite a broader decline in crypto market capitalisation — DBS Annual Report 2023
  • 2024 annual report: DBS stated that “new business included partnerships with stablecoin issuers and the launch of crypto options trading and structured notes” — DBS Annual Report 2024
  • 15 Feb 2023, company release: DBS reported 80% year-on-year growth in bitcoin traded on DBS Digital Exchange in 2022 and a doubling in bitcoin custodised with its digital-asset custody solution — DBS news release on DDEx activity

Financial news / other:

  • 10 Dec 2020, CNBC: DBS was reported to be setting up a digital-asset exchange offering tokenisation, trading and custody services to institutional and accredited investors — CNBC coverage of DBS digital exchange launch
  • 15 Feb 2023, Bloomberg: Bloomberg reported that DBS saw bitcoin trading rise on its digital exchange during the 2022 crypto downturn, corroborating that the activity was market-recognised and ongoing — Bloomberg on DBS Digital Exchange growth
  • 15 Feb 2023, CoinDesk: CoinDesk described DDEx as a crypto exchange limited to institutional and high-net-worth clients and noted close to 1,200 participants on the platform — CoinDesk on DBS DDEx

CONIFEX TIMBER

  • Rank: 166 | Expo: 0.34 | id_str: CA2073241044
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.4, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.6, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: company quarterly results / MD&A (2021, 2024), annual information form / annual financial statements (2022–2024), financial-news/legal coverage of the BC crypto-mining moratorium

Firm Overview

Conifex Timber Inc. is a Canadian forest-products company focused on timber harvesting, sawmilling and bioenergy generation in British Columbia. Within the sample period, it also pursued a revenue-diversification strategy centered on hosting and developing high-performance computing data-centre capacity tied explicitly to cryptocurrency mining at sites supported by its power assets.

Exposure Channels Identified

infrastr and miners are the relevant exposure channels. Conifex’s own 9 November 2021 quarterly-results release said its site was “ideally suited to host data center or other high-performance computing (“HPC”) operations, such as cryptocurrency mining,” and that it intended to host about 3 megawatts of bitcoin mining capacity on a trial basis with a partner. (conifex.com)

The evidence fits infrastr because management repeatedly framed the opportunity around hosting services, site suitability, power access and data-centre buildout rather than operating a standalone exchange, custody platform, treasury strategy or non-financial advisory service. A secondary miners weight is still warranted because the company explicitly referred to hosting bitcoin mining capacity and later litigated over BC Hydro’s suspension of service to new cryptocurrency mining projects that affected two proposed HPC projects. (conifex.com)

Weight Assignment

infrastr receives 0.6 and miners 0.4. The larger infrastr weight reflects that Conifex’s disclosed crypto adjacency was principally about using its power-generation and site infrastructure to host HPC / data-centre customers and expand a hosting business in phases; this is infrastructure provision under the project rules. miners receives a meaningful but smaller weight because management also specifically referenced bitcoin mining capacity and cryptocurrency-mining projects, indicating direct exposure to mining activity even if the equipment was expected to be invested in, operated and maintained by a partner rather than clearly by Conifex itself. holders, finserv, and bizserv are unsupported, and unclass is inappropriate because crypto-related activity was explicitly discussed by management and in filings. invbase = 0 because the retrieved sources do not show Conifex stock being discussed as a crypto proxy or management commenting on a crypto-driven shareholder base. (conifex.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is whether Conifex should be treated more as a host / infrastructure provider or as a miner. The 2021 company disclosure says a partner with relevant industry experience would invest in, operate and maintain the HPC equipment, which argues against a full miners classification, but the same disclosure also referred specifically to hosting bitcoin mining capacity, and later filings tied Conifex’s projects directly to the provincial suspension of cryptocurrency-mining interconnections. (conifex.com)

A second limitation is materiality. The retrieved 2024 MD&A revenue table shows lumber, by-products and bioenergy revenue lines, but no separate crypto or HPC revenue line, so crypto-related revenue appears planned or minor rather than established at scale during the sample period. I was able to retrieve at least one management quote from a company quarterly-results document, but not a full traditional call transcript passage discussing crypto in detail; certainty is therefore kept at 3 rather than 4. (conifex.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 9 Nov 2021, quarterly results / conference-call materials: “our site is ideally suited to host data center or other high-performance computing (“HPC”) operations, such as cryptocurrency mining” — Conifex Q3 2021 results
  • 9 Nov 2021, quarterly results / conference-call materials: “we intend to host approximately 3 megawatts of bitcoin mining capacity on a trial basis” — Conifex Q3 2021 results

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 14 May 2024, Q1 2024 MD&A: BC order suspended service to “certain new cryptocurrency mining projects” and “impacted two of our proposed high performance computing (“HPC”) projects” — Conifex Q1 2024 MD&A
  • 13 Aug 2024, Q2 2024 MD&A: Conifex said BC Hydro paused the sites under the temporary suspension, though Conifex argued its requests were for HPC and AI use cases rather than cryptocurrency miners — Conifex Q2 2024 MD&A
  • 2024 annual information form: Conifex disclosed that the 2022 provincial order suspending electricity service for new cryptocurrency mining projects “impacted two of our proposed HPC projects” — Conifex 2024 AIF
  • 13 Jun 2024, company release: management said the cryptocurrency moratorium forced it to halt development at two HPC sites identified by BC Hydro as promising for data-centre operations — Conifex corporate update
  • 2023 annual financial statements: no separate crypto asset or digital-asset holding line was disclosed in the balance sheet notes, supporting holders = 0.0 and AssetShare = 0 — Conifex 2023 annual financial statements

Financial news / other:

  • 14 Feb 2024, Lawson Lundell case note: the BC Supreme Court dismissed Conifex’s challenge to the regulation relieving BC Hydro of the obligation to provide electricity service to cryptocurrency mining projects — Lawson Lundell note on Conifex v. British Columbia
  • 5 Feb 2024, Business in Vancouver: Conifex was described as having sought BC Hydro power for cryptocurrency mining, corroborating market recognition of the project’s crypto-mining linkage — Business in Vancouver on crypto-mining moratorium case

USBC (ASE)

  • Rank: 167 | Expo: 0.34 | id_str: US4992382022
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (2023–2024), 10-K filings (2024), investor relations materials

Firm Overview

USBC (ASE) corresponds to Know Labs Inc. during the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, a U.S.-listed healthcare technology company focused on non-invasive glucose monitoring and related sensor intellectual property. Retrieved company materials and SEC filings consistently describe a medical-diagnostics and IP-development business rather than a crypto-exposed operating model. (d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net)

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto exposure was identified within the 2017–2024 sample period. The available 2023–2024 earnings-call materials discuss hardware development, clinical testing, algorithm development, FDA-oriented commercialization, patent licensing, and shareholder-value initiatives, but do not mention bitcoin, cryptocurrency, digital assets, mining, custody, exchanges, treasury holdings, or blockchain-related services. (d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net)

The 2024 Form 10-K likewise describes Know Labs’ sensor platform, clinical studies, commercialization plans, and financial condition without any detected references to bitcoin, cryptocurrency, or digital assets. Because the project rules require substantive crypto evidence rather than later-period pivots or incidental market speculation, all mechanism categories are unsupported for 2017–2024. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

unclass receives 1.0 because the retrieved earnings-call transcript evidence and SEC filing evidence for the sample period show no substantive crypto-related activity. holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, and infrastr are all 0.0. Although the company appears to have pivoted toward a bitcoin treasury strategy in 2025 after the sample window, that development falls outside January 2017 to December 2024 and is therefore excluded from classification here. invbase = 0 because I did not find evidence within the sample period that management described the stock as having a crypto-driven investor base or that financial media treated it as a crypto proxy during 2017–2024. (investors.usbc.xyz)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is temporal: current web results prominently surface the company’s 2025 rebrand and bitcoin-treasury pivot, but those events occurred after December 31, 2024 and cannot be used as in-sample crypto exposure evidence. Within the required sample period, the evidence was stable and consistently non-crypto. (investors.usbc.xyz)

A second limitation is that some Seeking Alpha transcript pages were inaccessible directly, but the company investor-relations site provided a retrievable August 14, 2024 webcast transcript, and additional 2023–2024 earnings-call PDFs showed no crypto references. This is sufficient to support an unclass assignment. (d1io3yog0oux5.cloudfront.net)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 14 Aug 2024, Q3 FY2024 earnings call transcript: management discussed “hardware development,” “clinical testing,” “algorithm development,” and “intellectual property,” with no crypto-related discussion — Know Labs Q3 FY2024 webcast transcript
  • 4 May 2023, Q1 FY2023 earnings-call presentation: searched PDF showed no matches for bitcoin or crypto — Seeking Alpha-hosted Q1 2023 earnings call PDF
  • 7 May 2024, Q1 FY2024 earnings-call presentation: searched PDF showed no matches for bitcoin or crypto — Seeking Alpha-hosted Q1 2024 earnings call PDF

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 14 Nov 2024, Form 10-K: business description focused on non-invasive glucose monitoring, clinical studies, and platform technology; searches found no references to bitcoin, cryptocurrency, or digital assets — Know Labs 2024 Form 10-K
  • 14 Aug 2024, Q3 FY2024 earnings release: company reported progress in healthcare-device development and financing, with no crypto disclosure — Know Labs Q3 FY2024 results release

Financial news / other:

  • 14 Aug 2024, investor relations calendar page: confirms availability of Q3 FY2024 earnings materials within the sample period and supports company identification as Know Labs — Know Labs IR calendar entry
  • 6 Aug 2025, company news release: later rebrand to USBC and bitcoin treasury strategy appears outside the sample period and was excluded from weighting — USBC news releases page

JSE

  • Rank: 168 | Expo: 0.33 | id_str: ZAE000079711
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: integrated annual report (2024), annual results materials (2022, 2024), audited financial statements (2024)

Firm Overview

JSE Ltd is the Johannesburg Stock Exchange, South Africa’s principal multi-asset exchange and market-infrastructure operator, providing listing, trading, clearing, investor-services and related market services. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, retrieved company materials show a traditional exchange and financial-market-infrastructure business, with only late-2024 discussion of prospective digital-asset initiatives rather than operating crypto activity. (group.jse.co.za)

Exposure Channels Identified

No in-sample crypto exposure mechanism is sufficiently established for classification into holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. The 2024 integrated annual report states that the JSE was “proactively investigating the trading of digital assets” and was “looking to list crypto-based products such as ETFs and ETNs,” but these passages describe strategy exploration and regulatory preparation, not an existing crypto exchange, custody, treasury, mining, or service business during 2024. (group.jse.co.za)

The retrieved 2024 annual results presentation and the 2025-published summary of 2024 results contain no matches for “crypto” or “bitcoin,” and the 2024 audited financial statements likewise showed no retrievable crypto-asset references. A 2022 interim-results document also showed no crypto or bitcoin matches, which supports the view that crypto was not a substantive operating exposure across the sample window. (group.jse.co.za)

Weight Assignment

unclass receives 1.0 because the strongest in-sample evidence shows only exploratory, forward-looking digital-asset planning rather than a demonstrated crypto operating mechanism. Under the project rules, prospective interest in future crypto-product listings is not enough to code finserv without evidence that the firm directly handled, listed, custodied, traded, or earned material fee income from crypto products during January 2017 to December 2024. holders, miners, bizserv, and infrastr are also unsupported, and invbase = 0 because I did not find evidence that management or financial media characterised JSE Ltd stock as a crypto proxy during the sample period. (group.jse.co.za)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is that the 2024 integrated annual report contains explicit digital-asset strategy language, including possible future crypto-based ETFs, ETNs, and blockchain-based infrastructure. However, those disclosures are framed as investigation, framework design, and regulatory engagement, so I treated them as pre-operational and insufficient for a non-zero mechanism weight under the stated exclusion and threshold rules. (group.jse.co.za)

A second limitation is transcript availability. I retrieved company-issued annual/interim results materials rather than a full verbatim analyst-call transcript, but these still provide an earnings-call/results anchor within the sample period and consistently show no substantive crypto operating discussion. Because the final classification is unclass = 1.0, certainty is reported as NA per the coding rules. (group.jse.co.za)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 4 Mar 2025, annual results presentation for FY2024: searched presentation showed no matches for bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain in the results deck — JSE Ltd Annual Results Presentation 2024
  • 2 Aug 2022, interim results booklet: searched document showed no matches for bitcoin or crypto — JSE Ltd Interim Results Booklet 2022
  • 3 Mar 2025, FY2024 results commentary: “Our strategy to build a diversified and resilient exchange group was supported by non-trading income” with no crypto references in the summary announcement — JSE FY2024 summarised results announcement

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2024 integrated annual report: “The JSE is proactively investigating the trading of digital assets, including cryptocurrency” and is “looking to list crypto-based products such as ETFs and ETNs,” indicating exploration rather than live in-sample activity — JSE Ltd Integrated Annual Report 2024
  • 2024 integrated annual report: “The proposed digital asset strategy was approved by the Board in November 2024” — JSE Ltd Integrated Annual Report 2024
  • 2024 audited consolidated AFS: searched document showed no matches for crypto, bitcoin, or digital assets in the financial statements — JSE Ltd Audited Consolidated AFS 2024

Financial news / other:

  • 2024 reporting-suite page: confirms the company’s annual/interim results materials and reporting documents used for classification — JSE Group Reporting Suite
  • 2024 integrated annual report industry-trends section: notes that South Africa’s FSCA had designated crypto assets as financial products, providing regulatory context rather than firm-level operating exposure — JSE Ltd Integrated Annual Report 2024

TWILIO 'A'

  • Rank: 169 | Expo: 0.33 | id_str: US90138F1021
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2023–2024), Q4 2023 and Q4 2024 earnings call transcripts

Firm Overview

Twilio Inc. is a U.S.-listed cloud communications and customer-engagement software company whose core products include messaging, voice, email, authentication, and customer-data tools. Across the January 2017 to December 2024 sample window, the retrieved evidence shows crypto appearing only as a customer vertical that affected usage volumes, not as a business line in which Twilio held, mined, traded, custodied, or directly serviced crypto assets.

Exposure Channels Identified

No project-defined crypto mechanism is clearly supported. The strongest crypto references in earnings calls concern demand from Twilio’s crypto-industry customers, with management explicitly describing that activity as relatively immaterial in 2024 and as a headwind or volume factor rather than a standalone crypto service line. (fool.com)

That does not meet finserv, because Twilio was not described as directly handling digital assets, operating an exchange, offering custody, or issuing crypto investment products. It also does not meet bizserv or infrastr under the threshold rules, because the evidence points to general-purpose communications software sold across industries rather than material crypto-specific services or infrastructure targeted primarily at crypto workloads. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

unclass receives 1.0 because the in-sample evidence indicates only indirect exposure through some crypto customers using Twilio’s ordinary communications platform. Management’s own descriptions frame crypto as a source of usage volatility or a modest volume contribution, not as a substantive operating mechanism under holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. invbase = 0 because I did not find evidence that management or financial media characterized Twilio stock as a crypto proxy during January 2017 to December 2024. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is whether serving crypto-industry customers through messaging and email APIs could qualify as bizserv. I excluded that here because the retrieved sources do not show crypto as a dedicated, material service vertical for Twilio; instead, crypto is discussed as one customer cohort within a broad horizontal communications platform, and management called the effect relatively immaterial in the February 13, 2025 discussion of FY2024 results. (fool.com)

Transcript coverage was sufficient to anchor the classification, but I did not retrieve evidence of a standalone crypto strategy presentation or crypto-specific revenue disclosure in Twilio’s filings. The 2023 and 2024 10-Ks also returned no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain,” which supports the decision to keep all mechanism weights at zero. (sec.gov)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 13 Feb 2025, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript: “from a crypto perspective, we do see those customers exhibiting stronger volume... it's relatively immaterial to the grand scheme of things” — Motley Fool Q4 2024 earnings call transcript
  • 14 Feb 2024, Q4 2023 earnings call transcript: “our Q4 revenue growth rate was negatively impacted by headwinds from customers in the crypto industry” — Motley Fool Q4 2023 earnings call transcript
  • 14 Feb 2024, Q4 2023 earnings call transcript: “Total Q4 organic revenue growth, excluding crypto customers, was 10% year over year” — Motley Fool Q4 2023 earnings call transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 27 Feb 2024, Form 10-K for FY2023: searched filing showed no matches for crypto, bitcoin, or blockchain — SEC Form 10-K 2023
  • 26 Feb 2025, Form 10-K for FY2024: searched filing showed no matches for crypto, bitcoin, or blockchain — SEC Form 10-K 2024
  • 13 Feb 2025, Q4/FY2024 results release: describes Twilio as a customer engagement platform and points investors to the call/transcript materials, with no crypto business line disclosure — Twilio announces fourth quarter and full year 2024 results

Financial news / other:

  • 14 Feb 2024, CNBC: coverage of Twilio’s post-earnings reaction focused on customer counts and Segment review, with no indication that the stock traded as a crypto proxy — CNBC coverage of Twilio after Q4 2023 results
  • Investor relations overview page: confirms Twilio’s core positioning as a customer engagement platform rather than a crypto-native business — Twilio Investor Relations

TRINITY CAPITAL

  • Rank: 170 | Expo: 0.33 | id_str: US8964423086
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2021–2024), Q3 2023 and Q3/Q4 2024 earnings call transcripts

Firm Overview

Trinity Capital Inc. is a U.S.-listed business development company and alternative asset manager focused on venture debt, equipment financing, asset-based lending, sponsor finance, and life sciences lending. Across the January 2017 to December 2024 sample window, the retrieved evidence shows a specialty finance lender serving high-growth companies, with only indirect crypto adjacency through some portfolio companies rather than a disclosed business line involving direct digital-asset ownership, mining, custody, exchange activity, or crypto-specific infrastructure.

Exposure Channels Identified

No project-defined crypto mechanism is clearly supported. The strongest crypto-adjacent evidence is that Trinity’s portfolio and website taxonomy include a “Crypto” tag and portfolio names such as Hut 8, CleanSpark, Applied Digital, and Inca Digital, while the 2024 Form 10-K schedule of investments also includes one portfolio line categorized as “Digital Assets Technology and Services.” (trinitycapital.com; sec.gov)

That does not meet finserv, because the available sources do not show Trinity operating a crypto exchange, brokerage, custody platform, payment network, token product, or investment vehicle directly handling digital assets. It also does not meet holders, miners, bizserv, or infrastr under the project rules, because the evidence indicates Trinity is a general lender to a wide set of innovative companies and does not show Trinity itself holding crypto on balance sheet, mining crypto, or deriving a clearly disclosed, material line of crypto-targeted services or infrastructure revenue. (sec.gov; seekingalpha.com; seekingalpha.com)

Weight Assignment

unclass receives 1.0 because the in-sample evidence supports, at most, indirect exposure through lending to some crypto- or digital-asset-adjacent portfolio companies. The retrieved earnings call coverage and filings describe Trinity as a diversified alternative lender and asset manager, not as a crypto holder, miner, financial intermediary in digital assets, or dedicated crypto infrastructure provider. invbase = 0 because I did not find evidence in management commentary or financial news that Trinity Capital’s stock was explicitly characterized as a crypto proxy during January 2017 to December 2024. (seekingalpha.com; sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is whether lending to crypto-related portfolio companies could qualify Trinity for finserv or bizserv. I exclude that here because the sources show portfolio-company exposure rather than Trinity itself directly handling digital assets or providing clearly crypto-specific operating services; under the mechanism rules, ordinary lending to a crypto company is not enough by itself.

Transcript retrieval was sufficient to anchor the classification, but the accessible transcript snippets did not surface any substantive management discussion of Bitcoin, blockchain, or crypto as a distinct reporting channel. Some investor-relations pages were inaccessible to direct scraping in this environment, so I relied on SEC filings, the public portfolio page, and searchable transcript result pages; that is adequate for an unclassified conclusion but would not support a higher-certainty positive mechanism assignment.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 30 Oct 2024, Q3 2024 earnings call transcript: management described Trinity as conducting its “Third Quarter 2024 Earnings Conference Call,” with the accessible transcript excerpt framing the business as Trinity Capital’s ordinary earnings discussion and not surfacing any crypto operating line — Seeking Alpha Q3 2024 earnings call transcript
  • 6 Mar 2024, Q4 2023 earnings call transcript: accessible excerpt shows a standard “Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2023 Earnings Conference Call” with no visible crypto-specific management strategy in the retrieved snippet — Seeking Alpha Q4 2023 earnings call transcript
  • 1 Nov 2023, Q3 2023 earnings call transcript: accessible excerpt identifies the regular quarterly earnings call and does not, in the retrieved material, indicate a crypto business line — Seeking Alpha Q3 2023 earnings call transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 26 Feb 2025, Form 10-K for FY2024: schedule of investments includes a portfolio classification line for “Portfolio Company Debt Securities- United States Digital Assets Technology and Services,” indicating some borrower exposure but not Trinity’s own direct crypto activity — SEC Form 10-K 2024
  • 29 Feb 2024, Form 10-K for FY2023: filing describes Trinity Capital as a lender making secured loans, equipment financings, and equity-related investments, without evidence of Trinity holding or transacting in crypto assets directly — SEC Form 10-K 2023
  • 1 Mar 2023, Form 10-K for FY2022: filing states Trinity may make debt, equipment financing, and limited equity-related investments in portfolio companies, again without a disclosed crypto operating segment or treasury holding policy — SEC Form 10-K 2022
  • 28 Feb 2022, Form 10-K for FY2021: filing presents Trinity as a business development company and specialty finance platform, with no evidence of direct crypto holdings, mining, custody, or exchange activity — SEC Form 10-K 2021

Financial news / other:

  • Portfolio page retrieved during the sample review: Trinity’s website lists a “Crypto” portfolio filter and names portfolio companies including Hut 8, CleanSpark, Applied Digital, and Inca Digital, supporting only indirect portfolio-company adjacency — Trinity Capital portfolio page
  • 22 Jan 2025, company press release: Trinity described its equipment finance business as funding companies across industries such as manufacturing, energy, space technology, climate tech, and AI, with no crypto segment disclosure in the retrieved release text — Equipment Financing: Over $1 Billion in Investments
  • 18 Jul 2025, company press release: Trinity described itself as a “leading alternative asset manager” providing an asset-based credit facility to Alt, reinforcing the firm’s identity as a lender rather than a crypto financial intermediary — Trinity Capital provides Alt Platform with up to $40 million asset based credit facility

AXOS FINANCIAL

  • Rank: 171 | Expo: 0.33 | id_str: US05465C1009
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (Q4 FY2022; Q2 FY2024), annual reports / 10-K filings (FY2023–FY2025), financial news on Binance.US banking relationships

Firm Overview

Axos Financial, Inc. is a U.S.-listed diversified financial services company whose main businesses are digital banking, securities clearing/custody, and investment advisory through subsidiaries including Axos Bank, Axos Clearing, and Axos Invest. Within the sample period, the crypto-relevant activity appears to have been limited but direct: management discussed a crypto trading initiative and later described servicing certain digital-asset-related deposit relationships while emphasizing that Axos did not extend crypto-collateralized credit.

Exposure Channels Identified

finserv applies because Axos management explicitly referred to “crypto trading” as a growth initiative in the securities business, which indicates direct crypto-related financial intermediation rather than merely providing back-office business services. Management also discussed Axos’s handling of Binance.US accounts and “bankruptcy trustee deposits related to failed cryptocurrency and digital asset businesses,” showing that the firm directly served crypto-linked financial customers within its banking platform. (fool.com)

I do not assign holders because I found no evidence that Axos held crypto on its own balance sheet as a treasury asset. I do not assign miners, bizserv, or infrastr because the retrieved sources point to banking, trading, custody, and related financial intermediation rather than mining, non-financial service provision, or dedicated crypto infrastructure supply. Axos’s annual reports describe its core securities business as clearing, custody, stock lending, margin lending, and digital wealth management, without any disclosed crypto asset holdings. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

finserv receives 1.0 because the in-sample evidence supports direct crypto-related financial services, but not any other mechanism. The strongest anchor is management’s August 4, 2022 earnings-call statement that Axos was investing in “crypto trading,” reinforced by January 30, 2024 management comments about prior digital-asset account relationships and the exit from that business after regulatory developments. I set invbase = 0 because I did not find evidence that Axos’s stock was explicitly treated in financial media or management commentary as a crypto proxy or as having a crypto-driven investor base. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is materiality. The evidence supports a real finserv channel, but the retrieved filings do not disclose a separate crypto revenue line or crypto asset line item, so the activity appears peripheral relative to Axos’s much larger conventional banking and securities businesses. That is why RevShare is coded 1 rather than higher, and AssetShare is coded 0. (sec.gov)

A second ambiguity is time variation: by January 30, 2024 management was discussing how the firm had exited the digital-asset business, so exposure was present during the sample but seems to have wound down rather than expanded through 2024. The available transcript coverage was sufficient to anchor classification, but I relied on accessible transcript excerpts and public filings rather than a full set of investor-relations webcast archives for every year from 2017 to 2024. (fintool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 4 Aug 2022, Q4 FY2022 earnings call transcript: management said, “we continue to incur incremental expenses related to the growth initiatives, such as crypto trading” — The Motley Fool Q4 FY2022 earnings call transcript
  • 30 Jan 2024, Q2 FY2024 earnings call transcript: analyst asked about “the digital asset business and how you guys exited,” and management said the exit followed SEC views that “most cryptocurrencies were securities” — Fintool Q2 FY2024 earnings call transcript
  • 30 Jan 2024, Q2 FY2024 earnings call transcript: management said Axos performed due diligence before opening Binance.US accounts and that it managed “bankruptcy trustee deposits related to failed cryptocurrency and digital asset businesses” — ROIC AI Q2 FY2024 earnings call transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2025 annual report for FY ended 30 Jun 2025: Axos described itself as providing “banking, clearing and custody, and investment advisory solutions,” with securities businesses earning fees from custody, clearing, stock lending, and margin lending, but no crypto asset balance-sheet disclosure — SEC FY2025 annual report
  • 31 Dec 2024 Form 10-Q: total assets were about $23.7 billion, and the filing did not surface any digital-asset or cryptocurrency asset line item — SEC quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024
  • 2025 annual report for FY ended 30 Jun 2025: the filing states Axos Clearing and AAS provide “comprehensive securities custody services” and Axos Invest provides “self-directed securities trading and digital wealth management services,” which supports a financial-intermediation reading rather than a non-financial service classification — SEC FY2025 annual report

Financial news / other:

  • 8 Jun 2023, CNBC/Reuters: Binance.US said banks were cutting off access to the U.S. banking system, and the report identified Axos Bank as one of the exchange’s disclosed U.S. banking partners — CNBC report on Binance.US banking partners
  • Seeking Alpha transcript index retrieved for coverage review: public transcript listing shows Axos earnings-call availability across 2020–2025, supporting transcript selection and time-series coverage — Seeking Alpha AX earnings transcript index

REMARK HOLDINGS

  • Rank: 172 | Expo: 0.33 | id_str: US75955K3005
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts index and Q3 2023 transcript, SEC 10-K filings (2022–2023), company website

Firm Overview

Remark Holdings, Inc. is a small U.S. technology company focused on AI and computer-vision software, including workplace safety, public safety, and fintech risk-analysis products. Across the retrieved 2022–2024 filings, website materials, and sampled earnings-call transcript coverage, the company presents itself as an AI software and solutions provider rather than a crypto-focused operating business. (remarkholdings.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

I do not identify a substantive crypto exposure channel for Remark Holdings during the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period. The accessible Q3 2023 earnings-call transcript contains no references to crypto, bitcoin, or blockchain, and keyword searches within the transcript returned no matches for those terms. (seekingalpha.com)

The retrieved 2022 and 2023 SEC annual reports likewise returned no matches for bitcoin, blockchain, or cryptocurrency, and the company website describes AI/video-analytics and fintech risk-analysis offerings without indicating direct digital-asset activity, mining, custody, exchange services, treasury holdings, or crypto-specific infrastructure. On the evidence retrieved, the firm is best coded as unclassified rather than assigned to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

unclass receives 1.0 because the searched transcript, filings, and company materials do not provide meaningful evidence of any substantive crypto mechanism exposure. I set invbase = 0 because I did not find management commentary or credible financial-news evidence showing that Remark Holdings’ stock traded as an explicit crypto proxy or that its investor base was directly tied to crypto-market sentiment. (seekingalpha.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main limitation is that, although public transcript coverage exists across many years, I could directly retrieve only a sampled accessible transcript rather than a full 2017–2024 set. Under the project rules, at least one transcript quote is required to anchor any non-unclassified assignment; here, the available transcript and filings instead support the absence of documented crypto exposure, so the firm remains unclass = 1.0. (seekingalpha.com)

A second limitation is that absence of evidence is not proof that the firm never explored crypto-adjacent ideas informally. However, because the searched SEC filings, transcript text, company website, and targeted news searches did not surface substantive crypto operations or balance-sheet exposure within the sample period, there is not enough support to assign any positive crypto weight. (sec.gov)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 20 Nov 2023, Q3 2023 earnings call transcript: opening remarks and available transcript text describe business updates, with no surfaced references to crypto, bitcoin, or blockchain in transcript keyword checks — Seeking Alpha Q3 2023 earnings call transcript
  • Transcript coverage review for 2017–2024: public index shows extensive Remark Holdings earnings-call transcript availability across the sample period, used to assess coverage even though sampled accessible transcripts did not show crypto discussion — Seeking Alpha MARK earnings transcripts index

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 25 Apr 2024, Form 10-K/A for fiscal year ended 31 Dec 2023: annual report describes Remark Holdings as a reporting company but searched text returned no matches for bitcoin, blockchain, or cryptocurrency — SEC FY2023 Form 10-K/A
  • 17 Apr 2023 / 1 May 2023 amendment context, Form 10-K for fiscal year ended 31 Dec 2022: searched filing text returned no matches for bitcoin, blockchain, or cryptocurrency — SEC FY2022 Form 10-K/A

Financial news / other:

  • Company website retrieved for business description: site emphasizes AI video analytics, workplace/public safety, and fintech risk-analysis products, with no identified crypto business line — Remark Holdings corporate website
  • Reuters-targeted and broader web searches during this run did not surface credible in-sample reporting of direct crypto operations, mining, treasury holdings, or exchange/custody activity for Remark Holdings — Bloomberg LEI entity page surfaced in search results

NETS DEAD - DELIST.13/02/18

  • Rank: 173 | Expo: 0.33 | id_str: DK0060745370
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2017 annual report / delisting filing context, company press releases, Reuters financial news

Firm Overview

Nets A/S was a Danish/Nordic payments-processing and financial infrastructure company serving banks and merchants across card acquiring, issuer services, e-commerce, and related payment software before its February 13, 2018 delisting following the Hellman & Friedman takeover. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, the retrieved sources describe Nets as a conventional digital-payments platform rather than a company with direct cryptocurrency operating exposure. (regnskaber.cvrapi.dk)

Exposure Channels Identified

I do not identify a substantive crypto exposure channel for Nets A/S under the project taxonomy. The clearest crypto-related item retrieved is a March 24, 2017 Reuters report stating that Nets would cooperate with Chainalysis to help Nordic banks validate bitcoin transactions and comply with anti-money-laundering rules; that points to compliance support around crypto rather than Nets itself holding, mining, trading, custodying, or issuing crypto products. (investing.com)

That evidence is still too limited to assign a positive bizserv or finserv weight, because the available materials do not show that crypto-related activity became a disclosed revenue line, a recurring strategic business segment, or a material management focus in the sampled period. The annual-report and takeover/delisting materials instead emphasize core payments, card services, merchant acquiring, and bank-processing activities, so the firm is best coded unclass = 1.0. (regnskaber.cvrapi.dk)

Weight Assignment

unclass receives 1.0 because the retrieved record shows, at most, a narrow AML/compliance partnership touching bitcoin transaction analysis, not a material crypto mechanism exposure of the kind required for holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. I set invbase = 0 because I did not find evidence that Nets’ equity traded as a crypto proxy or that management or financial media described its shareholder base as crypto-driven; the dominant documented equity event in-sample was the September 25, 2017 takeover offer and subsequent February 13, 2018 delisting. (investing.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that, because Nets was taken private and delisted on February 13, 2018, public post-2018 earnings-call transcript coverage is very limited, and I was not able to retrieve a conventional earnings-call transcript quote despite targeted searching. Under the coding rules, that means any non-unclassified assignment would in any case be capped at low certainty. (regnskaber.cvrapi.dk)

There is also a borderline classification issue: the 2017 Chainalysis partnership could be read as crypto-related compliance support for bank clients, which is closest to bizserv. I do not assign positive weight because the evidence retrieved does not establish materiality, persistence, or clear economic significance, and the broader source set consistently frames Nets as a mainstream payments processor. (investing.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • No public earnings-call transcript was retrievable in this run despite targeted transcript searches across standard sources; because Nets was taken private and delisted in early 2018, transcript availability appears limited — Nets takeover offer announcement

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2017 annual report context: filing states that “Nets A/S has been taken over by Hellman & Friedman and on that background, the company has been delisted from Nasdaq Copenhagen” — Nets Denmark A/S 2017 annual report PDF
  • 25 Sep 2017, company announcement: Nets announced a recommended all-cash takeover offer from Hellman & Friedman-controlled Evergood 5 AS — GlobeNewswire takeover announcement
  • 18 Jan 2017, company press release: Nets and SEB renewed card-processing cooperation focused on contactless, mobile payments, tokenization and 3D Secure, with no direct crypto business disclosed — Nets press release on SEB agreement

Financial news / other:

  • 24 Mar 2017, Reuters: Nets said it would cooperate with Chainalysis to help banks validate bitcoin transactions and comply with AML rules — Reuters item via Investing.com
  • Hellman & Friedman transaction summary: source describes Nets as a European paytech leader centered on merchant services, issuer services, and digital payments — Hellman & Friedman transaction page
  • 26 Jun 2017, company press release: Danish banks renewed a clearing agreement with Nets, reinforcing the company’s core role in conventional payment infrastructure rather than crypto — Nets clearing agreement press release

GFT TECHNOLOGIES

  • Rank: 174 | Expo: 0.32 | id_str: DE0005800601
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.8, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.2
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: Annual reports (2022–2023), 2021 earnings-call transcript metadata, company digital-currency press releases (2023–2024)

Firm Overview

GFT Technologies SE is a German-listed IT services and digital-engineering company focused mainly on financial services, insurance and selected industrial clients. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, the retrieved sources show that crypto-related exposure sits in its consulting, software engineering and platform-development work around blockchain, digital money, stablecoins, CBDCs and tokenisation rather than in proprietary crypto holding, mining, exchange or custody activity. (gft.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The main identified channel is bizserv. GFT repeatedly describes blockchain/DLT as part of its technology offering, highlights client work such as tokenising green bonds and blockchain-based asset-management use cases, and in 2023–2024 publicly promoted UDPN-related solutions for tokenised deposits, stablecoins and digital-asset tokenisation for banks and other regulated institutions. (gft.com)

I do not assign finserv because the evidence does not show GFT itself operating a crypto exchange, brokerage, custody platform, fund, or balance-sheet crypto product; rather, it develops and integrates technology for clients. I also do not assign holders, miners, or infrastr, as I found no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining operations, or mining/datacentre hardware supply directed at crypto workloads. (gft.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign bizserv = 0.8 because the evidence consistently shows substantive crypto-adjacent non-financial services activity: blockchain/DLT implementation, tokenisation projects, and Web3/digital-currency system development for banking clients. I leave unclass = 0.2 because the annual reports do not isolate crypto revenue as a standalone segment and often bundle blockchain/DLT with other “high-growth technologies” such as AI, cloud and data analytics, which limits precision on materiality. invbase = 0 because I did not retrieve evidence that GFT’s stock traded as a recognized crypto proxy or that management described a crypto-driven shareholder base. (cdn.financialreports.eu)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is transcript access: I was able to verify the existence of in-sample earnings-call transcripts for March 3, 2022 and March 2, 2023, but the scraped transcript pages were subscriber-gated, so only the visible opening lines and metadata were retrievable in this run. Under the project rules, that weakens the transcript anchor relative to a fully visible crypto passage, although the annual reports and company press releases provide clear corroboration. (marketscreener.com)

There is also a taxonomy boundary issue between bizserv and finserv. GFT’s UDPN work is clearly tied to digital currencies and tokenised assets, but the retrieved materials frame GFT as a technology and engineering contributor for banks, stablecoin issuers and network partners, not as the direct financial intermediary handling customer assets itself; accordingly, bizserv is the better fit. (gft.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 3 Mar 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript page: visible transcript metadata shows Marika Lulay opening the call with “I’m pleased with the outstanding performance in 2021”; full transcript text was subscriber-gated in this run — MarketScreener transcript page
  • 2 Mar 2023, 2022 earnings call transcript page: in-sample transcript confirmed by S&P Capital IQ / MarketScreener metadata, but substantive text was subscriber-gated — MarketScreener transcript page

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2022 annual report: GFT says it offers implementation skills “from cloud engineering to artificial intelligence and blockchain/DLT” — GFT Technologies SE Annual Report 2022 PDF
  • 2022 annual report: “revenue from artificial intelligence, cloud, DLT / blockchain, DevOps and data analytics was increased once again and accounted for 48% of total revenue,” but without a separate crypto line item — GFT Technologies SE Annual Report 2022 PDF
  • 2022 annual report: client examples include “Blockchain-based transactions” for VP Bank and “Tokenising green bonds” — GFT Technologies SE Annual Report 2022 PDF
  • 2023 annual report: GFT says its Universal Digital Payments Network is “a platform for digital payments based on blockchain technology” enabling exchange of CBDCs and regulated stablecoins, with hopes for future transaction-based revenue streams — GFT Technologies SE Annual Report 2023 PDF

Financial news / other:

  • 30 Oct 2023, company news: Deutsche Bank and SC Ventures completed a first digital-currency transfer and swap on UDPN; the page presents GFT as part of the UDPN ecosystem serving stablecoin/CBDC experimentation — GFT newsroom item
  • 17 Jan 2024, company news: UDPN, developed with contributions from GFT, is described as a DLT-backed network for interoperability between regulated stablecoins and CBDCs — GFT newsroom item
  • 25 Jun 2024, company news: UDPN launched tokenised deposit/stablecoin and digital-asset tokenisation systems; GFT stated it was using “deep expertise in legacy core banking systems” and “cutting-edge Web3 knowledge” to enable clients’ payment use cases — GFT newsroom item

VERISIGN

  • Rank: 175 | Expo: 0.31 | id_str: US92343E1029
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2020–2024 earnings-call transcripts, 2024 Form 10-K, domain-industry news on blockchain-related patents

Firm Overview

VeriSign, Inc. is a U.S.-listed internet infrastructure company best known for operating the registries for .com and .net and for providing critical DNS infrastructure services. Across the retrieved 2020–2024 materials, the company’s disclosed business remained centered on domain-name registry operations rather than direct cryptocurrency activity.

Exposure Channels Identified

I do not identify a substantive crypto exposure channel for VeriSign within the project taxonomy. The retrieved earnings-call transcripts focus on domain-name registrations, pricing, renewal rates, ICANN agreements, and DNS infrastructure, with no evidence that management described crypto holdings, mining, exchange/custody activity, or crypto-targeted infrastructure revenue. (fool.com)

The 2024 Form 10-K mentions “blockchain namespaces” only as a potential substitute technology that could reduce demand for traditional domain names, which indicates competitive risk from blockchain-based naming systems rather than participation in the crypto ecosystem. (sec.gov)

Industry news also shows VeriSign obtained patents related to blockchain-linked or cryptocurrency-backed domain-transfer concepts, but patent ownership alone is insufficient under the rules to assign bizserv, finserv, or infrastr without evidence of an operating business line, customer service, revenue stream, or management emphasis. (domainnamewire.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign unclass = 1.0 because the evidence points to no meaningful, credible, and substantive crypto business exposure during January 2017 through December 2024. The materials show only peripheral blockchain references: one risk-factor discussion about alternative namespaces and patent filings for possible blockchain-related domain applications. That does not satisfy the threshold for holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. I set invbase = 0 because I did not retrieve evidence that VeriSign traded as a recognized crypto proxy or that management discussed a crypto-driven investor base. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that I did not retrieve a full 2017–2019 transcript set in this run, though the in-sample transcript archive was confirmed and multiple accessible 2020–2022 transcripts were reviewed. That limitation does not alter the classification because the available transcript evidence and the 2024 Form 10-K consistently portray VeriSign as a DNS/domain-registry operator with no disclosed crypto operating segment. (seekingalpha.com)

There is also a taxonomy boundary issue around blockchain patents. VeriSign’s patents reference blockchain or cryptocurrency-enabled domain-transfer methods, but I found no evidence that the company commercialized these into a material service, handled digital assets directly, or generated crypto-related revenue. Accordingly, I treat these as incidental R&D/intellectual-property signals rather than a classified exposure channel. (domainnamewire.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 11 Feb 2021, Q4 2020 earnings call transcript: management highlighted “23 years of uninterrupted availability of the VeriSign DNS for .com and .net” and discussed cash, buybacks, registrations, and pricing, with no crypto-related operating activity — The Motley Fool transcript
  • 10 Feb 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: Jim Bidzos said VeriSign “continu[ed] to strengthen our critical Internet infrastructure,” again with discussion centered on DNS and domain registrations rather than crypto — The Motley Fool transcript
  • 28 Jul 2022, Q2 2022 earnings call transcript: management described “new growth opportunities such as .web” and hybrid-work execution, with no substantive crypto discussion — The Motley Fool transcript
  • 2020–2024 transcript archive page: confirms broad in-sample earnings-call coverage for VeriSign across 2020–2024 — Seeking Alpha VRSN transcript archive

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2024 Form 10-K: VeriSign states demand for its domains could be affected by “alternative namespaces with domain-name-like identifiers that are operated outside the single authoritative DNS root zone, including blockchain namespaces” — SEC Form 10-K HTML
  • 2024 annual report / 10-K package: company disclosures center on registry services, DNS operations, and domain-name economics, without a crypto business segment or crypto assets disclosure — SEC annual report PDF

Financial news / other:

  • 21 Jul 2020, Domain Name Wire: VeriSign received a patent for “Domain name blockchain user addresses,” indicating blockchain-related IP activity but not an operating crypto service — Domain Name Wire article
  • 10 May 2023, Domain Name Wire: VeriSign received a patent for “Transferring a domain name on a secondary blockchain market and in the DNS,” including cryptocurrency-backed transaction mechanics, again without evidence of a material commercial crypto business — Domain Name Wire article

JIAYIN GROUP ADR 1:4

  • Rank: 176 | Expo: 0.31 | id_str: US47737C1045
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (2023), Form 20-F filings (2023–2024), investor relations materials

Firm Overview

Jiayin Group Inc. is a China-focused fintech platform listed in the United States through ADSs, and its core business is facilitating consumer loans and providing borrower acquisition, risk management, and related financial-technology services to institutional funding partners. Across the retrieved materials for January 2017 through December 2024, the company is described as an online lending and risk-management platform rather than as a participant in cryptocurrency markets. (ir.jiayin-fintech.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

I do not identify a substantive crypto exposure channel for Jiayin Group under the project taxonomy. The retrieved earnings-call transcripts discuss loan origination volume, borrower acquisition, risk control, smart operations, AI application, and technology capabilities, but I found no management discussion of bitcoin, blockchain, digital assets, mining, exchange activity, custody, or crypto-backed financial products. (fintool.com)

The company’s investor relations materials and annual-report descriptions likewise characterize Jiayin as a fintech platform connecting underserved borrowers with financial institutions, with business emphasis on consumer finance and credit technology rather than crypto services or crypto infrastructure. I also did not retrieve evidence that the stock was framed in the financial press as a crypto proxy or that management commented on a crypto-driven shareholder base. (ir.jiayin-fintech.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign unclass = 1.0 because the evidence does not show meaningful, credible, and substantive crypto exposure during January 2017 through December 2024. The accessible transcript evidence is centered on lending facilitation and risk management, and the filing-level/company descriptions do not disclose crypto holdings, mining activity, direct crypto financial services, crypto-focused business services, or crypto-targeted infrastructure. I set invbase = 0 because I did not find explicit evidence that Jiayin traded as a recognized crypto proxy or had an investor base demonstrably tied to crypto sentiment. (fintool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that I retrieved accessible transcript text primarily for 2023 rather than a full 2017–2024 transcript panel, and some IR-hosted or third-party pages were partially inaccessible in this run. However, because the available earnings calls and filing descriptions consistently portray Jiayin as a consumer-lending fintech platform with no substantive crypto discussion, that limitation does not change the classification. (fintool.com)

Another limitation is that the SEC HTML pages were only partially accessible through the browser tool, so I relied on search-result snippets and company IR descriptions for corroboration of the business model. I found no filing snippet, transcript passage, or news result indicating balance-sheet crypto, crypto transaction services, mining operations, or blockchain infrastructure revenue. (sec.gov)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 21 Aug 2023, Q2 2023 earnings call transcript: management highlighted “our loan origination volume saw a remarkable increase of 77.8%, and our net revenue experienced a strong growth of 57.4%,” with discussion focused on lending growth and operating efficiency rather than crypto — Fintool transcript
  • 21 Aug 2023, Q2 2023 earnings call transcript: searches within the transcript returned no matches for “blockchain” or “bitcoin” — Fintool transcript search results
  • Q3 2023 earnings call transcript: management said “borrower acquisition and risk control services will continue to account for the vast majority of our revenue,” indicating a consumer-finance operating model rather than crypto activity — Fintool transcript
  • Q3 2023 earnings call transcript: searches within the transcript returned no matches for “blockchain” or “bitcoin” — Fintool transcript search results

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2023 Form 20-F: SEC filing snippet describes Jiayin Group Inc. as a Cayman holding company primarily operating in China through subsidiaries and VIE arrangements, with no crypto-related disclosure evident in the retrieved result — SEC Form 20-F snippet
  • 2024 Form 20-F: filing search result confirms annual report coverage for fiscal year ended December 31, 2024, and no crypto-related terms were retrieved from the filing search in this run — SEC Form 20-F result
  • 27 Mar 2025 press release covering fiscal year 2024 results: Jiayin reported loan facilitation volume, revenue, operating income, and R&D expense, with no indication of crypto assets or crypto revenue streams — Jiayin IR press release

Financial news / other:

  • Investor relations homepage: Jiayin states it is “a leading fintech platform in China” facilitating connections between underserved borrowers and financial institutions — Jiayin IR homepage
  • IR events page: archived conference-call listings and annual-report links support coverage of the company as a conventional fintech/lending platform, not a crypto firm — Jiayin IR events page
  • November 2024 investor-presentation listing: presentation materials are described as standard investor-relations content for Jiayin’s fintech business, with no crypto exposure visible in the retrieved listing — Jiayin presentations page

ONECONNECT FINL. TECH. ADR 1:30

  • Rank: 177 | Expo: 0.30 | id_str: US68248T2042
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 1.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
