  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (Q1 2020, Q3 2024), 20-F filings (2023–2024), company annual/ESG materials

Firm Overview

OneConnect Financial Technology Co. Ltd. is a China-based technology-as-a-service provider for financial institutions, listed in the U.S. as ADRs under OCFT and also listed in Hong Kong. Its disclosed business focus is digital banking, digital insurance, and related fintech software solutions for banks and insurers, with blockchain described as one component of its broader technology stack rather than as a crypto-asset business. (ir.oneconnectft.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

I identify bizserv as the relevant exposure channel. In the Q1 2020 earnings call, management said blockchain was used as an underlying technology in bundled solutions for customers, including cross-border trade and personal credit assessment, and explicitly said the company did not separately calculate revenue from blockchain because it was embedded within broader solution packages. (fool.com)

That evidence supports crypto-adjacent non-financial services, not finserv: the company appears to sell technology solutions to financial institutions rather than operate a crypto exchange, custody platform, token product, treasury holding program, or mining business. Later company materials and 2024 results descriptions continue to frame OneConnect as a technology service provider using AI, big data, and blockchain for financial-sector digital transformation, without evidence of direct digital-asset handling. (ir.oneconnectft.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign bizserv = 1.0 because the available evidence shows substantive discussion of blockchain-related service provision, but only as embedded software/technology services delivered to financial-institution clients. I assign zero to holders, miners, finserv, and infrastr because I found no evidence during January 2017 through December 2024 that OneConnect held crypto as treasury assets, mined crypto, directly intermediated crypto financial products or trading, or supplied dedicated crypto-mining/ blockchain compute infrastructure. invbase = 0 because I did not find explicit evidence that the stock traded as a recognized crypto proxy or that management described a crypto-driven investor base. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is taxonomy-related: management discussed blockchain repeatedly, but the evidence points to enterprise fintech software usage rather than direct involvement with crypto-assets. Under the project rules, that fits bizserv rather than finserv or infrastr. (fool.com)

A second limitation is that some SEC filing pages and transcript repositories were only partially accessible in-browser, so I relied on accessible transcript text, EDGAR filing-index pages, and company disclosures rather than a fully scraped multi-year transcript panel. Even with that limitation, the retrieved sources consistently portray OneConnect as a financial-software vendor using blockchain as an enabling technology, not as a crypto-holder, miner, exchange, custodian, or crypto-infrastructure firm. Because blockchain-related revenue is not separately disclosed, I code RevShare = 1 as minor and AssetShare = 0. (fool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 7 May 2020, Q1 2020 earnings call transcript: management said, “we're using the blockchain technologies in supporting our solutions” in “cross-border trading” and “personal credit assessment solutions,” indicating blockchain-enabled service provision rather than direct crypto intermediation — Motley Fool transcript
  • 7 May 2020, Q1 2020 earnings call transcript: management said “we normally don't separate, calculate the revenues generated from these underlying technologies because we're more focused on the solutions,” implying blockchain revenue is bundled and peripheral — Motley Fool transcript
  • 14 Nov 2024, Q3 2024 earnings call transcript: remarks focused on digitalization challenges, product capabilities, digital banking and overseas expansion, with no discussion of crypto trading, custody, mining, or treasury holdings — Q3 2024 transcript PDF

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 23 Apr 2024, Form 20-F filing index for FY2023: SEC filing confirms annual-report coverage for OneConnect Financial Technology Co., Ltd. for year ended 31 Dec 2023; no crypto-specific operating segment is evident from the retrieved filing materials — SEC filing index
  • 24 Apr 2025, Form 20-F filing index for FY2024: SEC filing confirms annual-report coverage for year ended 31 Dec 2024; accessible filing metadata identifies the company as a technology-services filer rather than a digital-asset issuer or miner — SEC filing index
  • 2024 ESG report excerpt: company states it has “world-leading technology capabilities for financial services, including artificial intelligence (AI), Big Data Analytics, Blockchain, and more,” framing blockchain as a general capability within fintech services — SEC-hosted ESG exhibit excerpt

Financial news / other:

  • 25 Apr 2025 ESG press release summarizing 2024 activity: OneConnect describes itself as a “leading technology service provider” focused on digital transformation across the financial sector — OneConnect IR press release
  • 16 Mar 2024 full-year 2023/2024-results press release: OneConnect describes itself as a “technology-as-a-service provider for the financial services industry in China” and discusses product optimization and financial-institution customers rather than crypto business lines — OneConnect IR results release
  • 2024 company news item: OneConnect says it leverages “artificial intelligence, big data, and blockchain” as enabling technologies for financial institutions, consistent with enterprise service exposure rather than direct crypto-asset activity — OCFT company news

INTERCONTINENTAL EX.

  • Rank: 178 | Expo: 0.30 | id_str: US45866F1049
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (2018–2020), ICE annual reports / 10-K filings (2019, 2021, 2024), shareholder letter / company materials on Bakkt

Firm Overview

Intercontinental Exchange, Inc. is the U.S.-listed owner of the New York Stock Exchange and a broader operator of exchanges, clearing houses, market data, and mortgage technology platforms. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample, its crypto exposure came through incubating, funding, and retaining an economic interest in Bakkt, a regulated digital-asset platform offering bitcoin futures, custody, and related digital-asset services. (s2.q4cdn.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports finserv because management repeatedly described Bakkt as a digital-asset ecosystem involving physically delivered bitcoin futures, custody/warehouse services, and broader digital-asset market infrastructure. On the Q3 2018 call, ICE said Bakkt planned to launch “a daily, physically delivered bitcoin future and a warehouse for storage,” and on the Q3 2019 call it said Bakkt had completed the first physical delivery of bitcoin in a regulated environment. (fool.com)

I do not assign holders because I found no evidence that ICE itself pursued a corporate treasury strategy of holding crypto on its own balance sheet; the exposure was through ownership and development of Bakkt rather than direct HODL activity. I do not assign miners, bizserv, or infrastr because the retrieved sources point to direct digital-asset financial intermediation and custody, not mining, non-financial support services, or dedicated mining/compute infrastructure. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

I assign finserv = 1.0 because the crypto-related activity documented for ICE during 2018–2024 is entirely concentrated in Bakkt’s exchange/custody/digital-asset marketplace functions, which fit the project’s financial-services channel. The exposure appears strategically meaningful enough to be discussed on multiple earnings calls and in annual-report materials, but still peripheral relative to ICE’s overall exchange, data, and mortgage businesses, so I code RevShare = 1 as minor rather than material; I code AssetShare = 0 because the evidence indicates equity-investment exposure to Bakkt rather than crypto assets dominating ICE’s own balance sheet. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main boundary issue is whether some of Bakkt-related language could justify infrastr because management referred to “trusted infrastructure” and ICE technology/security capabilities. Under the project rules, I keep the firm in finserv because the cited products were regulated bitcoin futures, custody, wallet, and digital-asset marketplace functions that directly handled digital assets rather than merely supplying neutral hardware or compute. (fool.com)

A second limitation is materiality calibration: ICE’s filings discuss Bakkt as an investment and strategic initiative, but crypto revenue is not separately disclosed as a major ICE operating segment in the retrieved materials. That supports a strong mechanism classification but only a low revenue-share code. I did not find explicit evidence that ICE stock itself was treated by management or major financial media as a primary crypto proxy, so invbase = 0. (s2.q4cdn.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 31 Oct 2018, Q3 2018 earnings call transcript: management said Bakkt planned to launch “a daily, physically delivered bitcoin future and a warehouse for storage,” directly evidencing crypto financial intermediation and custody — Motley Fool transcript
  • 7 Feb 2019, Q4 2018 earnings call transcript: management said ICE invested in strategic initiatives “including fixed income, mortgages and along with our partners, the launch of Bakkt,” and described blockchain/digital-asset use cases around Bakkt — Motley Fool transcript
  • 31 Oct 2019, Q3 2019 earnings call transcript: management said Bakkt “successfully launched its first ever physically settled bitcoin future” and completed “the first ever physical delivery of Bitcoins” in a regulated environment — Motley Fool transcript
  • 4 Feb 2021, Q4 2020 earnings call transcript: CEO said ICE seeded Bakkt to “build a regulated ecosystem that would support the full life cycle of a digital asset” and had launched futures, options, wallet, and custody capabilities — Motley Fool transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2019 Form 10-K: ICE disclosed that “Bakkt launched a custodial solution for Bitcoin futures traded on ICE Futures U.S.,” confirming direct crypto financial-services exposure — SEC 2019 10-K
  • 2021 Form 10-K / annual report: ICE disclosed that Bakkt completed its merger with VIH on 15 Oct 2021 and that ICE retained an interest in the public digital-asset company — ICE 2021 Annual Report PDF
  • 2024 Form 10-K: ICE said its Bakkt equity-method investment had been reduced to zero and discussed Bakkt’s business model and digital-asset market volatility as ongoing risk factors — SEC 2024 10-K
  • 3 Feb 2022, Q4 2021 earnings release: ICE reported it “recognized a $1.4 billion gain upon our deconsolidation of Bakkt,” corroborating that the exposure was via ownership/investment in a crypto financial-services affiliate rather than direct treasury crypto — ICE Q4 2021 earnings release
  • 2018 shareholder letter: ICE said Bakkt planned to enable institutions and consumers to “buy, sell, store and spend digital assets” in a regulated ecosystem — ICE 2018 Letter to Shareholders

Financial news / other:

  • 11 Jan 2021, transaction announcement: Bakkt was described as the “digital asset marketplace launched by Intercontinental Exchange in 2018,” with services spanning cryptocurrency and digital-asset use cases — Bakkt transaction announcement
  • 23 Feb 2024, financial profile article: Forbes reported that less than 1% of Bakkt’s revenue came from crypto services, which is useful context for coding ICE’s crypto-linked revenue exposure as minor rather than central — Forbes article

INTERACTIVE BROKERS GROUP A

  • Rank: 179 | Expo: 0.30 | id_str: US45841N1072
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: 2021 Q4 earnings call transcript, 2023 Form 10-K / annual report, company and partner product disclosures

Firm Overview

Interactive Brokers Group, Inc. is a large U.S.-listed electronic brokerage firm based in Connecticut that provides trading, clearing, and custody-related access across global asset classes for individuals, advisors, hedge funds, and introducing brokers. Within that broader brokerage model, its crypto exposure during 2017–2024 came from enabling customers to trade cryptocurrencies through third-party crypto service providers integrated into the IBKR platform.

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports finserv because Interactive Brokers explicitly offered cryptocurrency trading to customers and advisors through regulated partners, charging transaction commissions and integrating crypto access into its brokerage platform. Management discussed the crypto product on earnings calls, while the annual report described customer crypto trading and custody arrangements through Paxos Trust Company or Zero Hash.

I do not assign holders because the filings state that IBKR does not have legal title to customers’ cryptocurrency assets and does not maintain the keys or wallets needed to access them. I do not assign infrastr or bizserv because the firm is not merely supplying neutral software or consulting services; it is offering customer-facing brokerage access to crypto markets, which fits the project’s financial-services category.

Weight Assignment

I assign finserv = 1.0 because all retrieved crypto evidence points to a single mechanism: Interactive Brokers offers brokerage access to cryptocurrency trading through third-party exchange, execution, custody, and related services embedded in its platform. The activity is clearly real and repeatedly disclosed, but management described client uptake as limited and the annual report shows customer crypto assets at only $172 million against a much larger overall balance sheet, so I code RevShare = 1 as minor and AssetShare = 1 as minor rather than material. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main boundary issue is whether the crypto-related balance-sheet line created by SAB 121 should imply holders. I do not code holders because the 2023 annual report explicitly says IBKR does not provide execution, custody, or safeguarding for the customers’ crypto assets, does not control the keys, and has no legal title or claim to those assets; the recognized safeguarding asset/liability is therefore an accounting consequence of platform activity rather than a treasury investment. (sec.gov)

A second limitation is investor-base amplification. Management acknowledged in January 2022 that crypto brought only “a small number” of new clients and that take-up was disappointing, which argues against coding IBKR as a major crypto-proxy stock during the sample period. I therefore keep invbase = 0 despite clear operating exposure through crypto brokerage services. (fool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 18 Jan 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: management said, “We introduced our crypto offering. U.S. individuals, RIAs, hedge funds, and introducing brokers, as well as individuals in over 100 different countries can now all trade crypto through our partner, Paxos,” directly evidencing crypto brokerage activity — Motley Fool transcript
  • 18 Jan 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: CEO said, “We are seeing a small number of new clients coming to us because of crypto,” but that take-up among existing clients was “kind of disappointing,” useful for coding minor revenue relevance and invbase = 0 — Motley Fool transcript
  • 18 Jan 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: management said IBKR charged “0.12% to 0.18% of trade value” on crypto trades, confirming fee-based financial-services exposure rather than passive holding — Motley Fool transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2023 Form 10-K / annual report: IBKR disclosed, “Customers, including both individuals and advisors, can trade Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), Litecoin (LTC) and Bitcoin Cash (BCH) through Paxos Trust Company or Zero Hash,” confirming direct crypto financial-services exposure — SEC 2023 Annual Report PDF
  • 2023 Form 10-K / annual report: risk factors said IBKR “rel[ies] on third-party Cryptocurrency Service Providers” and that eligible customers can “buy, sell and hold Cryptocurrency Assets in an account in the customer’s name at the CSP,” showing the brokerage-access model — SEC 2023 Annual Report PDF
  • 2023 Form 10-K / annual report: IBKR stated “IB LLC does not provide execution, custody or safeguarding services for the customers’ Cryptocurrency Assets” and “does not maintain (or have access to) the cryptographic key information,” excluding a holders classification — SEC 2023 Annual Report PDF
  • 2023 Form 10-K / annual report: IBKR reported the fair value of customers’ crypto-assets recognized under SAB 121 was $172 million at December 31, 2023, up from $80 million at December 31, 2022, supporting a minor AssetShare code rather than a major one — SEC 2023 Annual Report PDF
  • 2024 Form 10-K: IBKR continued to disclose that customers “can trade Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), Litecoin (LTC) and Bitcoin Cash (BCH) through Paxos Trust Company or Zero Hash LLC,” showing the crypto brokerage business remained active through 2024 — SEC 2024 Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • 13 Sep 2021, Bloomberg report summary: Bloomberg reported that Interactive Brokers was introducing cryptocurrency trading through a partnership with Paxos and listed Bitcoin, Ethereum, Litecoin, and Bitcoin Cash, corroborating the launch of the crypto brokerage product — Bloomberg result summary
  • 7 Jun 2022, partner announcement: Paxos said Interactive Brokers had “already launched” financial-advisor crypto trading and quoted IBKR saying it was offering access to cryptocurrency markets for advisors’ clients — Paxos newsroom
  • Product disclosure page, accessed via company materials: IBKR advertised cryptocurrency trading on its platform at commissions of 0.12% to 0.18% through Paxos or Zero Hash, consistent with a fee-based crypto financial-services model — Interactive Brokers crypto pricing page

DIGITALOCEAN HOLDINGS

  • Rank: 180 | Expo: 0.30 | id_str: US25402D1028
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2022–2024 Form 10-K filings, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript

Firm Overview

DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc. is a U.S.-listed cloud infrastructure provider focused on developers, startups, and small-to-medium-sized businesses, offering compute, storage, networking, and platform tools. Across the searched 2017–2024 sample, the retrieved evidence presents DigitalOcean as a general cloud and hosting platform rather than a firm with documented, substantive crypto-specific operating exposure. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto-exposure channel is evidenced in the retrieved materials. The company’s 2022, 2023, and 2024 Form 10-K filings returned no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain,” which weighs strongly against coding holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. (sec.gov)

The transcript search did identify accessible earnings-call materials, satisfying the transcript-search step, but the retrieved Q4 2024 earnings call discusses cloud, AI/ML, data-center capacity, and customer cohorts without any crypto, blockchain, bitcoin, or mining discussion. That makes DigitalOcean a general IT/cloud company in this sample rather than a documented crypto infrastructure provider under the project’s explicit-evidence standard. (fool.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign unclass = 1.0 because the searched transcript and filing evidence does not show deliberate crypto holdings, mining activity, crypto financial services, non-financial crypto services, or crypto-targeted infrastructure. Although DigitalOcean operates cloud infrastructure generally, the classification rules require explicit evidence of material crypto- or mining-directed workloads for infrastr, and that evidence was not found in the retrieved sources. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main borderline issue is whether a general cloud-computing company should receive infrastr exposure simply because crypto firms could in principle use its servers. Under the project’s exclusion rule, that is not enough: general-purpose cloud providers qualify only when there is explicit evidence of material crypto- or mining-directed demand, and the retrieved 10-Ks and earnings-call materials do not provide that evidence. (sec.gov)

A second limitation is source accessibility for older transcripts: Seeking Alpha transcript pages were listed but several direct fetches failed, so the accessible Motley Fool transcript and SEC filings carried most of the evidential weight. Even so, because this firm is coded unclass = 1.0, no certainty score is assigned under the instructions. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 25 Feb 2025, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript: management said it would “share several product innovation and customer use cases across both core cloud and AI/ML,” with no crypto-related discussion in the retrieved transcript, anchoring the absence of explicit crypto exposure — Motley Fool transcript
  • 25 Feb 2025, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript: management discussed “incremental capacity for both AI and our core cloud offerings” at its Atlanta data center, consistent with general cloud infrastructure rather than crypto-targeted infrastructure — Motley Fool transcript
  • Seeking Alpha transcript index, covering 2021–2024 calls: transcript availability was confirmed, but direct retrieval of several historical transcript pages failed during scraping — Seeking Alpha transcript index

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 25 Feb 2025, 2024 Form 10-K filing page: SEC filing detail confirms DigitalOcean’s 2024 annual report for the period ended 31 Dec 2024; text search of the filing found no matches for crypto, bitcoin, or blockchain — SEC filing index, 2024 10-K
  • 21 Feb 2024, 2023 Form 10-K filing page: SEC filing detail confirms the 2023 annual report for the period ended 31 Dec 2023; text search of the filing found no matches for crypto, bitcoin, or blockchain — SEC filing index, 2023 10-K
  • 22 Feb 2023, 2022 Form 10-K filing page: SEC filing detail confirms the 2022 annual report for the period ended 31 Dec 2022; text search of the filing found no matches for crypto, bitcoin, or blockchain — SEC filing index, 2022 10-K
  • 2024 annual report HTML: retrieved annual report contains no matching references to “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain,” reinforcing the unclassified coding — SEC 2024 Form 10-K HTML
  • 2023 annual report HTML: retrieved annual report contains no matching references to “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain,” reinforcing the unclassified coding — SEC 2023 Form 10-K HTML
  • 2022 annual report HTML: retrieved annual report contains no matching references to “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain,” reinforcing the unclassified coding — SEC 2022 Form 10-K HTML

Financial news / other:

  • 18 Nov 2025 prospectus-style company summary referencing the latest 10-K: described DigitalOcean as an IaaS cloud platform for developers, startups, and SMBs, with no crypto characterization — SEC-linked company summary
  • Web search across Reuters-style and broader news queries for DigitalOcean plus crypto/blockchain/bitcoin did not retrieve credible evidence of DigitalOcean being treated as a crypto proxy stock or crypto-focused operator during the sample period — Search results set

4 SC

  • Rank: 181 | Expo: 0.29 | id_str: DE000A3E5C40
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: company annual reports (2022–2024), company conference-call / quarterly materials (Q3 2023), company FY2024 results release

Firm Overview

4SC AG is a German clinical-stage biotech company listed in Frankfurt and focused on oncology drug development, especially resminostat (Kinselby) for cutaneous T-cell lymphoma. Across the retrieved 2017–2024 investor materials, the company presents itself as a small biopharmaceutical developer centered on clinical, regulatory, partnering, and financing activities rather than any crypto-related business. (4sc.de)

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive crypto exposure channel was identified for 4SC AG. The retrieved annual reports for 2022, 2023, and 2024 contain no matches for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” or “blockchain,” which weighs against coding holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. (4sc.de)

The accessible investor-call material retrieved from the company website likewise discusses resminostat development, commercialization, partnering, and cash runway, with no crypto, digital-asset, blockchain, or mining discussion. In the project’s decision tree, that leaves 4SC as unclassified rather than any of the crypto-exposed mechanism categories. (4sc.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign unclass = 1.0 because the searched transcript-style / conference-call material and filing-equivalent annual reports do not show deliberate crypto holdings, mining activity, crypto financial services, non-financial crypto services to crypto firms, or crypto-targeted infrastructure. The evidence instead consistently describes 4SC as a biotech issuer focused on drug development, regulatory review, partnering, and financing. (4sc.de)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that fully transcribed earnings-call text was not readily available from major transcript databases in the retrieved sources; however, the company’s own conference-call/webcast index and quarterly reporting materials were accessible, and the available Q3 2023 investor material contained no crypto-related discussion. Because the firm is coded unclass = 1.0, no certainty score is assigned under the instructions. (4sc.com)

Another limitation is that news-search results did not surface credible financial-media coverage portraying 4SC as a crypto proxy stock or crypto-adjacent operator during January 2017 to December 2024. The absence of such coverage is consistent with, but not by itself determinative of, the unclassified coding. (sa.marketscreener.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 19 Oct 2023, Q3 2023 investor material: “4SC is focused primarily on the development of resminostat (Kinselby) and in particular, preparation for the commercialization of resminostat (Kinselby)” — 4SC Q3 2023 Announcement
  • 19 Oct 2023, Q3 2023 investor material: as of 30 September 2023 management discussed cash funds and financing runway, with no crypto-related discussion in the retrieved text — 4SC Q3 2023 Announcement
  • Company conference-call archive covering 2023–2024 confirms investor call / webcast availability, but retrieved accessible materials did not show crypto references — 4SC Conference Calls & Webcasts

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 28 Mar 2025, Annual Report 2024: 4SC describes itself as “a biotech company” focused on resminostat/Kinselby and lists financing and regulatory milestones, with no bitcoin/crypto/blockchain references found — 4SC Annual Report 2024
  • 22 Mar 2024, Annual Report 2023: text search found no matches for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” or “blockchain” — 4SC Annual Report 2023
  • 17 Mar 2023, Annual Report 2022: text search found no matches for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” or “blockchain” — 4SC Annual Report 2022

Financial news / other:

  • 28 Mar 2025, company FY2024 results release: CEO said 4SC was “one hundred percent focused on the development of resminostat,” inconsistent with any documented crypto business line — 4SC FY2024 results release
  • MarketScreener company profile/news listing describes 4SC as a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company; retrieved listing did not indicate crypto characterization — MarketScreener company page

TANLA PLATFORMS

  • Rank: 182 | Expo: 0.29 | id_str: INE483C01032
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.7, infrastr 0.3, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: company investor-call / quarterly materials (FY25, with archive evidence for earlier calls), company product pages, integrated report / annual-report materials, financial news

Firm Overview

Tanla Platforms Ltd is an Indian listed communications-platform company headquartered in Hyderabad and focused on CPaaS, enterprise messaging, anti-spam, anti-scam, and customer-engagement software. Across the retrieved 2017–2024 materials, its crypto-adjacent exposure comes not from digital assets or crypto finance, but from using blockchain / distributed-ledger architecture inside telecom-compliance, settlement, consent, and messaging-trust products. (tanla.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

Tanla does not appear to hold crypto on balance sheet, mine crypto, or operate a crypto exchange, brokerage, custody, or investment product. Instead, the evidence points to non-financial blockchain-enabled software and services such as Trubloq, settlement reports, consent management, and anti-phishing audit trails, which fits bizserv under the project rules rather than finserv. (tanla.com)

A smaller infrastr weight is also warranted because Tanla explicitly markets blockchain-enabled “SSOT” and settlement architecture as a traceability layer across communications workflows. Still, the dominant economic activity remains application-layer enterprise software and services, not hardware, mining compute, colocation, or energy infrastructure purpose-built for crypto workloads, so bizserv should dominate the mix. (tanla.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign bizserv = 0.7 and infrastr = 0.3. The larger bizserv weight reflects that Tanla’s blockchain exposure is presented mainly as software-enabled compliance, trust, reconciliation, anti-spam, and anti-scam services sold to enterprises, banks, telcos, and platform users rather than direct handling of digital assets. The smaller infrastr component reflects the company’s repeated description of blockchain/SSOT as a foundational transaction-recording layer within its messaging ecosystem, but the available evidence does not support coding Tanla as a primary crypto-mining or crypto-compute infrastructure supplier. (moneycontrol.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that the accessible Tanla investor-call archive page did not render transcript text directly, and one downloadable transcript fetch timed out, so the earnings-call anchor relies on retrieved quarterly-results / conference-call material and transcript archive availability rather than a long multi-year transcript set. That said, the accessible FY25 investor material and multiple company product pages consistently describe blockchain-enabled products, which stabilizes the classification. (tanla.com)

Another limitation is that the evidence supports blockchain-enabled enterprise software exposure, but not crypto-asset exposure in the narrower sense of coins, tokens, custody, or trading. I therefore exclude holders, miners, and finserv, and also set invbase = 0 because the retrieved sources did not show Tanla being explicitly characterized in credible financial media as a crypto proxy stock driven by crypto-investor sentiment. (tanla.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 21 Jan 2025, Q3 FY25 results / earnings-call notice: management said, “We were the first to launch and implement PE/TM binding on our Trubloq platform,” tying quarterly investor material to its blockchain-linked compliance platform — Tanla Q3 FY25 results
  • 24 Apr 2025, Q4 FY25 results / conference-call notice: Tanla described itself as “India’s largest CPaaS provider” focused on SaaS, OTT, RCS, and platform scaling rather than crypto-asset activities — Tanla Q4 FY25 results
  • Investor-call archive page confirms transcript availability on the company site, though accessible text was limited in retrieval — Transcript of Investor call

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 3 Jul 2024, Integrated Annual Report FY24: Tanla’s FY24 report was filed through BSE and is presented as an integrated annual report for the CPaaS / digital-interactions business rather than a crypto-asset or mining company — Tanla Integrated Annual Report FY24
  • Company product page: “Leverage the power of blockchain to create a Single Source of Truth (SSOT) for all your digital interactions” — SSOT Enabled by Blockchain
  • Company product page: “Streamline settlement processes with Tanla's blockchain-enabled settlement reports” — Settlement Reports

Financial news / other:

  • 28 Feb 2023, Moneycontrol: Tanla “leveraged a blockchain platform to create an audit trail” for spam/phishing messages sent to authorities — Moneycontrol article
  • 19 Jul 2023, HDFC Securities note: “Trubloq is a blockchain-based platform developed on DLT” — HDFC Securities note via Moneycontrol
  • 31 Jul 2024, Geojit note: Tanla’s products include platforms “using blockchain technology to reduce spam” — Geojit note via Moneycontrol

9F ADR 1:20

  • Rank: 183 | Expo: 0.29 | id_str: US65442R2085
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2022 Form 20-F, 2024 interim results press release / 6-K, investor-relations event calendar

Firm Overview

9F Inc. is a China-based Nasdaq-listed digital technology and consumer-finance platform company whose historical businesses have included lending facilitation, wealth-management related offerings, insurance brokerage, and other fintech services. In the retrieved 2017–2024 materials, the company is described as a digital technology service provider for institutional partners and consumer-finance activities rather than as a crypto, blockchain, mining, custody, or digital-asset business. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

No meaningful crypto-exposure channel could be substantiated for 9F across the searched 2017–2024 source set. The key annual filing defines the company’s operations around wealth-management services, internet securities service, fintech business, fund sales, insurance brokerage, and prior lending activities, but the filing search returned no matches for “blockchain,” “bitcoin,” “cryptocurrency,” or “digital asset.” (sec.gov)

The 2024 interim-results release likewise discusses technical-services revenue, sale income, marketable securities, and ongoing business transformation, but does not indicate crypto holdings, mining, exchange/brokerage/custody activity, or crypto-specific infrastructure/services. Because the project requires substantive evidence rather than incidental adjacency, I assign no weight to holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

I assign unclass = 1.0. The retrieved filings and investor-relations materials support classifying 9F as a conventional fintech / digital financial services issuer with no documented crypto mechanism exposure during January 2017 through December 2024. The absence of any crypto terminology in the accessible 20-F, combined with non-crypto descriptions in interim results and IR materials, is stronger evidence for no meaningful exposure than for any partial category assignment. (sec.gov; sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that accessible earnings-call transcript text was sparse. The investor-relations event calendar confirms at least a September 27, 2019 “Second Quarter Earnings Conference Call,” but the retrieved event page did not expose transcript content, so no usable crypto-related earnings-call quote could be extracted. Under the coding rules, that prevents any non-unclassified assignment from being anchored by a transcript quote. (ir.9fgroup.com)

Another limitation is that the latest full annual report for fiscal 2024 was not yet available on time; 9F filed a late-filing notice on April 30, 2025 stating that its 2024 Form 20-F could not yet be filed because audited U.S. GAAP financial statements were not available. Still, the available 2022 Form 20-F and 2024 interim 6-K are sufficient to conclude that no credible 2017–2024 crypto exposure was evidenced in the searched materials, and there was no direct evidence of crypto-focused investor-base trading spillovers, so invbase = 0. (sec.gov; ir.9fgroup.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 27 Sep 2019, company IR event page: investor-relations archive shows “9F Inc. Second Quarter Earnings Conference Call,” but accessible transcript text was not retrievable and no crypto-related statement could be verified — 9F Event Calendar item

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 16 May 2023, Form 20-F for FY2022: 9F defines itself around “wealth management services (including internet securities service, fintech business, fund sales and insurance brokerage service)” and related finance activities, with no disclosed crypto line of business — 9F 2022 Form 20-F
  • 16 May 2023, Form 20-F keyword check: no matches found in the filing for “blockchain,” “bitcoin,” “cryptocurrency,” or “digital asset” — 9F 2022 Form 20-F
  • 27 Sep 2024, Form 6-K / H1 2024 results: 9F described itself as “a digital technology service provider aiming to empower institutional partners with advanced financial technologies,” with discussion of technical-services revenue and investments but no crypto activity — 9F H1 2024 results 6-K
  • 30 Apr 2025, NT 20-F relating to FY2024: the company said its 2024 Form 20-F was delayed because audited financial statements were not yet available — 9F NT 20-F

Financial news / other:

  • Investor-relations annual reports page lists annual reports for 2019–2022, consistent with 9F’s public-financial-reporting history but without any visible crypto framing in the archive metadata — 9F Annual Reports archive
  • 30 May 2025, company IR press release: Nasdaq late-filing notice describes 9F’s issue as delayed filing compliance and audit completion, not crypto-related business developments — 9F Nasdaq notice release

TWITTER DEAD - DELIST.28/10/22

  • Rank: 184 | Expo: 0.29 | id_str: US90184L1026
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.6, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.4
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: Form 10-K / annual report (2021), Q4 2021 earnings call transcript, financial news on Twitter’s crypto product initiatives and internal crypto team

Firm Overview

Twitter, Inc. was a U.S.-listed social-media platform company until its October 2022 take-private transaction. During the 2017–2024 sample window, its core business remained advertising and platform monetization, but by 2021–2022 it had introduced limited crypto-related user features such as Bitcoin tipping and explored broader Web3 / blockchain-related creator tools. (sec.gov; Reuters via Investing.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports a minority but real finserv classification because Twitter explicitly enabled users to send creators payments “using a variety of payment methods … including bitcoin,” which is direct crypto-payment functionality rather than mere discussion of crypto culture. The 2021 Form 10-K also lists Tips as a launched creator-monetization product and specifically states that users could send currency or Bitcoin to creators. (sec.gov)

The earnings-call anchor is the February 10, 2022 Q4 2021 call, where CEO Parag Agrawal said Twitter had “a small internal team” looking at opportunities arising from “the broader crypto ecosystem,” including cryptocurrencies, DeFi projects, and applications built on blockchains, to benefit creators and consumers on Twitter. This confirms active strategic exploration, but the retrieved sources do not show crypto as a major revenue driver, balance-sheet asset, mining business, or infrastructure business. (The Motley Fool transcript)

Weight Assignment

I assign finserv = 0.6 and unclass = 0.4. The positive weight reflects direct platform-level crypto payment functionality and management’s explicit discussion of product opportunities tied to crypto and blockchain ecosystems. However, crypto remained peripheral to Twitter’s overall advertising-led business, with no evidence of treasury crypto holdings, mining activity, or material crypto-segment revenue; therefore the classification should not be a full pure-play assignment. (sec.gov; The Motley Fool transcript; TechCrunch)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is whether Twitter’s crypto functionality is best viewed as a true financial-service mechanism or merely a product feature layered onto a non-financial platform. I resolve this toward partial finserv because the company directly enabled Bitcoin-based creator payments, but the available sources do not quantify transaction volume or fee income, so materiality appears low. (sec.gov)

Another limitation is that the firm was delisted on October 28, 2022 after Elon Musk’s acquisition, so post-transaction 2023–2024 public-company transcript coverage is unavailable. News reports about Binance’s role and Twitter’s blockchain ambitions are useful corroboration, but they do not establish major economic crypto exposure for the listed entity before delisting, and I found no direct evidence of crypto-proxy investor-base trading sufficient for invbase = 1. (Bloomberg; Reuters via Investing.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 10 Feb 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: “We have a small internal team which is looking at opportunities in terms of how we might harness this change” in the “broader crypto ecosystem” — The Motley Fool transcript
  • 10 Feb 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: management described the opportunity set as including “cryptocurrencies,” DeFi projects, and applications built on blockchains — The Motley Fool transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 16 Feb 2022, Form 10-K for FY2021: Twitter said it launched products to help people and businesses get paid on Twitter, including “Tips,” which lets users send “currency or Bitcoin to creators” — Twitter 2021 Form 10-K
  • 16 Feb 2022, Form 10-K keyword check: no matches were found for “blockchain,” “cryptocurrency,” or “digital asset,” indicating crypto exposure was present but narrow and feature-specific rather than a broad disclosed segment — Twitter 2021 Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • 10 Nov 2021, TechCrunch: Twitter said it was formally launching a crypto team as “a center of excellence for all things blockchain and web3,” exploring crypto payments, creator monetization, and NFT tooling — TechCrunch
  • 28 Oct 2022, Reuters: report said Twitter had already begun exploring ways to incorporate blockchain technology under Jack Dorsey and had launched an internal crypto team in November 2021 — Reuters via Investing.com
  • 28 Oct 2022, Bloomberg: Binance said it invested in Musk’s Twitter deal and wanted to help bring social media and Web3 together, corroborating external market perception of Twitter as experimenting with crypto-related features around the take-private date — Bloomberg

FRANKLIN RESOURCES

  • Rank: 185 | Expo: 0.28 | id_str: US3546131018
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.2, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcript excerpts (2024), company investor materials (2022, 2024), Franklin digital-asset product pages, ETF filing

Firm Overview

Franklin Resources, Inc. is the listed parent of Franklin Templeton, a global asset manager in the Financials sector. During 2017–2024, its crypto-related activity appeared mainly through digital-asset investment products and tokenized fund infrastructure rather than treasury crypto holdings or mining.

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports a primary finserv classification because Franklin Templeton directly offers crypto investment products and related digital-asset strategies. Management said on the November 4, 2024 earnings call that the firm had launched the Franklin Bitcoin ETF and Franklin Ethereum ETF, making it easier for clients to access digital-asset investment opportunities, and 2022 company materials also described new digital-asset SMA strategies. (Fintool transcript excerpt; Franklin Resources 2022 executive commentary)

A secondary infrastr weight is also warranted because Franklin Templeton built blockchain-enabled fund plumbing, including the first U.S.-registered mutual fund to use a public blockchain to process transactions and record share ownership, and its digital-assets capability page says its team has been active since 2018 building blockchain-based technology solutions and running node validators. This is more than incidental marketing language, but the firm is still fundamentally an asset manager monetizing products and client access, so infrastructure should remain subordinate to finserv. (Fintool transcript excerpt; Franklin Templeton digital assets page)

Weight Assignment

I assign finserv = 0.8 and infrastr = 0.2. The larger weight goes to finserv because the clearest economic channel is regulated crypto and digital-asset investment products: spot bitcoin ETF sponsorship, ethereum ETF launch in 2024, digital-asset SMAs, and tokenized fund offerings for clients. The smaller infrastr weight captures the firm’s blockchain operating stack and validator activity, which are clearly real but appear to support the investment-product franchise rather than constitute a standalone infrastructure business. (Fintool transcript excerpt; Franklin Resources 2022 executive commentary; Franklin Templeton digital assets page; Franklin Bitcoin ETF 10-K)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is whether blockchain-enabled fund administration should be coded partly as finserv or partly as infrastr. I resolve this with a minority infrastr weight because the company explicitly describes blockchain-based technology solutions and validator activity, but the monetized client-facing exposure in 2017–2024 is still mainly investment products and asset-management services. (Franklin Templeton digital assets page)

A second limitation is materiality. The available sources clearly establish substantive crypto activity, but the retrieved parent-company earnings materials do not isolate crypto revenue as a large share of Franklin Resources’ total operating revenues, which were $8.478 billion for fiscal 2024 in the earnings release; accordingly RevShare is kept at 1 and AssetShare at 0 for the listed parent. I also did not find direct evidence that BEN traded primarily as a crypto-proxy stock during 2017–2024, so invbase = 0. (Franklin Resources Q4 2024 earnings release; Reuters via Investing.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 4 Nov 2024, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript: “In the digital asset space, we continue to look for new ways to leverage blockchain technology.” — Fintool transcript
  • 4 Nov 2024, Q4 2024 earnings call transcript: “this year, we launched Franklin Bitcoin ETF and Franklin Ethereum ETF” to make client access to digital-asset opportunities easier — Fintool transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2022 fiscal-year executive commentary: Franklin said it partnered with Eaglebrook Advisors to offer two digital-asset SMAs, “Digital Assets Core” and “Digital Assets Core Capped,” investing in 10 to 15 of the largest digital assets — Franklin Resources 2022 executive commentary
  • 2022 fiscal-year executive commentary: Franklin said it expanded the Franklin OnChain U.S. Government Money Fund, “the first US registered mutual fund to use blockchain to process transactions and record share ownership” — Franklin Resources 2022 executive commentary
  • 1 Jul 2024, Franklin Bitcoin ETF annual report: the ETF is a Franklin-sponsored vehicle holding bitcoin exposure for investors; the filing notes Franklin Resources was an affiliate of the seed capital investor — Franklin Bitcoin ETF 10-K
  • 4 Nov 2024, Q4 FY2024 earnings release: parent-company total operating revenues were $8.478 billion for fiscal 2024, indicating crypto was present but not a dominant disclosed revenue line — Franklin Resources Q4 2024 earnings release

Financial news / other:

  • Franklin Templeton digital-assets capability page: the firm says its 50+ person team has been active since 2018 “building blockchain-based technology solutions, developing a range of investment strategies and running node validators” — Franklin Templeton digital assets page
  • 21 Nov 2024, Reuters: Franklin Templeton sought SEC approval for a crypto index ETF, showing continued expansion of direct crypto investment-product offerings — Reuters via Investing.com
  • 12 Sep 2023, Bloomberg: Franklin Templeton joined the race to offer a U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF, corroborating market recognition of the firm’s direct crypto-financial-services push — Bloomberg

ALLIANCE RSO.PTNS.L P UT LP.

  • Rank: 186 | Expo: 0.28 | id_str: US01877R1086
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 1.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2022, 2024), Q1 2024 and Q4 2024 earnings call transcripts

Firm Overview

Alliance Resource Partners LP is a U.S.-listed energy partnership focused primarily on coal production and royalty operations, with additional oil and gas mineral interests. Within the 2017–2024 sample period, its crypto exposure arises from a small in-house bitcoin mining activity tied to underutilized electricity at a mine site rather than from financial services or treasury allocation. (investor.arlp.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports miners only. Management stated on the Q1 2024 earnings call that the partnership began mining bitcoin in the second half of 2020 as a pilot project to monetize underutilized electricity load at its River View mine, and it disclosed both owned bitcoin and crypto-mining PP&E on that call. (nasdaq.com)

The 2024 10-K corroborates that Bitiki began crypto-mining activities in the second half of 2020 as a bitcoin mining pilot project, and the filing also records a $22.4 million increase in the fair value of digital assets during 2024. Because the bitcoin holdings stem from self-mining and there is no evidence in the retrieved sources of a separate treasury accumulation policy, the holders category is excluded under the instructions’ mined-coin rule. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

I assign miners = 1.0. The crypto channel identified in both earnings-call and filing evidence is direct bitcoin mining using surplus electricity capacity at a mine operation, with associated mining equipment and mined digital assets on the balance sheet. I do not assign weight to holders because the available evidence indicates the bitcoin position was generated operationally through mining rather than acquired as a deliberate treasury investment, and I do not assign infrastr because ARLP appears to be mining for its own account rather than supplying crypto infrastructure to third parties. (nasdaq.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The main ambiguity is whether ARLP’s retained mined bitcoin warrants some holders weight. I resolve this against holders because the retrieved evidence frames the activity as a mining pilot and subsequent mining operation, not as a stand-alone bitcoin treasury strategy during 2017–2024. (nasdaq.com)

A second limitation is materiality. The available sources clearly establish real crypto-mining activity, but they also indicate it is small relative to the partnership’s core coal and royalty businesses; accordingly RevShare is set at 1 and AssetShare at 1 rather than a higher code. I did not find explicit evidence in the retrieved earnings materials or mainstream financial news that ARLP was primarily discussed as a crypto-proxy stock during the sample period, so invbase = 0. (fool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 29 Apr 2024, Q1 2024 earnings call transcript: “In the second half of 2020, we started mining Bitcoin as a pilot project to monetize already paid for yet underutilized electricity load at our River View mine.” — Nasdaq transcript
  • 29 Apr 2024, Q1 2024 earnings call transcript: “Since then, we have mined and now own Bitcoin valued at approximately $30 million at the end of the 2024 quarter.” — Nasdaq transcript
  • 29 Apr 2024, Q1 2024 earnings call transcript: “Our crypto mining net property, plant, and equipment book balance at the end of the 2024 quarter was $7.3 million.” — Nasdaq transcript
  • 3 Feb 2025, discussing FY2024 results on the Q4 2024 earnings call: management said bitcoin mining “began in 2020 as a pilot project to monetize underutilized electricity load capacity at our mines” — Motley Fool transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 31 Dec 2024, Form 10-K: the filing states that “Bitiki began crypto-mining activities during the second half of 2020 as a bitcoin mining pilot project to monetize already paid for, yet underutilized, electricity load” — SEC 2024 10-K
  • 31 Dec 2024, Form 10-K: ARLP disclosed that it “recorded a $22.4 million increase in the fair value of our digital assets reflecting the increase in the price of bitcoin during 2024” — SEC 2024 10-K
  • 31 Dec 2022, Form 10-K: ARLP remained a coal-focused partnership listed on Nasdaq under ARLP, providing context that crypto was an ancillary activity rather than the core business — SEC 2022 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • 30 Apr 2024, Investing.com earnings-call summary: ARLP’s growth commentary highlighted “new industry opportunities like data centers and Bitcoin mining,” corroborating market recognition of the mining activity as a side business — Investing.com
  • Nov 2023, investor presentation: ARLP described itself as a diversified energy company centered on coal mining, royalties, and selected new ventures, supporting the view that bitcoin mining was not the dominant business line — ARLP investor presentation

DATA#3

  • Rank: 187 | Expo: 0.28 | id_str: AU000000DTL4
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (2020, 2023, H1 2024, FY2024), annual report / investor materials

Firm Overview

Data#3 Limited is an Australian-listed information technology services and solutions provider focused on cloud, modern workplace, security, data and analytics, and connectivity offerings for enterprise and government customers. Its retrieved investor materials and earnings transcripts portray a conventional IT reseller/services business rather than a crypto-focused or crypto-adjacent operating model. (investor.data3.com, company-announcements.afr.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive crypto exposure channel is evidenced in the retrieved sources, so the firm is classified as unclass. Searches across multiple earnings-call transcripts from 2020 through 2024 did not surface management discussion of bitcoin, crypto, blockchain, digital assets, mining, custody, exchange activity, or crypto-linked infrastructure. (in.marketscreener.com, in.marketscreener.com, in.marketscreener.com, in.marketscreener.com, in.marketscreener.com)

The 2023 annual report and FY24 results material likewise showed no references to crypto-related terms in the retrieved documents. On that basis, none of holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr is supported by explicit evidence during the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period. (annualreports.com, company-announcements.afr.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign unclass = 1.0. Although transcript coverage was available, the retrieved earnings-call and filing-style materials do not show meaningful discussion of digital assets, blockchain services, mining operations, treasury holdings, or crypto-specific infrastructure, so the threshold for a non-zero crypto-exposure mechanism is not met. I also assign invbase = 0 because I did not find evidence in the retrieved materials or search results that Data#3 was treated as a crypto proxy stock or discussed as having a crypto-driven investor base. (in.marketscreener.com, annualreports.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that the accessible transcript sources were third-party transcript pages rather than Data#3-hosted webcast transcripts. However, because several transcript years were checked and keyword searches were uniformly negative, this does not appear to be a close classification call. (in.marketscreener.com)

Another limitation is that one transcript quote is normally required to anchor non-unclassified assignments; here, no crypto-related transcript quote was retrievable because no crypto discussion was found. That absence supports unclass rather than lowering certainty on a positive classification. None identified beyond that. (in.marketscreener.com, annualreports.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 19 Aug 2020, FY2020 earnings call transcript: no retrieved references to bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain in the transcript search results — MarketScreener transcript
  • 22 Aug 2023, FY2023 earnings call transcript: no retrieved references to bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain in the transcript search results — MarketScreener transcript
  • 15 Feb 2024, H1 2024 earnings call transcript: no retrieved references to bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain in the transcript search results — MarketScreener transcript
  • 21 Aug 2024, FY2024 earnings call transcript: no retrieved references to bitcoin, crypto, or blockchain in the transcript search results — MarketScreener transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2023 annual report: retrieved document search found no references to blockchain, bitcoin, crypto, or digital assets — Data#3 Annual Report 2023
  • 21 Aug 2024, FY24 market release: results announcement presents Data#3 as an Australian business technology solutions leader with record gross sales and no retrieved crypto-related disclosures — FY24 ASX release

Financial news / other:

  • Investor Centre company description: Data#3 describes itself as a leading Australian IT services and solutions provider focused on helping customers solve business challenges with technology solutions — Data#3 Investor Centre
  • MarketScreener company transcript index: available 2020–2024 call coverage was used to test for crypto discussion across the sample, with no substantive crypto evidence found — MarketScreener transcript index

BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON

  • Rank: 188 | Expo: 0.28 | id_str: US0640581007
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 4
  • RevShare: 0 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings call transcripts (2021–2022), BNY annual reports (2021, 2022, 2024), company press release and financial news

Firm Overview

Bank of New York Mellon is a U.S.-listed global custody bank and financial services group headquartered in New York, with core activities in asset servicing, issuer services, clearing, payments, wealth, and investment management. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, its crypto exposure appears through institutional digital-asset custody, fund administration, and related servicing rather than treasury holdings or mining. (bny.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports finserv because BNY explicitly described an integrated digital-assets offering, said it had “solidified” its leadership in servicing crypto funds, and discussed partnerships tied to crypto-fund servicing on its January 18, 2022 earnings call. (news.alphastreet.com)

That classification is reinforced by the October 11, 2022 launch of its U.S. Digital Asset Custody platform and by management’s October 2022 earnings-call statement that the bank was “now live” with digital-asset custody for institutional clients. The 2024 annual report further states that BNY’s platform offers custody and administration for Bitcoin and Ether and that its digital-asset fund services include accounting, administration, transfer agency, and ETF services. (bny.com)

I do not assign holders because the evidence concerns custody and servicing for clients or products, not a deliberate corporate treasury allocation. I do not assign infrastr, bizserv, or miners because the retrieved sources frame BNY’s role as regulated financial intermediation and asset servicing, not hardware, datacenter, consulting-only support, or mining activity. (bny.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign finserv = 1.0 because the crypto exposure mechanism is consistently and explicitly described as custody, administration, ETF servicing, and crypto-fund servicing for institutional clients and digital-asset products. Although BNY also discusses broader tokenization and blockchain themes, the concrete, evidenced channel affecting the equity is still financial-services exposure rather than non-financial support or infrastructure. The 2024 annual report says this business had a de minimis impact on assets, liabilities, revenues, and expenses, so the mechanism is clear even if current materiality is low. invbase = 0 because I did not find explicit evidence during the sample period that BK was discussed by management or mainstream financial news as a primary retail crypto-proxy stock. (bny.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that the accessible transcript evidence came from third-party transcript hosts rather than a BNY-hosted transcript archive, though the quoted passages are specific and internally consistent across years. (news.alphastreet.com)

Another limitation is that BNY’s crypto activity evolved over time: 2021 discussion emphasized crypto-fund servicing and strategic buildout, 2022 added live custody, and 2024 disclosed Bitcoin/Ether custody plus ETF-related fund services. That temporal evolution affects materiality assessment, but not the top-line classification, which remains clearly finserv. (news.alphastreet.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 18 Jan 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: “Digital assets... our investments in building an industry-first integrated digital and traditional assets offering are clearly showing positive initial results” — AlphaStreet transcript
  • 18 Jan 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: “We solidified our leadership in servicing crypto funds, with the announcement of our partnership with Grayscale Investments” — AlphaStreet transcript
  • 17 Oct 2022, Q3 2022 earnings call transcript: “we are now live with our Digital Asset Custody platform in the U.S.” — GuruFocus transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2021 annual report: BNY says it formed an enterprise Digital Assets unit in early 2021 and was providing fund services for digital-asset-linked products including those from Grayscale Investments — BNY Annual Report 2021
  • 11 Oct 2022, company press release: BNY announced its Digital Asset Custody platform was live in the U.S. for institutional clients — BNY press release
  • 2022 annual report: BNY states it was the first global systemically important bank to provide custody of digital assets and that the platform went live in October 2022 — BNY Annual Report 2022
  • 2024 annual report: “Our Digital Asset Custody platform offers custody and administration services for Bitcoin and Ether for select U.S. institutional clients” and related digital-asset initiatives had a “de minimis impact” on assets, liabilities, revenues, and expenses — BNY Annual Report 2024

Financial news / other:

  • 18 Mar 2021, Reuters-reported item: BNY invested in Fireblocks as it deepened its focus on digital assets and crypto storage infrastructure for institutions — Investing.com Reuters pickup
  • 24 Sep 2024, Bloomberg: BNY was moving closer to custody services for Bitcoin and Ether held by exchange-traded product clients after SEC review — Bloomberg
  • Digital assets solutions page, retrieved company materials: BNY markets digital-asset custody and related institutional digital-finance capabilities as part of its service stack — BNY Digital Assets Solutions

QUICKLOGIC

  • Rank: 189 | Expo: 0.28 | id_str: US74837P4054
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2019, 2024), Q4 2023 earnings call transcript

Firm Overview

QuickLogic Corporation is a U.S.-listed fabless semiconductor company focused on embedded FPGA intellectual property, low-power SoCs, discrete FPGAs, and AI software for edge and endpoint applications. Across the reviewed 2017–2024 source set, the company’s disclosed business emphasis is semiconductor IP, government and commercial design contracts, and AI/edge products rather than any identified crypto-related activity.

Exposure Channels Identified

I do not identify a substantive crypto exposure channel for QuickLogic during January 2017 to December 2024. The reviewed 10-K filings did not show references to bitcoin, blockchain, crypto, digital assets, or mining, and the accessible earnings-call transcript reviewed also contained no such discussion.

I therefore do not assign holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. There is also no evidence in the reviewed sources that QuickLogic was treated by management or mainstream financial reporting as a crypto-proxy equity, so invbase = 0.

Weight Assignment

I assign unclass = 1.0 because the retrieved evidence supports a standard semiconductor and embedded-IP business model, with no credible indication that crypto affects the equity through treasury holdings, mining, financial intermediation, crypto-focused service provision, or crypto-directed infrastructure. The absence of crypto terminology in both filings and the reviewed transcript is especially important here, because the classification rules require substantive evidence rather than adjacency or speculation.

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that some third-party transcript hosts, especially Seeking Alpha, were not directly accessible through the browser tool, so transcript retrieval relied on an accessible third-party transcript page plus SEC filings. However, because the classification is negative rather than based on a subtle boundary call, and because the searched filings also showed no crypto-related disclosure, this limitation does not change the result.

Another limitation is that QuickLogic’s products are programmable semiconductor and IP offerings, which in principle could be used in many end markets. But the instructions require explicit evidence of crypto-directed infrastructure or services, and I did not find such evidence in the reviewed sources.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 27 Feb 2024, Q4 2023 earnings call transcript: management described QuickLogic as an IP- and semiconductor-growth story, stating “The short story here is the IP business model we launched in 2020 is delivering strong results” — Fintool transcript
  • 27 Feb 2024, Q4 2023 earnings call transcript: searches of the accessible transcript returned no matches for crypto-related terms — Fintool transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 13 Mar 2020, Form 10-K for fiscal 2019: QuickLogic described itself as “a fabless semiconductor company” developing eFPGA IP, SoCs, and AI software — SEC 2019 10-K
  • 13 Mar 2020, Form 10-K for fiscal 2019: text searches of the filing returned no matches for bitcoin, blockchain, crypto, or digital assets — SEC 2019 10-K
  • 25 Mar 2025 filing covering fiscal 2024: QuickLogic described its business as programmable silicon and software platforms including eFPGA IP, SoCs, discrete FPGAs, and AI software — SEC 2024 10-K
  • 25 Mar 2025 filing covering fiscal 2024: text searches of the filing returned no matches for bitcoin, blockchain, crypto, digital assets, or mining — SEC 2024 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • Search review across major web results during the sample-focused retrieval did not produce credible financial-news evidence of QuickLogic operating as a crypto business, miner, custodian, exchange, or crypto-proxy stock — Search results summary

LIVE NATION ENTM.

  • Rank: 190 | Expo: 0.27 | id_str: US5380341090
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2017, 2023, 2024), Q3 2023 earnings call transcript

Firm Overview

Live Nation Entertainment, Inc. is a U.S.-listed live entertainment company whose core activities span concert promotion, venue operations, sponsorship, and ticketing through Ticketmaster. Across the reviewed 2017–2024 materials, the company’s disclosed business focus is live events and ticketing economics rather than any identified crypto-related operating or balance-sheet activity.

Exposure Channels Identified

I do not identify a substantive crypto exposure channel for Live Nation Entertainment during January 2017 to December 2024. The reviewed SEC filings did not show references to bitcoin, blockchain, crypto, digital assets, or NFTs in the company’s 2017 and 2024 annual reports, and the accessible Q3 2023 earnings-call transcript contained only a passing comment rejecting “easy crypto money” in the context of sponsorship/payment partners rather than describing any crypto business line. (sec.gov)

I therefore do not assign holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr. There is also no evidence in the reviewed sources that management or mainstream financial reporting treated Live Nation as a crypto-proxy equity, so invbase = 0. (roic.ai)

Weight Assignment

I assign unclass = 1.0 because the retrieved evidence supports a conventional live-entertainment and ticketing business model with no credible indication that crypto affects the stock through treasury holdings, mining, direct crypto financial intermediation, crypto-focused service provision, or crypto-directed infrastructure. The one transcript reference to crypto is exclusionary rather than affirmative, which is important under the instruction to require substantive exposure rather than incidental mention. (roic.ai)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that several company-hosted transcript PDFs for 2023–2024 were listed on the investor-relations site but could not be directly fetched through the browser tool because of access errors, so transcript evidence relied on one accessible third-party transcript page plus SEC filings. However, because the classification is negative and the available transcript quote does not indicate a crypto business line, this limitation does not appear outcome-changing. (investors.livenationentertainment.com)

A second limitation is that blockchain ticketing and NFT ticketing are topics sometimes discussed in the broader live-events industry, but the classification rules require explicit firm-level evidence. I did not find such evidence in the reviewed Live Nation filings or transcript materials for the sample period. (sec.gov)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 31 Oct 2023, Q3 2023 earnings call transcript: management said, “Thankfully, we didn’t take any of the easy crypto money at the time” when discussing payment-side partners — Roic AI transcript
  • 31 Oct 2023, Q3 2023 earnings call transcript: the same accessible transcript otherwise discussed ticketing, tours, sponsorship, and payment partners rather than any crypto business activity — Roic AI transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 22 Feb 2024, Form 10-K for fiscal 2023: SEC filing index identifies the annual report document for Live Nation Entertainment, Inc. — SEC filing index 2023 10-K
  • 23 Feb 2018, Form 10-K for fiscal 2017: text searches of the filing returned no matches for bitcoin, blockchain, crypto, digital asset, or NFT — SEC 2017 10-K
  • 21 Feb 2025 filing covering fiscal 2024: text searches of the filing returned no matches for bitcoin, blockchain, crypto, digital asset, or NFT — SEC 2024 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • Investor-relations archive for 2017–2024 lists earnings materials and transcripts across reporting periods, supporting transcript retrieval efforts for the sample window — Live Nation Financial Results archive
  • 9 Mar 2026, Reuters pickup on antitrust litigation: current financial-news framing centers on ticketing market power and pricing, not crypto exposure — Investing.com Reuters pickup

YOUGOV

  • Rank: 191 | Expo: 0.27 | id_str: GB00B1VQ6H25
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: annual reports and results archive (2017–2024), earnings call transcripts (2017, 2021, 2022, 2024)

Firm Overview

YouGov plc is a UK-listed international market research and data analytics company whose core activities center on polling, syndicated data products, and custom research services. In the reviewed 2017–2024 investor materials, the company is described as a data-and-insights business rather than a crypto operator, holder, miner, exchange, custodian, or crypto-infrastructure provider. (corporate.yougov.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

I do not identify a substantive crypto exposure channel for YouGov plc during January 2017 to December 2024. The company’s financial-reports archive shows a continuous set of annual reports and results materials across the sample window, while the accessible earnings-call transcript pages retrieved for 2017, 2021, 2022, and 2024 returned no matches for “crypto” or “blockchain.” (corporate.yougov.com)

Although YouGov has published consumer-facing content and survey work touching on blockchain or cryptocurrency as research topics, that reflects coverage of public attitudes and advertising themes rather than firm-level participation in crypto markets. Under the classification rules, such topical commentary does not support holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr, and I also found no credible evidence that financial media treated YouGov itself as a crypto-proxy stock, so invbase = 0. (yougov.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign unclass = 1.0 because the retrieved evidence supports a conventional market-research and analytics business with no documented crypto treasury position, mining activity, direct crypto financial intermediation, crypto-focused non-financial service line, or crypto-directed infrastructure exposure. The absence of crypto-related discussion across the sampled earnings-call transcripts is especially important because the instructions require substantive firm-level exposure rather than incidental mention of industry topics. (corporate.yougov.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that some company PDF filings and transcript documents could be indexed in search results but were not directly fetchable through the browser tool, so filing review relied partly on the company’s financial-reports archive and search-result snippets rather than full in-browser PDF text extraction. However, the accessible transcript pages spanning 2017, 2021, 2022, and 2024 all failed crypto-term searches, and I did not find corroborating financial-news evidence of any crypto business line, which makes the negative classification reasonably stable. (corporate.yougov.com)

A second limitation is that YouGov appears in some crypto-related web results because it conducts surveys about consumer attitudes toward digital assets and because third parties cite those surveys. Those mentions concern YouGov as a polling provider, not as a crypto-exposed operating company, so I exclude them from mechanism weighting. (fameex.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 29 Oct 2024, 2024 earnings call transcript: text search of the accessible transcript page returned no match for “crypto” — MarketScreener transcript
  • 29 Oct 2024, 2024 earnings call transcript: text search of the accessible transcript page returned no match for “blockchain” — MarketScreener transcript
  • 22 Mar 2022, H1 2022 earnings call transcript: text search of the accessible transcript page returned no match for “crypto” or “blockchain” — MarketScreener transcript
  • 23 Mar 2021, H1 2021 pre-recorded earnings call transcript: text search of the accessible transcript page returned no match for “crypto” or “blockchain” — MarketScreener transcript
  • 9 Oct 2017, 2017 earnings call transcript: text search of the accessible transcript page returned no match for “crypto” or “blockchain” — MarketScreener transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • Financial reports archive lists FY17 through FY24 annual reports and full-/half-year results for YouGov plc across the sample period — YouGov Financial Reports archive
  • 11 Nov 2024, FY24 Annual Report and Accounts: search snippet identifies the 2024 annual report and frames the year around strategy, AI, management, and ESG rather than crypto operations — YouGov 2024 Annual Report PDF
  • 8 Nov 2017, FY17 Annual Report and Accounts: company archive confirms publication of the FY17 annual report within the sample window — YouGov Financial Reports archive
  • 9 Oct 2017, FY17 Full-Year Results: search-result snippet describes investment in product development, technology, panel, and the Data Intelligence Unit, with no indication of crypto activity — FinancialReports.eu FY17 results snippet

Financial news / other:

  • 2021 company article: YouGov discussed blockchain in the context of advertising attribution technology, indicating editorial/commentary coverage rather than a disclosed crypto business line — YouGov article
  • 2025 third-party news item citing a YouGov survey on UK adults’ crypto holdings reflects YouGov as a polling source, not as a crypto operator or proxy equity — FameEX summary of YouGov survey
  • Search review of transcript listings and financial-report materials did not produce credible evidence of YouGov operating as a miner, exchange, custodian, treasury holder, or crypto-infrastructure supplier — MarketScreener transcript archive

BRILLIANT EARTH GROUP A

  • Rank: 192 | Expo: 0.27 | id_str: US1095041000
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 2024 Form 10-K, 2022 and 2023 earnings-call transcripts, investor relations materials

Firm Overview

Brilliant Earth Group, Inc. is a U.S.-listed digitally native jewelry retailer focused on ethically sourced diamonds, engagement rings, and fine jewelry.
