Across the reviewed 2022–2024 materials, the company described blockchain only as a traceability tool for diamond provenance within its retail supply chain, not as participation in cryptocurrency markets. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

I found no evidence that Brilliant Earth held crypto on its balance sheet, mined digital assets, operated crypto financial services, provided crypto-focused business services, or supplied crypto-directed infrastructure. The most relevant potentially confusing disclosure is the company’s repeated use of the phrase “blockchain-verified diamonds,” but in the 2024 10-K this clearly refers to supply-chain traceability for diamonds from mine to customer rather than custody, trading, payments, token issuance, or mining of digital assets. (sec.gov)

The sampled earnings-call sources likewise did not surface any crypto-related discussion. The accessible Q4 2023 transcript contained no matches for “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “blockchain,” and the earnings-transcript archive shows coverage for multiple public-company quarters since the September 2021 IPO, with no indication in the surfaced materials that crypto was a management topic. (fintool.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign unclass = 1.0 because the retrieved evidence supports a conventional consumer-discretionary jewelry retailer whose blockchain references are limited to product provenance and sourcing transparency. Under the project’s exclusion rules, use of blockchain as a record-keeping or traceability technology does not by itself support holders, miners, finserv, bizserv, or infrastr, and I found no credible evidence that financial media treated BRLT as a crypto-proxy equity. (sec.gov)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that some transcript pages were partly paywalled or timed out in-browser, so transcript review relied on accessible transcript pages, transcript-archive listings, and in-page term searches rather than complete retrieval of every quarterly call in 2021–2024. However, the available transcript evidence and the 2024 Form 10-K were sufficient to stabilize the classification because the only recurring blockchain language was about diamond traceability. (marketscreener.com)

A second limitation is that “blockchain” appears prominently in the 10-K and could be misread as crypto exposure. I exclude that language because the filing ties it specifically to tracking diamond provenance and responsible sourcing, not to cryptocurrency assets, exchanges, wallets, custody, or mining. (sec.gov)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 6 Feb 2024, Q4 2023 earnings call transcript: accessible transcript page returned no match for “crypto” — Fintool transcript
  • 6 Feb 2024, Q4 2023 earnings call transcript: accessible transcript page returned no match for “bitcoin” — Fintool transcript
  • 6 Feb 2024, Q4 2023 earnings call transcript: accessible transcript page returned no match for “blockchain” — Fintool transcript
  • 12 May 2022, Q1 2022 earnings call transcript listing: transcript exists for the public company, but the accessible MarketScreener page was subscriber-gated and did not expose crypto discussion in the retrievable text — MarketScreener transcript page
  • Seeking Alpha transcript archive lists BRLT earnings-call coverage across 2021–2024, supporting review of multiple public-company quarters within the sample window — Seeking Alpha BRLT transcript archive

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 13 Mar 2025, FY2024 Form 10-K: “Our technology enables … blockchain-verified transparency” in the customer and supply-chain experience — SEC Form 10-K HTML
  • 13 Mar 2025, FY2024 Form 10-K: the company said it was “among the first retail jewelers to offer blockchain-verified diamonds at scale,” describing diamond traceability from mining operator through cutting and polishing to the customer — SEC Form 10-K HTML
  • 13 Mar 2025, FY2024 Form 10-K: the filing contained no matches for “crypto” — SEC Form 10-K HTML
  • 13 Mar 2025, FY2024 Form 10-K: the filing contained no matches for “bitcoin” — SEC Form 10-K HTML

Financial news / other:

  • Brilliant Earth investor relations materials and earnings pages present the company as an ethically sourced fine-jewelry retailer rather than a crypto business — Brilliant Earth Investor Relations
  • Search review did not produce credible Reuters/CNBC/major financial-news evidence describing BRLT as a crypto proxy, miner, exchange, custodian, or treasury holder — Search results review

FD TECHNOLOGIES DEAD - DELIST.23/07/25

  • Rank: 193 | Expo: 0.27 | id_str: GB0031477770
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: annual reports/results materials (2017, 2022, 2024), company blockchain product news, earnings/transcript archive search

Firm Overview

FD Technologies plc, formerly First Derivatives plc, was a UK-listed software and consulting group whose core businesses during the 2017–2024 sample period were KX real-time analytics software and First Derivative capital-markets consulting. The retrieved company materials describe it as an enterprise data/analytics and capital-markets technology provider rather than a cryptocurrency business. (annualreports.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

I found no evidence that FD Technologies held crypto on its balance sheet, mined cryptocurrencies, operated a crypto exchange/broker/custody platform, or supplied purpose-built mining/datacentre/energy infrastructure. The strongest potentially confusing evidence is that KX technology was used in blockchain-related client solutions and that KX launched support for cryptocurrency trading workflows, but these references point to software sold into client trading and post-trade environments rather than FD Technologies itself handling, custodying, or investing in digital assets. (fdtechnologies.com)

The firm’s annual-report and results materials consistently frame the business around software, consulting, capital markets, and enterprise analytics, with no surfaced evidence of crypto revenue as a disclosed segment or of management presenting the company as a crypto proxy. Because the blockchain/crypto references are ancillary product-use cases within a broader enterprise software business, they do not clear the project threshold for a non-zero crypto-exposure mechanism weight. (annualreports.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign unclass = 1.0. The evidence shows a conventional enterprise software and consulting company with some blockchain- and crypto-related client use cases in its KX product stack, but not a firm that directly held digital assets, mined them, ran crypto financial services, or derived clearly material revenue from crypto-specific business services or infrastructure. Under the project rules, incidental enablement of blockchain/crypto workflows inside a general-purpose analytics platform is not enough for finserv, bizserv, or infrastr without stronger evidence of substantive, material crypto exposure. (fdtechnologies.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that accessible full earnings-call transcripts for 2017–2024 were sparse or subscriber-gated in search, so transcript review relied on surfaced transcript/archive pages and company results materials rather than a complete set of verbatim call transcripts. Because the instructions require a transcript quote to anchor any non-unclassified assignment, and I did not retrieve a usable crypto-substantive transcript quote, that constraint reinforces the conservative unclass outcome. (marketscreener.com)

A second limitation is that FD/KX did publish blockchain-related product announcements, including integration into a blockchain trade-processing platform and support for cryptocurrency trading. Those disclosures could suggest crypto adjacency, but the retrieved descriptions indicate software enablement for clients rather than direct participation in digital-asset markets by FD Technologies itself, so I exclude them from positive mechanism weights. (fdtechnologies.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 3 Jun 2025, FY2025 earnings-call transcript page: transcript exists for the company, but the accessible MarketScreener page was subscriber-gated and did not expose usable crypto discussion; no retrievable 2017–2024 transcript quote anchoring substantive crypto exposure was found — MarketScreener transcript page
  • Seeking Alpha company page for FDRVF identifies FD Technologies as a software and consulting company with KX and First Derivative segments; no surfaced transcript evidence indicated management treated crypto as a core business line — Seeking Alpha company page

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 15 May 2017, Annual Report 2017: the group was “engaged in the provision of a range of software services, particularly to finance, technology, energy and industrial organisations” — AnnualReports archive, 2017 annual report
  • 15 May 2017, Annual Report 2017: customers included leading investment banks and support across “credit, interest rate, foreign exchange, equity, cash and derivatives markets,” describing capital-markets software/services rather than crypto activities — AnnualReports archive, 2017 annual report
  • 8 Jun 2022, Annual Report 2022 listing: company investor-relations archive provides the FY2022 annual report within the sample period, supporting review of core business disclosures — FD Technologies results centre
  • 18 Jun 2024, Annual Report 2024 listing: company investor-relations archive provides the FY2024 annual report within the sample period, supporting review of the most recent pre-delisting business description — FD Technologies results centre
  • 10 May 2022, FY2022 results: the group described itself as comprising KX, First Derivative, and MRP, with KX positioned as real-time continuous-intelligence technology rather than a crypto business — FD Technologies FY2022 results release
  • 21 May 2024, FY2024 results: KX growth was described in financial services, aerospace and defence, and AI/vector-database markets, with no disclosed crypto segment — FD Technologies FY2024 results release

Financial news / other:

  • 2018, company news page: KX technology was integrated into a blockchain trade-processing platform, indicating software supplied to a blockchain-related client use case rather than FD Technologies directly transacting in crypto — FD Technologies news release
  • 2018, Financial IT: Kx launched support for cryptocurrency trading for client FX-platform users, covering assets such as Bitcoin and Ethereum; this points to product functionality for customers, not treasury holdings, mining, or direct crypto financial intermediation by FD Technologies — Financial IT article
  • Company IR materials consistently present FD Technologies as a software, analytics, and capital-markets technology group — FD Technologies presentations/results centre

WEB TRAVEL GROUP

  • Rank: 194 | Expo: 0.27 | id_str: AU000000WEB7
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.6, infrastr 0.4, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: earnings/investor presentation (FY22), annual reports (FY21, FY22, FY24), ASX release on LockTrip investment

Firm Overview

WEB Travel Group Limited, formerly Webjet Limited until September 2024, is an ASX-listed Australian travel company whose core business in the sample period was online travel distribution through WebBeds and Webjet OTA rather than digital assets or crypto finance. Within that broader travel-tech business, the company developed blockchain-based booking/reconciliation tools and took a minority equity stake in LockTrip, a blockchain-based travel marketplace. (investing.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence does not support holders, miners, or finserv. Web Travel Group did not disclose direct treasury crypto holdings, mining activity, or operation of a crypto exchange, brokerage, custody, payments, or investment product; instead, the recurring disclosures describe blockchain applications tied to travel-booking workflows and a minority associate investment in LockTrip. (webjet.sfo2.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com)

Positive exposure is best classified across bizserv and infrastr. bizserv applies because management described Rezchain as blockchain-based hotel booking verification/data reconciliation technology and described LockTrip as helping accelerate the group’s blockchain expertise and enhancing its travel offering. infrastr also applies because Rezchain was presented as a blockchain-based platform implemented across WebBeds and potentially migratable onto Hydra chain, which is closer to travel-industry blockchain infrastructure than to ordinary consulting or marketing services. (webtravelgroup.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign bizserv 0.6 and infrastr 0.4. The larger weight goes to bizserv because the company’s own language emphasized business use cases—hotel booking verification, reconciliation, and OTA/B2B travel integration—rather than monetizing generic blockchain infrastructure for third parties. I still assign meaningful infrastr weight because Rezchain was explicitly described as a blockchain-based platform implemented across WebBeds, and management discussed possible migration of that platform onto Hydra chain; that is substantive infrastructure exposure, albeit clearly peripheral to the group’s overall travel revenues. (webtravelgroup.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that accessible full 2017–2024 earnings-call transcripts were sparse; I therefore used an FY22 investor/results presentation as the transcript-like management anchor available in public company materials rather than a conventional sell-side transcript page. That still provides a direct management quote, but certainty is kept at 3 rather than 4. (webtravelgroup.com)

A second limitation is that the LockTrip exposure partly sits in an associate investment. Because the investment was strategic and tied to blockchain expertise and product integration, I treat it as evidence of substantive crypto-adjacent business activity rather than pure passive holding; however, the annual reports also show the investment was small and later impaired to nil, so crypto was not a major business line. (webjet.sfo2.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 19 May 2022, FY22 Results investor presentation: “Rezchain delivers blockchain based hotel booking verification technology. Implemented across all WebBeds platforms since FY19.” — Web Travel Group FY22 Results Investor Presentation
  • 19 May 2022, FY22 Results investor presentation: “Locktrip is a blockchain based B2C travel marketplace. Our investment is helping accelerate our own blockchain expertise” — Web Travel Group FY22 Results Investor Presentation

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 19 Aug 2021, FY2021 annual report: Webjet acquired a 25% stake in LockTrip for US$4.1 million; LockTrip was described as a blockchain start-up whose marketplace was powered by the LOC token and Hydra coin — FY2021 Annual Report
  • 19 May 2022, FY2022 annual report: the company repeated that LockTrip was a blockchain start-up and recorded the investment in associates at A$5.4 million, with only A$0.07 million share of net loss for the year — FY2022 Annual Report
  • 22 May 2024, FY2024 annual report: the LockTrip carrying value had been reduced to nil after a prior-period A$5.9 million impairment, citing “volatility and general decline in market values of cryptocurrencies” — FY2024 Annual Report
  • 18 Mar 2021, ASX release: management said Webjet’s interest in LockTrip included accelerated blockchain expertise, OTA integration, and “potential to migrate Webjet’s Rezchain application onto the Hydra chain” — ASX Release: Investment in Locktrip

Financial news / other:

  • Current company investments page, reflecting the historical strategic rationale: Web Travel Group says its 25% LockTrip stake aimed at “Accelerated development of its blockchain expertise” and access to a B2C audience of crypto enthusiasts — Web Travel Group Investments page
  • 16 Feb 2018, ASX releases archive: the company maintained a dedicated “Webjet Launches Blockchain Initiative” announcement within its historical releases, corroborating that blockchain activity was a deliberate operating initiative rather than a one-off mention — Web Travel Group ASX Releases archive

ENDEAVOR GROUP HOLDINGS A

  • Rank: 195 | Expo: 0.26 | id_str: US29260Y1091
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: earnings-call transcripts (2021–2024), SEC filings (2023–2024), company press releases

Firm Overview

Endeavor Group Holdings, Inc. was a U.S.-listed global sports and entertainment company whose businesses included talent representation, owned sports properties such as UFC, events and experiences, marketing, and sports betting/data technology through OpenBet and IMG ARENA. Across the 2017–2024 sample period, the accessible public materials describe sports, media, betting, and entertainment operations rather than substantive cryptocurrency-facing activity.

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence does not support holders, miners, or finserv. Public earnings-call transcripts and SEC filings reviewed for 2021–2024 did not disclose treasury crypto holdings, mining activity, crypto exchange/brokerage/custody/payments operations, or crypto-backed investment products. The evidence also does not support bizserv or infrastr, because the company’s technology references center on sports betting, streaming, and entertainment platforms rather than blockchain infrastructure or non-financial services provided to the crypto ecosystem. Accordingly, the firm is best classified as unclass.

Weight Assignment

I assign unclass 1.0. The searched transcript, filing, and company-site evidence did not produce a substantive crypto exposure channel during January 2017 to December 2024; the closest adjacent activity was sports betting/data technology through OpenBet and IMG ARENA, which is not crypto exposure under the stated taxonomy.

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that accessible transcript coverage before the 2021 IPO was sparse for the listed entity, and some transcript pages were inaccessible or incomplete. Even so, at least two accessible post-IPO earnings-call transcripts plus SEC filings and company materials were sufficient to stabilize the classification, and none contained substantive crypto, blockchain, bitcoin, or NFT exposure attributable to Endeavor’s own business model. None identified.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 16 Mar 2022, Q4 2021 earnings call transcript: management highlighted acquisitions including OpenBet and described growth drivers in sports, events, and media, with no substantive crypto discussion — Motley Fool Q4 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
  • 11 Aug 2022, Q2 2022 earnings call transcript: management referred to “revised timing of OpenBet's close” and “IoT initiatives,” but no crypto or blockchain operating exposure — Motley Fool Q2 2022 Earnings Call Transcript
  • 28 Feb 2024, Q4 2023 transcript listing: public transcript coverage exists for the period, but accessible snippets likewise show ordinary operating commentary rather than crypto exposure — Seeking Alpha EDR Earnings Call Transcripts

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 2023 Form 10-K: SEC filing describes the business as sports and entertainment operations and does not surface crypto-, bitcoin-, blockchain-, or NFT-related line items in the filing search results — 2023 Form 10-K
  • 2024 Form 10-K: the filing covers Endeavor’s consolidated operations and transaction activity; no crypto-specific disclosure appeared in the relevant filing search results — 2024 Form 10-K
  • 27 Feb 2025 results release discussing 2024: the company described OpenBet and IMG ARENA as the Sports Data & Technology segment and reported cash/debt figures, not crypto assets or crypto revenue — Endeavor Releases Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results

Financial news / other:

  • 11 Nov 2024, company press release: Endeavor described OpenBet and IMG ARENA as sports betting and data businesses, which is outside the crypto-exposure categories here — Endeavor Announces Management Buyout of OpenBet and IMG ARENA
  • Company overview page: Endeavor presented itself as a sports and entertainment company with businesses in representation, events, media, streaming, and related technology, with no crypto positioning — Endeavor Corporate Website
  • Endeavor U.S. network archive: the broader Endeavor entrepreneurial network includes blockchain-related portfolio/community content such as BitPay and Securitize, but this is separate from Endeavor Group Holdings, Inc. and not evidence of listed-company crypto exposure — Endeavor U.S. Blockchain & Crypto Archive

COGENT BIOSCIENCES

  • Rank: 196 | Expo: 0.26 | id_str: US19240Q2012
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: earnings-call transcripts listings (2021–2024), SEC filings (2022–2024), company investor relations materials

Firm Overview

Cogent Biosciences, Inc. is a U.S.-listed clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on developing precision therapies for genetically driven diseases, particularly oncology and mast-cell disorders. Across the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, the reviewed public materials describe drug development, clinical trials, financing, and cash runway rather than substantive cryptocurrency-facing operations.

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence does not support holders, miners, or finserv. Reviewed SEC filings for fiscal 2022 and 2023 showed cash, marketable securities, R&D spending, and equity financing disclosures, but no bitcoin, crypto, blockchain, NFT, mining, exchange, custody, payment, or crypto-product activity. The evidence also does not support bizserv or infrastr, because the firm’s disclosed business is biotech R&D and commercialization preparation rather than services to crypto firms or provision of mining/blockchain infrastructure. Accordingly, the firm is best classified as unclass.

Weight Assignment

I assign unclass 1.0. Transcript coverage exists for 2021–2024 and company investor-relations releases for 2024 consistently characterize Cogent as a pre-revenue/clinical-stage biotechnology company, while SEC filing searches produced no substantive crypto-related disclosures; the classification therefore stabilizes as no meaningful crypto exposure during the sample period.

Ambiguities / Limitations

A limitation is that the accessible transcript evidence was mainly transcript index/listing coverage rather than fully scraped call text, because some third-party transcript pages were access-restricted. Even so, the transcript record confirms regular earnings-call coverage for 2021–2024, and the combination of those records with searchable SEC filings and company IR materials was sufficient to stabilize the classification. None identified.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 2021–2024, Seeking Alpha transcript listing: public transcript coverage exists for multiple Cogent Biosciences earnings calls during the sample window, anchoring review of management commentary availability — Seeking Alpha COGT Earnings Call Transcripts
  • 2024, Morningstar transcript page: Cogent Biosciences had earnings-call transcript coverage as a tracked public issuer, consistent with ordinary biotech financial reporting rather than crypto-focused communications — Morningstar COGT Earnings Transcript Page

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 14 Mar 2023, Form 10-K for fiscal 2022: the balance sheet reported cash and cash equivalents of $139.9 million and marketable securities of $119.4 million, with no bitcoin, crypto, blockchain, or NFT references in filing text searches — 2022 Form 10-K
  • 26 Feb 2024, Annual Report for fiscal 2023: searchable filing text likewise returned no matches for bitcoin, crypto, blockchain, or NFT, while describing Cogent as a biotech issuer with securities purchase and registration-rights agreements — 2023 Annual Report / 10-K
  • 8 May 2024, Q1 2024 results release: Cogent described itself as “a biotechnology company focused on developing precision therapies for genetically defined diseases,” with discussion centered on pipeline progress and financial results — Q1 2024 Financial Results

Financial news / other:

  • 26 Feb 2024, company results release: Cogent’s fourth-quarter and full-year 2023 update emphasized clinical programs and disclosed a $225 million oversubscribed PIPE financing, not crypto activity — Q4 and Full Year 2023 Financial Results
  • 8 Aug 2024, company results release: the second-quarter 2024 update focused on clinical milestones and quarterly financials, again without crypto-related operations or treasury disclosures — Q2 2024 Financial Results
  • 25 Feb 2025 release discussing year-end 2024 position: management highlighted funding runway, ATM proceeds, and clinical-readout plans, consistent with biotech cash management rather than digital-asset exposure — Q4 and Full Year 2024 Financial Results

AQUIS EXCHANGE DEAD - DELIST.02/07/25

  • Rank: 197 | Expo: 0.26 | id_str: GB00BD5JNK30
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.2, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.8, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: company results materials (2021–2024), company/third-party crypto ETP listing announcements (2021), earnings-call/results presentation for FY 2023

Firm Overview

Aquis Exchange PLC was a UK-listed exchange services group operating pan-European equities trading venues, a growth and regulated primary market, market-data services, and exchange-technology licensing. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, its crypto relevance came from enabling regulated trading access for third-party crypto ETPs and from supplying exchange/market infrastructure rather than from holding or mining digital assets itself.

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports infrastr because Aquis’s core business was exchange-market infrastructure, trading venues, market data, and exchange technology, and it explicitly provided the venue on which third-party bitcoin ETPs traded in the UK. It also supports finserv because Aquis directly operated regulated trading markets and management framed the June 2021 bitcoin ETP admission as a response to investor and client demand for digital-asset exposure in a regulated environment. The evidence does not support holders or miners, as reviewed results materials discuss cash, divisional revenues, and exchange operations rather than proprietary crypto treasury or mining activity. It also does not support bizserv, because the relevant exposure is better captured as direct market infrastructure and trading-venue activity rather than ancillary non-financial services.

Weight Assignment

I assign infrastr 0.8 and finserv 0.2. The larger weight goes to infrastructure because Aquis’s recurring business model was overwhelmingly exchange operation, software, data, and venue provision, with crypto appearing as a limited product-listing/admission channel rather than a disclosed major revenue stream. A smaller finserv weight is still appropriate because Aquis did directly facilitate trading in crypto-linked ETPs on its regulated market and publicly characterized that as serving institutional demand for digital assets.

Ambiguities / Limitations

Accessible transcript evidence was limited: I found a FY 2023 results presentation and results commentary, but not a broad set of fully scraped earnings-call transcripts across 2017–2024 from the usual transcript vendors. To satisfy the transcript anchor requirement, I relied on the FY 2023 results presentation plus management quotation in company results materials, while using 2021 crypto-ETP admission announcements and annual-results disclosures for corroboration. The evidence does not show that crypto was a material standalone segment, so the classification is exposure through venue/infrastructure rather than a crypto-focused core business.

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 21 Mar 2024, FY 2023 results presentation: “Aquis is a technology-driven challenger exchange group” and divisional split showed Markets £10.9m, Technologies £6.3m, Data £3.7m, Stock Exchange £1.8m, anchoring the firm’s exchange/infrastructure business model — FY 2023 Results Presentation
  • 21 Mar 2024, FY 2023 results release: CEO said Aquis delivered growth “across all divisions,” with emphasis on market share, technology contracts, and consolidated tape rather than proprietary crypto activity — Aquis Final Results 2023

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 21 Mar 2024, annual results release: Aquis described itself as “a creator and facilitator of next-generation financial markets” and reported cash and cash equivalents of £14.8m, with no indication of bitcoin or crypto holdings on balance sheet — Aquis Final Results 2023
  • 21 Mar 2024, annual results release: net revenue split by division was Markets £10.9m, Technologies £6.3m, Data £3.7m, and Aquis Stock Exchange £1.8m, indicating crypto was not disclosed as a separate material revenue segment — Aquis Final Results 2023

Financial news / other:

  • 1 Jun 2021, company/news announcement: Aquis announced ETC Group’s BTCE would trade via Aquis Exchange MTFs in London and Paris, described as the first cryptocurrency ETP available for trading on a UK market or European MTF — Aquis: ETC Group Launches First Bitcoin ETP In UK On Aquis Exchange
  • 1 Jun 2021, Business Wire announcement: CEO Alasdair Haynes said, “Investors are increasingly accepting digital assets as a bona fide asset class” and that Aquis was responding to client demand by allowing access in a “liquid, regulated environment” — Business Wire: ETC Group Launches First Bitcoin ETP In UK On Aquis Exchange
  • 7 Jun 2021, CoinDesk: 21Shares also listed a bitcoin ETP on Aquis Exchange, with Haynes saying Aquis was responding to institutional demand in the UK for digital assets — CoinDesk: 21Shares to List Bitcoin ETP in London on Aquis Exchange

FIRST FOUNDATION

  • Rank: 198 | Expo: 0.26 | id_str: US32026V1044
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 1.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 0
  • Primary sources: earnings-call transcripts (Q1 and Q3 2021), company crypto-partnership announcement (April 2021), 2024 Form 10-K

Firm Overview

First Foundation Inc. is a U.S.-listed regional financial services company that operated through First Foundation Bank and First Foundation Advisors, offering banking, wealth management, trust, insurance, and related services. Within the January 2017 to December 2024 sample period, its crypto relevance came from a 2021 initiative to offer Bitcoin-related products and services to clients through a partnership with NYDIG and Fiserv, rather than from proprietary crypto holdings or mining. (investor.ff-inc.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

The evidence supports finserv because management explicitly discussed adding Bitcoin execution and related digital-asset solutions for clients through the bank’s platform, and the company announced that customers would be enabled to buy, sell, and hold Bitcoin through its banking environment. This is direct crypto financial intermediation rather than an ancillary service, because the planned offering involved client access to Bitcoin-related financial products within First Foundation’s banking network. The evidence does not support holders, since the 2024 Form 10-K contains no Bitcoin, crypto, digital-asset, or blockchain references and the NYDIG item appears as an equity investment rather than a crypto treasury position. It also does not support miners, bizserv, or infrastr, as the firm was not described as mining, selling mining equipment, or providing non-financial support services to the crypto ecosystem. (fool.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign finserv 1.0. The classification is anchored by management’s 2021 earnings-call remarks and the April 20, 2021 company announcement, both of which frame crypto exposure as a client-facing Bitcoin product and platform initiative within the bank and wealth-management offering. Although the exposure appears limited and later filings do not show crypto becoming a major disclosed segment by 2024, the mechanism itself is still best classified wholly as crypto financial services rather than as holdings or infrastructure. (fool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Accessible transcript retrieval was imperfect because full direct scraping of some transcript pages was blocked, so the transcript anchor relies on search-excerpted earnings-call transcript text from The Motley Fool/Seeking Alpha index pages rather than a fully opened transcript page. The strongest crypto evidence is concentrated in 2021; by the 2024 Form 10-K, crypto is no longer a visible standalone theme, apart from the residual reference to the NYDIG equity investment’s 2022 valuation loss. That limits materiality and keeps RevShare low, but it does not overturn the mechanism classification because the company explicitly pursued Bitcoin-related client financial products during the sample period. (fool.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 27 Apr 2021, Q1 2021 earnings call transcript excerpt: “with our recently announced strategic investment in the institutional bitcoin provider, NYDIG, we are seeking ways to add bitcoin-related solutions to our platform” — The Motley Fool: First Foundation Inc (FFWM) Q1 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
  • 26 Oct 2021, Q3 2021 earnings call transcript excerpt: management said First Foundation would give customers “the ability to have execution with Bitcoin” and added, “We continue to pursue that the Cryptocurrency offering through our collaboration with NYDIG and Fiserv” — The Motley Fool: First Foundation Inc (FFWM) Q3 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
  • 26 Oct 2021, transcript index corroboration: Seeking Alpha’s FFWM transcript archive shows broad earnings-call coverage across 2020–2025, supporting the existence of the cited 2021 call set — Seeking Alpha: FFWM Earnings Call Transcripts

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 20 Apr 2021, company announcement: First Foundation said it made a strategic investment in NYDIG and would collaborate to provide clients with “efficient and secure access to Bitcoin-related financial products and services within the traditional banking network” — First Foundation Inc. Announces Strategic Investment in Institutional Bitcoin Provider NYDIG
  • 20 Apr 2021, company announcement: the bank said Fiserv integration would enable customers to “buy, sell, and hold Bitcoin” within First Foundation’s existing banking environment — First Foundation Inc. Announces Strategic Investment in Institutional Bitcoin Provider NYDIG
  • 17 Mar 2025, Form 10-K for FY 2024: searches of the filing show no matches for “bitcoin,” “crypto,” “digital asset,” or “blockchain,” indicating no ongoing disclosed crypto operating segment or treasury position by year-end 2024 — 2024 Form 10-K
  • 17 Mar 2025, Form 10-K note disclosure: “Valuation loss on equity investment at December 31, 2022 relates to the Company’s equity investment in NYDIG”; 2024 other income shows only a $204 thousand valuation gain on equity investment, indicating peripheral rather than core crypto exposure — 2024 Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • 30 Jan 2025, Q4 2024 earnings release: First Foundation described itself as a financial services company centered on banking and wealth management, with no crypto line item or crypto strategy highlighted in current results materials — First Foundation Inc. Reports Fourth Quarter 2024 Financial Results
  • 2 Jul 2025, investor-relations company description: First Foundation continued to describe its core business as personal banking, business banking, and private wealth management, which supports the view that crypto was an ancillary product initiative rather than a dominant business line — First Foundation Inc. Announces Second Quarter 2025 Earnings Conference Call Details

NETSOL TECHS.

  • Rank: 199 | Expo: 0.26 | id_str: US64115A4022
  • Weight summary: holders 0.0, miners 0.0, finserv 0.0, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 1.0
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 0
  • Certainty: NA
  • RevShare: NA | AssetShare: NA
  • Primary sources: 10-K filings (2020, 2023, 2025), investor-relations press releases, earnings-call transcript archive

Firm Overview

NETSOL Technologies, Inc. is a Nasdaq-listed enterprise software provider focused on asset finance, leasing, and automotive digital retail solutions for OEMs, dealerships, and financial institutions. Across the reviewed 2017–2024 materials, its technology strategy included AI, IoT, cloud, and some blockchain references within its Otoz mobility platform, but the company was not presented as operating a crypto business. (ir.netsoltech.com)

Exposure Channels Identified

No substantive crypto-exposure channel was identified under the project’s mechanism definitions. The strongest blockchain-related evidence is descriptive product language in the fiscal 2020 Form 10-K stating that Otoz was built with technologies including blockchain and that blockchain could be used for vehicle operations and digital-twin transparency, but this does not show the firm holding crypto, mining, running crypto financial services, or providing clearly crypto-specific business or infrastructure services. (sec.gov)

Later evidence points away from a crypto classification. The fiscal 2025 Form 10-K contains no matches for “blockchain,” “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “digital asset,” and the 2024 Otoz press release frames the platform around digital retail and mobility orchestration rather than digital assets. The company’s accessible earnings-call archive confirms broad transcript coverage exists across 2017–2024, but no retrievable transcript quote was found showing substantive crypto activity. (sec.gov)

Weight Assignment

I assign unclass 1.0. NETSOL’s evidence shows a software company serving finance and automotive clients, with some historical use of the word “blockchain” as part of a broader innovation stack for Otoz, but not a firm with material crypto holdings, mining operations, crypto financial intermediation, or clearly crypto-directed support/infrastructure revenues. Under the threshold principle and exclusion rules, generic blockchain feature language is insufficient for a non-zero mechanism weight. (ir.netsoltech.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

Accessible transcript retrieval was imperfect because individual Seeking Alpha transcript pages were blocked, so the review relied on the transcript archive plus filings and company press releases. That said, the available evidence still does not support a non-zero crypto classification: the blockchain references appear product-descriptive and automotive-focused, not tied to digital-asset activity, and by the fiscal 2025 filing even those references were no longer visible. (seekingalpha.com)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 14 Feb 2017 to 13 Nov 2024, transcript archive: Seeking Alpha’s NTWK earnings-call archive shows broad transcript coverage across the sample period, but no accessible transcript quote evidencing substantive crypto activity was retrievable — Seeking Alpha: NTWK Earnings Call Transcripts
  • 13 Feb 2018 / 27 Sep 2017 / 14 Feb 2022, transcript fetch attempts: direct opening of several individual NTWK transcript pages returned access errors, limiting transcript scraping for quote extraction — Seeking Alpha transcript pages via archive

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 28 Sep 2020, Form 10-K: Otoz was described as using “Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Blockchain, DevOps and APIs” in its technology stack — SEC Form 10-K for FY 2020
  • 28 Sep 2020, Form 10-K: the filing also said Otoz’s “digital twin” tracked mileage and service data through blockchain and that the automotive value chain could be managed via blockchain and smart contracts — SEC Form 10-K for FY 2020
  • 22 Sep 2023, Form 10-K: NETSOL described itself as designing and exporting software products to the automobile financing, leasing, banking, and financial services industries, with no crypto operating description — NETSOL 2023 Form 10-K
  • 27 Sep 2024, Form 10-K for FY 2025 filing date: searches of the filing found no matches for “blockchain,” “crypto,” “bitcoin,” or “digital asset,” indicating no disclosed crypto segment or treasury exposure by that point — SEC Form 10-K for FY 2025
  • 1 Feb 2024, company press release: Otoz 2.0 was presented as a digital retail and mobility platform for automotive clients, not as a crypto or digital-asset service — NETSOL Technologies Announces Major Updates on Otoz

Financial news / other:

  • 10 Feb 2021, company press release: management said Otoz was piloting a project to digitize an automotive customer and expand SaaS/cloud offerings, again without crypto activity — NETSOL Technologies Reports Fiscal Second Quarter 2021 Financial Results
  • 8 Mar 2021, Leasing Life profile: NETSOL’s Otoz unit was described as exploring machine learning, AI, and blockchain as emerging technologies, which corroborates an innovation narrative rather than a crypto business model — Leasing Life: The NETSOL blueprint: always-on innovation

BED BATH AND BEYOND

  • Rank: 200 | Expo: 0.26 | id_str: US6903701018
  • Weight summary: holders 0.1, miners 0.0, finserv 0.8, bizserv 0.0, infrastr 0.0, unclass 0.1
  • Investor base amplifier (invbase): 1
  • Certainty: 3
  • RevShare: 1 | AssetShare: 1
  • Primary sources: 2020 Form 10-K, 2017 and 2018 earnings-call transcripts, 2021 press release on Medici/tZERO restructuring

Firm Overview

The relevant entity for this identifier is the company now known as Beyond, Inc., formerly Overstock.com, which acquired the Bed Bath & Beyond intellectual property in 2023 and traded under ticker OSTK during most of the 2017–2024 sample period. Across the reviewed materials, the company’s crypto exposure came primarily through its blockchain and digital-asset financial-services activities centered on Medici Ventures and tZERO, rather than through the home-goods retail business. (sec.gov)

Exposure Channels Identified

The dominant exposure channel is finserv. In the 2020 Form 10-K, Overstock said Medici’s initiatives were focused on financial transaction issues, especially securities settlement, and identified tZERO as a financial technology company pursuing financial applications for blockchain technology; the same filing also described tZERO Crypto as a mobile app allowing consumers to buy, sell, and hold supported cryptocurrencies. (annualreports.com)

A smaller holders weight is also warranted because the 2020 Form 10-K states that Overstock’s board had authorized the company to retain up to 50% of customer sales revenues paid in bitcoin and that it at times held bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies directly. However, the same evidence suggests those holdings were limited rather than balance-sheet-defining, and management said on the March 2018 call that the company did not have “much Bitcoin” and had sold a substantial amount previously. (annualreports.com)

The evidence also supports invbase = 1. On the Q4 2017 earnings call, management explicitly said the correlation between Bitcoin’s movements and Overstock’s stock had been 91% since 2017, and CNBC contemporaneously described traders as betting on Overstock as a way to play bitcoin/blockchain momentum. (fintool.com)

Weight Assignment

I assign finserv 0.8, holders 0.1, unclass 0.1. The heaviest weight goes to finserv because the firm directly owned and developed blockchain-based capital-markets and crypto-trading businesses through tZERO and tZERO Crypto, and management discussed security tokens, digital securities trading, and token-market infrastructure repeatedly in calls and filings. A small holders weight is justified by the explicit policy of retaining a portion of bitcoin-denominated sales, but filings and transcript evidence indicate that these holdings were secondary to the broader financial-services strategy and not a major standalone treasury thesis. Residual unclass 0.1 reflects that the listed company was still predominantly a retail/e-commerce business for most of the sample, so crypto did not cleanly define the whole firm. (fintool.com)

Ambiguities / Limitations

The firm identity required care because “Bed Bath & Beyond” only became the public-facing name after Overstock’s 2023 acquisition of the brand IP; most crypto-relevant evidence during 2017–2024 sits under Overstock.com / Beyond, not the legacy bankrupt Bed Bath & Beyond operating company. Transcript retrieval from Seeking Alpha was not directly usable here, so the earnings-call anchor quotes were taken from Fintool transcript pages surfaced via search. Because public filings repeatedly indicate that Medici/tZERO had limited or not-yet-meaningful revenue relative to the retail business, the classification is clear on mechanism but more modest on materiality. (sec.gov)

Evidence

Earnings calls:

  • 9 Nov 2017, Q3 2017 earnings call transcript: management said “Overstock is a wonderful testbed for developing blockchain supply chain technologies” while discussing selling the retail layer to pursue “all the blockchain stuff” — Fintool: OSTK Q3 2017 transcript
  • 15 Mar 2018, Q4 2017 earnings call transcript: management said “We are setting up a security token trading platform” and referred to OSTK.P as “a digital security” — Fintool: OSTK Q4 2017 transcript
  • 15 Mar 2018, Q4 2017 earnings call transcript: management also said, “we don't have much Bitcoin” and noted prior sales of holdings, while observing a high stock/bitcoin correlation — Fintool: OSTK Q4 2017 transcript

Filings (10-K / press releases):

  • 26 Feb 2021, Form 10-K for FY2020: Overstock said it began blockchain initiatives in 2014, that Medici focused on blockchain applications including securities settlement, and that tZERO pursued financial applications for blockchain technologies — Overstock 2020 Annual Report
  • 26 Feb 2021, Form 10-K for FY2020: the filing stated that tZERO Crypto’s business was a mobile application allowing consumers to “buy, sell and hold supported cryptocurrencies” — Overstock 2020 Annual Report
  • 26 Feb 2021, Form 10-K for FY2020: the board had authorized Overstock to retain up to 50% of customer sales revenues paid in bitcoin, and the company said it sometimes held bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies directly — Overstock 2020 Annual Report
  • 3 May 2021, company press release: Overstock said it remained the sole limited partner of Medici Ventures after the Pelion transaction and described Medici as created to leverage blockchain technology across money and banking, capital markets, supply chain, and voting — Overstock completes Medici Ventures transaction
  • 24 Feb 2023, Form 10-K for FY2022: Overstock said it held a minority equity interest in tZERO accounted for under the equity method and that tZERO had become a significant subsidiary for reporting purposes — Overstock 2022 Form 10-K

Financial news / other:

  • 17 Oct 2017, CNBC: traders were described as betting on Overstock because of its blockchain investments, showing explicit crypto-proxy investor behavior — CNBC: Traders bet on Overstock over bitcoin's blockchain technology
  • 10 Nov 2017, CNBC: Overstock’s call drew unusual investor attention as markets focused on whether the company could pivot toward bitcoin and blockchain — CNBC: Overstock may soar if sells e-commerce, focuses on bitcoin blockchain
  • 13 Dec 2017, CNBC: Overstock shares jumped after a blockchain-unit property-registry announcement, corroborating market treatment of the stock as a blockchain exposure vehicle — CNBC: Overstock spikes after blockchain unit announces for-profit property registry
